Introduction: Start New Hero
序章: 建立新英雄

Introduction: Restore Game
序章: 讀取進度

Introduction: Go to Town
序章: 前往城鎮

Introduction: Continue Normally
序章: 正常繼續

Introduction: Where to, Hero?
序章: 英雄，要往哪去？

Introduction: The software you are now using was produced through the efforts of many people: designers, artists, programmers, musicians, and lots of other hard-working folks.
序章: 您現在使用的這套軟體，是由許多人共同努力打造的成果：設計師、美術、程式設計師、音樂家，以及許多辛勤付出的夥伴。

Introduction: If you make copies of this software for any reason other than to make a personal backup, you are not only breaking the law, but raising the cost of software for all legitimate users.  PLEASE DO NOT MAKE ILLEGAL COPIES OF THIS GAME!
序章: 若你基於個人備份以外的任何理由複製本軟體，不僅觸犯法律，更會推高所有正當使用者的軟體成本。請勿非法複製本遊戲！

Introduction: Oh, by the way . . .  You will need the information contained in the printed documentation to successfully complete this game.  In other words, it's not just the law . . . It's a good idea.
序章: 對了…你會需要印刷手冊裡的資訊，才能順利完成本遊戲。換句話說，這不只是法律問題…也是個好主意。

Introduction: 10 minute rest
序章: 休息 10 分鐘

Introduction: 30 minute rest
序章: 休息 30 分鐘

Introduction: 60 minute rest
序章: 休息 60 分鐘

Introduction: Sleep all night
序章: 睡到天亮

Introduction: Stay awake
序章: 保持清醒

Introduction: You just can't sleep during the daytime.
序章: 你在白天就是睡不著。

Introduction: You awake as the sun begins to rise.
序章: 你在旭日初升時醒來。

Introduction: How long, Hero?
序章: 要多久呢，英雄？

Introduction: You're too impatient to rest right now.
序章: 你現在太焦躁，靜不下來休息。

Introduction: After %d minutes rest, you feel better.
序章: 休息了%d分鐘後，你感覺好多了。

Introduction: Your hunger has been totally satisfied.  You don't need another piece of fruit.
序章: 你已經完全飽了。不需要再吃水果了。

Introduction: The fruit is very soft and juicy. It would be impossible to keep in your pack.
序章: 這水果軟軟的、很多汁，根本沒辦法帶在包裡。

Introduction: The stone has been moved.
序章: 石頭被挪開了。

Introduction: The large stone appears to be ancient and deliberately placed.  Marks carved into the stone almost look like writing.
序章: 這塊大石頭看起來很古老，還是刻意放在這裡的。石頭上刻的痕跡看起來幾乎像是某種文字。

Introduction: In a hole, hidden beneath the ancient stone, someone has placed a scroll.
序章: 在這塊古老石頭下方的洞裡，有人放了一卷捲軸。

Introduction: You took the scroll.
序章: 你拿起了捲軸。

Introduction: The stone has the words "Erana's Peace" carved upon the top.  There are some runes carved along the side.
序章: 石頭的頂端刻著「伊瑞娜的安寧之地」字樣。側面還刻著一些符文。

Introduction: It reads:  "If thy Will is Magic, so shall I share.  Open this Stone and claim what is there."
序章: 內容寫著：「若汝意志乃魔法者，吾願與爾分享。開啟此石，領取所有寶藏。」

Introduction: The hole that was beneath the stone is empty.
序章: 原本在石頭下方的洞現在是空的。

Introduction: No amount of physical effort will be able to move this stone.
序章: 再怎麼費力氣也動不了這塊石頭。

Introduction: The grasses in the meadow are covered over with flowers.
序章: 草地上鋪滿了鮮花。

Introduction: All kinds of colorful and fragrant flowers and grasses cover the meadow.
序章: 各式各樣的繽紛花朵和香草覆蓋著整個草地。

Introduction: The meadow is an oasis at the edge of the snowline.  The rocky hillside looks very steep and slippery.
序章: 這個草地是雪線邊緣的綠洲。岩石山坡看起來陡峭又滑溜。

Introduction: You have all the flowers you need.
序章: 你已經有了需要的花朵。

Introduction: As you pick a variety of the sweet-smelling flowers, they seem to glow in your hands.  You put them safely away.
序章: 當你摘下各式各樣香氣四溢的花朵時，它們在你手上似乎閃閃發光。你小心地把它們放好。

Introduction: You take another handful of the lovely, fragrant flowers.
序章: 你又摘了一把香氣迷人的花朵。

Introduction: This entire area is magical.  In particular, the fruit on the tree radiates magic, and you sense that a spell has been placed on the large stone.  All of the magic has a warm, nurturing feel to it.
序章: 整個區域都充滿魔法。特別是樹上的果實散發著魔法氣息，你感覺大石頭上也被施加了某種魔法。所有這些魔法都給人溫暖、滋養的感受。

Introduction: The very air in this meadow radiates feelings of calmness and peace.  You wouldn't be able to tell if the spell worked here.
序章: 這個草地的空氣本身就散發出寧靜和平的氣息。你甚至無法分辨法術在這裡是否會起作用。

Introduction: You can't imagine hurting anything in this meadow.
序章: 你無法想像在這個草地裡傷害任何東西。

Introduction: The sky is clear.
序章: 天空晴朗無雲。

Introduction: The sun feels warm.
序章: 陽光暖洋洋的。

Introduction: The stars shine.
序章: 繁星閃爍。

Introduction: To the north, the high, snowy peaks of the Dragon Smoke mountains are clearly visible.
序章: 北方遠處，龍煙山脈高聳的雪峰清晰可見。

Introduction: You are at the snowline.  All around you, except for the forest, the rocks and mountains glisten with slippery snow.
序章: 你已經來到雪線。除了森林以外的四周，岩石和山脈都被滑溜的積雪所覆蓋。

Introduction: The rock faces are slippery with melted snow.
序章: 岩石表面因融化的雪水而溼滑。

Introduction: You sleep comfortably among the fragrant flowers.
序章: 你舒舒服服地在香氣四溢的花叢中睡著了。

Introduction: The sweet, juicy fruit of the tree is amazingly satisfying and refreshing.
序章: 樹上香甜多汁的果實令人滿足又清爽。

Introduction: The scroll vanishes even as you read the magical runes upon it.  You now have the knowledge to cast a "Calm" spell.
序章: 當你讀著捲軸上的魔法符文時，捲軸化為虛無。你現在已經掌握了施放「鎮定術」的知識。

Introduction: The meadow lies covered with a blanket of flowers, unusual for this early in the spring.  It is warm, even though surrounded by the late snows of winter.
序章: 春天才剛開始，草地上卻已經覆滿了一片花海——這很不尋常。儘管被冬天的殘雪包圍，這裡卻溫暖如春。

Introduction: A large, carved stone lies flat on the ground.  You feel as though someone gentle was watching over you.  You feel that you are safe here.
序章: 一塊巨大的石雕平放在地上。你感到彷彿有某個溫柔的人在守護著你。你感到自己在這裡很安全。

Introduction: The air has the fresh, clean scent of the mountains, accompanied by numerous perfume-like fragrances.
序章: 空氣中飄散著山區的清新氣息，混合著各式各樣香氛般的花香。

Introduction: There is an aura of magic throughout this meadow.  It seems to be benevolent and restorative.
序章: 整個草地籠罩在魔法的光輝中。這魔法似乎既善良又有療癒的力量。

Introduction: There is no further purpose in casting the Open spell.
序章: 沒必要再施放「開啟術」了。

Introduction: The tree's blossoms are soft, green and fragrant.
序章: 樹上的花朵柔軟、青翠又芬芳。

Introduction: The fruit sparkles in the sunlight, yet it appears soft and fuzzy.
序章: 果實在陽光下閃閃發光，但看起來軟軟的、毛毛的。

Introduction: The fruit glows and shimmers in the moonlight.  It produces a fragrance like a sun-ripened strawberry.
序章: 果實在月光下閃閃發亮、搖搖欲墜。散發出像熟透草莓的香氣。

Introduction: The small tree is most amazing.  It bears blossoms and fruit at the same time, and the fruit on its boughs seems to shyly appear and disappear, shimmering.
序章: 這棵小樹最是不可思議。它同時開著花和結著果，枝頭上的果實似乎害羞地時隱時現、閃爍著。

Introduction: There is no need to climb the little tree.
序章: 不需要爬這棵小樹。

Introduction: Thick bushes block your path to the south.
序章: 濃密的灌木叢擋住了你向南的路。

Introduction: There is a cave entrance large enough to admit a man.
序章: 有一個足夠讓人進出的洞穴入口。

Introduction: It's already unlocked.
序章: 它已經開著了。

Introduction: The chest is already open.
序章: 寶箱已經開著了。

Introduction: You don't have the right tools -- you'll have to find another way to open it.
序章: 你沒有合適的工具——你得想想別的辦法打開它。

Introduction: It's locked -- you'll have to use another skill to open this.
序章: 它上了鎖——你得用另一種技能才能打開。

Introduction: The dead Ogre's chest lies open, its contents glittering brightly.
序章: 死掉的食人妖的寶箱打開著，裡面的東西閃閃發光。

Introduction: The dead Ogre's chest lies open and empty.
序章: 死掉的食人妖的寶箱打開著，但裡面空空如也。

Introduction: How much more calm can he get?
序章: 他還能更冷靜嗎？

Introduction: You've wasted a spell.
序章: 你浪費了一個法術。

Introduction: You don't have a knife.
序章: 你沒有刀子。

Introduction: The Ogre is already dead.  It would do no good to kill him again.
序章: 食人妖已經死了。再殺它一次也沒用。

Introduction: Ok, he'll be a dazzled cadaver.
序章: 好吧，他就成了個被眩暈的屍體囉。

Introduction: You're kidding, right?
序章: 你開玩笑呢，對吧？

Introduction: There is a dead Ogre here.
序章: 這裡有一隻死掉的食人妖。

Introduction: The chest has a heavy lid with a strong lock.
序章: 寶箱有個沉重的蓋子和堅固的鎖。

Introduction: The Ogre is carrying a chest in his massive arms.
序章: 食人妖的巨大臂膀裡抱著一個寶箱。

Introduction: This Ogre is in a bad mood.
序章: 這隻食人妖心情很差。

Introduction: And big.  Very BIG.
序章: 而且超大隻。非常非常大。

Introduction: There is a big, ugly, dead Ogre here.
序章: 這裡有一隻又大又醜、已經死掉的食人妖。

Introduction: This Ogre will never eat again.
序章: 這隻食人妖再也吃不了東西了。

Introduction: This Ogre looks like it would rather eat you.
序章: 這隻食人妖看起來寧願吃了你。

Introduction: Hostile intent is evident.  You prepare for battle.
序章: 它明顯滿懷惡意。你做好了戰鬥的準備。

Introduction: You need to get closer to the dead Ogre.
序章: 你需要更靠近這隻死亡的食人妖。

Introduction: There is nothing here to open.
序章: 這裡沒有什麼可以打開的。

Introduction: The dead Ogre's chest is empty.
序章: 死掉的食人妖的寶箱是空的。

Introduction: The dead Ogre's chest contains 1 gold and 43 silver, which you take and put away.
序章: 死掉的食人妖的寶箱裡裝著一枚金幣和四十三枚銀幣，你把它們拿走了。

Introduction: The Ogre is dazzled by your spell.
序章: 食人妖被你的法術眩暈了。

Introduction: A search of the dead Ogre's body produces no treasure.
序章: 搜索死掉的食人妖的屍體沒有找到任何寶藏。

Introduction: However, the treasure chest he was carrying lies beside his body.
序章: 不過，他身旁還放著他原本抱著的寶箱。

Introduction: The dead Ogre's huge club is much too heavy for you to lift.
序章: 死掉的食人妖的巨大狼牙棒重得你根本拿不起來。

Introduction: You force the Ogre's chest open.
序章: 你強行打開了食人妖的寶箱。

Introduction: The chest is locked.
序章: 寶箱上了鎖。

Introduction: Your spell is too weak to open the Ogre's chest.
序章: 你的法術力量太弱，打不開食人妖的寶箱。

Introduction: You're wasting a spell.
序章: 你在浪費法術。

Introduction: There's nothing here to open.
序章: 這裡沒有什麼可以打開的。

Introduction: The chest is held shut by the Ogre's massive arms.
序章: 寶箱被食人妖巨大的臂膀夾住了。

Introduction: standing
序章: 站著

Introduction: Your spell unlocks the Ogre's chest.
序章: 你的法術打開了食人妖的寶箱。

Introduction: The Ogre appears to have recovered from your spell.
序章: 食人妖似乎已經從你的法術中恢復過來了。

Introduction: You retrieve your daggers.
序章: 你拿回了你的匕首。

Introduction: The Ogre's chest is already unlocked.
序章: 食人妖的寶箱已經打開了。

Introduction: The lock on the Ogre's chest clicks open.  The dead Ogre's chest contains 1 gold and 43 silver, which you take and put away.
序章: 食人妖寶箱上的鎖喀地打開了。死掉的食人妖的寶箱裡裝著一枚金幣和四十三枚銀幣，你把它們拿走了。

Introduction: A magical aura emanates from the cave's entrance.
序章: 洞穴入口散發著魔法的光輝。

Introduction: You want to fetch THAT?
序章: 你想去撿那個？

Introduction: I don't know what you want to fetch.
序章: 我不知道你想撿什麼。

Introduction: The ground shows the beginning of new spring grass.
序章: 地面上冒出了新春的草芽。

Introduction: Don't open the Ogre -- it's all gucky and squishy inside.
序章: 別打開食人妖——裡面全都黏糊糊、軟趴趴的。

Introduction: You don't seem to have a weapon to charge.
序章: 你好像沒有可以充能的武器。

Introduction: Your weapon is now magically charged.
序章: 你的武器已經被施加了魔法充能。

Introduction: The rocks look like they've been dropped in place by a gigantic hand.
序章: 這些石頭看起來像是被某個巨人的手丟在這裡的。

Introduction: You see some of the trees of the forest.
序章: 你看到了森林中的一些樹木。

Introduction: You'd better not.  The bear isn't wearing a flea collar (just a locked chain).
序章: 最好別亂來。熊沒有戴跳蚤項圈（只是一條上了鎖的鐵鍊）。

Introduction: The bear seems to find your flowers tasty, but not very filling.
序章: 熊似乎覺得你的花朵好吃，但吃不飽。

Introduction: The Bear won't let you near the lock.
序章: 熊不會讓你靠近那把鎖。

Introduction: Try as you might, you can't pick the lock.  It must be magical.
序章: 再怎麼努力你也撬不開這把鎖。肯定是魔法鎖。

Introduction: Although looking somewhat scorched, the bear is still very dangerous.
序章: 雖然看起來有點焦黑，熊還是非常危險。

Introduction: Stunned, the bear is temporarily frozen.
序章: 熊被震懾住了，暫時動不了。

Introduction: On one side of this cavern is a creature which looks like a large bear.  There is something attached to his leg.
序章: 這個洞穴的一側有一隻看起來像大熊的生物。它的腿上綁著什麼東西。

Introduction: The bear appears docile for the time being.
序章: 熊目前看起來很溫馴。

Introduction: The bear looks menacing and angry.  There is a locked chain attached to its leg.
序章: 熊看起來凶狠又憤怒。它的腿上綁著一條鐵鍊。

Introduction: You have made this bear very happy; it would be wise to take advantage of his current mood.
序章: 你讓這隻熊非常高興；趁著它現在心情好，你應該好好利用這個機會。

Introduction: You know that bears can't talk!
序章: 你知道熊是不會說話的啦！

Introduction: Hi there!  I represent Ditto Realty.  We have some LOVELY properties that are PROVEN bargains.  We know that's true, because we've sold them so many times.
序章: 嗨，朋友！我代表迪圖房地產公司。我們有些超棒的房產都是保證划算的便宜貨。我們知道這是真的，因為我們賣過那麼多次。

Introduction: Beyond the bear, the cave seems to continue.  The bear blocks the way.
序章: 熊的另一邊，洞穴似乎還要繼續。但熊擋住了路。

Introduction: An eerie luminescence emanates from a chamber deeper within the cave.
序章: 一種詭異的光芒從洞穴深處的某個空間散發出來。

Introduction: A very large bear rears up as you approach.  It looks hungry and dangerous.
序章: 當你靠近時，一隻巨大的熊站了起來。它看起來既飢餓又危險。

Introduction: As your eyes adjust from sunlight to darkness, you examine the interior of this eerie cavern.  You sense something moving off to your right.
序章: 當你的眼睛從陽光逐漸適應黑暗時，你打量著這個詭異洞穴的內部。你感覺到右邊有東西在動。

Introduction: The Kobold's key disappears as you turn it in the lock.
序章: 當你用地精的鑰匙轉動鎖時，鑰匙消失了。

Introduction: There is no magic in the cave.
序章: 洞穴裡沒有魔法。

Introduction: There is an aura of magic throughout the cavern.  It seems to center on the bear
序章: 整個洞穴籠罩在魔法的光輝中。魔法的中心似乎在熊身上

Introduction: Once your eyes get adjusted to the dark, there's still nothing to see in here.
序章: 當你的眼睛適應了黑暗，你發現這裡根本什麼也看不到。

Introduction: What could you possibly do with a stalagmite... or is it a stalactite?
序章: 你能拿石筍……或者是石鐘乳……怎麼辦呢？

Introduction: The stalagmites grow slowly... or are they stalactites?
序章: 石筍生長得很緩慢……或者那是鐘乳石？

Introduction: What could you possibly do with a stalactite... or is it a stalagmite?
序章: 你究竟能拿鐘乳石做什麼……或者那是石筍？

Introduction: The stalactites grow slowly... or are they stalagmites?
序章: 鐘乳石生長得很緩慢……或者那是石筍？

Introduction: The Kobold doesn't look very calm.  In fact, it looks totally unaffected by your spell.
序章: 地精看起來一點都不平靜。實際上，它完全不受你的咒語影響。

Introduction: You just bumped into something on the floor.  It feels like a large wooden chest, but you don't see anything there.
序章: 你剛撞上地板上的東西。感覺像是一個大木箱，但你什麼都看不見。

Introduction: You will have to open it first.  The chest seems to be locked.
序章: 你必須先打開它。寶箱似乎被鎖住了。

Introduction: You've already taken the treasure.
序章: 你已經拿走了寶物。

Introduction: You are too far away to get the Kobold's treasure.
序章: 你距離地精的寶物太遠了。

Introduction: The key doesn't fit in the chest.  It must be for a different lock.
序章: 鑰匙塞不進寶箱。一定是別的鎖用的。

Introduction: You have no skill at picking locks.
序章: 你沒有開鎖的本領。

Introduction: You've already unlocked the chest.
序章: 你已經打開了寶箱。

Introduction: You don't see any locks here.
序章: 這裡沒有鎖。

Introduction: You're not close enough.
序章: 你離得不夠近。

Introduction: You will have to open the chest first.  It seems to be locked.
序章: 你必須先打開寶箱。它似乎被鎖住了。

Introduction: The chest is now empty.  Funny thing, that.
序章: 寶箱現在是空的。真奇怪。

Introduction: There is no sign of any other treasure in the cavern.
序章: 洞穴裡沒有其他寶物的跡象。

Introduction: You don't *see* any treasure here.
序章: 你看不到這裡有什麼寶物。

Introduction: Where the chest was, you see numerous pieces of gold and silver.
序章: 寶箱原本的位置，你看到許多金幣和銀幣。

Introduction: You see the outline of a chest glowing in the south end of the cavern.
序章: 你在洞穴南端看到一個發光的寶箱輪廓。

Introduction: You can detect a faint glimmering of magic from the shattered remains of the chest.
序章: 你能感知到破碎寶箱殘骸中微弱的魔法閃爍。

Introduction: A strong magical aura radiates from the Kobold's key.
序章: 地精的鑰匙散發出強大的魔法光暈。

Introduction: There no longer seem to be any magical effects or spells active in this part of the cavern.
序章: 這部分洞穴中似乎不再有任何魔法效應或咒語活躍。

Introduction: Uh oh, you've woken the Kobold.
序章: 哎喲，你驚動了地精。

Introduction: You now have the Kobold's key.  Nice work!
序章: 你現在拿到了地精的鑰匙。幹得漂亮！

Introduction: The fungus is slimy and firmly attached to the cave walls.  You'd better leave it alone.
序章: 菌類又溼又滑，緊貼在洞壁上。你最好別碰它。

Introduction: Phosphorescent fungi illuminate the cavern with an unearthly glow.
序章: 磷光菌以詭異的光芒照亮洞穴。

Introduction: You retrieve the dropped key.
序章: 你撿起掉落的鑰匙。

Introduction: You see a large, brass key lying on the ground.  A faint glow emanates from the key.
序章: 你看到一把大銅鑰匙躺在地上。鑰匙散發出微弱的光芒。

Introduction: Your foot slips on the wet cavern floor and makes a scuffing noise.
序章: 你的腳在溼滑的洞穴地面上滑了一下，發出擦擦聲。

Introduction: There are no monsters here to attack.
序章: 這裡沒有怪物可以攻擊。

Introduction: That's what you're doing.  Keep going!
序章: 那就是你在做的。繼續加油！

Introduction: Uh oh.  You weren't stealthy enough.  You have awoken the Kobold.
序章: 哎喲。你不夠謹慎。你驚動了地精。

Introduction: You can't reach the key from here.
序章: 你從這裡夠不到鑰匙。

Introduction: The Kobold won't let you near his key.
序章: 地精不會讓你靠近它的鑰匙。

Introduction: You take the Kobold's glowing key.
序章: 你拿起了地精發光的鑰匙。

Introduction: VERY carefully, you remove the key from around the Kobold's neck.
序章: 你非常小心地把鑰匙從地精的脖子上取下來。

Introduction: The strange creature is dressed in ragged clothes.  It has large, pale eyes. It must have spent its entire life in these caves!
序章: 這奇怪的生物穿著破爛衣服。它有雙又大又蒼白的眼睛。它一定在這些洞穴裡度過了整個人生！

Introduction: A large, brass key hangs from a thong around the creature's neck.
序章: 一把大銅鑰匙掛在生物脖子上的皮繩上。

Introduction: The Kobold seems more interested in frying you with Ball Lightning than in talking to you.
序章: 地精似乎對用閃電球炸你比跟你聊天更感興趣。

Introduction: The invisible lid of the invisible chest lifts.  A ripple of magical heat in the air informs you of a trap narrowly avoided by your skillful use of the Open spell.
序章: 隱形寶箱的隱形蓋子打了開來。空氣中的魔法熱浪告訴你，你精妙地使用開啟術躲過了一個陷阱。

Introduction: You open the chest, remove its contents, and close it again.  You are now 10 gold and 60 silver pieces richer.
序章: 你打開寶箱、取出裡面的東西、再關上。你現在多了 10 金幣和 60 銀幣。

Introduction: You don't have time for that.
序章: 你沒時間做那事。

Introduction: You pry at the sealed lid of the chest, and...
序章: 你撬著封死的寶箱蓋子，然後……

Introduction: This section of cave has an eerie quality.  You see a kobold resting on a ledge across from you.
序章: 洞穴這一段有股詭異的感覺。你看到一隻地精在對面的突出岩石上休息。

Introduction: Wow!  The chest must have been booby-trapped.  You can really feel the damage from the blast.
序章: 哇！寶箱一定裝了陷阱。你真的能感受到爆炸的傷害。

Introduction: You hear a very satisfying "Snick" as your pick catches the lock just right.
序章: 你聽到一聲令人滿意的「喀」聲，你的撬鎖工具完美地卡進了鎖。

Introduction: Then you sense powerful magics dissipating around the lock, and your hands shake as you realize you have narrowly avoided some sort of Magical Doom.
序章: 然後你感受到強大的魔法在鎖周圍消散，當你意識到你勉強躲過某種魔法厄運時，你的手開始發抖。

Introduction: Your fingers shift slightly, and you feel an odd prickling sensation coming from the lock.  You have a bad feeling about this.
序章: 你的手指輕輕移動，你感到鎖傳來奇怪的刺痛感。你有種不好的預感。

Introduction: You hack and pry at the chest, but you are not strong enough to force its lid.
序章: 你砍著寶箱並試圖撬開，但你力氣不夠，無法強行打開蓋子。

Introduction: The Kobold casts a spell with which you are not familiar.
序章: 地精施展了一個你不熟悉的法術。

Introduction: You add ten gold and sixty silver coins to your money pouch.
序章: 你把十枚金幣和六十枚銀幣加進了錢袋。

Introduction: You go!  You no stay here!
序章: 你滾！你別在這裡待著！

Introduction: You don't need to cast Fetch -- everything here is in easy reach.
序章: 你不需要施展取物術──這裡的東西都隨手可得。

Introduction: This part of the cavern is spacious and multi-leveled.  Phosphorescent fungus illuminates the cavern with an unearthly glow.
序章: 洞穴的這部分寬敞且多層次。磷光菌以詭異的光芒照亮洞穴。

Introduction: On a rocky platform above the cave floor is a Kobold, one of the race of ugly, miserly creatures known for their skill in Magic.
序章: 在洞穴地面上方的岩石平台上，有一隻地精。這是一個醜陋、吝嗇的種族，以擅長魔法而聞名。

Introduction: There's nothing to open here.
序章: 這裡沒有東西可以打開。

Introduction: There are no passive spells to trigger here.
序章: 這裡沒有被動法術可以觸發。

Introduction: You'd better not.  This cave is not recommended by the Michelin Guide.
序章: 你最好別。米其林指南不推薦這個洞穴。

Introduction: The stalactites look much like the stalagmites, except that they go the other way.
序章: 鐘乳石看起來很像石筍，除了方向相反。

Introduction: A faint glimmer of sunlight barely illuminates the cave entrance.
序章: 微弱的陽光勉強照亮了洞穴入口。

Introduction: The Kobold eats its meals on this plain stone table.
序章: 地精在這個簡樸的石頭桌子上吃飯。

Introduction: No time for that now!
序章: 現在沒時間那樣做！

Introduction: You pick up the Kobold's mushrooms and put them in your pack.
序章: 你撿起地精的蘑菇，放進你的背包。

Introduction: Some sort of food (mushrooms?) sits in a pile on the stone table.  It looks almost edible... to a Kobold.
序章: 某種食物（蘑菇？）堆在石桌上。看起來幾乎可以吃……對地精來說。

Introduction: To the south and east is the forest from which you came. To the north and west are steep cliffs. There are some unusual plants growing out of the side of the cliffs.
序章: 南邊和東邊是你來時的森林。北邊和西邊是陡峭的懸崖。懸崖側面長著一些不尋常的植物。

Introduction: They are spitting what seems to be some kind of seed.
序章: 它們吐出似乎是某種種子的東西。

Introduction: The cliff walls are very steep, and they look difficult to climb.
序章: 懸崖壁非常陡峭，看起來很難爬。

Introduction: You've never seen anything quite like them. They're pretty, in a grotesque way.
序章: 你從來沒見過這樣的東西。它們有種怪異的美感。

Introduction: You'd like to do that, wouldn't you?
序章: 你想那樣做，是吧？

Introduction: You have no need to take the flowers.
序章: 你沒有必要摘花。

Introduction: Since the flowers have stopped spitting, the seed might be a little harder to get to.
序章: 既然花已經停止吐種子了，種子可能會有點難拿。

Introduction: Not now!
序章: 現在不行！

Introduction: As you attempt to draw your weapon, you lose your balance momentarily.
序章: 你試圖拔出武器時，一時失去了平衡。

Introduction: Playing catch with the Spirea plants would certainly be fun, but this seed has a future elsewhere.
序章: 跟繡線菊植物玩接球遊戲肯定很有趣，但這顆種子有別的用處。

Introduction: You lose your concentration.
序章: 你失去了專注。

Introduction: Your skill with the Open spell is not great enough to affect the plants.
序章: 你的開啟術技巧還不夠強，無法影響植物。

Introduction: There is no need to be so destructive.
序章: 沒必要這麼有破壞性。

Introduction: That spell would damage the seed.  You don't want to do that.
序章: 那個法術會傷害種子。你不想那樣做。

Introduction: You can't see it.
序章: 你看不到它。

Introduction: The large seed spins as it travels through the air.
序章: 大種子在空中飛行時旋轉著。

Introduction: There's not much point to that now.
序章: 現在那樣做沒什麼意義。

Introduction: Why do you think you're standing like this. Trying to catch Flying Water?
序章: 你為什麼站成這樣。想接住飛水？

Introduction: You can't catch the seed from down on the ground.
序章: 你從地上抓不到種子。

Introduction: Perhaps it would be better to find something else to throw.
序章: 也許找別的東西扔比較好。

Introduction: You might have tried to throw a rock at the seed, but it's too late now.
序章: 你可能想過扔石頭打種子，但現在太晚了。

Introduction: You get in position for a good throw.
序章: 你擺好姿勢準備投擲。

Introduction: You need a good rock to throw.
序章: 你需要一塊好石頭來扔。

Introduction: The meadow clearing in which you stand is grassy and littered with blown pine needles and small mossy rocks.
序章: 你站著的草地空地長滿了草，散落著被吹落的松針和長滿苔蘚的小石頭。

Introduction: Wait a second!
序章: 等等！

Introduction: The leaves look tough and fibrous.
序章: 葉子看起來又硬又有纖維。

Introduction: You've already made a mess of that flower.
序章: 你已經把那朵花搞得一團糟了。

Introduction: There's not much point in that, now that you've already done what you came here to do.
序章: 既然你已經做完了來這裡的目的，那樣做就沒什麼意義了。

Introduction: This looks like a good place to you.
序章: 這看起來像個不錯的地方。

Introduction: You valiantly scrabble against the cliff wall, but it looks like your climbing skill could stand some improvement.
序章: 你勇敢地在懸崖壁上亂抓，但看起來你的攀爬技巧還有待改進。

Introduction: Perhaps there is some other way to get to your objective.
序章: 也許還有其他方式到達你的目標。

Introduction: You look for an appropriate place to climb.
序章: 你尋找適合爬的地方。

Introduction: You pick up a few small rocks.
序章: 你撿起了幾塊小石頭。

Introduction: Unless you just took a kangaroo potion, that plant is too far up the cliff to jump for.
序章: 除非你剛喝了袋鼠藥水，不然那株植物在懸崖上太高了，跳不到。

Introduction: You see the seed floating above the shattered remains of the plant.
序章: 你看到種子浮在被摧毀的植物殘骸上方。

Introduction: You take the seed.
序章: 你拿起種子。

Introduction: You don't need a rock for anything.
序章: 你沒有必要用石頭做什麼。

Introduction: It looks difficult. You look around for a good place to climb.
序章: 看起來很難。你四處尋找適合爬的地方。

Introduction: You put the seed into your pack and climb down.
序章: 你把種子放進背包並爬了下去。

Introduction: You remain unconscious on the ground for an indeterminate amount of time. As you finally revive, you realize that you've caused yourself a fair amount of damage.
序章: 你昏迷在地上好一陣子。當你終於清醒過來時，你意識到你對自己造成了相當大的傷害。

Introduction: Ohhhhhhh!
You place the seed into your pack.
序章: 你把種子放進背包。

Introduction: You lose concentration, and the spell fades.  You will have to practice some more.
序章: 你失去了專注，咒語消散了。你還得多練習。

Introduction: You get into a good position.
序章: 你擺好位置。

Introduction: You might have tried to cast a spell at the seed, but it's too late now.
序章: 你可能想過對種子施法，但現在太晚了。

Introduction: You can see pines, cedars, birches and other trees frequently associated with mountain forests.
序章: 你能看到松樹、雪松、樺樹和其他常見於山區森林的樹木。

Introduction: Thick undergrowth stops you from moving north.
序章: 濃密的灌木叢擋住了你向北的去路。

Introduction: Dense brush blocks your path to the south.
序章: 密集的灌叢擋住了你向南的去路。

Introduction: That's one mean Ogress!
序章: 那真是隻狠辣的女食人妖！

Introduction: You furtively grasp the Magic Mirror.
序章: 你偷偷地抓住了魔鏡。

Introduction: There's no time for idle chatter.
序章: 沒時間閒聊。

Introduction: There is a bat, all folded up within his wings, hanging on the wall.
序章: 有隻蝙蝠用翅膀把自己包起來，掛在牆上。

Introduction: You'd better not -- it's not your turn at bat.
序章: 你最好別──現在還輪不到你。

Introduction: The bat must be using a digitized encoding program, as you don't seem to understand the high-pitched squeaks.
序章: 蝙蝠一定在用數位編碼程式，因為你似乎聽不懂那些尖銳的吱吱聲。

Introduction: It's a broken gate -- every well-furnished apartment should have one.
序章: 這是扇破門──每間布置得好的公寓都應該有一扇。

Introduction: The tag says "Manufactured by Broom Motors, Ltd."  This must be one of those new sports brooms.
序章: 標籤上寫著「掃帚馬達公司製造」。這一定是那種新型運動掃帚。

Introduction: Whatever's in this bucket, it really stinks.
序章: 這桶裡不管裝的是什麼，真的臭死了。

Introduction: You're positive that Baba Yaga could make a fortune if she sold it on the open market.
序章: 你很確定芭芭雅嘎要是在公開市場上賣它，一定能發大財。

Introduction: The fire burns hot under the kettle.
序章: 火在茶壺下面燒得很熱。

Introduction: There's a lot of SOMETHING cooking away in the huge black cauldron.
序章: 巨大的黑色大鍋裡有什麼東西在煮。

Introduction: The smell is awful, the color is sickening, and you don't even want to know what's being prepared for dinner.
序章: 味道很難聞，顏色令人作嘔，你甚至不想知道在準備什麼晚餐。

Introduction: The stone hearth and chimney radiates too much heat.
序章: 石頭壁爐和煙囪散發出太多熱度。

Introduction: Okay, Okay, so you think there should be a window here?
序章: 好吧、好吧，你認為這裡應該有窗戶？

Introduction: The glass seems to distort the view of the outside world... but which world is it?
序章: 玻璃似乎扭曲了外部世界的景象……但那是哪個世界呢？

Introduction: Window?  What window?
序章: 窗戶？什麼窗戶？

Introduction: If you didn't know better, you would swear the mural on the wall looks just like an underground cave.
序章: 如果你不了解的話，你會發誓牆上的壁畫看起來就像一個地下洞穴。

Introduction: Upon reading the note, you realize it is a recipe for "Frog Legs Fricassee".
序章: 看完紙條後，你發現這是份「炸青蛙腿」的食譜。

Introduction: Look pets... we have a visitor!
序章: 「看啊，寵物…我們來客人了！」

Introduction: Powers of Night, Shadows of Day -- Heed now my Words! Henceforth you STAY!
序章: 「夜晚之力，白晝之影——聽我話！從此你留下！」

Introduction: Well Dearies!  What shall we have for supper today?
序章: 「好啊，親愛的們！今晚吃什麼？」

Introduction: Hero sandwiches? I had something more formal in mind.
序章: 「英雄三明治？我想要點更正式的。」

Introduction: Now how does that spell go...?
序章: 「那咒語要怎麼唸…？」

Introduction: Hear me, oh Powers Of Klatha and Mana! Turn now my guest Into species called Rana!
序章: 「聽我說啊，克拉瑟和法力之力！把我的客人變成蛙族！」

Introduction: I learned that spell from Erasmus, kids.  Doesn't it look delicious?
序章: 「我從伊拉斯謨那裡學的咒語，孩子們。看起來美味嗎？」

Introduction: BE STILL!
序章: 「別動！」

Introduction: Critics!  We can't all be gourmands, I suppose.
序章: 「美食家呢！我們也不是都是美食家。」

Introduction: So you're the one who's trying to be a hero around here.  The only good hero's a dead hero, I always say!
序章: 「所以你就是想在這裡逞英雄的傢伙。我常說，好英雄就是死英雄！」

Introduction: But I do have a need for a brave fool.  Are YOU brave?
序章: 但我確實需要個勇敢的傻瓜。你勇敢嗎？

Introduction: Yes
序章: 是的

Introduction: No
序章: 不是

Introduction: You make little froggy sounds, trying your best to indicate your agreement.
序章: 你發出小青蛙的叫聲，盡力表示同意。

Introduction: Wellll...
If you're willing to do a small little teensy favor for me, I might reconsider having you for supper.
序章: 「如果你願意幫我做個小小的小忙，我也許會重新考慮把你當晚餐吃。」

Introduction: I need the root of a mandrake plant that grows in the graveyard.  Will you be a sweet and fetch me some?
序章: 「我需要在墓園裡長的曼陀羅根。親愛的，你能去給我拿一些嗎？」

Introduction: Anything but Frog Legs Fricassee!, you think. You croak your agreement to the task.
序章: 「什麼都好，就別是炸青蛙腿！」你想著。你用呱呱聲表示同意這任務。

Introduction: And I had my mouth watering for frog.  Oh well!
序章: 「我本來還滿想吃青蛙呢。算了！」

Introduction: Hear what I say, And hear me right: Mandrake must be pulled At precisely Midnight!
序章: 「聽好我的話，聽清楚：曼陀羅要在午夜時分拔起！」

Introduction: This I tell you And this I say: Return with the root 'Ere the break of next day.
序章: 「這是我告訴你的，我這樣說：天亮前帶著根回來。」

Introduction: Hear what I say And know I don't lie: Bring back the root Or else you will die!!
序章: 「聽我說，相信我不會說謊：把根帶回來，不然你會死！」

Introduction: You, Shoo!
Well, you won't be so tough after you've simmered in a little wine sauce.
序章: 「好啦，你在紅酒醬裡燉一下就不會那麼硬了。」

Introduction: Bon appetite, Dearies!
序章: 「好胃口，親愛的們！」

Introduction: It's just as well.  I'd lose my dinner if you were brave.
序章: 「也好啦。你要是勇敢的話我就沒胃口了。」

Introduction: Nothin' says lovin' like frog legs in the oven.
序章: 「什麼都比不上烤青蛙腿能表達愛意。」

Introduction: No mandrake for me, eh?  What a waste of spells you turned out to be.
序章: 「沒有曼陀羅給我？你這浪費法術的傢伙。」

Introduction: Actually, since it's my breakfast time, we'll have some amphibian omelette with bacon and legs.
序章: 「實際上，既然現在是我吃早餐的時間，我們就吃炒兩棲動物加培根和腿吧。」

Introduction: You again?
序章: 「又是你？」

Introduction: Hello, Hero.
序章: 「你好，英雄。」

Introduction: Powers that rule Over regions soggy: Change this creature Back into a froggy!
序章: 「統治濕潤地域的力量：把這生物變回青蛙！」

Introduction: Ooooh... yummy!  I'm glad you returned.
序章: 「喔…好吃！我很高興你回來了。」

Introduction: What have you DONE to me? How DARE you use my own spell against me? I'll, I'll... Oh, oh!
序章: 「你對我做了什麼？你敢用我自己的咒語對付我？我會…我會…哦，哦！」

Introduction: Hear me, O Elements of Air and Wind. Give me the power to save my own skin!
序章: 「聽我說，空氣與風的元素。給我拯救我自己的力量！」

Introduction: Back so soon?
序章: 「這麼快就回來？」

Introduction: Ah!  Here it is.
序章: 「啊！找到了。」

Introduction: Spirits of Mist And Creatures of Bog: Transform my guest To the shape of a Frog.
序章: 「迷霧之精與沼澤生物：把我的客人變成青蛙的形狀。」

Introduction: Yum, Yum.  Froggie Frappe!
序章: 「好吃，好吃。青蛙小冰沙！」

Introduction: Did you bring me my mandrake like you promised?
序章: 「你有像約好的那樣給我帶曼陀羅來嗎？」

Introduction: Well?  Where is it?
序章: 「好？在哪？」

Introduction: What's the matter?  Got a frog in your throat?
序章: 「怎麼了？喉嚨卡了隻青蛙？」

Introduction: I suppose I'll have to turn you back into whatever it is that you were. Pity!  You're much more appetizing this way.
序章: 「我想我得把你變回原本的樣子。可惜了！你這樣看起來美味多了。」

Introduction: Creatures of Bog And Spirits of Fog: Return the true form To this rather dumb Frog.
序章: 「沼澤生物與霧靈：把真實的形態還給這隻相當笨的青蛙。」

Introduction: Now, did you put it in your backpack?
序章: 「那，你把它放進背包裡了嗎？」

Introduction: You try your best to croak out your name, or at least let the witch know that you do indeed have one.
序章: 你盡力用呱呱聲說出你的名字，至少要讓女巫知道你確實有個名字。

Introduction: As you make a feeble croaking sound, you try to nod your head.
序章: 當你發出微弱的呱呱聲時，你試著點了點頭。

Introduction: Ah...that's it! Frog Legs Fricassee.
序章: 「啊…那就是了！炸青蛙腿。」

Introduction: Once again you're a frog, and once again you can't move.  You find it very exasperating!
序章: 你再次變成了青蛙，再次動不了。你覺得這很惱人！

Introduction: Your body is frozen by the power of the witch's spell.
序章: 你的身體被女巫的咒語凍住了。

Introduction: The witch is hopping mad.
序章: 女巫氣得跳腳。

Introduction: Lovies, it's about time we dine.
序章: 「親愛的們，該吃飯了。」

Introduction: Kids!  We have it!
序章: 「孩子！我們拿到了！」

Introduction: That's it!  The final ingredient.  Now we can make our greatest creation...
序章: 「就是它！最後的材料。現在我們可以製作最偉大的創作…」

Introduction: MANDRAKE MOUSSE!
序章: 「曼陀羅慕斯！」

Introduction: What's that, children?  You think we should reward our lackey here?
序章: 「怎麼了，孩子？你們認為我們應該獎勵這小嘍囉？」

Introduction: Very well, ex-frog.  I'll let you live this time.  Next time, though, it's frog legs for sure!
序章: 「很好，前青蛙。這次我饒了你。不過下次，一定是青蛙腿！」

Introduction: So... Go!
序章: 「所以…滾！」

Introduction: Stubborn, aren't you?
序章: 「你很固執呢？」

Introduction: Now, Oh-Soon-To-Be-Supper... I don't suppose you have a name?
序章: 現在，唉呀就要變晚餐啦…我猜你沒有名字？

Introduction: Well, if you won't be a sweet, then you'll be my meat!
序章: 「好吧，如果你不乖，就變成我的肉！」

Introduction: This I vow: Stay there now!
序章: 「這是我發誓的：現在待著！」

Introduction: Who would have guessed that little chicken hut would have this much room in it.
序章: 誰會猜到那個小雞腳小屋裡面會有這麼大的空間。

Introduction: You make a note to yourself to get the name of Baba Yaga's interior decorator.
序章: 你在心裡記下要去問芭芭雅嘎的室內設計師的名字。

Introduction: The floor is tidy, with a pile of straw off the screen in one corner.
序章: 地板很整潔，角落裡有堆稻草在螢幕外。

Introduction: A quick look around shows that this is a creepy place, despite the pot -- or is it a cauldron? -- simmering briskly on the fire.  Look at the size of that spider!
序章: 快速掃一眼就知道這是個詭異的地方，儘管鍋子——或者說是大鍋？——在火上咕嘟咕嘟地煮著。看看那隻蜘蛛的大小！

Introduction: The grisly remains of some man-like animal.
序章: 某種人形生物的可怕遺骨。

Introduction: Owning this skull helps Baba Yaga keep a head of the game.
序章: 擁有這骷髏幫助芭芭雅嘎走在遊戲的前面。

Introduction: Is that a SMILE on that spider's face?
序章: 那隻蜘蛛的臉上有微笑嗎？

Introduction: You can almost read the spider's thoughts:  "Yes!  Yes!  Get your hand over here so I can eat!"
序章: 你幾乎可以讀出蜘蛛的想法：「是的！是的！把你的手放過來我才能吃！」

Introduction: Step into my parlor!
序章: 踏進我的起居室吧！

Introduction: The kitchen knives on the table look old but sharp.  You can't quite make out what is in the bottles.
序章: 桌上的廚房刀看起來又舊又銳。你看不太清楚瓶子裡裝著什麼。

Introduction: Isn't that a telephone wire insulator?
序章: 那不是電話線絕緣體嗎？

Introduction: You HATE houses with cobwebs in the corners, especially when there's an immense (and hungry-looking) spider squatting cheerily in the middle of the cobweb.
序章: 你討厭角落有蜘蛛網的房子，特別是當有隻巨大的（且看起來很餓的）蜘蛛高興地蹲在蜘蛛網中央的時候。

Introduction: You have to get there, first.
序章: 你必須先到達那裡。

Introduction: You have second thoughts, and you decide not to attempt such a potentially dangerous excursion.
序章: 你改變了主意，決定不嘗試這樣可能很危險的遠征。

Introduction: What's to climb?
序章: 有什麼好爬的？

Introduction: Those are the biggest chicken legs you've ever seen.
序章: 那是你見過最大的雞腿。

Introduction: The green slime dripping off of the fence top makes you realize that climbing is not a good idea.
序章: 從柵欄頂端滴下來的綠色粘液讓你意識到攀爬不是個好主意。

Introduction: The sharp ends of the fence are covered with some nasty-looking green slime.
序章: 柵欄的尖端被一些令人厭惡的綠色粘液覆蓋。

Introduction: This stuff reminds you of the slime eating the barstool away at the tavern.
序章: 這東西讓你想起在酒館裡吃掉酒吧凳子的那些粘液。

Introduction: Of course, most fences aren't poisoned on top!
序章: 當然，大多數的柵欄頂端都不會有毒！

Introduction: You've seen one, you've seen them all, I say.
序章: 我說，你看過一個，就等於看過所有的。

Introduction: Baba Yaga is the most powerful Ogress around.  If you have any brains, you'll stay away from her.
序章: 「芭芭雅嘎是這一帶最強大的女食人妖。如果你有腦子，就別靠近她。」

Introduction: All the other skulls have glowing eyes.  But me?... noooo!  I don't need lovely glowing eyes!  Just open and close the gate, that's all I'm good for.
序章: 「所有其他的骷髏都有發光的眼睛。但我？…不要！我不需要漂亮的發光眼睛！只要開關門，這就是我唯一的用處。」

Introduction: But if you can give me a glowing gem for my eyes, then I'll let you in the gate.
序章: 「但如果你能給我個發光的寶石當眼睛，我就放你進門。」

Introduction: All I ask is for a little gem or jewel that glows in the dark. You're the big hero, so go find one!
序章: 「我要的就是在黑暗中發光的小寶石或珠寶。你是大英雄，去找一個吧！」

Introduction: Baba Yaga's hut will squat if you say the rhyme.
序章: 「如果你說出那句韻文，芭芭雅嘎的小屋就會蹲下。」

Introduction: You're some hero if you don't even know what an ogre is.
序章: 「你要是連食人妖是什麼都不知道，就太不像英雄了。」

Introduction: The hut will squat if you say, 'Hut of brown, Now sit down.'
序章: 「如果你說『棕色的小屋，現在坐下』，小屋就會蹲下。」

Introduction: Those bone brains are Baba Yaga's spies, so they get glowing eyes.
序章: 「那些骨頭腦子是芭芭雅嘎的間諜，所以他們有發光的眼睛。」

Introduction: Right!
序章: 沒錯！

Introduction: I won't let you in until I get a gem so I can see!
序章: 「在我得到寶石能看見之前，我不會放你進去！」

Introduction: I don't need the fur.  I think the 'bald look' gives me a certain distinction, don't you?
序章: 「我不需要毛髮。我覺得『禿頭造型』給了我某種風格，你說對不？」

Introduction: The gate's gone.
序章: 門消失了。

Introduction: The eyes of the large skull on the gate flash merrily away at you.
序章: 門上大骷髏的眼睛朝你閃閃發光。

Introduction: The large skull on the gate seems to stare vacantly at you.
序章: 門上的大骷髏似乎茫茫然地盯著你。

Introduction: This place already looks like a nightmare.  Eating magic mushrooms would likely make it even worse.
序章: 這個地方看起來已經像噩夢了。吃魔法蘑菇可能會讓它更糟。

Introduction: Oh, the rhyme.  Yeah, you're right, that was part of the deal.  O.k., the rhyme is 'Hut of Brown, Now Sit Down'.

Introduction: Nothing happens.

Introduction: You hear a voice in your head asking, 'What is the rhyme?'

Introduction: What an awful creature, to turn me into a FROG!, you say to yourself. Someday, I must return the favor.

序章: 你講了個關於下列的韻文：

Introduction: Bye!
You tell the hut: For Beauty I am not a star, There are others finer by far. But my face, I don't mind it, Because at least I'm behind it.   It's people in FRONT that I jar!
序章: 你對小屋說：論美貌我不是明星，別人遠勝於我。但我的臉我不介意，因為起碼我在身後。真正煩擾的是站在前面的人！

Introduction: Baba Yaga does not welcome strangers! You have to deal with me before you can enter.

Introduction: All the other skulls have glowing eyes. But me?....noooo!  I don't need lovely glowing eyes!  Just open and close the gate, that's all I'm good for.

Introduction: From a distance, you hear the witch intone:
序章: 從遠處，你聽到女巫低聲吟誦：

Introduction: You tell the hut: There once was an alien from Venus, Whose body was shaped like a...

序章: 小屋打斷道：「不是那個！」

Introduction: The force of magic in this clearing is nearly overwhelming.  The skull on the gatepost radiates magic slightly more strongly than the other skulls.  The hut on chicken legs is "artifact-level" magic -- it makes your ears ring.
序章: 這片空地的魔法力量幾乎令人難以承受。柵欄上的頭骨散發的魔法比其他頭骨強一點。雞腿小屋是「神器級」魔法—它讓你的耳朵嗡嗡響。

Introduction: Now???
序章: 現在？??

Introduction: You know the deal!  Get me a glowing gem for my eyes, and I'll let you in.
序章: 「你知道交易！給我一個發光寶石裝在我的眼睛裡，我就讓你進去。」

Introduction: Do you agree to the deal?
序章: 「你同意這個交易嗎？」

Introduction: Your eyes are drawn to the incredible hut on chicken legs.
序章: 你的目光被那個不可思議的雞腿小屋吸引了。

Introduction: The skulls on top of the fence have eerily glowing eyes. The large skull on the fence seems to stare blankly at you.

Introduction: Perhaps it's a tower of power.

序章: 「你似乎知道我所有的笑點，所以……就給我消失！」

Introduction: Your thoughts give you away, but before you can DO anything...
序章: 「你的想法暴露了你，但在你能做任何事情之前……」

Introduction: Not in MY house you don't....
序章: 「不在我的房子裡你不行……」

Introduction: Baba Yaga is good at curses and shape-changing spells. She has a nasty temper, and doesn't believe in 'Safe Hex', so it's best to stay on her good side.
序章: 「芭芭雅嘎擅長詛咒和變身咒語。她脾氣很壞，不相信『安全咒語』，所以最好和她相處融洽。」

Introduction: You have to watch her. She cheats at cards.
序章: 「你得小心她。她在玩牌時作弊。」

Introduction: So do you!
序章: 「你也是！」

Introduction: She started it first.  Besides, I wanted her to get a taste of her own medicine.
序章: 「她先開始的。而且，我想讓她嚐嚐自己的滋味。」

Introduction: Shame she still beats you.
序章: 「可惜她還是打敗了你。」

Introduction: They are supposed to have a warlock working with them.  From what I've seen, he's more a nincompoop than a necromancer.
序章: 「他們應該有一個術士和他們一起工作。根據我看到的，他更像一個笨蛋而不是死靈法師。」

Introduction: Basically, anyone who uses magic is a magic user; anyone who casts spells is a spellcaster.
序章: 「基本上，任何使用魔法的人都是魔法使用者；任何施展法術的人都是施法者。」

Introduction: However, to be a wizard, you need to have undergone initiation into the 'Wizard's Institute of Technocery' in the land of Shapeir.
序章: 「然而，要成為法師，你需要在沙佩爾地區經過『法師技術學會』的入門儀式。」

Introduction: Magicians and sorcerers are wizards who specialize.
序章: 「魔術師和術士是專門從事特定領域的法師。」

Introduction: I myself, strive to master all aspects of Magic, unlike those narrow-minded specialists.
序章: 「我本人努力掌握魔法的各個方面，不像那些狹隘的專家。」

Introduction: You wouldn't want to lose your amateur standing, after all.
序章: 「畢竟，你不想失去你的業餘愛好者身份。」

Introduction: I prefer to think of myself as a dilettante.
序章: 「我更傾向於把自己視為一個多才多藝者。」

Introduction: Does that mean that you tease pickles?
序章: 「那表示你在取笑醃黃瓜嗎？」

Introduction: A countercurse is a cure for a curse. It usually works against the one who cast the curse in the first place.
序章: 「反詛咒是對詛咒的救治。它通常對施咒者本身有效。」

Introduction: The countercurse for the curse of the Baron goes:
序章: 「對男爵詛咒的反詛咒是：」

Introduction: Come a Hero from the East...
序章: 「英雄自東方來…」

Introduction: Free the man from in the beast...
序章: 「救人於野獸之中…」

Introduction: Bring the child from out the band...
序章: 「救孩童脫離匪幫…」

Introduction: Drive the Curser from the land.
序章: 「驅逐施咒者離此地。」

Introduction: So, to break the curse, a hero must get rid of Baba Yaga, in addition to all the other things.  It does not reflect well on Baba Yaga.
序章: 「所以，要破除詛咒，英雄必須除去芭芭雅嘎，以及所有其他事情。這對芭芭雅嘎名聲不太好。」

Introduction: Nothing reflects well upon that Ogress.  She has a face that would break anything but a Magic Mirror.
序章: 「那個食人妖身上沒什麼能襯托良好形象的。她的臉能破毀一切，除了魔法鏡。」

Introduction: The curse Baba Yaga placed upon Baron von Spielburg was a fine example of creative cursing.
序章: 「芭芭雅嘎對史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的詛咒是創意詛咒的典範。」

Introduction: Upon von Spielburg and all his clan, This the curse I now demand:
序章: 「對馮·史畢柏格與其全族，我現下令此詛咒：」

Introduction: What I will shall come full measure,    So shall ye lose all that ye treasure.
序章: 「我欲之物必成事實，    爾等將失去一切珍寶。」

Introduction: Of course, the problem is that for every curse there is an equal and opposite countercurse.
序章: 「當然，問題在於每一個詛咒都有相等且相反的反詛咒。」

Introduction: Now that's a spell with charm.
序章: 「那真是個有魅力的法術。」

Introduction: Its true name is Erasmus's Razzle Dazzle, by the way.
序章: 「順帶一提，它的真名是伊拉斯謨的眩目術。」

Introduction: You can cast the spell and anyone looking directly at its coruscating light is blinded for awhile and can't do anything.
序章: 「你可以施放法術，任何直視其閃爍光芒的人會被暫時致盲而無法行動。」

Introduction: Never met her. I only built this vacation home here a century ago.
序章: 「沒見過。我只是一百年前在這裡蓋了個度假屋。」

Introduction: Erana has a nice little magical meadow in her name northwest of here. Pretty respectable Spellcaster, I imagine.
序章: 「伊瑞娜在西北方有個以她名字命名的漂亮小魔法草地。我想她是個相當令人尊敬的法師。」

Introduction: The protection spell she cast over the town is fairly good, but I believe she missed a few places.
序章: 「她對城鎮施放的保護法術相當不錯，不過我認為她遺漏了幾個地方。」

Introduction: You're just jealous because the only thing anyone named after you was a soft drink.
序章: 「你只是嫉妒，因為以你名字命名的唯一東西是飲料。」

Introduction: I thought that Erasmus's Razzle Dazzle Root Beer" was a WONDERFUL name for a product.  It's a shame the company went broke afterwards."
序章: 「我覺得『伊拉斯謨的眩目術根啤酒』是個很棒的產品名稱。可惜公司後來倒閉了。」

Introduction: I am a wizard of some reknown.
序章: 「我是個頗負盛名的法師。」

Introduction: What am I, some sort of object?  Am I invisible or something that you talk about me behind my back in front of my face?  Sheesh, no respect.
序章: 「我是什麼，某種物品嗎？我隱形還是什麼，你居然在我面前背後說我？唉呀，真沒尊重。」

Introduction: It's hard to take anyone very seriously who wears such a ridiculous hat.
序章: 「很難認真對待戴著這麼荒唐帽子的人。」

Introduction: 'Fetch' is a useful spell for catching and retrieving small objects.
序章: 「『取物術』是個用來抓取和取回小物件的實用法術。」

Introduction: Good for putting things in their place.
序章: 「很適合把東西放在其位置。」

Introduction: Like uppity familiars?
序章: 「像那傲慢的熟悉者？」

Introduction: I'd put you in your place, but I'm above such things.
序章: 「我會把你放在位置上，但我自居於此等之上。」

Introduction: Besides, my place is above your reach.
序章: 「再者，我的位置在你無法及之處。」

Introduction: Henry is a hermit who lives by the Flying Falls to the south.  Great sense of humor.
序章: 「亨利是個隱士，住在南邊飛瀑附近。幽默感十足。」

Introduction: That means he actually laughs at Erasmus's jokes.
序章: 「那表示他真的會笑伊拉斯謨的笑話。」

Introduction: I think he borrowed something the last time I saw him, but I can't remember exactly what it was.
序章: 「我想他上次見面時借了什麼東西，但我記不清確切是什麼。」

Introduction: Oh, well, Henry is a great one to talk to.
序章: 「噢，嗯，亨利是個很好談的人。」

Introduction: But 'e 'ardly ever 'appens to 'ave much to say.
序章: 「但他幾乎從不多說。」

Introduction: You need to complete your quest here before you can become initiated as a Wizard.
序章: 「你必須先完成此地的任務，才能成為法師。」

Introduction: If Magic be the lute of life, play on!
序章: 「如果魔法是生命的琴弦，那就奏響吧！」

Introduction: The quote was Music", not "Magic"!  And it was "food", not "lute"."
序章: 「引文是『音樂』，不是『魔法』！而且是『食物』，不是『琴弦』。」

Introduction: How about:  Magic makes the world go 'round"?"
序章: 「怎麼樣：『魔法讓世界轉動』？」

Introduction: MONEY makes the world go 'round!
序章: 「金錢讓世界轉動！」

Introduction: Oh!
Very well, then.  To put it in my own words:  Magic is the essence and the soul of life, and the Wizard is her poet"."
序章: 「好吧。用我自己的話說：『魔法是生命的本質與靈魂，法師是它的詩人』。」

Introduction: Actually, it was Merlin who said that first, but he used the term Magician", not "Wizard"."
序章: 「實際上，那是梅林先說的，但他用的是『魔術師』，不是『法師』。」

Introduction: Fenrus, there are times when you would look better as a newt!
序章: 「芬魯斯，有時候你做隻蠑螈會更好看！」

Introduction: Why is that?
序章: 「為什麼？」

Introduction: Because newts can't talk!
序章: 「因為蠑螈不會說話！」

Introduction: Neither can most rats.
序章: 「大多數老鼠也不會。」

Introduction: Just a little something to challenge the mind and magic with that I like to play every now and again.  Fenrus isn't much of a challenge any more.
序章: 「只是我不時喜歡玩的挑戰心智和魔法的小遊戲。芬魯斯已經不太能挑戰我了。」

Introduction: That's because I always win.
序章: 「那是因為我總是贏。」

Introduction: I still think you're cheating somehow!
序章: 「我還是覺得你在作弊！」

Introduction: Anyway, the game involves casting four spells:  'Flame Dart', 'Open', 'Fetch', and 'Trigger'.
序章: 「不管怎樣，遊戲涉及施放四個法術：『火焰鏢』、『開啟術』、『取物術』和『觸發術』。」

Introduction: And to make it worth your while, if you beat me, I'll teach you a new spell called 'Dazzle'.
序章: 「為了讓你有所收穫，如果你打敗我，我會教你一個叫『眩目術』的新法術。」

Introduction: There are many types of magic mirrors.  One of the more common types is the one that has a strange face popping up every so often.
序章: 「有很多種魔法鏡。較常見的一種是時不時會冒出奇怪臉孔的那種。」

Introduction: Reminds me of the one in the master bedroom.
序章: 「讓我想起主臥室裡的那個。」

Introduction: That's not magical.
序章: 「那不是魔法。」

Introduction: No, but it does have a strange face peering from it whenever you are in the room.
序章: 「不，但當你在房間裡時，總有個奇怪的臉從裡面往外看。」

Introduction: At any rate, I used to have a nice magic hand mirror I misplaced around here somewhere.  It could be used to reflect a spell back upon the caster.
序章: 「不管怎樣，我以前有個漂亮的魔法手鏡，不知怎地在這附近弄丟了。它可以用來將法術反彈回施咒者身上。」

Introduction: So, if you happen upon a small golden magic mirror with a dragon handle on it, you'll know it's mine.
序章: 「所以，如果你碰巧發現一面有龍把手的小金色魔法鏡，就知道是我的。」

Introduction: It was the flagon with the dragon.  The mirror had a leer.
序章: 「那是有龍的酒瓶。鏡子有個譎笑。」

Introduction: Oh, that's right.
序章: 「噢，沒錯。」

Introduction: Well, if you find a magic mirror with a face on it, you'll know it reflects spells.
序章: 「嗯，如果你找到一面有臉的魔法鏡，就會知道它能反射法術。」

Introduction: Isn't that the Vampire who fell in love with throats?
序章: 「那不是愛上喉嚨的吸血鬼嗎？」

Introduction: No, no! That's a 'neck romancer'!
序章: 「不，不！那是『頸戀者』！」

Introduction: A necromancer is a magic user who has a rather unsavory relationship with corpses.
序章: 「死靈法師是與屍體有著相當不適關係的魔法使用者。」

Introduction: Does that mean a necromancer has ghoul friends?
序章: 「那表示死靈法師有鬼怪朋友嗎？」

Introduction: It means a necromancer is someone who deals with the undead.
序章: 「它表示死靈法師是處理不死生物的人。」

Introduction: Canasta with cadavers and Spades with shades, eh?
序章: 「跟屍體玩卡納斯塔，跟幽靈玩黑桃，哈？」

Introduction: He laughs too much when he casts his spells.
序章: 「他施法時笑得太多了。」

Introduction: That's because his jokes are funnier than yours.
序章: 「那是因為他的笑話比你的好笑。」

Introduction: That spell lets you open things, with a mere gesture.
序章: 「那個法術讓你只需一個姿勢就能打開東西。」

Introduction: If you're really good at it, you can open locks.  Useful when you lose your keys.
序章: 「如果你真的很擅長，你可以打開鎖。當你丟失鑰匙時很有用。」

Introduction: Your keys are in the study under the skull.
序章: 「你的鑰匙在書房骷髏下面。」

Introduction: Why would they be there?
序章: 「為什麼會在那裡？」

Introduction: They're skeleton keys, aren't they?
序章: 「那些是骨頭鑰匙，不是嗎？」

Introduction: Looks to me as if she missed the back part of the alley, and the spell only applies to areas in town above the ground.  Pretty shoddy, if you ask me!
序章: 「在我看來，她遺漏了巷子的後半部分，法術只適用於城鎮地面以上的區域。如果你問我，相當馬虎！」

Introduction: Nobody asked you.
序章: 「沒人問你。」

Introduction: Erana's spell prevents the use of excessive violence or hostile magic within Spielburg's walls, with the exception of a couple of places.
序章: 「伊瑞娜的法術在史畢柏格的城牆內防止過度暴力或敵意魔法的使用，除了幾個地方。」

Introduction: It's important for a magic user to know as many spells as possible. You never know just what might come in handy.
序章: 「魔法使用者知道盡可能多的法術是很重要的。你永遠不知道什麼派上用場。」

Introduction: Magical traps are a good way to keep thieves out of your chests and other places.
序章: 「魔法陷阱是把小偷擋在箱子和其他地方外的好方法。」

Introduction: Put a fire trap on a chest and when someone goes to open it, he goes up in smoke.
序章: 「在箱子上設置火焰陷阱，當有人試著打開它時，他就化為煙霧。」

Introduction: Just so long as you don't forget about it and try to open the chest yourself.
序章: 「只要你別忘了它，自己試著打開箱子就好。」

Introduction: I only got a little singed the last time.
序章: 「上次我只是稍微被燒焦了。」

Introduction: Trigger is used to set off an existing spell.
序章: 「觸發術用來激發現有的法術。」

Introduction: If a chest has a magical trap on it, you can set the trap off by casting a 'Trigger' spell.
序章: 「如果箱子上有魔法陷阱，你可以施放『觸發術』來激發陷阱。」

Introduction: Just make sure you're out of range of the trap.
序章: 「只要確保你在陷阱範圍外。」

Introduction: I can set up other spells and let someone else 'Trigger' them.
序章: 「我可以設置其他法術，讓別人『觸發』它們。」

Introduction: I fixed Henry the Hermit's cave for him so that he can do a lot of magic with only one spell.
序章: 「我為隱士亨利修理他的洞穴，這樣他只需一個法術就能進行大量魔法。」

Introduction: The only thing I've seen the brigand warlock cast is sneezing powder.
序章: 「我見過山賊術士施放的唯一東西是打噴嚏粉。」

Introduction: Zara has a real flair for the theatric.  Her entrance at the magic shop is a bit showy but effective.
序章: 「札拉真的很有戲劇天份。她在魔法店的出場有點浮誇但很有效。」

Introduction: It's a pity she has no sense of humor.
序章: 「可惜她沒有幽默感。」

Introduction: Just because she doesn't laugh at your jokes doesn't mean that she lacks a sense of humor.
序章: 「只因為她不笑你的笑話不表示她沒有幽默感。」

Introduction: It just means that she has good taste.
序章: 「它只表示她品味不錯。」

Introduction: Behind the wizard's stern and forbidding appearance you see a twinkle in his eye.  You suspect that he is not always as serious as he appears.
序章: 在巫師嚴肅而令人敬畏的外表背後，你看到他眼中的一絲閃光。你懷疑他並不總是看起來那麼認真。

Introduction: Erasmus is my Familiar.
序章: 「伊拉斯謨是我的熟悉者。」

Introduction: Isn't that the other way around?
序章: 「那不是相反嗎？」

Introduction: Familiar is my Erasmus?  Well, I've always found Erasmus to be overly familiar....
序章: 「熟悉者是我的伊拉斯謨？嗯，我一直認為伊拉斯謨過於親近…」

Introduction: No, no.  I mean, aren't you MY Familiar?
序章: 「不，不。我是說，你不是我的熟悉者嗎？」

Introduction: Tsk, tsk.  All these years and you still don't know.
序章: 「嘖嘖。這麼多年了你還是不知道。」

Introduction: What's there to say.  For a rat, I'm a really great guy.
序章: 「有什麼好說的。作為老鼠，我真的是個好人。」

Introduction: Although for such a great guy, you're really a rat.
序章: 「雖然你是個那麼好的傢伙，但你真的是隻老鼠。」

Introduction: Fenrus looks back at you with a grin.
序章: 芬魯斯咧嘴回頭看著你。

Introduction: These stones are the base of the tower spire.
序章: 這些石頭是塔尖的基座。

Introduction: Yes!  It's the columnation of your adventures thus far (Ionic, isn't it?).
序章: 是！這是你迄今冒險的柱式結構（愛奧尼亞式的，不是嗎？）。

Introduction: This stone is the base of the tower spire.
序章: 這塊石頭是塔尖的基座。

Introduction: Yes.
序章: 是。

Introduction: No.
序章: 不。

Introduction: I do so enjoy playing the Mage's Maze.
序章: 「我真的很喜歡玩法師迷宮。」

Introduction: Come in and sit down.
序章: 「進來坐下。」

Introduction: Fenrus, our guest has arrived.
序章: 「芬魯斯，我們的客人到了。」

Introduction: Cheese, please!
序章: 「起司，拜託！」

Introduction: Ahh!
Did you hear the joke about the witches broom?
序章: 「你聽過關於巫婆掃帚的笑話嗎？」

Introduction: That's odd. It's sweeping the valley!
序章: 「這有點奇怪。它正在清掃山谷！」

Introduction: Oh, That's a pity!
序章: 「哦，真是可惜啊！」

Introduction: Let's just brush that one aside, shall we?
序章: 「那我們就別提這個了，好吧？」

Introduction: Do you know the difference between a Cheetaur and a comma?
序章: 「你知道獵豹人和逗號的差別嗎？」

Introduction: A Cheetaur has claws at the end of its paws, and a comma is a pause at the end of a clause!
序章: 「獵豹人的爪子在牠的爪掌尖端，而逗號是句子末尾的停頓！」

Introduction: Spoilsport!
序章: 「掃興鬼！」

Introduction: Do you know which has more legs, one cow or no cow?
序章: 「你知道哪個腿更多，一隻牛還是沒有牛？」

Introduction: You're right!  NO cow has more than four legs.  These are too easy.
序章: 「你說得對！沒有牛腿數超過四條。這些太簡單了。」

Introduction: Too easy, huh?  Well, I'll just have to think of a better one.
序章: 「太簡單了？好吧，我得想個更好的笑話。」

Introduction: Do you know what time it is when Otto walks through your door?
序章: 「你知道奧圖走進你家門的時間是幾點嗎？」

Introduction: Time to get a new door!
序章: 「該換個新門了！」

Introduction: So you've meet Otto already.
序章: 「所以你已經見過奧圖了。」

Introduction: Do you know what's big, purple, and eats ungefaehr-liche dinge?
序章: 「你知道什麼又大又紫，還吃危險東西嗎？」

Introduction: Big, purple ungefaehr-liche dinge eaters!
序章: 「又大又紫的危險東西吃貨！」

Introduction: Well... let me figure out something tougher.
序章: 「嗯。..讓我想想更難的。」

Introduction: Large purple ungefaehr-liche dinge eaters are all too common in the forest these days.
序章: 「現在森林裡又大又紫的危險東西吃貨到處都是。」

Introduction: Is it true that a monster can't hurt you if you're carrying a torch?
序章: 「如果你拿著火把，怪物就傷不了你，是真的嗎？」

Introduction: Actually, it depends on how fast you carry it!
序章: 「實際上，那要看你揮得多快啊！」

Introduction: Do you know the difference between a wounded Triceratops and a magic user casting the 'Fly' spell?
序章: 「你知道受傷的三角龍和法師施放『飛行術』的差別嗎？」

Introduction: One is a raging Saurus and the other is a soaring Magus!
序章: 「一個是狂怒的索魯斯，另一個是騰空的魔法師！」

Introduction: Darn, I thought I made that one up myself!
序章: 「該死，我以為是我自己想出來的！」

Introduction: I figured one was a dying lizard and the other was a flying wizard.
序章: 「我以為一個是奄奄一息的蜥蜴，另一個是飛行的巫師。」

Introduction: Sorry, Fenrus, but 'He who tells the jokes gets to make up the punch lines'.
序章: 「抱歉，芬魯斯，『講笑話的人有權決定笑點』。」

Introduction: Do you know what you get when a Tyrannosaurus running 25 mph eastward meets another Tyrannosaurus running 15 mph westward?
序章: 「你知道一隻以時速 25 英里向東跑的暴龍遇上一隻以時速 15 英里向西跑的暴龍會發生什麼嗎？」

Introduction: Tyrannosaurus wrecks!
序章: 「暴龍大碰撞！」

Introduction: That was a smashing good joke, I think.
序章: 「我想那是個很不錯的笑話。」

Introduction: It would be polite to wait until Erasmus puts down his cup of tea before asking any more questions.
序章: 等伊拉斯謨放下他的茶杯再問他其他問題會更有禮貌。

Introduction: It would be best to wait until Erasmus puts down his cup of tea before asking any questions.
序章: 等伊拉斯謨放下他的茶杯再問他任何問題是最好的做法。

Introduction: As you stare at the steepness of the staircas, you figure that either the wizard is a fitness fanatic, or he uses a lot of "teleport" spells.
序章: 看著陡峭的樓梯，你心想巫師要嘛是健身狂，要嘛就經常用『傳送術』。

Introduction: The table is elaborately carved on the sides.  Erasmus has a teacup on the table in front of him.
序章: 桌子側面雕刻精美。伊拉斯謨面前的桌上放著一個茶杯。

Introduction: The walls look like they were formed out of living rock.  (You just hope it doesn't get hungry while you're here.)
序章: 牆壁看起來像是由活石形成的。(你只希望它在你待著期間不會餓肚子。)

Introduction: To the east, the peaks of the Wolf's Bane Mountains are clearly visible.
序章: 向東方向可以清楚看見狼禍山脈的山峰。

Introduction: That's odd.  You could have sworn it was nighttime, yet daylight is streaming in through the window.  When is this place, anyway?
序章: 這很奇怪。你本來可以發誓這是夜間，但陽光卻從窗戶射進來。這地方到底是什麼時候啊？

Introduction: Click on the Magic Flask to drink a Magic Power Potion while playing the game.
序章: 遊戲時點擊魔法瓶可以喝一個魔法藥水。

Introduction: The drink burns as it goes down.
序章: 這個飲料喝下去時會灼燒。

Introduction: You don't have a Magic Power Potion.
序章: 你沒有魔法藥水。

Introduction: Sorry, but you don't know that spell.  (Now don't you feel guilty that you lied to Erasmus about knowing it?)
序章: 抱歉，你不知道那個魔法。(現在你是不是為對伊拉斯謨撒謊說你會而感到內疚？)

Introduction: As I promised, I shall now teach you the spell known as 'Erasmus's Razzle Dazzle'.
序章: 「如我承諾，我現在要教你叫『伊拉斯謨的眩目術』的魔法。」

Introduction: The magic just isn't there.
序章: 根本沒有足夠的魔法。

Introduction: Well!  You don't have to rub it in, you know!
序章: 「好吧！你不用這樣嘲笑我，你知道吧！」

Introduction: Too much for you?  I guess you should work on your magic.  Maybe next time you'll be more of a challenge.
序章: 「這對你來說太難了？我想你應該多練習魔法。也許下次會更有挑戰。」

Introduction: Guess I still have the knack for the old Maze game.
序章: 「看來我對老迷宮遊戲還是有一手的。」

Introduction: You hear the sound of dainty footsteps behind you.
序章: 你聽到身後傳來輕快的腳步聲。

Introduction: There's no path to the west, only bushes.
序章: 西邊沒有路，只有灌木叢。

Introduction: The eastern path is completely blocked by bushes.
序章: 東邊的路被灌木叢完全擋住。

Introduction: The grass seems to grow well here.
序章: 這裡的草長得很茂盛。

Introduction: The castle of the Baron Stefan von Spielburg, lord of this valley.
序章: 史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的城堡，這個山谷的領主。

Introduction: Nobody is here to open the portcullis.
序章: 沒有人在這裡打開城門。

Introduction: Come in.  Come in.  You have been expected.  Baron von Spielburg awaits you in the castle.
序章: 「進來。進來。我們一直在等你。馮·史畢柏格男爵在城堡裡等你。」

Introduction: Well, enter the castle grounds!
序章: 「那就進入城堡吧！」

Introduction: Who's there?  Come out and show yourself!
序章: 「誰在那兒？出來讓我看看！」

Introduction: Are you speaking to me?
序章: 「你在跟我說話嗎？」

Introduction: Please step where I can see you.
序章: 「請走到我能看到的地方。」

Introduction: You can't see much of the gatekeeper over the battlements.
序章: 你無法從城牆看到守門人的太多細節。

Introduction: Baba Yaga
序章: 芭芭雅嘎

Introduction: Baroness
序章: 男爵夫人

Introduction: Baron
序章: 男爵

Introduction: Barnard
序章: 巴納德

Introduction: Brigands
序章: 山賊

Introduction: Castle
序章: 城堡

Introduction: Curse
序章: 詛咒

Introduction: Elsa
序章: 艾爾莎

Introduction: Graveyard
序章: 墓地

Introduction: Guards
序章: 衛兵

Introduction: Hero
序章: 英雄

Introduction: Hut
序章: 小屋

Introduction: Job
序章: 工作

Introduction: Open Gate
序章: 打開城門

Introduction: Brigand Leader
序章: 山賊首領

Introduction: Loss
序章: 喪失

Introduction: Brigand Warlock
序章: 山賊巫師

Introduction: Monsters
序章: 怪物

Introduction: Name
序章: 名字

Introduction: Protection
序章: 保護

Introduction: Reward
序章: 獎勵

Introduction: Stable
序章: 馬廄

Introduction: Treasure
序章: 寶藏

Introduction: Valley
序章: 山谷

Introduction: Yorick
序章: 尤瑞克

Introduction: Very well.
序章: 「好吧。」

Introduction: Just a minute while I raise the gate.
序章: 「稍等，我馬上升起城門。」

Introduction: This powerful Ogress is the cause of all the Baron's misfortune.  She is a vile creature who knows much magic.
序章: 「這個強大的女食人妖是男爵所有不幸的根源。她是個邪惡的生物，懂得很多魔法。」

Introduction: The Baroness died soon after the birth of her daughter.  The Baron still mourns her.
序章: 「男爵夫人在生下女兒不久後就去世了。男爵仍在為她哀悼。」

Introduction: My Lord sees no one since the loss of his son and daughter.
序章: 「自從失去兒子和女兒後，我的主人就不見任何人。」

Introduction: The Baronet Barnard von Spielburg was a proud young man and had the dark hair and eyes of his father the Baron.
序章: 「男爵少爺巴納德·馮·史畢柏格是一個驕傲的年輕人，有著像他父親男爵一樣的黑髮和眼睛。」

Introduction: He rode off one morning and his horse returned without him.  The horse had been raked by the claws of some large animal.
序章: 「他一個早上騎馬出去，只有馬回來了。馬被某個大動物的爪子划傷了。」

Introduction: There is still a reward offered for the safe return of the Baronet, but I fear he is dead.
序章: 「仍然有獎勵懸賞男爵少爺的安全返回，但我恐怕他已經死了。」

Introduction: There are now too few guards to take out the brigands which rob our people and drive away the traders.
序章: 「現在衛兵太少，無法擊滅搶劫我們人民和驅趕商人的山賊。」

Introduction: They hide somewhere near the edge of this valley.  The brigands are protected by a magic user and they have a shrewd leader.
序章: 「他們躲在山谷邊緣附近的某個地方。山賊由一個法師保護，他們還有一個精明的首領。」

Introduction: This is the castle of Baron Stefan von Spielburg.
序章: 「這是史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的城堡。」

Introduction: The Baron ordered Baba Yaga to leave and she first cursed the graveyard. When he then sent the Baronial Guards after her, only their Captain returned to the castle.
序章: 「男爵命令芭芭雅嘎滾蛋，她首先詛咒了墓地。當他派遣男爵衛隊追捕她時，只有他們的隊長回到城堡。」

Introduction: The Captain spoke the words, "Hear me, oh mighty Baron.  Thou hast angered me, and thus you are cursed.  All that you value shall you lose; all that you treasure shall be taken from you," in a strange voice.
序章: 隊長用奇怪的聲音說：「聽我的，偉大的男爵。你惹惱了我，所以你被詛咒了。你所珍視的一切都將失去；你所寶貴的一切都將被奪走。」

Introduction: The captain then dissolved into a pool of sticky blackness.
序章: 「隊長然後化成了一灘黏稠的黑色物質。」

Introduction: Elsa von Spielburg was a lovely young child with braided blond hair and bright blue eyes like her mother the Baroness used to have.
序章: 「艾爾莎·馮·史畢柏格曾是一個可愛的小女孩，有著梳成辮子的金髮和明亮的藍眼睛，就像她的母親男爵夫人一樣。」

Introduction: Even the jester Yorick joined the search for her.
序章: 「連弄臣尤瑞克也加入了搜索她的行列。」

Introduction: The Baron sent a troop of guards to storm Baba Yaga's hut, but I think their skulls sit atop her fence.  Elsa von Spielburg would be about eighteen now.
序章: 「男爵派遣一隊衛兵突襲芭芭雅嘎的小屋，但我想他們的頭骨現在就在她的籬笆上。艾爾莎·馮·史畢柏格現在差不多十八歲了。」

Introduction: Baba Yaga cursed the graveyard such that the dead can not rest at night. One can not go near the graveyard after dark without risking his own death unless one uses protection.
序章: 「芭芭雅嘎詛咒了墓地，使得死人在夜間無法安息。不用保護的話，任何人都不能在天黑後靠近墓地而不冒死亡的危險。」

Introduction: The Baron von Spielburg lost most of his guards trying to defeat Baba Yaga.
序章: 「馮·史畢柏格男爵在試圖擊敗芭芭雅嘎時失去了大部分衛兵。」

Introduction: 'Hero of the Realm of Spielburg' is a title of respect and honor. A true hero can release this land from its curse using his intelligence and courage.
序章: 「『史畢柏格領地的英雄』是一個尊敬和榮譽的稱號。真正的英雄可以用他的聰慧和勇氣解救這片土地。」

Introduction: Baba Yaga has her hut to the northwest.  It is a very dangerous place and many Baronial Guards have been lost there.
序章: 「芭芭雅嘎的小屋在西北方。那是一個非常危險的地方，許多男爵衛隊都在那裡喪生。」

Introduction: The stablekeeper could use a strong young person to help clean out the stables.
序章: 「馬廄管理員需要一個強壯的年輕人幫忙清理馬廄。」

Introduction: It does not pay much, but it is good, honest work.
序章: 「報酬不多，但這是誠實的工作。」

Introduction: If you would like to take the job, come on in.
序章: 「如果你想接這份工作，進來吧。」

Introduction: The leader of the brigands must be a good strategist.
序章: 「山賊的首領必定是個很好的戰略家。」

Introduction: He has organized and lead many raids which result in much treasure and little loss on the part of the brigands.  Without their leader, the brigands would soon flee.
序章: 「他組織並領導了許多掠奪行動，獲得大量寶藏但山賊的損失很少。沒有他們的首領，山賊很快就會逃散。」

Introduction: Ten years ago the Baron's daughter Elsa was stolen away by magic.  Five years back, the Baronet Barnard von Spielburg went on a hunt from which he did not return.  This was all clearly part of Baba Yaga's curse.
序章: 「十年前，男爵的女兒艾爾莎被魔法偷走。五年前，男爵少爺巴納德·馮·史畢柏格去狩獵後就沒有回來。這一切顯然都是芭芭雅嘎詛咒的一部分。」

Introduction: Not much is known about him, but he seems to use more magic potions and powders than spells.
序章: 「對他瞭解不多，但他似乎用的是魔法藥劑和粉末多於咒語。」

Introduction: There are many foul creatures stalking the forest since the Baron has too few guards to protect this valley from them.
序章: 「森林裡有許多可怕的生物在徘徊，因為男爵太少衛兵無法保護山谷。」

Introduction: My name is Karl.
序章: 「我叫卡爾。」

Introduction: There is an unguent you can purchase from the Healer which protects against the dead that walk by night.
序章: 「你可以從治療師那裡買到一種軟膏，可以保護你免受夜間行走的死人傷害。」

Introduction: There is still a reward offered for the safe return of the Baronet and Elsa von Spielburg of 50 gold.
序章: 「仍然為男爵少爺和艾爾莎·馮·史畢柏格的安全返回懸賞 50 金幣。」

Introduction: For the capture or death of the Brigand Warlock there is the reward of 50 gold.
序章: 「捕獲或擊斃山賊巫師有 50 金幣的獎勵。」

Introduction: For the capture or death of the Brigand leader there is the reward of 50 gold and the title of 'Hero of Spielburg'.
序章: 「捕獲或擊斃山賊首領有 50 金幣的獎勵和『史畢柏格英雄』的稱號。」

Introduction: Cleaning up after the horses is a good way to build up your muscles.
序章: 「清理馬匹是鍛鍊肌肉的好辦法。」

Introduction: Many merchants have been robbed by the brigands.  There is a merchant in town who could tell you about his robbery.
序章: 「許多商人都被山賊搶劫過。城裡有個商人可以告訴你他被搶的事。」

Introduction: Spielburg Valley was once a prosperous and peaceful place before the Baron lost his family.
序章: 「在男爵失去家人之前，史畢柏格山谷曾經是個繁榮與和平的地方。」

Introduction: Now the Baron will not leave his castle and does not know the ruin this land has come to.
序章: 「現在男爵不肯離開他的城堡，不知道這片土地已經成廢墟。」

Introduction: He was a very jolly fool who loved to laugh.  He was devoted to Elsa and swore he would not return until Elsa was safe.
序章: 「他是個非常活潑愛笑的傻瓜。他對艾爾莎很執著，發誓在艾爾莎安全之前不會回來。」

Introduction: The mountains in the distance ring the valley of Spielburg, creating a formidable barrier to travelers.
序章: 遠處的山脈環繞著史畢柏格山谷，對旅人構成了可怕的障礙。

Introduction: The portcullis has been raised, and the way to the castle is clear.
序章: 城門已經升起，進城堡的路已經暢通。

Introduction: The portcullis presents a severe obstacle for entry to the castle.
序章: 城門對進入城堡造成了嚴重的阻擋。

Introduction: The castle is securely locked for the night.
序章: 城堡在夜間被牢牢鎖上。

Introduction: The gate is already open.
序章: 城門已經打開了。

Introduction: After climbing down the wall you says to yourself, "Self," you says, "that wasn't too bad."
序章: 從牆上爬下來後，你對自己說：「嘿，」你說，「這還不算太糟。」

Introduction: Once more the guard raises the portcullis.
序章: 守衛再次升起城門。

Introduction: That smarts.  Maybe you should move a little more quickly.
序章: 那很疼。也許你應該快一點。

Introduction: The guard, sensing something is awry, raises the portcullis.
序章: 守衛感覺到異常，升起了城門。

Introduction: Experience is the best teacher.
序章: 經驗是最好的老師。

Introduction: Practice makes perfect.
序章: 熟能生巧。

Introduction: Try, try again etc...
序章: 再接再厲…

Introduction: Take a break.  It's Mueller time.
序章: 休息一下。是時候放鬆了。

Introduction: The gatehouse is more difficult to climb than it first appeared.
序章: 城門樓比起初看起來還要難爬。

Introduction: Remember what happened to Humpty Dumpty.
序章: 記得蛋頭先生的下場嗎？

Introduction: The gate guard keeps a vigilant eye out for trouble as he paces back and forth on the castle parapet.  He walks with the easy stride of a veteran soldier.
序章: 城門衛兵在城牆頂上踱步，警惕地尋找麻煩。他的步伐輕鬆自在，是老兵的風範。

Introduction: The sky is clear except for a few clouds.
序章: 天空清澈，只有幾朵浮雲。

Introduction: The towers stand out above the castle walls.
序章: 塔樓在城牆上高聳入雲。

Introduction: The gatehouse is a massive structure with a portcullis closing off access to the castle.
序章: 城門樓是堅實的建築，落下的鐵柵欄擋住了城堡的入口。

Introduction: The guards won't allow you to do that.
序章: 衛兵不會讓你這樣做。

Introduction: You're not in a good place to climb.
序章: 你現在的位置不適合爬。

Introduction: Leave the climbing to those who know how.
序章: 爬牆還是讓懂的人去吧。

Introduction: You can't see anything through the window from here.
序章: 從這裡透過窗戶什麼都看不到。

Introduction: The building's appearance is deceptive;  it evidently extends well into the castle wall.  From your present vantage point, the barracks looks like it could house some sixty guards.
序章: 這棟建築的外觀很會騙人；它明顯深入城牆內部。從你目前的角度看，兵營似乎可以容納六十多名衛兵。

Introduction: The castle's central keep rises above the rear of the courtyard.
序章: 城堡的中央堡壘在院落後方高聳。

Introduction: The castle wall feels very solid.
序章: 城牆摸起來非常堅固。

Introduction: This is the chimney to the baking oven.
序章: 這是烤爐的煙囪。

Introduction: The troops once proudly followed this crest.
序章: 部隊曾經以這個紋章為榮。

Introduction: The barracks entrance.
序章: 兵營的入口。

Introduction: There is no answer to your knock.  The guards must all be asleep.
序章: 沒人回應你的敲門聲。守衛一定都睡著了。

Introduction: The guards access the walkway through the towers.
序章: 衛兵通過塔樓進入走道。

Introduction: The castle has a garden area with a doorway leading from the castle keep.
序章: 城堡有一個花園區，從城堡堡壘有一扇門通過去。

Introduction: The guard looks like he is either very tired or very lazy.
序章: 這衛兵看起來要不是累死了，要不就是懶死了。

Introduction: I'm not here to answer your stupid questions.  Go talk to Karl the gatekeeper.  He talks to anyone about almost anything.
序章: 「我不是來回答你笨問題的。去找城門守衛卡爾。他什麼都跟人談。」

Introduction: These are the mountains that ring the valley of Spielburg.
序章: 這些是環繞史畢柏格山谷的群山。

Introduction: It's an empty storage pot.
序章: 這是個空的儲藏罐。

Introduction: Go away.  This is just the barracks for us guards.
序章: 「走開。這只是我們衛兵的兵營。」

Introduction: This is the barracks area.  To the north, you see the castle's central keep. To the east, you see the main courtyard and the stables beyond.
序章: 這是兵營區。北方是城堡的中央堡壘。東邊是主院落和後面的馬廄。

Introduction: Along the wall to the southeast is the gatehouse.  A line of bushes partially obscures a defensive pit along the wall. To the west, you can see the barracks and a man standing in front of them.
序章: 沿著牆往東南方是城門樓。一排灌木叢部分遮擋了牆邊的防禦溝。西邊可以看到兵營和站在前面的一個人。

Introduction: The guard would object.
序章: 衛兵會反對。

Introduction: You barely get to sleep when the guard on night patrol kicks you out.
序章: 你才剛要睡著，巡邏的守衛就把你踢出去了。

Introduction: The guard chuckles as you make a fool of yourself.
序章: 衛兵看著你出糗，咧嘴笑了。

Introduction: The sky goes on forever when it's this clear.

序章: 大門已經用鎖和門栓從裡面牢牢封住了。

Introduction: That's not too bright an idea while the guards are watching.
序章: 在守衛注視下這麼做不太聰明。

Introduction: The guards look to be middle-aged, but in good physical condition.  Their clothing is faded and patched.  Even the horns look dull.
序章: 守衛看起來中年紀，但身體狀況良好。他們的衣服褪色且打過補丁。連角都看起來黯淡無光。

Introduction: I told you not to mention it.  Now you've hurt his feelings, and he won't talk until he gets over it.
序章: 「我告訴過你別提起這個。現在你傷害了他的感受，他要等到平復才會說話。」

Introduction: Bald spot
序章: 禿斑

Introduction: Barracks
序章: 軍營

Introduction: Daughter
序章: 女兒

Introduction: Name
序章: 名字

Introduction: Karl
序章: 卡爾

Introduction: You're embarrassing the poor fellow.
序章: 你讓這可憐傢伙很尷尬。

Introduction: Pierre's very proud of his moustache, but don't mention his bald spot.
序章: 「皮埃爾對他的鬍子很自豪，但別提他的禿斑。」

Introduction: No one enters the castle without the Baron's permission.
序章: 「沒有男爵的許可，任何人都進不了城堡。」

Introduction: The Baron sees no one.
序章: 「男爵不見任何人。」

Introduction: The barracks are off to my right, but you have no business going there.
序章: 「軍營在我右邊，但你沒有理由去那裡。」

Introduction: This is the castle of Baron Stefan von Spielburg.
序章: 「這是史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的城堡。」

Introduction: The Baron's daughter has been gone for years.
序章: 「男爵的女兒已經失蹤多年了。」

Introduction: My name is Frederick.
序章: 「我的名字是弗雷德里克。」

Introduction: Karl is the gatekeeper. He is a good one to answer questions.
序章: 「卡爾是門衛。他很樂意回答問題。」

Introduction: His name is Pierre.
序章: 「他的名字是皮埃爾。」

Introduction: The light casts a welcome glow on such a moonless night.
序章: 在這樣無月的夜晚，燈光投出令人歡迎的光芒。

Introduction: The guard is dressed in worn, but neatly patched, clothing.  He has obviously spent a lot of time maintaining that magnificent mustache.  (But you'd better not mention his bald spot.)
序章: 守衛穿著破舊但整潔打補丁的衣服。他顯然花了很多時間維護那個漂亮的鬍子。(但你最好別提他的禿斑。)

Introduction: Name
序章: 名字

Introduction: I'm not talking to you.
序章: 「我不跟你說話。」

Introduction: Better not touch the guards.  They look tough.
序章: 最好別惹守衛。他們看起來很兇悍。

Introduction: Why don't you ask Karl?
序章: 「你怎麼不去問卡爾？」

Introduction: Don't get me mad.
序章: 「別惹我生氣。」

Introduction: His name is Frederick.
序章: 「他的名字是弗雷德里克。」

Introduction: My name is Pierre.
序章: 「我的名字是皮埃爾。」

Introduction: The Baron von Spielburg and the Baronet await you in the great hall.
序章: 「史畢柏格男爵和男爵之子在大廳等你。」

Introduction: After a wonderful meal with the Baron and his son, a peaceful night's sleep in a featherdown bed, and a filling breakfast in bed, you are ready to go adventuring once more.
序章: 與男爵和他的兒子共享美食，在羽絨床上安穩入眠，還有在床上享用豐盛的早餐，你已經準備好再次踏上冒險之旅。

Introduction: I'm Frederick.
序章: 我是弗雷德里克。

Introduction: I'm Pierre.
序章: 「我是皮埃爾。」

Introduction: This is the central keep of Baron Stefan von Spielburg's castle.  The castle grounds look like no one has taken care of them.  The castle is still impressive close up, but it looks rather dirty.
序章: 這是史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵城堡的主堡。城堡的周圍看起來沒人照料。城堡近距離看起來仍然很宏偉，但看起來相當髒亂。

Introduction: The castle walls are very high.  You judge that you would not be able to climb them.
序章: 城堡的牆壁非常高。你判斷你爬不上去。

Introduction: You barely get to sleep when the guard on night patrol kicks you out.
序章: 你才剛要睡著，巡邏的守衛就把你踢出去了。

Introduction: The ground is almost bare here due to the pacing of so many guards.
序章: 由於很多守衛不停走來走去，這裡的地面幾乎是光禿禿的。

Introduction: Go west?  And get scratches all over your nice new clothes? I think not.
序章: 往西走？把你漂亮的新衣服搞得滿是刮痕？我看不行。

Introduction: The only good Goblin is a dead Goblin!
序章: 只有死亡的哥布林才是好哥布林！

Introduction: What's he staring at?
序章: 他在盯著什麼？

Introduction: Strange place to take a nap.
序章: 在奇怪的地方打盹。

Introduction: He's getting a sun tan?
序章: 他在曬太陽？

Introduction: A good Goblin!
序章: 一個好哥布林！

Introduction: Does anyone know CPR?
序章: 有人會人工呼吸嗎？

Introduction: Limp as a dishrag.
序章: 軟得像抹布。

Introduction: He lost.
序章: 他輸了。

Introduction: You've seen one dead Goblin, you've seen 'em all.
序章: 你看過一具死哥布林，就等於看過他們全部。

Introduction: You know he's broke.
序章: 你知道他沒錢。

Introduction: He never had any money.
序章: 他從來沒有錢。

Introduction: You've already taken his money.
序章: 你已經拿過他的錢了。

Introduction: You find 5 silvers concealed in a pouch.  You take the silvers.
序章: 你在小袋子裡找到了5個銀幣。你把銀幣拿了。

Introduction: You find 4 silvers tucked in his tunic.  He has no use for the silvers anymore, so into your pocket they go.
序章: 你在他的短上衣裡找到了4個銀幣。他不需要這些銀幣了，所以你把它們放進口袋。

Introduction: This poor slob was the big loser at the poker game.  He didn't even have lunch money.
序章: 這個可憐傢伙在撲克牌遊戲中輸得最慘。他甚至連午餐錢都沒有。

Introduction: It's your lucky day!  This was the big winner at last night's poker game. You take his 35 silvers.
序章: 你的幸運日！這傢伙是昨晚撲克牌遊戲的大贏家。你拿走了他的35個銀幣。

Introduction: In this Goblin's left shoe you find 8 silvers.  You look around and slip the silvers into your pocket.
序章: 在這個哥布林的左鞋裡你找到了8個銀幣。你四下張望，把銀幣偷偷放進口袋。

Introduction: Clutched in his hand are 4 silvers.  You take the money.
序章: 他的手裡緊握著4個銀幣。你拿走了這些錢。

Introduction: This guy lost all his dough to a loan shark.
序章: 這傢伙把所有的錢都輸給了高利貸。

Introduction: The Goblin government lost his paycheck.
序章: 哥布林政府弄丟了他的薪水。

Introduction: He didn't have anything.
序章: 他什麼都沒有。

Introduction: It's R2D4!
序章: 是R2D4!

Introduction: It's Hollywood, a virtual Goblin!
序章: 是好萊塢，一個虛擬哥布林！

Introduction: It's Droopy!
序章: 是垂頭喪氣！

Introduction: It's Sneaky!
序章: 是鬼鬼祟祟！

Introduction: It's Nickaroo, a real Goblin!
序章: 是尼卡魯，一個真正的哥布林！

Introduction: It's JohnnyFive!
序章: 是約翰尼五號！

Introduction: A Goblin steps out of the bush.
序章: 一個哥布林從灌木叢裡走出來。

Introduction: One strange bush!
序章: 真奇怪的灌木叢！

Introduction: Wow!  You threw that dead goblin a long way!
序章: 哇！你把那具死哥布林扔得很遠！

Introduction: The Goblin has a mean look in its eyes.  You prepare for battle.
序章: 哥布林眼神兇狠。你準備戰鬥。

Introduction: You're in the Goblin Central Combat Training Zone.  You wonder where all the goblins are coming from, but don't worry about it.  It's just one of those little unsolved mysteries of the universe.
序章: 你在哥布林中央戰鬥訓練區。你好奇這些哥布林是從哪裡來的，但別擔心。這只是宇宙那些未解之謎中的一個小謎團。

Introduction: Walking through those bushes could make you subject to a grave penalty.
序章: 走過那些灌木叢可能會讓你面臨嚴重的懲罰。

Introduction: It's just a bush.
序章: 這只是一叢灌木。

Introduction: It is a common bush.
序章: 這是普通的灌木。

Introduction: It's a small tree.
序章: 這是一棵小樹。

Introduction: The handsome Centaur has a look of pride and dignity as he rakes his field.
序章: 英俊的半人馬邊整地邊展現出尊嚴和驕傲的神情。

Introduction: The majestic Centaur watches you carefully.
序章: 莊嚴的半人馬小心地注視著你。

Introduction: Centaurs
序章: 半人馬

Introduction: Farm
序章: 農場

Introduction: Field
序章: 田地

Introduction: Fruit
序章: 水果

Introduction: Harvest
序章: 收穫

Introduction: Healer
序章: 治療師

Introduction: Hilde
序章: 希爾德

Introduction: Leader
序章: 領袖

Introduction: Farmer's Mart
序章: 農民市集

Introduction: Name
序章: 名字

Introduction: Vegetables
序章: 蔬菜

Introduction: I'm sorry.  I know little about the Baron and his family.
序章: 「我很抱歉。我對男爵和他的家族知之甚少。」

Introduction: The brigands attacked me a few months ago.  I am a strong fighter and my hooves are deadly, but there were too many of them.
序章: 「山賊幾個月前襲擊了我。我是個強悍的戰士，我的蹄子很致命，但他們人太多了。」

Introduction: My right leg was broken, and there is no pride for a Centaur who cannot run. If it had not been for the unlikely intervention of their leader, I would be dead.
序章: 「我的右腿被折斷了，一個無法奔跑的半人馬沒有尊嚴可言。要不是他們領袖的出手相救，我早就死了。」

Introduction: We Centaurs are a very ancient and proud people.
序章: 「我們半人馬是古老而驕傲的民族。」

Introduction: I can grow enough on this little plot to feed my family and the village. You should see this field during the summer.
序章: 「我在這小塊地上種的東西足夠養活我的家人和村子。你應該看看夏天時這片田地的模樣。」

Introduction: There is much work for me to do now and I bid you good day.
序章: 「我現在有很多工作要做，祝你午安。」

Introduction: In a couple of months, I'll have some cherries.  The apples won't ripen until fall.
序章: 「再過兩個月，我就會有櫻桃。蘋果要到秋天才會成熟。」

Introduction: We grow fine fruit in our little orchard and sell it at our stand in town.
序章: 「我們在小果園裡種著上等的水果，並在城裡的攤位賣出。」

Introduction: There you will find fresh cherries in the spring, peaches in summer, and apples in the fall.
序章: 「你會在那裡找到春季的新鮮櫻桃、夏季的桃子和秋季的蘋果。」

Introduction: It is truly a sight to see.
序章: 「那真是美景一樁。」

Introduction: The Healer lives just east of here.  She is very skilled with potions.
序章: 「治療師住在這兒的東邊。她很擅長製作藥水。」

Introduction: Hilde is a bright little filly.  I'm very proud of her.
序章: 「希爾德是個聰慧的小馬駒。我為她感到驕傲。」

Introduction: The leader came up after the brigands attacked me and forced them to stop, ordering them not to hurt people from the town.
序章: 「山賊襲擊我後，那位領袖出現了，強制他們停手，命令他們不得傷害城裡的人。」

Introduction: Then, to my amazement, the leader gave me a healing potion and had me carried to the Healer's house.
序章: 「然後，令我驚訝的是，那位領袖給了我一瓶治療藥水，還讓人把我抬去治療師的家。」

Introduction: It was hard to judge the leader, whose face was obscured by a large helm, but there was compassion unlike what one expects from a brigand.
序章: 「很難判斷那位領袖的為人，因為他的臉被大頭盔遮住了，但他表現出的同情心卻不像一般山賊該有的。」

Introduction: Our stand is in the northeast corner of Spielburg.
序章: 「我們的攤位在史畢柏格的東北角。」

Introduction: The produce is not of the finest this time of year, but you will not find better than Pferdefedern fruit come harvest time.
序章: 「現在這季節的農產品不是最上等的，但到了收穫季，你找不到比費德芬水果更好的。」

Introduction: I am Heinrich Pferdefedern.  I live in town with my daughter Hilde who sells our produce on Market Street.
序章: 「我是海因里希·費德芬。我和我的女兒希爾德住在城裡，她在市集街賣我們的農產品。」

Introduction: There are a few carrots and potatoes left from last season, but I'm just starting to plant the crops now.
序章: 「上一季還剩下一些胡蘿蔔和馬鈴薯，但我現在才開始種植新的作物。」

Introduction: You must go see my daughter, Hilde, who sells our fruits and vegetables on Market Street.
序章: 「你一定要去看我的女兒希爾德，她在市集街賣我們的水果和蔬菜。」

Introduction: Looking at the Centaur's muscles, his  hoe, and his four gleaming hooves, you get the feeling that it might not be the best of ideas.  Besides, he reminds you of your old friend Flicka.
序章: 看著半人馬結實的肌肉、他的鋤頭，還有他四隻閃亮的蹄子，你有種感覺這可能不是個好主意。而且，他讓你想起了你的老朋友弗利卡。

Introduction: You have no reason to hurt the Centaur.  Put that rock away!
序章: 你沒有理由傷害這位半人馬。放下那塊石頭！

Introduction: This tree will never bear fruit.
序章: 這棵樹永遠結不了果。

Introduction: It's a pine tree.
序章: 這是一棵松樹。

Introduction: Three pots wait for the day when they will hold dirt and flowers.
序章: 三個花盆等待著有朝一日被填滿泥土和鮮花。

Introduction: It is a sturdy iron rake with a wooden handle.
序章: 這是一把結實的鐵耙，有著木製的柄。

Introduction: Fields must be carefully tended to produce the best crops.
序章: 為了種出最好的作物，田地必須得到細心照料。

Introduction: The Centaur turns toward you.
序章: 半人馬轉身面向你。

Introduction: Noticing that you are addressing him, the Centaur stops and gives you his attention.
序章: 注意到你在跟他說話，半人馬停了下來，把注意力轉向你。

Introduction: The Centaur stops raking as you approach.
序章: 當你靠近時，半人馬停下鋤地。

Introduction: The forest surrounds the farmer's small fields and orchard.  The crops have yet to be planted.  There are only a few weeds and some grasses at the edge of the forest.
序章: 森林圍繞著農民的小田地和果園。作物還沒有種植。森林邊緣只有一些雜草和草叢。

Introduction: To the east, you can see a small house.  To the west, you see the forest. To the north, beyond the farmer's orchard, you can see the forest.
序章: 往東，你能看到一棟小房子。往西，你看到森林。往北，在農民果園的外側，你能看到森林。

Introduction: The field is hoed and fertilized.
序章: 田地已經被耙過並施過肥。

Introduction: The evening is clear and the stars are bright.  Dark clouds pass over the moon.
序章: 夜晚天朗氣清，星光璀璨。烏雲飄過月亮。

Introduction: The sky is a piercing blue with scudding white clouds.
序章: 天空是刺眼的藍色，白雲飛快地掠過。

Introduction: The garden's dark dirt awaits a new day's sunshine.
序章: 花園的黑色泥土等著新一天的陽光。

Introduction: You see a nicely manicured field.  A Centaur rakes the rows.
序章: 你看到一片經過精心整理的田地。一位半人馬正在耙著田壟。

Introduction: The farmer has painstakingly removed all the rocks from his fields.
序章: 農民費力地從田裡清除了所有石頭。

Introduction: The late snow has made the mountains very pretty.  Fortunately, our valley is sheltered, so the frost has not come to my fields.
序章: 「遲來的雪讓山脈變得很美。幸運的是，我們的山谷被保護著，所以霜凍沒有來到我的田地。」

Introduction: Don't throw things here.  The farmer wouldn't appreciate it.
序章: 別在這兒扔東西。農民會不高興的。

Introduction: It wouldn't be nice to set the Centaur's crops on fire.
序章: 放火燒掉半人馬的作物可不是個好主意。

Introduction: This is the Centaur's shovel.
序章: 這是半人馬的鏟子。

Introduction: The trees have tiny fruits forming.
序章: 樹上結著小小的果實。

Introduction: The tree hasn't been planted yet.
序章: 這棵樹還沒有被種下。

Introduction: The ornamental vases are empty.
序章: 裝飾花瓶是空的。

Introduction: You can see the roofs of the town over the fence.
序章: 你可以看到圍牆外城鎮的屋頂。

Introduction: The town gate is wide open during the day.  You would look silly climbing the wall.
序章: 城門在白天敞開著。爬牆會讓你看起來很傻。

Introduction: Climbing this wall is too difficult for your level of skill.  Keep practicing.
序章: 爬這面牆對你目前的技能等級來說太難了。繼續練習吧。

Introduction: Realizing you may hit somebody on the other side of the wall, you put the rock away.
序章: 意識到你可能會打到牆那一邊的人，你放下了石頭。

Introduction: The creature looks more like a small flying lizard than a bird.
序章: 這生物看起來更像一隻小飛蜥蜴，而不是鳥。

Introduction: You can't reach the birdlike creature from here.
序章: 你從這裡無法接觸到這隻類似鳥的生物。

Introduction: The road winds northward toward a walled castle.
序章: 道路向北蜿蜒至一座城牆環繞的城堡。
