Introduction: The door seems to be barred on the inside. Perhaps you should knock.
序章: 門似乎從裡面被閂上了。也許你該敲一下。

Introduction: There is no answer.
序章: 沒有人回應。

Introduction: You hear the voice of the healer saying:  "The last time you were here, I found I was missing some potions."
序章: 你聽到治療師的聲音說：「你上次來的時候，我發現我少了一些藥水。」

Introduction: I don't like thievery.  Go away!
序章: 「我不喜歡小偷。滾開！」

Introduction: The door appears to be barred on the inside.
序章: 門似乎從裡面被閂上了。

Introduction: Your skill in lock picking is insufficient to open a sardine can -- with a key!
序章: 你的開鎖技能還不夠強，甚至連有鑰匙的沙丁魚罐頭都打不開！

Introduction: Your lock-picking skill will do you no good here.  The door is barred from the inside.
序章: 你的開鎖技能在這裡幫不上忙。門是從裡面被閂上的。

Introduction: You politely knock on the door.
序章: 你禮貌地敲了一下門。

Introduction: After a moment, you hear the inside bolt slide open.
序章: 過了一會兒，你聽到裡面的門栓被拉開。

Introduction: A voice from inside says "Come on in."
序章: 裡面傳來一個聲音說：「進來吧。」

Introduction: The road leads north to a castle in the distance and south to the crossroads. To the west you can see a farmer's fields.
序章: 道路向北通往遠處的城堡，向南通往十字路口。往西，你可以看到農民的田地。

Introduction: The house looks cheery and well-kept.  It has the sign of the Healer above the entrance.
序章: 房子看起來明亮整潔。入口上方掛著治療師的招牌。

Introduction: In front of the hut is a small garden in which grow the kinds of plants and herbs that are used for making potions.
序章: 小屋前有個小花園，裡面種著用來製作藥水的各種植物和草藥。

Introduction: It is made of twigs, leaves, and herbs, probably stolen from the healer's garden.
序章: 它是由樹枝、葉子和草藥做成的，可能是從治療師的花園偷來的。

Introduction: There is a birdlike creature sitting in the nest.
序章: 一隻類似鳥的生物坐在巢裡。

Introduction: In the nest is an object which appears to be a ring, glinting in the sunlight.
序章: 巢裡有一個物體看起來像是一枚戒指，在陽光下閃閃發光。

Introduction: It is empty now.
序章: 它現在是空的。

Introduction: What's left of the nest is charred beyond recognition.
序章: 巢剩下的部分已經被燒得面目全非。

Introduction: The remnants of the nest are scattered on the ground.
序章: 巢的殘骸散落在地面上。

Introduction: The nest sits on the branch.
序章: 巢坐在樹枝上。

Introduction: You see nothing in the remains of the nest.
序章: 你在巢的殘骸裡看不到任何東西。

Introduction: There's not enough left to bother with.
序章: 沒有足夠的東西值得麻煩。

Introduction: It's a mess, and you don't need it, anyway.
序章: 這是一團亂，反正你也用不著。

Introduction: It would be too messy to stow in your pack, and it doesn't appear to be good for anything.
序章: 放進背包裡會太亂，而且看起來也沒什麼用處。

Introduction: Perhaps you can do that.
序章: 也許你可以試試。

Introduction: The nest is out of reach, even using your sword.
序章: 即使用劍，你也無法夠到巢。

Introduction: Your dagger misses the nest and falls to the ground; you'll have to keep practicing.
序章: 你的匕首沒有擊中巢，掉到了地上；你還得繼續練習。

Introduction: You don't need to do that now.
序章: 你現在不需要做那個。

Introduction: There is a plowed field to the west.
序章: 西邊有一片犁過的田地。

Introduction: There is a forest to the east.
序章: 東邊有一片森林。

Introduction: You pick up a shiny gold ring.
序章: 你撿起了一枚閃亮的金戒指。

Introduction: It takes a lot of skill and practice to climb this tree.
序章: 爬這棵樹需要很多技能和練習。

Introduction: You carefully bend down and reach into the nest.
序章: 你小心地彎下身子，伸手進入巢裡。

Introduction: In the nest, you find a gold ring.
序章: 在巢裡，你找到了一枚金戒指。

Introduction: You take the ring and move carefully back along the limb.
序章: 你拿起戒指，小心地沿著樹枝往回走。

Introduction: Made it!  Now back down the tree.
序章: 成功了！現在下樹吧。

Introduction: You place the nest on the ground. It is of no use to you.
序章: 你把巢放在地上。它對你沒有用處。

Introduction: You take a lovely gold ring from the nest.
序章: 你從巢裡取出一枚漂亮的金戒指。

Introduction: You place the ring into your pack and the nest on the ground.
序章: 你把戒指放進背包，把巢放在地上。

Introduction: The bright smell of fresh herbs mingles with the aroma of wood smoke as you come up to a hut by the side of the road.
序章: 當你走向路邊的小屋時，新鮮草藥的香味與木煙的氣息混合在一起。

Introduction: You see a glinting object in the remains of the nest.
序章: 你在巢的殘骸裡看到一個閃閃發光的物體。

Introduction: That's what happens sometimes when you go out on a limb.
序章: 有時候走上樹枝時就會發生這種事。

Introduction: You see nothing in the nest.
序章: 你在巢裡什麼也看不到。

Introduction: Try, try again, etc...
序章: 再試一次，以此類推…

Introduction: You can't do that while you're up in the tree!
序章: 你在樹上時不能這樣做！

Introduction: You pick up a few small rocks.
序章: 你撿起了幾塊小石頭。

Introduction: As you try to cast a spell from your high perch on the tree, you lose your concentration, and...
序章: 當你試著從樹上的高處施法時，你失去了專注力，然後…

Introduction: That spell is no longer useful here (but you did get some practice).
序章: 那個法術在這裡已經沒有用了（但你確實練習了一下）。

Introduction: The apothecary bottle sign indicates that the occupant of the house is skilled with potions.
序章: 藥瓶的招牌表明這棟房子的主人擅長製作藥水。

Introduction: You can see nothing in the dark.
序章: 你在黑暗中什麼也看不到。

Introduction: The curtains obscure your vision.  You can only make out a clutter of bottles, jars, and pots on the sill.
序章: 窗簾遮住了你的視線。你只能看到窗台上雜亂的瓶子、罐子和壺。

Introduction: The tree is a large oak.
序章: 這是一棵大橡樹。

Introduction: There is a nest on the limb that stretches toward the hut.
序章: 樹枝上有一個巢，樹枝伸向小屋。

Introduction: It's a barrel.
序章: 這是一個木桶。

Introduction: It's just a wooden barrel.
序章: 這只是一個木製的木桶。

Introduction: The healer's bed looks very comfortable.
序章: 治療師的床看起來很舒服。

Introduction: Since it has been awhile since you took a bath, you decide not to sleep in the healer's bed.
序章: 因為你有一陣子沒洗澡了，你決定不睡在治療師的床上。

Introduction: It looks like cheesecloth.
序章: 看起來像是起司布。

Introduction: The fire is very hot.
序章: 火很熱。

Introduction: Magic Acorn
序章: 魔力橡果

Introduction: Fairy Dust
序章: 妖精粉

Introduction: Green Fur
序章: 綠色毛皮

Introduction: Flowers from Erana's Peace
序章: 伊瑞娜的安寧之地的鮮花

Introduction: Let's see, to make the Dispel Potion I still need: %s %s %s %s %s
序章: 讓我想想，要做解除魔法藥水我還需要：%s %s %s %s %s

Introduction: Flying Water
序章: 飛水

Introduction: You see a middle-aged woman with rosy cheeks and smile lines around her eyes.
序章: 你看到一位中年婦女，紅潤的雙頰和眼角帶著笑紋。

Introduction: Magic Acorn
序章: 魔力橡果

Introduction: Troll Beard
序章: 巨魔鬍鬚

Introduction: Cheetaur Claws
序章: 獵豹人爪子

Introduction: Dispel Potion
序章: 解除魔法藥水

Introduction: Dryad
序章: 樹精

Introduction: Fairy Dust
序章: 妖精粉

Introduction: Farmer
序章: 農夫

Introduction: Flasks
序章: 藥瓶

Introduction: Flowers
序章: 鮮花

Introduction: Flying Water
序章: 飛水

Introduction: Fairy Ring
序章: 妖精環

Introduction: Green Fur
序章: 綠色毛皮

Introduction: Healing Potion
序章: 治療藥水

Introduction: Power Potion
序章: 力量藥劑

Introduction: Mandrake Root
序章: 曼德拉根

Introduction: Mushrooms
序章: 蘑菇

Introduction: Erana's Peace
序章: 伊瑞娜的安寧之地

Introduction: Pet
序章: 寵物

Introduction: Potions
序章: 藥水

Introduction: Pterosaur
序章: 翼龍

Introduction: Reward
序章: 獎勵

Introduction: Ring
序章: 戒指

Introduction: Son
序章: 兒子

Introduction: Spell Components
序章: 法術成分

Introduction: Floating Spirits
序章: 漂浮靈魂

Introduction: Stamina
序章: 耐力

Introduction: Undead Unguent
序章: 不死藥膏

Introduction: Vigor Potion
序章: 活力藥水

Introduction: Zombies
序章: 喪屍

Introduction: She is a rather motherly-looking woman with a look of vagueness that belies her shrewdness.  She wears an apron over her dress and a scarf wrapped around her head.
序章: 她是一位看起來很像母親的女性，眼神飄渺卻掩不住她的敏銳。她穿著圍裙，頭上綁著頭巾。

Introduction: My ring is shaped in gold like a braid of the herb Athelas with entwined leaves.  I will give six golds to the person who returns my ring.
序章: 「我的戒指是用黃金打造，造型像是艾瑟拉斯草編織的花辮，葉子交織在一起。我會給找到我戒指的人六枚金幣。」

Introduction: I have already given you your reward for the return of my ring.
序章: 「我已經給過你找到我戒指的獎勵了。」

Introduction: The only place you can get a magic acorn from is a Dryad's oak tree.
序章: 「魔力橡果只能從樹精的橡樹上取得。」

Introduction: Poor man, he hasn't been the same since his son and daughter were taken from him.
序章: 「可憐的人，自從他的兒子和女兒被帶走後，他就再也不像以前那樣了。」

Introduction: The Trolls around here are tough monsters that cannot stand the light of day and so are found only at night or in caves.
序章: 「這一帶的巨魔是很難纏的怪物，無法忍受日光，所以只在夜間或山洞裡出沒。」

Introduction: They are very difficult to kill, but I will pay two healing potions for the beard of a troll.
序章: 「它們很難殺死，但我願意為巨魔的鬍鬚付出兩瓶治療藥水。」

Introduction: Those brutes!  I'm always having to heal someone they've beaten up and robbed.  I hate thieves.
序章: 「那些野獸！我總是要治療被它們毆打和搶劫的人。我討厭小偷。」

Introduction: You can see Spielburg castle from my yard outside.  The Baron lives there with only his guards for company, so I hear.
序章: 「從我外面的院子裡能看到史畢柏格城堡。我聽說男爵住在那裡，只有衛兵陪伴。」

Introduction: The Cheetaur looks like a cross between a panther and a man.  It is vicious and tough.
序章: 「獵豹人看起來像是黑豹和人類的混合體。它很兇殘且強悍。」

Introduction: Unless you are a very tough fighter, you had best try to get away from it. If you do manage to kill it, then I will pay you five silvers for each claw.
序章: 「除非你是很強的戰士，否則最好還是逃跑。如果你真的殺死它，我會為每根爪子付你五枚銀幣。」

Introduction: The dear child, I can still picture her with her beautiful blond hair done up in braids.  She was so sweet.  It is hard to believe she is gone.
序章: 「親愛的孩子，我還能想像她那美麗的金色頭髮編成花辮。她那麼溫柔。很難相信她已經不在了。」

Introduction: If you know the ingredients and then bring them here, I can make you such a potion.
序章: 「如果你知道配方的材料並帶來，我可以為你製作這種藥水。」

Introduction: I had heard that there was a Dryad in the woods somewhere.  I've never met anyone who actually spoke with her before.  How very interesting.
序章: 「我聽說森林裡的某處有樹精。我從未遇過真正和她說過話的人。真有意思。」

Introduction: Fairy Dust obviously comes from fairies.  They dance around mushroom rings when it is night.  Fairies are magical beings, so be careful around them.
序章: 「妖精粉顯然來自妖精。當夜幕降臨時，她們在蘑菇圈周圍舞蹈。妖精是魔法生物，所以要小心她們。」

Introduction: Would you believe the brigands almost killed him a while back?  Fortunately their leader made them bring Heinrich here where I could heal him.
序章: 「你能相信山賊曾幾乎殺死他嗎？幸好他們的首領命令他們把海因里希帶到我這裡，我才能救他。」

Introduction: I will pay you one silver for each empty flask you bring me so I can use it for more potions.
序章: 「我會為你帶來的每個空藥瓶付一枚銀幣，這樣我就能用它製作更多藥水。」

Introduction: I use flowers from Erana's Peace to the north in nearly all my potions. They have some of the magic of the place even when they are dried.  I will pay you five silvers for a flask full of flowers.
序章: 「我在幾乎所有藥水中都使用北方伊瑞娜的安寧之地的花朵。即使乾燥後，它們仍保有那個地方的魔力。我會為一瓶滿滿的花朵付你五枚銀幣。」

Introduction: I'm not sure what kind of water that is.
序章: 「我不確定那是什麼水。」

Introduction: A Fairy Ring is a circle of mushrooms.  Fairies dance in them at night, which makes the mushrooms very magical.  Those fairies are cute, but they play rough!
序章: 「妖精環是由蘑菇圍成的圓圈。妖精在夜間在其中舞蹈，使蘑菇充滿魔力。那些妖精很可愛，但她們玩得很粗野！」

Introduction: I've never heard of a monster around here with green fur.  Rare things are sometimes by their nature magical.
序章: 「我從未聽說過這裡有綠色毛皮的怪物。稀有物品有時生來就具有魔法性質。」

Introduction: Well, my name is Amelia Appleberry, but mostly I am just known as the Healer.  I will be happy to sell you some potions.  I live here with my pet, Pterry.
序章: 「嗯，我叫阿蜜莉亞·蘋果莓，但大多數人都叫我治療師。我很樂意賣你一些藥水。我和我的寵物派瑞住在這裡。」

Introduction: This potion is used to heal damage and keep wounds from getting worse.  It has gotten pretty dangerous around here, and a Healing Potion can be quite useful in a serious situation.
序章: 「這種藥水用來治療傷害並防止傷口惡化。這裡變得很危險，治療藥水在危急情況下能派上大用場。」

Introduction: I heard the brigand Leader ordering those nasty men to bring poor Heinrich here after they hurt him so.  I gave them all a good scolding, you can imagine.
序章: 「我聽到山賊首領下令那些壞傢伙把可憐的海因里希帶到我這裡，在傷害他之後。你可以想像我狠狠地罵了他們一頓。」

Introduction: Power Potions restore the energy needed to cast spells.
序章: 「法力藥水恢復施放法術所需的能量。」

Introduction: I have the skill to use my magic to create potions.  I am a bit proud about it; not everyone can do that, you know.
序章: 「我有用魔法創造藥水的技能。我對此有點自豪；不是每個人都能做到的。」

Introduction: Mandrake root is used in a variety of spells, mostly for evil purposes. Mandrake must be pulled from a dead man's grave at midnight.  The root is particularly powerful.
序章: 「曼德拉根用於各種法術，大多是邪惡目的。曼德拉根必須在午夜從死人的墳墓拔起。這種根特別強大。」

Introduction: I need the mushrooms from a fairy ring to make Power Potions.  I will pay you one gold for some.
序章: 「我需要妖精環的蘑菇來製作法力藥水。我會為你付一枚金幣。」

Introduction: Almost due north of here is the meadow called 'Erana's Peace'.
序章: 「幾乎正北方是叫做『伊瑞娜的安寧之地』的草地。」

Introduction: It is a very magical area and I understand that it is a place of safety.  It is beautiful all year, for the flowers are always in bloom.
序章: 「這是個非常具有魔法的地方，我聽說那是個安全的地方。它全年都很美麗，因為花朵總是在盛開。」

Introduction: Oh, that's my pet, Pterry, the pterosaur.  He has a girl friend, Pteresa, with a nest in the oak outside my door.  Pterry keeps me company and listens while I chatter as I work.
序章: 「哦，那是我的寵物派瑞，一隻翼龍。牠有個女朋友叫派特雷莎，在我門外的橡樹上築巢。派瑞陪伴我，在我工作時聽我嘮叨。」

Introduction: I make and sell Healing Potion, Power Potion, Vigor Potion, and Undead Unguent.
序章: 「我製作和販賣治療藥水、法力藥水、活力藥水和不死藥膏。」

Introduction: They are a species of flying lizard.  I understand they can grow quite large in the south.
序章: 「它們是飛行蜥蜴的一種。我聽說它們在南方能長得相當大。」

Introduction: I will give six golds to the person who returns my ring.  It was a gift when I graduated from the College of Healing.
序章: 「我會給找到我戒指的人六枚金幣。那是我從治療學院畢業時的禮物。」

Introduction: My ring is shaped in gold like a braid of the herb Athelas with entwined leaves.  I don't know how I lost it, I hardly ever take it off.
序章: 「我的戒指是用黃金打造，造型像是艾瑟拉斯草編織的花辮，葉子交織在一起。我不知道怎麼掉的，我幾乎不曾脫下它。」

Introduction: He was just a young dashing man when he last rode off; he never returned.
序章: 「他最後一次騎馬離開時還是個年輕英俊的男人；他再也沒有回來。」

Introduction: I always need ingredients for making potions.  Right now, I need some of Erana's Flowers, Magic Mushrooms, Troll Beard, and, let me think ... Oh, yes, Cheetaur Claws.
序章: 「我總是需要製作藥水的材料。現在我需要一些伊瑞娜的鮮花、魔法蘑菇、巨魔鬍鬚，讓我想想……哦，對，獵豹人爪子。」

Introduction: I will be happy to pay you if you bring me some.  I'll also buy back any empty flasks you have so that I can recycle them.  Waste not, want not, after all.
序章: 「如果你帶一些來，我很樂意付你錢。我也會回購你的空藥瓶，這樣我就能回收利用。畢竟，浪費不得啊。」

Introduction: Floating Spirits are the ghosts which usually hang around graveyards at night.  They are dangerous, so don't go near them unless you use Undead Unguent first.
序章: 「漂浮靈魂是通常在夜間在墳墓周圍徘徊的幽靈。它們很危險，所以除非你先使用不死藥膏，否則不要靠近它們。」

Introduction: Stamina is a measure of the energy the body uses as you work or play.
序章: 「耐力是衡量你工作或玩樂時身體使用的能量。」

Introduction: Undead Unguent is used to drive off the minor undead such as zombies or floating spirits.  It doesn't last long, so you should use it near where you are going to encounter such things.
序章: 「不死藥膏用來驅趕小型不死生物，如喪屍或漂浮靈魂。效果不持久，所以你應該在即將遭遇這些東西的地方使用它。」

Introduction: Vigor Potion is used to revitalize yourself after vigorous exercise.  It helps to restore stamina.
序章: 「活力藥水用來在劇烈運動後恢復活力。它有助於恢復耐力。」

Introduction: There are not too many zombies in this land, too damp, I suppose.  There are some floating spirits, I have heard.
序章: 「這片土地上沒有太多喪屍，太潮濕了，我想。我聽說有一些漂浮靈魂。」

Introduction: You find that you have less money than you thought, and can't afford to buy a potion.
序章: 你發現你的錢比你想的少，買不起藥水。

Introduction: You'll have to be more specific.
序章: 你得說得更清楚一點。

Introduction: I need all my flasks for potions.  You'll have to get one somewhere else.
序章: 「我所有的藥瓶都用來做藥水。你得去別的地方拿。」

Introduction: I don't have any.
序章: 「我沒有。」

Introduction: I hope this will help you.
序章: 「我希望這對你有幫助。」

Introduction: Please, show some respect.
序章: 「拜託，請尊重一下。」

Introduction: Thank you, I always need flasks.
序章: 「謝謝，我總是需要藥瓶。」

Introduction: Oh, thank you for finding my ring.  How I've missed this.  Here are six golds and two Healing Potions for your reward!
序章: 「哦，謝謝你找到我的戒指。我多麼想念它。這是六枚金幣和兩瓶治療藥水作為你的獎勵！」

Introduction: So you helped the Dryad; that's nice.  She does keep the forest around here healthy.  So that's how to make a Dispel Potion, is it?  Thanks for letting me know.
序章: 「你幫助了樹精；真好。她確實保持著這一帶的森林健康。所以這就是製作解除魔法藥水的方法嗎？謝謝你讓我知道。」

Introduction: I wouldn't know what to do with that.
序章: 「我不知道拿這個怎麼辦。」

Introduction: Thank you, but I have all the flowers I need.
序章: 「謝謝，但我已經有我需要的所有鮮花。」

Introduction: Thank you.  I can always use flowers from Erana's Peace in potion making. Here are your silvers.
序章: 「謝謝。我總是能在製作藥水時用上伊瑞娜的安寧之地的花朵。這是你的銀幣。」

Introduction: You put them away.
序章: 你把它們收起來。

Introduction: Thank you.  I'll save this for a later use.
序章: 「謝謝。我會存起來以後用。」

Introduction: Those Meeps sound so interesting.  I'd like to meet them sometime.  I'll get to work on that potion of yours.
序章: 「那些咪普聽起來很有趣。我想有機會見見它們。我來開始製作你的藥水吧。」

Introduction: So, you say this is one of the ingredients for the Dispel Potion.  Well, I had better get started making it.
序章: 「所以，你說這是解除魔法藥水的一種材料。好吧，我最好開始製作它。」

Introduction: Thank you.  I'm sure that I'll find a good use for Fairy Dust.
序章: 「謝謝。我相信我會找到妖精粉的好用途。」

Introduction: I don't need any more Flying Water.  Thank you anyway.
序章: 「我不需要更多飛水了。不過還是謝謝。」

Introduction: Thank you for the offer, but I really don't need any water.
序章: 「謝謝你的提議，但我真的不需要任何水。」

Introduction: The Healer makes some sort of arcane gesture over the flask of water, then looks surprised.
序章: 治療師對著水瓶做出某種奧祕的手勢，然後看起來很驚訝。

Introduction: I'm sorry, but this water does not seem to have any magical potential at all.  This can't be the `Flying Water' you described.
序章: 「抱歉，但這水似乎完全沒有任何魔法潛力。這不可能是你描述的『飛水』。」

Introduction: The Healer makes some sort of arcane gesture over the flask of water, then smiles.
序章: 治療師對著水瓶做出某種奧祕的手勢，然後微笑。

Introduction: Flying Water.  How clever.
序章: 「飛水。真聰明。」

Introduction: Thank you, but I have enough mushrooms.
序章: 「謝謝，但我已經有足夠的蘑菇。」

Introduction: These are very nice.  I'll dry them and grind them into a powder.
序章: 「這些很不錯。我會把它們曬乾然後研成粉末。」

Introduction: Let me think, oh yes, I said these are worth a gold.  Here you are.
序章: 「讓我想想，哦是的，我說過這些值一枚金幣。給你。」

Introduction: You put it away.
序章: 你把它收起來。

Introduction: You have ruined the mushrooms with toadstools.  I can not buy your mushrooms.
序章: 「你用毒菌毀了蘑菇。我不能買你的蘑菇。」

Introduction: Do you mean to tell me that you actually managed to kill a Cheetaur?  You're quite a hero, aren't you?
序章: 「你是在告訴我你真的殺死了獵豹人？你真是個英雄啊？」

Introduction: Here is your money. You might think about purchasing a Healing Potion in case you have to fight such monsters again.
序章: 「這是你的錢。你可能要考慮買一瓶治療藥水，以防你得再次對付這樣的怪物。」

Introduction: Don't tell me you actually killed a Troll?  Why, you are really amazing. I haven't had any Troll's Beard for a long time.  There are two healing potions for the beard.
序章: 「別告訴我你真的殺死了巨魔？天哪，你真是太了不起了。我很久沒有得到巨魔的鬍鬚了。鬍鬚有兩瓶治療藥水。」

Introduction: They are ingredients the healer uses to make her potions.
序章: 它們是治療師用來製作藥水的材料。

Introduction: Soup is good for you.  Mmmm Mmmm good.
序章: 湯對你很好。嗯嗯嗯真好。

Introduction: It's not your soup.
序章: 這不是你的湯。

Introduction: It's a ladder up to the loft where the Healer has her personal quarters.  No, you can't go up the ladder.
序章: 這是通向治療師個人房間的閣樓樓梯。不，你不能上樓梯。

Introduction: Don't be nosy.
序章: 別多管閒事。

Introduction: The pterosaur's litter box.
序章: 翼龍的貓砂盆。

Introduction: Amelia uses a wide variety of pots to make her magical potions.
序章: 阿蜜莉亞用各種各樣的鍋來製作她的魔法藥水。

Introduction: Do you want to burn yourself?  Those pots are hot!
序章: 你想燙到自己嗎？那些鍋很燙！

Introduction: You see a winged lizard much like the one you saw outside.
序章: 你看到一隻翼龍，很像你在外面看到的那隻。

Introduction: Small on a prehistoric scale, but still a pterosaur.
序章: 按史前標準來說很小，但仍然是翼龍。

Introduction: I cannot make you another Dispel Potion unless you can convince the Dryad to give you another magic acorn.  I do not think that is likely.
序章: 「除非你能說服樹精給你另一個魔力橡果，否則我無法為你製作另一瓶解除魔法藥水。我不認為那可能。」

Introduction: You tell the healer that you have been to visit the Dryad of the Woods, and that she gave you a Magic Acorn and told you the formula for a potion to dispel enchantments.
序章: 你告訴治療師你去拜訪了森林樹精，她給了你一個魔力橡果並告訴你製作解除魔法藥水的配方。

Introduction: Oh, by the way, if you happen to find a ring on your adventures, I lost my favorite gold ring.  I will give a reward to the one who returns it.
序章: 「哦，順便說一下，如果你在冒險中碰巧找到戒指，我弄丟了我最喜歡的金戒指。我會給找到它的人獎勵。」

Introduction: Here is the potion of Dispel!
序章: 「這是解除魔法藥水！」

Introduction: Use it by splashing it on the victim of a magic spell.  Mind you, this won't work if the spell is caused by a magic item; that type of magic can only be removed by taking away the item.
序章: 「用它潑在魔法咒語的受害者身上。注意，如果咒語是由魔法物品引起的，這不會起作用；那種魔法只能透過拿走物品來消除。」

Introduction: Please leave me to make the potion, and when you return I shall have it for you.
序章: 「請讓我製作藥水，當你回來時我就會為你準備好。」

Introduction: If you know the ingredients for a Dispel Potion and bring them here, I can make you such a potion.
序章: 「如果你知道解除魔法藥水的材料並帶來，我可以為你製作這種藥水。」

Introduction: The Dispel Potion will take a while to make.  Why don't you come back later?
序章: 「解除魔法藥水需要一些時間製作。你何不稍後回來？」

Introduction: Well, what can I do for you this time?
序章: 「嗯，這次我能為你做什麼？」

Introduction: The fragrances of herbs mingle with some rather unpleasant odors as you step into the healer's house.
序章: 當你踏入治療師的房子時，草藥的香氣與一些相當難聞的氣味混合在一起。

Introduction: You put the reward away, and tell her she is welcome, as you leave to avoid being kissed again.
序章: 你把獎勵收起來，告訴她不客氣，然後離開以避免再被親吻。

Introduction: Lovely day, isn't it?  My, you look very healthy for an adventurer.  You must be new.
序章: 「美好的一天，不是嗎？哇，你作為一個冒險家看起來很健康。你一定是新手。」

Introduction: What can I do for you?  I can sell you healing potions if you like.
序章: 「我能為你做什麼？如果你願意，我可以賣你治療藥水。」

Introduction: I also buy spell components if you are interested in gathering some for me. Don't mind me, I always have so much to do around here.
序章: 「我也收購法術材料，有興趣幫我採集的話儘管說。別理我，我這兒事情多得很。」

Introduction: You see a clean but very cluttered little hut filled with bottles and potions.  The Healer is busy puttering around with her potions.
序章: 你看見一間乾淨但凌亂不堪的小屋，擺滿了瓶瓶罐罐和各式藥水。治療師正忙著在她的藥水堆裡翻騰。

Introduction: There is a small flying creature preening itself on the rafter.  A ladder leads to the loft above.  A pot over the fire simmers with herbs.  It is pleasantly warm in here.
序章: 一隻小飛行生物在梁上理羽毛。一把梯子通向上面的閣樓。火上的鍋子裡飄著草藥香。這兒溫暖舒服極了。

Introduction: You can buy potions, get information, or sell ingredients here.
序章: 你可以在這裡買藥水、打聽消息，或賣材料。

Introduction: There is an aura of magic throughout this small cabin.
序章: 整間小屋籠罩著魔法氣息。

Introduction: Come back in the morning, please.
序章: 「請早上再來。」

Introduction: You don't really think that is the correct way to win this game, do you?
序章: 你不會真的認為那是贏遊戲的正確方法吧？

Introduction: You see bottles and flasks of a wide variety of different potions.
序章: 你看見各式各樣的瓶子和燒瓶裡裝著五花八門的藥水。

Introduction: You carefully grab a couple of Healing Potions and conceal them under your cape.
序章: 你小心翼翼地抓起幾瓶治療藥水，藏在你的披風下。

Introduction: If you're going to steal, at least wait until the Healer is not looking.
序章: 你要偷的話，至少等治療師不看的時候。

Introduction: You're getting greedy.  She'll notice if you take any more.
序章: 你貪心了。你再拿她就會發現的。

Introduction: The healer uses this table as a workbench.
序章: 治療師把這張桌子當成工作台。

Introduction: You see a few odds and ends (not to mention This and That).
序章: 你看見各式各樣的東西（更別提那些雜七雜八的）。

Introduction: The view from the window has been obscured by soot from the fire.
序章: 窗戶外的景色被爐火的煙灰遮住了。

Introduction: It's snow.
序章: 那是雪。

Introduction: The cave looks very eerie and ominous.
序章: 洞穴看起來陰森詭異。

Introduction: Better watch out -- those icicles look sharp!
序章: 小心點──那些冰錐看起來很尖銳！

Introduction: Although the cave mouth looks like a mouth, and opens and shuts like a mouth, I'm afraid it doesn't know how to talk like a mouth.
序章: 雖然洞口看起來像嘴巴，開閉起來也像嘴巴，但我怕它不知道怎麼像嘴巴那樣說話。

Introduction: From the blue color of his skin and his huge height, you can tell this is a Frost Giant.
序章: 從他皮膚的藍色和巨大的身軀，你能看出這是個霜巨人。

Introduction: The gem glows like an aurora in the night sky.
序章: 那顆寶石像夜空的極光一樣閃耀。

Introduction: The Giant looks at you in disgust.  Apparently, you did not offer what he desires.
序章: 巨人厭惡地看著你。看來你沒給他想要的東西。

Introduction: Bargain
序章: 討價還價

Introduction: Brauggi
序章: 布勞基

Introduction: Hunger
序章: 飢餓

Introduction: Mead
序章: 蜂蜜酒

Introduction: Northlands
序章: 北國

Introduction: Find me some fruit for to mellow my mead horn, Gift I will give of a gem that now glows --
序章: 「給我找點水果來緩和我的蜂蜜酒啊，我會賞你這顆會發光的寶石──」

Introduction: Jewel from Jotunheim, flare of the frost flame, Fetch to me fruit that will fill up my fists!
序章: 「約頓海姆的寶石啊，冰焰的光芒，給我拿來滿滿一拳頭的水果！」

Introduction: (It looks like it will take a lot of fruit to fill up THOSE fists!)
序章: （要填滿那樣的拳頭看來得花不少水果啊！）

Introduction: Brauggi I be, to boast of my boldness, Strong as the storm that sends forth the snow.
序章: 「我就是布勞基，以膽識自豪，強似送來白雪的風暴。」

Introduction: Fiercer in fighting than foes in their fury, Fear now this Frost Giant fighter and flee!
序章: 「戰鬥中比敵人猛，所向披靡，怕我這霜巨人戰士吧，逃吧！」

Introduction: Fiercer than frost's bite, my fury is fearless, My axe it is edged as is the ice.
序章: 「比寒冰的咬傷更兇悍，我的怒火無畏，我的斧刃如同冰鎚。」

Introduction: This warrior is willing to wield now the war drum, The battleaxe blazes and bellows for blood.
序章: 「這戰士如今願意揮舞戰鼓，戰斧熊熊燃燒，呼號著要血！」

Introduction: Highpoint of harvest, the finest of flavor, Apples or oranges, peaches or pears.
序章: 「豐收的高峰，最上乘的滋味，蘋果或柳橙，桃子或梨子。」

Introduction: Glow of the frost flame that fills up the night field, A jewel that is flawless, the finest of gems.
序章: 「充滿夜空的冰焰光芒，無瑕的寶石，最精美的珍珠。」

Introduction: Hollow my food house, for my hunt has been hindered, By having to hurry here from my home.
序章: 「我的食物倉空了啊，因為狩獵受阻，得匆匆從家趕來這裡。」

Introduction: Mead is the mother's milk, mighty yet mellow That brings joy to Giants and madness to men.
序章: 「蜂蜜酒是母親的奶啊，強大卻溫和，給巨人帶來歡樂，給人類帶來瘋狂。」

Introduction: Far from the frost field, fares forth this fighter, Hunger has hurled me hither from home.
序章: 「遠離冰雪地啊，這戰士踏上征途，飢荒把我從家鄉硬生生扔到這裡。」

Introduction: My name it is known in the Northlands as Brauggi, Barter with blade's clash, or bargain with me.
序章: 「北國都知道我叫布勞基，用刀劍碰撞來交易，或者跟我討價還價。」

Introduction: The log is covered with a layer of late winter snow.
序章: 原木被厚厚的晚冬積雪覆蓋著。

Introduction: Leave it alone; you don't know where that log's been.
序章: 別碰它；你不知道那根木頭哪來的。

Introduction: I'm sure that was fun; why don't you talk to a twig next?
序章: 我敢說那很有趣；下一個何不去跟樹枝說話？

Introduction: You can see a small patch of sky through the trees.
序章: 你能透過樹木看到一小片天空。

Introduction: That's it; reach for the sky!
序章: 就是那樣；夠著天空吧！

Introduction: It's a bit late in the season for skiing.
序章: 這季節滑雪有點晚了。

Introduction: Maybe if you reach those mountains, you'll become a true hero.
序章: 或許如果你登上那些山峰，你就會成為真正的英雄。

Introduction: I don't think talking to a mountain is healthy.
序章: 我不覺得跟山說話是健康的舉動。

Introduction: Fruit you have found, to fill all my food stores, Thus filled the bargain, my gem you have bought.
序章: 「果實你找到了，填飽了我的食物倉，交易這樣成了，寶石就是你的。」

Introduction: Brauggi has bartered, and all has been answered; The mead it may mellow, and now I head home.
序章: 「布勞基完成交易了，一切都成了；蜂蜜酒能讓我舒坦，如今我要回家了。」

Introduction: Courage you carry,  but skill it is scanty; Face not this foe  before fiercer you fight.
序章: 「你有膽量啊，但技巧還差得遠；別跟我這敵手交手，除非你變得更兇悍。」

Introduction: Brauggi strides off through the cave on his way back to his Northern home.
序章: 布勞基大步踏過洞穴，踏上回北國家園的征途。

Introduction: Your fruit definitely makes a generous pile in the Giant's cupped hands, but they're not full yet.
序章: 你的水果在巨人的雙手窩裡堆成不錯的一堆，但還沒滿。

Introduction: Running on the ice is apparently not a very good idea.
序章: 在冰上跑看起來不是什麼好主意。

Introduction: As you begin to cast your spell...
序章: 當你開始施法時…

Introduction: Oops.  You're not carrying any fruit for the Frost Giant.
序章: 哎呀。你身上沒有霜巨人要的水果。

Introduction: Your meagre selection of fruit makes a paltry pile at the bottom of the Giant's cupped hands.  You'll need to get quite a few more apples to fill THOSE hands!
序章: 你那可憐兮兮的水果在巨人雙手窩底堆成一小堆。你得多拿好幾個蘋果才能填滿那樣的手！

Introduction: As you start to throw...
序章: 當你開始投擲時…

Introduction: The mountains rise quickly from this point and are covered with snow.  To the west, you can see that the snow has melted in most of the forest, but here it is still heavy.
序章: 山峰從此處陡峭上升並覆滿白雪。往西看，你能看到森林大部分的雪已融化，但這邊的雪還厚著呢。

Introduction: Brauggi has departed on the long journey to his homeland.
序章: 布勞基已踏上返回家鄉的漫長征途。

Introduction: Brauggi has become impatient with your questions and gone elsewhere.  You will have to return another time.
序章: 布勞基對你的問題不耐煩了，去別的地方了。你得改天再來。

Introduction: The Giant (who does not seem pleased at your casting a spell in his presence) radiates a magical aura.  The strongest magic centers on the glowing gem at his belt.
序章: 巨人（看起來對你在他面前施法不太高興）散發著魔法氣息。最強的魔法聚集在他腰上那顆會發光的寶石上。

Introduction: The blue in the sky is just like the blue sky of your home.  You feel a sudden pang of homesickness, and begin to understand Brauggi's loneliness.
序章: 天空的藍色就像你家鄉的藍天。你突然湧起鄉愁，開始理解布勞基的孤寂。

Introduction: To touch the sky, what dreams you have!
序章: 想觸摸天空，你的夢想好遠啊！

Introduction: Snow covers rock, scissors cut snow, and rock breaks scissors.  It's very Zen.
序章: 雪蓋住石頭，剪刀剪碎雪，石頭砸爛剪刀。很禪嘛。

Introduction: Before you take that snow and put it in your mouth, ask yourself a question: "Do I feel lucky?"
序章: 在你把那雪拿進嘴裡之前，問問自己一個問題：「我感覺幸運嗎？」

Introduction: The rock seems to listen patiently, but doesn't respond.
序章: 石頭好像在耐心傾聽，但沒有回應。

Introduction: Snow covers these rocks like a blanket.
序章: 雪像毯子一樣覆蓋著這些石頭。

Introduction: It would be fun to make a snowball, but you really haven't time.
序章: 堆雪球是很有趣，但你真的沒時間。

Introduction: There's snow all around this area.  In fact, it seems rather colder here than in the rest of the valley.
序章: 這地方四周都是雪。事實上，這兒看起來比山谷其他地方冷得多。

Introduction: It would be fun to make a snowball, but you really haven't time.
序章: 堆雪球是很有趣，但你真的沒時間。

Introduction: It's a small patch of yellow snow.
序章: 那是一小片黃雪。

Introduction: The last thing you need is a yellow snowball.
序章: 你最不需要的就是黃雪球。

Introduction: You look at the apple cores, and quickly decide they are of no use to you. Besides, they look disgusting.
序章: 你看著蘋果核，迅速決定它們對你沒用。而且它們看起來噁心得要死。

Introduction: The baby Meep is just too quick for you.  Besides, they eat too much.
序章: 小咪普快得你根本追不上。而且它們食量太大了。

Introduction: The baby Meep sure is cute.
序章: 小咪普真的很可愛。

Introduction: You keep seeing differently-colored Meeps come out of each hole.  They either lead to large underground caverns, or they are all connected under the earth.
序章: 你不斷看見不同顏色的咪普從每個洞冒出來。它們要嘛通向大型地下洞穴，要嘛全部在地底連通。

Introduction: There is a wad of bright green fur on the ground.
序章: 地上有一團亮綠色的毛皮。

Introduction: The meadow is covered with a light carpet of grass, broken up by the Meep holes.
序章: 草地被薄薄的草皮覆蓋著，中間散佈著咪普的洞。

Introduction: Hey, like, we eat them, you know.
序章: 「嘿，咦，我們吃它們啊，你知道吧。」

Introduction: Cute, huh?  The tunnels down here just crawl with those things.  Get in the way all the time, you know?
序章: 「可愛吧？底下的隧道裡到處都是那些傢伙。老是擋路，你知道吧？」

Introduction: Gee boss, I really don't know much about that at all.  Hey, sorry.
序章: 「天啊老大，我對那個真的不太了解。嘿，抱歉。」

Introduction: Gee boss, I really don't know much about that at all.  Hey, sorry.
序章: 「天啊老大，我對那個真的不太了解。嘿，抱歉。」

Introduction: Hey, like fur is good stuff, keeps us warm.  Mine's the best -- it's like green, you know?
序章: 「嘿，咦，毛皮是好東西啊，能讓我們暖和。我的最讚──是綠色的，你知道吧？」

Introduction: Oh, you want some green fur?  I think I have some somewhere.
序章: 「哦，你想要綠毛皮？我想我某個地方放著呢。」

Introduction: We, like, live in tunnels under the ground, you know.
序章: 「咦，我們住在地底的隧道裡，你知道吧。」

Introduction: I found a scroll lying around in the woods one day.  You want it?  It's yours.
序章: 「我有天在樹林裡找到一卷捲軸。你想要？它就是你的。」

Introduction: We are happy Meeps, living in our happy holes.  Don't worry.  Be happy!
序章: 「我們是快樂的咪普，住在我們快樂的洞裡。別擔心。要開心！」

Introduction: We use rocks for doors.  They keep us dry and warm in our holes.
序章: 「我們用石頭當門。它們讓我們的洞裡乾爽又暖和。」

Introduction: That thing?  I found it lying around in the woods one day.  You want it? It's yours.
序章: 「那東西？我有天在樹林裡找到的。你想要嗎？它就是你的。」

Introduction: The Meep scrambles back into its hole as you try to touch it.
序章: 當你試著碰咪普時，它匆匆鑽回洞裡。

Introduction: You realize that you have never seen more than one green Meep at a time. Perhaps there is only one.
序章: 你意識到你從沒同時見過一隻以上的綠咪普。或許只有一隻。

Introduction: There seems to be an abundant supply of blue and purple Meeps.  If only they'd stay still for a moment, you'd be able to count them.
序章: 看起來有大量的藍咪普和紫咪普。要是它們肯靜一下，你就能數清楚了。

Introduction: The Meeps have brightly-colored, soft-looking fur.
序章: 咪普長著色彩繽紛、軟呼呼的毛皮。

Introduction: Apples
序章: 蘋果

Introduction: Baby
序章: 寶寶

Introduction: Hey, just ask me about anything you want to know.
序章: 「嘿，有什麼你想知道的儘管問我。」

Introduction: Fur
序章: 毛皮

Introduction: Holes
序章: 洞穴

Introduction: Meeps
序章: 咪普

Introduction: Rocks
序章: 石頭

Introduction: Scroll
序章: 捲軸

Introduction: You hear squeaky muttering from beneath the ground.  It seems the Meeps are having quite a discussion about you.
序章: 你聽見地底傳來吱吱嘎嘎的碎碎念。看來咪普們正在熱烈討論你呢。

Introduction: You don't need to do that.
序章: 你不需要那樣做。

Introduction: You walk away from the hole to get out of the rock's way.
序章: 你走開了，讓開那塊石頭。

Introduction: You pick up the green fur.
序章: 你撿起綠毛皮。

Introduction: The magical runes fade from the paper before your eyes can focus on them.
序章: 魔法符文在你的眼睛聚焦之前就從紙上褪去了。

Introduction: You pick up the Spell Scroll.  As the magical runes fade, you find you now know how to cast the "Detect Magic" spell.
序章: 你撿起魔法捲軸。當符文褪去時，你發現自己現在知道怎麼施展「偵測魔法」法術了。

Introduction: Hiya, hiya. Nice to seeya 'gain.
序章: 「嘿啊，嘿啊。很高興又見到你。」

Introduction: Hiya, hiya.  Pleased to meetcha.
序章: 「嘿啊，嘿啊。很高興認識你。」

Introduction: Hearing a rumble from the Meep hole, you quickly slip out of the way.
序章: 聽見咪普洞裡傳來隆隆聲，你迅速閃到一邊。

Introduction: You detect no other magic here.
序章: 你在這裡沒有偵測到其他魔法。

Introduction: All of the Meeps are hiding deep within their holes.  Your vicious attack has them terrified.
序章: 所有咪普都躲在洞裡深處。你的凶悍攻擊嚇得它們要死。

Introduction: You see blue Meeps, purple Meeps, and occasionally a green Meep.  They seem to be very shy; whenever you approach one, it hides under its rock.
序章: 你看見藍咪普、紫咪普，偶爾還有綠咪普。它們看起來超害羞；你一靠近，它就躲到石頭底下。

Introduction: You are in the Meeps' Peep.  The colorful, furry Meeps timidly pop out of their holes from time to time.
序章: 你在咪普的窩裡。色彩繽紛、毛茸茸的咪普們不時膽小地冒出洞口。

Introduction: The Open spell only works on locks and small closed things.
序章: 「開啟術」只對鎖和小型密閉物品有效。

Introduction: You can't talk to any of the Meeps. They are all hiding deep within their holes, terrified after your vicious attack.
序章: 你沒辦法跟任何咪普說話。它們都躲在洞裡深處，被你的凶悍攻擊嚇得要死。

Introduction: The mountains are very steep here.
序章: 這邊的山峰陡得要命。

Introduction: Large, heavy-looking rocks cover the many holes.  The Meeps must be stronger than they look to lift these rocks so easily.
序章: 大塊沉重的石頭蓋著許多洞。咪普們要夠強悍才能這麼輕鬆搬起這些石頭。

Introduction: No way!  Those Meeps are too fast and the rocks too hard for you to want to try that again!
序章: 沒門！那些咪普太快、石頭太硬了，你不會想再試一次！

Introduction: It looks like a Spell Scroll.
序章: 看起來像一卷魔法捲軸。

Introduction: The trees and bushes look much like those you've seen elsewhere in the valley.
序章: 樹木和灌木看起來跟你在山谷其他地方見過的很像。

Introduction: I just flew in from the coast and BOY! are my arms tired.
序章: 「我剛從海邊飛過來，哇，我的手臂累死了。」

Introduction: Let us spin, then, you and I, when the evening is buried beneath the sky. It must be Tease Eliot, the ghost poet!
序章: 「那麼讓我們旋轉吧，你和我，當傍晚埋沒在天空底下時。」准是逗趣·埃利奧特，那個幽靈詩人！

Introduction: The lady is lovely as she lofts along, but beware her fatal touch!
序章: 那位女士飄然而過時美麗動人，但當心她致命的觸碰！

Introduction: The large stone you are facing has been engraved with this message:
序章: 你面前的這塊大石頭上刻著這樣的訊息：

Introduction: Here lies Lester Moore Gored six times by a wild boar.
序章: 「此墓：萊斯特·摩爾，被野豬戳傷六次。」

Introduction: No Les, no Moore.
序章: 「沒有萊斯，沒有摩爾。」

Introduction: The grate is clearly rusted shut.  You won't be able to open it.
序章: 那鐵柵欄明顯生鏽卡死了。你沒辦法打開。

Introduction: A massive grate covers a hole, keeping you from seeing the bottom.
序章: 一扇巨大的鐵柵欄蓋著一個洞，擋住你的視線不讓你看底下。

Introduction: The large tombstone seems to have a barely legible epitaph:
序章: 那塊大墓碑上的墓誌銘看起來幾乎無法辨認：

Introduction: Here lies an atheist...
序章: 「這裡躺著一個無神論者…」

Introduction: All dressed up and nowhere to go.
序章: 「衣著華麗卻無處可去。」

Introduction: Growing up out of the grave is an evil-looking plant with a slimy, poisonous sheen.
序章: 從墳墓中長出一株邪惡的植物，散發著黏稠、有毒的光澤。

Introduction: The carving on most of the gravestones seems to have been either intentionally defaced or worn with time.
序章: 大多數墓碑上的雕刻似乎要麼被故意毀損，要麼被時間磨蝕。

Introduction: The red root growing out of one of the graves has a strange and evil appearance.
序章: 從一個墳墓中長出的紅色根莖有著奇異而邪惡的外觀。

Introduction: Everything here is already dead.
序章: 這裡的一切都已死亡。

Introduction: You don't get ghosts.  Ghosts get YOU!
序章: 你不是在遇見鬼怪，鬼怪在遇見你！

Introduction: You recall from somewhere that the most likely time to find ghosts is at night.
序章: 你從某處想起，最有可能找到鬼怪的時間是在夜晚。

Introduction: You see transparent, decaying, writhing, slimy, undead fragments of undulating ectoplasm!
序章: 你看到透明、腐爛、扭動、黏稠的不死幽靈在波動著！

Introduction: I DO believe in spooks!  I DO believe in spooks!  I DO, I DO, I DO believe in spooks!!
序章: 我相信幽靈！我相信幽靈！我真的相信幽靈！！

Introduction: Don't annoy the rock 'n roll ghosts, unless you want to twist and shout with them!
序章: 不要激怒搖滾幽靈，除非你想和牠們扭動尖叫！

Introduction: The ghost won't say boo to you.
序章: 這幽靈不會對你吱聲。

Introduction: You can't seem to have a meaningful conversation with the ghosts.  Their thoughts are on another plane altogether.
序章: 你似乎無法和這些幽靈進行有意義的對話。牠們的思想完全在另一個世界。

Introduction: But are they friendly spirits?  Just listen!  "Boo!"  Nope, guess not.
序章: 但牠們是善良的靈魂嗎？聽聽看！「嘩！」不，看來不是。

Introduction: Had I the ears to hear, or a mouth to speak, I'd do my best to make you shriek!
序章: 「如果我有耳朵來聽，或有嘴來說，我會盡力讓你尖叫！」

Introduction: Casper he's not.
序章: 牠不是卡斯帕。

Introduction: Boo hoo, I'm so unhappy here, says the ghost with a grave demeanor.
序章: 「嘸嘸，我在這裡太不開心了，」幽靈嚴肅地說。

Introduction: Climbing this wall is too difficult for your current skill level, but keep practicing.
序章: 攀爬這面牆對你現在的技能等級來說太困難了，但要繼續練習。

Introduction: This must be the town graveyard.  What a run-down place!
序章: 這一定是鎮上的墓地。多麼破敗的地方！

Introduction: You wrench the mandrake plant free from the tombstone on which it has grown. You hear a scream like that of a dying child as you yank the root from the ground.
序章: 你用力從墓碑上掰下曼德拉草。當你從地面拉起根莖時，你聽到像瀕死的孩子一樣的尖叫。

Introduction: It appears that the spirits frolic here during the dark hours.
序章: 看起來靈魂在黑暗時刻在這裡嬉戲。

Introduction: You wrench the mandrake plant free from the tombstone on which it has grown. Somehow you thought this would be a lot harder.  You wonder if you did this right.
序章: 你用力從墓碑上掰下曼德拉草。不知怎地，你以為這會困難得多。你想知道你是否做對了。

Introduction: The root shrivels and twists in your hands until nothing is left. Astonished, you look at your empty hands.
序章: 根莖在你手中枯縮扭曲，直到什麼都沒有了。驚訝地，你看著你的空手。

Introduction: It's certainly creepy here even during the daylight hours.
序章: 即使在白天，這裡也確實令人毛骨悚然。

Introduction: Your magical abilities are useless against the undead.
序章: 你的魔法能力對不死生物無用。

Introduction: Practicing your knife throwing here could be a grave mistake.
序章: 在這裡練習刀投可能會是一個嚴重的錯誤。

Introduction: Your magical abilities are useless against the undead.
序章: 你的魔法能力對不死生物無用。

Introduction: You sense a spiritual coldness.
序章: 你感受到一股精神上的寒冷。

Introduction: What a surprise -- there's magic all around here!  (After all, SOMETHING is making those spirits fly around, and it isn't the wind.)
序章: 多麼驚人——這裡到處都是魔法！（畢竟，有什麼東西在讓那些靈魂飛來飛去，而且不是風。）

Introduction: The valley floor is covered by fine, moist soil with few large rocks.
序章: 山谷地面被細膩、潮濕的土壤覆蓋，大岩石很少。

Introduction: I wouldn't if I were you -- what if you actually managed to grab a ghost or fetch a fetch?  You'd feel foolish (if not downright ghoulish).
序章: 如果我是你就不會——如果你真的抓到一個幽靈或施展取物術的取物術呢？你會感到愚蠢（如果不是徹底令人厭惡的話）。

Introduction: Nothing grows in the dirt of the old graveyard.  Some of the graves appear to have been disturbed.
序章: 古老的墓地裡沒有任何東西生長。有些墳墓似乎被擾動過。

Introduction: The graves seemed to be placed haphazardly in the tiny graveyard.  All appear to be old and poorly maintained.
序章: 這些墳墓似乎在這個小墓地中隨意放置。所有的都看起來又舊又維護不良。

Introduction: The only things that can be opened here are graves, and you don't want to
序章: 這裡唯一能打開的是墳墓，而你不想…

Introduction: It just ghosts to show you, "Sticks and stones may break YOUR bones"... but these ghosts haven't got any.
序章: 幽靈只是向你展示「木棍和石頭可能會打斷你的骨頭」…但這些幽靈沒有任何骨頭。

Introduction: You feel a tingling sensation as you rub the unguent all over your body.
序章: 當你在全身塗抹軟膏時，你感到一陣刺痛。

Introduction: The grass glows ghostly.
序章: 草地發出幽靈般的光芒。

Introduction: The grass grows greener on the other side of the cemetery.
序章: 墓地另一邊的草長得更綠。

Introduction: Sealed tombs are best left that way.
序章: 密封的墓穴最好保持那樣。

Introduction: This is the tomb in which the Spielburg nobility have traditionally been buried.
序章: 這是史畢柏格貴族傳統上被埋葬的墓穴。

Introduction: The forest surrounds the graveyard on three sides.
序章: 森林從三面包圍著墓地。

Introduction: The wall is too difficult for your level of skill, but keep practicing.
序章: 這道牆對你的技能等級來說太困難了，但要繼續練習。

Introduction: You would have trouble convincing people you are a Hero if you climbed the wall into town during the day.
序章: 如果你在白天爬牆進城，你將難以說服人們你是英雄。

Introduction: The town wall looks more massive from this side.
序章: 從這一邊看，城鎮的牆看起來更龐大。

Introduction: If ya want a good time, try the 'Dragon's Breath' at the bar.
序章: 「如果你想玩得愉快，試試酒吧裡的『龍息』。」

Introduction: I think I hear your momma calling you.
序章: 「我好像聽到你媽媽在叫你。」

Introduction: Bug off.
序章: 「滾開。」

Introduction: Why don'tcha leave me alone?  I ain't done nothin'.
序章: 「你為什麼不留我一個人？我什麼都沒做。」

Introduction: Your boot's untied.
序章: 「你的靴子沒綁好。」

Introduction: You look nervous.  Why don'tcha take a walk and cool off.
序章: 「你看起來很緊張。你為什麼不去散步冷靜一下。」

Introduction: Hey, I'm just trying to make a buck.  No need to give me a hard time.
序章: 「嘿，我只是想賺點錢。不必給我難堪。」

Introduction: Next to the town's wall, you notice a man who appears to be waiting for someone.  His dagger looks like a particularly deadly weapon, and the thief handles it with great skill.
序章: 在城鎮的牆邊，你注意到一個似乎在等待某人的男人。他的匕首看起來是一件特別致命的武器，而這個盜賊用極高的技巧操縱它。

Introduction: Bruno looks very adept at throwing daggers, an altogether dangerous man.  You suspect he sees a lot through those shifty eyes.
序章: Bruno 擅長投擲匕首，一個完全危險的人。你懷疑他透過那些狡詐的眼睛看到很多東西。

Introduction: Dragon's Breath
序章: 龍息

Introduction: Cemetery
序章: 墓地

Introduction: Chief
序章: 首領

Introduction: Crusher
序章: 粉碎者

Introduction: Goblins
序章: 哥布林

Introduction: Thieves' Guild
序章: 盜賊公會

Introduction: Yaga's Hut
序章: 雅嘎的小屋

Introduction: Monsters
序章: 怪物

Introduction: Otto
序章: 奧圖

Introduction: Password
序章: 密碼

Introduction: Sheriff
序章: 治安官

Introduction: What's in it for me?  If ya give me a silver, I might have a bit of info you can use.
序章: 「我能得到什麼？如果你給我一個銀幣，我可能會告訴你一些有用的信息。」

Introduction: For two gold, I'll tell ya how to get into old Baba Yaga's hut.
序章: 「用兩個金幣，我會告訴你如何進入芭芭雅嘎的小屋。」

Introduction: Give me dough, ya know.
序章: 「給我錢，你知道。」

Introduction: A little silver might loosen my lip.
序章: 「一點銀幣可能會讓我多嘴。」

Introduction: For ten golds, I'll tell ya where to find some of the robbers what have been robbing people coming into the valley.
序章: 「用十個金幣，我會告訴你在哪裡找到一些搶劫進入山谷的人的盜賊。」

Introduction: Get outta my face.
序章: 「離我遠點。」

Introduction: Bruno nods and makes the countersign.
序章: Bruno 點了點頭，做出了反簽。

Introduction: Ya need ta talk to Sneak about the password.  Ya can find him in the alley at night.
序章: 「你需要和 Sneak 談論密碼。你可以在夜晚的小巷裡找到他。」

Introduction: Yeah, right, I already gave ya the sign once.  I don't like ta do things twice.
序章: 「是啊，沒錯，我已經給過你一次暗號了。我不喜歡做兩次。」

Introduction: Hahahaha!  Yeh, the antwerp is a good monster for you to fight.  It loves to play.
序章: 「哈哈哈！是的，安特衛普是一個適合你戰鬥的好怪物。牠喜歡玩。」

Introduction: She's an ugly hag who lives in a weird house who knows some magic.  Her place is due west of the Baron's place.  Can't miss it.
序章: 「她是一個醜陋的老巫婆，住在一個奇怪的房子裡，懂一些魔法。她的地方在男爵地方的正西。不會看不到。」

Introduction: And for two gold, I'll tell ya how to get into her hut.
序章: 「再加兩個金幣，我會告訴你如何進入她的小屋。」

Introduction: Ya just take the road north from here past the healer's and go till ya get to his castle.
序章: 「你只需從這裡向北沿著路經過治療師的地方，直到到達他的城堡。」

Introduction: Everyone loves Dragon's Breath.
序章: 「每個人都喜歡龍息。」

Introduction: Go south and you'll see a hollow log.  Then head west until ya can go south again.  Then go east until you can go south again.  From there go west.  Them brigands just love vistors.
序章: 「向南走，你會看到一根空心原木。然後向西走直到你可以再向南走。然後向東走直到你可以再向南走。從那裡向西走。那些山賊就喜歡訪客。」

Introduction: He's the only one in the valley who can beat me at daggers.
序章: 「他是山谷裡唯一能在匕首上打敗我的人。」

Introduction: He's a pal of mine.  He usually hangs out in the bar.  A real sweet guy. Give him a slap on the back for me.
序章: 「他是我的朋友。他通常在酒吧裡閒晃。一個真正好的傢伙。替我在他背上拍一下。」

Introduction: The little pests are just northwest of the cemetery.  They're a bunch of wimps.
序章: 「這些小害蟲就在墓地的西北。牠們一群懦弱的傢伙。」

Introduction: They hide it in the graveyard.  It's under one of the tombstones.  You can see the thieves if you go there at night.
序章: 「牠們把它藏在墓地裡。在某個墓碑下面。如果你在夜晚去那裡，你可以看到盜賊。」

Introduction: She's got some good potions.  Her hut is just to the north of here.
序章: 「她有些不錯的藥水。她的小屋就在這裡的正北面。」

Introduction: I seen her one time yelling out to the house, 'Hut of brown, Now sit down.' That is one spooky dame, let me tell you.
序章: 「我見過她有一次對房子大喊，『褐色的小屋，現在坐下。』那是一個令人毛骨悚然的女人，讓我告訴你。」

Introduction: Lots'a monsters in this valley.  Most of 'em are wimps, though.
序章: 「這個山谷裡有很多怪物。但大多數都是懦弱的傢伙。」

Introduction: Just call me Bruno.
序章: 「就叫我 Bruno。」

Introduction: The Sheriff and the goon are pals.  Otto even lives with the Sheriff and his wife.
序章: 「治安官和那個傢伙是朋友。奧圖甚至和治安官以及他的妻子住在一起。」

Introduction: The thieves' password?  Sorry, I can't risk losing my thief license.  Chief wouldn't like it if I blabbed that to the likes'a you.
序章: 「盜賊的密碼？抱歉，我不能冒著失去我的盜賊執照的風險。首領如果我對你這樣的人透露會不高興。」

Introduction: He's some kinda magic user.  And he laughs a lot.  That's all I know.
序章: 「他是某種魔法使用者。而且他笑得很多。這就是我知道的一切。」

Introduction: How much silver will you pay?
序章: 你會付多少銀幣？

Introduction: Okay, whaddaya wanna know.
序章: 「好的，你想知道什麼。」

Introduction: Don't mess around with Bruno.  You don't have enough money.
序章: 不要和 Bruno 惹麻煩。你沒有足夠的錢。

Introduction: For another silver, I can tell ya about where to go to find the Baron, Baba Yaga, or some goblins who have a lot of treasure.
序章: 「再加一個銀幣，我可以告訴你去哪裡找到男爵、芭芭雅嘎或某些有很多寶藏的哥布林。」

Introduction: So if ya want some information, hand me some cash.
序章: 「所以如果你想要一些信息，給我一些錢。」

Introduction: A mapseller from Shapeir used to have his stand here, but he's moved on.
序章: 一個來自夏皮爾的地圖商人曾經在這裡有他的攤位，但他已經搬走了。

Introduction: The town gates are open.
序章: 城鎮的大門是開放的。

Introduction: The town gates are closed and barred at night.
序章: 城鎮的大門在夜間關閉並上了門閂。

Introduction: The gate is kept open during the day.
序章: 大門在白天保持開放。

Introduction: Climbing the wall proves too difficult for your current level of skill, but keep practicing.
序章: 攀爬牆證明對你現在的技能等級來說太困難了，但要繼續練習。

Introduction: After making sure nobody is watching, you climb over the town's wall.
序章: 確保沒有人在看著之後，你爬過城鎮的牆。

Introduction: The breeze is cool, but you feel a shiver deeper than just the cold.  You are really on your own now in a very dangerous place.
序章: 微風很涼爽，但你感到一陣比寒冷更深的寒顫。你現在真的在一個非常危險的地方獨自一人。

Introduction: Heavy forest looms to the south.  The trees are just ordinary trees.
序章: 濃密的森林聳立在南邊。這些樹木只是普通的樹木。

Introduction: What can be seen of the sky through the trees is bright and clear.
序章: 透過樹木可以看到的天空是明亮而清晰的。

Introduction: The full moon portends dangerous times.
序章: 滿月預示著危險的時刻。

Introduction: The sign has been carved from local oak.
序章: 這個招牌是用當地的橡木雕刻的。

Introduction: It's flat.  It's made of dirt.  Hey, maybe it's a road!
序章: 它是平的。它是由泥土製成的。嘿，也許它是一條路！

Introduction: The north road winds its way through the woods.
序章: 北邊的路蜿蜒穿過樹林。

Introduction: The road from town branches here.  The north fork leads past the healer's hut to the Baron's castle.  You remember having entered the valley along the eastern road, just before a storm closed the pass.
序章: 來自城鎮的路在這裡分支。北邊的叉路經過治療師的小屋通往男爵的城堡。你還記得沿著東邊的路進入山谷，就在暴風雨關閉了山口之前。

Introduction: You're on the road again!
序章: 你又在路上了！

Introduction: Spielburg roads -- paving the way to a better future.  (Only they haven't gotten around to paving this one yet.)
序章: 史畢柏格的路——為美好的未來鋪路。（只是他們還沒有抽空鋪這條。）

Introduction: The main road by which you entered this valley winds its way through the dense forest.
序章: 你進入這個山谷的主要路線蜿蜒穿過濃密的森林。

Introduction: The eastern fork is the road which brought you into this valley.
序章: 東邊的叉路是帶你進入這個山谷的路。

Introduction: It looks sort of grey, and sort of hard, actually quite a bit like a rock. Yep, that's what it is.
序章: 它看起來有點灰色，有點硬，實際上相當像一塊石頭。是的，就是這樣。

Introduction: The carved wooden sign reads "SPIELBURG."
序章: 雕刻的木牌上寫著「史畢柏格」。

Introduction: The town gates are closed and barred for the night.
序章: 城鎮的大門在夜間關閉並上了門閂。

Introduction: The town gate is closed and locked for the night.
序章: 城鎮大門在夜間關閉並上了鎖。

Introduction: The town wall is made of very sturdy carved rock.
序章: 城鎮的牆是由非常堅固的雕刻岩石製成的。

Introduction: The town gate is wide open.  You would look silly climbing the wall.
序章: 城鎮大門敞開著。你爬牆會看起來很愚蠢。

Introduction: It used to be used for wheeling and dealing, but the wheels won't turn anymore.
序章: 它曾經被用來交易，但輪子不再轉動了。

Introduction: Somebody left the cart before the horse.  (Maybe the horse wasn't quite as broken down.)
序章: 有人把車放在馬前面。（也許馬沒有那麼老舊。）

Introduction: It's an old, broken down cart.
序章: 這是一輛又舊又破損的車。

Introduction: Well, it's broke, that seems to be about cart for the horse around here.
序章: 嗯，它壞了，這似乎是這裡關於馬的車的情況。

Introduction: No one seems to care much about keeping the town clean any more.
序章: 似乎沒有人再多關心保持城鎮清潔了。

Introduction: This cart has seen better days.  Now it's more like a pile of firewood.
序章: 這輛車曾經有過更好的日子。現在它更像一堆柴火。

Introduction: My foot is caught in this trap.  If I had hands like you, instead of paws, I could free myself.
序章: 「我的腳被這個陷阱卡住了。如果我有像你一樣的手，而不是爪子，我可以自己解救。」

Introduction: Walk around to the other side of the fox, where the trap is.
序章: 走到狐狸的另一邊，陷阱在那裡。

Introduction: The fox is gone.
序章: 狐狸消失了。

Introduction: My foot is caught in this cruel trap and I am in great pain.  Surely you could take a minute and set me free.
序章: 「我的腳被這個殘忍的陷阱卡住了，我感到很大的痛苦。你肯定能抽出一分鐘時間讓我自由。」

Introduction: I am in such pain and agony I cannot think.  Help me, good adventurer, please.
序章: 我痛得要死，根本想不了。好心的冒險者，拜託幫幫我。

Introduction: You don't have to put me out of my misery, you just have to get me out of this trap.
序章: 你不用讓我痛快地死去，只要把我從這個陷阱裡救出來就行。

Introduction: There seems to be a fox north of the road.
序章: 北邊道路有隻狐狸。

Introduction: That was strange, no sooner did you kill the fox than it vanished.
序章: 真怪，你剛殺死狐狸，牠就消失了。

Introduction: Hmmm.  Perhaps it needed a stronger spell.
序章: 嗯…也許得用更強的法術。

Introduction: Your powerful Open spell springs the trap.
序章: 你強大的開啟術引發了陷阱。

Introduction: You spring the trap.
序章: 你啟動了陷阱。

Introduction: In exchange for your kindness, I will give you some advice and a bit of information.  First of all, it sometimes pays off to be polite, even to rude people.
序章: 為了回報你的善心，我會給你一些建議和資訊。首先呢，禮貌待人有時是值得的，就算對方很粗魯也不例外。

Introduction: As for the amusing tidbit -- Baba Yaga put an enchantment on the Baron's Daughter some years back.  To break the spell you need to talk to the Dryad. Au Revoir, Ta-ta for now, Be seeing you.
序章: 至於這則趣事──芭芭雅嘎多年前在男爵之女身上下了詛咒。要破解詛咒得去和樹精談談。再見，暫時再會，回頭見。

Introduction: Help me, Brave and Kind Hero.
序章: 幫幫我，勇敢善良的英雄。

Introduction: Please help me, Brave and Kind Hero.
序章: 拜託幫幫我，勇敢善良的英雄。

Introduction: Please have mercy on a creature who is in great pain.
序章: 求求你，對這隻深陷痛苦的生物發發慈悲。

Introduction: The fox does not seem to want to fight.
序章: 狐狸好像不想打架。

Introduction: The trap seems to be caught on something.  You can't pull it loose.
序章: 陷阱好像卡在什麼東西上。你沒辦法把它拉鬆。

Introduction: There is an aura of magic around the fox.
序章: 狐狸周圍有股魔法的氣息。

Introduction: There is no magic present.
序章: 這裡沒有魔法。

Introduction: There is no point to that.
序章: 這沒意義。

Introduction: The fox is in such pain that it's already dazzled.
序章: 狐狸痛得已經被眩暈了。

Introduction: You realize, of course, I really find it hard to relax with this thing on my leg.
序章: 當然，你明白吧，我實在很難在腿上戴著這東西的情況下放鬆。

Introduction: It looks like a dinosaur trudging grumpily to work.
序章: 看起來像隻恐龍不高興地拖著腳步去工作。

Introduction: It looks like a dinosaur skipping merrily back home from work.
序章: 看起來像隻恐龍歡快地跳著回家去。

Introduction: This trap is an especially nasty piece of work.  Besides the inch-long spiky bits, it... well, never mind; suffice it to say that you would not enjoy being caught in it.
序章: 這陷阱特別是個討厭的傢伙。除了那一英吋長的尖刺外，還有…算了；反正你一定不會想被困在裡面。

Introduction: It appears that a recent avalanche has sealed off the road leading out of the valley.
序章: 看來最近一場雪崩堵住了通往山谷外的道路。

Introduction: It's a ring formed of large and rather unusual-looking mushrooms.
序章: 那是由又大又奇怪的蘑菇形成的一個圓環。

Introduction: The fairy ring is luminous at night.
序章: 妖精圈在夜晚會發光。

Introduction: You pick a handful of the smaller mushrooms and carefully put them away in your backpack.
序章: 你摘下一把小蘑菇，小心翼翼地放進背包裡。

Introduction: I'll bet you're sorry you said that.
序章: 我打賭你現在後悔說那句話了。

Introduction: You place the dust carefully away in an empty flask.
序章: 你小心翼翼地把粉末放進空瓶子裡。

Introduction: You hold out your hand... what else could you do?
序章: 你伸出手…你還能幹什麼呢？

Introduction: As the fairy dust sifts through your fingers, you realize that you need something in which to hold it.
序章: 當妖精粉末從你的手指間漏下時，你才意識到需要什麼東西來裝著它。

Introduction: The trees look more vibrant than most of the forest.
序章: 這些樹看起來比森林裡大多數的樹更充滿生氣。

Introduction: There is a ring of mushrooms in the center of the clearing.
序章: 空地中央有一個蘑菇圓環。

Introduction: As the fairy dust sifts through your fingers, you realize that you needed something in which to put it.
序章: 當妖精粉末從你的手指間漏下時，你才意識到需要什麼東西來裝著它。

Introduction: To whom are you talking?
序章: 你在和誰說話？

Introduction: You detect faint emanations of magic near the ring of mushrooms.
序章: 你在蘑菇圓環附近偵測到微弱的魔法氣息。

Introduction: It's either a stone, a pebble, a mass of stony material, a solid mineral deposit, a block, or a rock.  I'll let you decide for yourself.
序章: 它是石頭、卵石、一堆石質物質、固態礦物堆積、一塊東西，或一塊岩石。我讓你自己決定。

Introduction: This used to be a member of a famous rock group, but it decided to split the scene.
序章: 這曾經是個著名搖滾樂團的成員，但它決定離開現場。

Introduction: The trees seem more dense and lush than the rest of the woods.
序章: 這些樹看起來比森林其他地方更密集、更翠綠。

Introduction: The trees here look thicker and healthier than in other parts of the forest.
序章: 這裡的樹看起來比森林其他地方更粗壯、更健康。

Introduction: This man looks very tough.  From his clothing, you guess him to be a member of the Thieves' Guild.
序章: 這個人看起來非常硬漢。從他的衣著，你猜測他是盜賊公會的一員。

Introduction: Dead men tell no tales.
序章: 死人不會講話。

Introduction: He's dead already; don't dull your daggers!
序章: 他已經死了；別磨鈍你的匕首！

Introduction: You detect no magic here.
序章: 你在這裡沒偵測到魔法。

Introduction: You waste a spell.
序章: 你浪費了一個法術。

Introduction: The brigand is too angry to make calm.
序章: 山賊太生氣了，無法被鎮定。

Introduction: You waste a spell. Fetch is only good for fetching small, visible objects.
序章: 你浪費了一個法術。取物術只能用來取小的、看得見的物體。

Introduction: Just wait. The nice brigand will throw you his knife.
序章: 只要等著。那個好心的山賊會把匕首扔給你。

Introduction: The dead brigand lies very still.
序章: 死山賊靜靜地躺著。

Introduction: You see a hard-looking character who appears to be a thief.  He must be one of the Brigands!
序章: 你看到一個看起來很硬漢的傢伙，看起來像個盜賊。他一定是其中一個山賊！

Introduction: Get closer to him.
序章: 靠近他。

Introduction: The brigand is too angry to calm.
序章: 山賊太生氣了，無法冷靜。

Introduction: Your Flame Dart streaks towards the startled brigand.
序章: 你的火焰鏢朝驚呆的山賊射去。

Introduction: You waste a spell.  Fetch is only good for fetching small, visible objects.
序章: 你浪費了一個法術。取物術只能用來取小的、看得見的物體。

Introduction: Just wait.  The nice brigand will throw you his knife.
序章: 只要等著。那個好心的山賊會把匕首扔給你。

Introduction: The buildings of Spielburg can be seen beyond the town wall.
序章: 在城牆外可以看到史畢柏格的建築物。

Introduction: So what's this about, anyway?
序章: 這到底是怎麼回事？

Introduction: Her Nibs is starting to get suspicious about us.
序章: 那位高高在上的傢伙開始懷疑我們了。

Introduction: What's the bee in her bonnet?
序章: 她怎麼了？

Introduction: Seems the 'hero' wandering around here has her leery.
序章: 看起來在這裡亂晃的『英雄』讓她提防。

Introduction: She thinks he's going to go for the gold on her head.
序章: 她認為他會去追那頭上的金子（懸賞）。

Introduction: What's it ta do with us?
序章: 這跟我們有什麼關係？

Introduction: She's been asking too many questions 'bout us.
序章: 她一直在問太多關於我們的問題。

Introduction: And the laughing jackass's eyeing me.  I had to sneak out.
序章: 那個愛笑的傻子一直盯著我。我不得不偷偷溜出來。

Introduction: Then we got to avoid the ambush and use the back for a bit, 'til the heat is off.
序章: 那麼我們得避開伏擊，用後門一陣子，等風吹過去。

Introduction: You retrieve your dagger.
序章: 你拿回你的匕首。

Introduction: Hey, watchu doin' here?  Spyin', I'll wager!
序章: 嘿，你在這裡幹嘛？偵查，我敢打賭！

Introduction: And remember the 'word'.
序章: 還有，記住那個『密碼』。

Introduction: What word?
序章: 什麼密碼？

Introduction: The 'word' what lets ya in so that Fred goes away.
序章: 那個『密碼』讓你進去，這樣佛瑞德就會消失。

Introduction: Oh yeah, sure.  What is it?
序章: 哦，是啊，當然。是什麼？

Introduction: Hiden Goseke.  Ya better learn it.
序章: 希登·格塞克。你最好學起來。

Introduction: You think I'm a dummy or somethin'?
序章: 你以為我是個傻瓜或什麼的？

Introduction: Say the 'word' before ya go in the door or ya might make Fred mad.
序章: 進門前說出那個『密碼』，不然你可能會惹火佛瑞德。

Introduction: Ya don't want Fred ta get mad.
序章: 你不想讓佛瑞德生氣。

Introduction: Hey, no problem.  What's the 'word' again?
序章: 嘿，沒問題。那個『密碼』是什麼來著？

Introduction: Hiden Goseke.
序章: 希登·格塞克。

Introduction: Hiden Goseke.  Got it.
序章: 希登·格塞克。明白了。

Introduction: I gotta get back before the Chief misses me.  Be back in a bit.
序章: 我得在頭兒發現我之前回去。等一下就回來。

Introduction: Hiden Goseke.  See ya.
序章: 希登·格塞克。回見。

Introduction: Maybe we should just make our move now.
序章: 也許我們該現在就採取行動。

Introduction: Naw, let's wait for the creep to go first.
序章: 不，我們先等那傢伙離開。

Introduction: While she's busy with him, we take over.
序章: 趁她忙著應付他的時候，我們接管。

Introduction: She'll take him out easy, then we take her out.
序章: 她會輕鬆解決他，然後我們解決她。

Introduction: Where's the back door, then?
序章: 那後門在哪裡？

Introduction: Where the bouncer hops around.  Ya got your key still?
序章: 保鑣蹦蹦跳跳的地方。你還有鑰匙嗎？

Introduction: Yeah.
序章: 是的。

Introduction: Don't lose it.  I got the only other one.
序章: 別丟了。我只有另一把。

Introduction: You'll haveta search the rock for the keyhole.  It's hidden good.
序章: 你得在石頭上搜尋鑰匙孔。它藏得很好。

Introduction: The forest is very overgrown near here.
序章: 這附近的森林長得特別茂盛。

Introduction: You search around this area for a bit, but you don't find anything very useful.
序章: 你在這個區域搜了一會兒，但沒找到什麼有用的東西。

Introduction: The branches of the brush block the spell from the brigand.
序章: 灌木的枝條阻擋了來自山賊的法術。

Introduction: The brigand is too angry to calm down.
序章: 山賊太生氣了，沒辦法冷靜下來。

Introduction: Just wait.  The nice brigand will throw you his knife.
序章: 只要等著。那個好心的山賊會把匕首扔給你。

Introduction: You find nothing else on the brigand's body.
序章: 你在山賊的身上找不到別的東西。

Introduction: You find a single key on the brigand's body, and put it away.
序章: 你在山賊的身上找到一把鑰匙，並把它收起來。

Introduction: The old archery target looks as though it has not been used in quite some time.
序章: 那個老舊的射箭靶子看起來已經好久沒用過了。

Introduction: You are using the old archery target for dagger throwing practice.
序章: 你用那個老舊的射箭靶子來練習投擲匕首。

Introduction: You can feel the holes where daggers and arrows once hit the target.
序章: 你可以感受到匕首和箭曾經擊中靶子的洞孔。

Introduction: There are too many bushes between you and the target.
序章: 你和靶子之間有太多灌木。

Introduction: The trees of the forest make this a pleasantly shady archery range.
序章: 森林的樹木使這裡成為一個令人愉快的陰涼射箭場。

Introduction: This tree is covered with old, dried fruit from last year.
序章: 這棵樹被去年的老舊乾果覆蓋。

Introduction: You're sharp enough to realize that climbing over these bushes could result in some thorny problems.
序章: 你聰明到意識到爬過這些灌木可能會引起一些棘手的問題。

Introduction: The fruit on this tree is withered, and swarming with hornets.  You think better about picking it.
序章: 這棵樹上的果子已經枯萎，而且蜜蜂群聚。你決定還是別摘了。

Introduction: This is the South wall of Spielburg.  An old target leans against the wall.
序章: 這是史畢柏格的南牆。一個老舊的靶子靠在牆上。

Introduction: There are acorns here and there on the ground.
序章: 地上到處都是橡樹果。

Introduction: There is a large, slightly glowing acorn on the ground.
序章: 地上有一個很大的、微微發光的橡樹果。

Introduction: No thanks; the tree already has plenty of acorns.
序章: 不用了；樹上已經有很多橡樹果了。

Introduction: You needn't dull your blade playing woodsman.
序章: 你不必因為玩木匠就磨鈍你的刀。

Introduction: As you begin to form thoughts of violence...
序章: 當你開始萌生暴力的念頭時…

Introduction: Something about the old oak tree makes you reluctant to climb upon it.
序章: 那棵老橡樹有什麼東西讓你不太想爬上去。

Introduction: The large, gnarled oak seems to draw your attention.
序章: 那棵又大又扭曲的橡樹似乎吸引了你的注意。

Introduction: You'd better alder your strategy -- the trees mostly just beech about their more poplar neighbors, or pine fir the fjords.  Oak-ay?
序章: 你最好改變你的策略──這些樹大多只是抱怨他們更受歡迎的鄰居，或者為峽灣感到難受。沒問題吧？

Introduction: The ancient bark crumbles beneath your fingers where thousands of tiny forest denizens have chewed their way towards the heart of this tree.
序章: 古老的樹皮在你的手指下碎裂，數千個微小的森林居民已經咀嚼著通往這棵樹心臟的路。

Introduction: In decay, this ancient tree lies as a bleak reminder that you really shouldn't have missed that last dentist appointment.
序章: 在衰敗中，這棵古樹躺在那裡，悲涼地提醒你，你真不應該錯過上次的牙醫約診。

Introduction: The old log is too broken up to talk to you.
序章: 那根老木頭破得太厲害，沒辦法和你說話。

Introduction: Most of the bushes around here seem to be bramble and are very thorny.
序章: 這裡周圍的大多數灌木似乎是荊棘，非常有刺。

Introduction: You did WHAT???
序章: 你做了什麼？？？

Introduction: She's beyond your reach.
序章: 她超出你的範圍。

Introduction: She's rooted to the ground.
序章: 她根在地上。

Introduction: The Dryad is not in at the moment.  Please leaf a message with your species name and branch.
序章: 樹精現在不在。請留下你的物種名稱和分支訊息。

Introduction: The stag remains out of your reach.
序章: 那隻雄鹿仍然超出你的範圍。

Introduction: Does a Dryad bark?
序章: 樹精會叫嗎？

Introduction: No, but she's a real birch.
序章: 不會，但她是個真正的難相處傢伙。

Introduction: Don't make an ash of yourself.
序章: 別讓自己出醜。

Introduction: The Dryad appears to be a woman made of wood and leaves.
序章: 樹精看起來像是一個由木頭和葉子組成的女人。

Introduction: The hills are far too far to finger.
序章: 那些山丘太遠，沒辦法碰到。

Introduction: Majestic mountains rising in the north are silhouetted against the crystal blue sky.
序章: 北邊雄偉的山峰在晶藍色的天空中映出剪影。

Introduction: You see an acorn fall slowly to the ground.
序章: 你看到一個橡樹果慢慢地掉到地上。

Introduction: Then you shall aid me, and I shall aid you in your quest.
序章: 那麼你要幫助我，我也會在你的任務中幫助你。

Introduction: Bring me a seed from the Spore Spitting Spirea of the North that I may plant it elsewhere in order to preserve these rare and magical plants.
序章: 給我帶來一粒來自北邊的孢子噴發繡線菊的種子，這樣我可以在別處種植它，以保護這些稀有的魔法植物。

Introduction: Thus will you become a true friend of the forest.
序章: 「這樣你就會成為森林的真正朋友。」

Introduction: You pick up the Dryad's gift of a Magic Acorn.
序章: 你拿起樹精贈與的魔法橡實。

Introduction: You drop the seed into the Dryad's limbs.
序章: 你把種子掉進樹精的枝幹中。

Introduction: Until you have done so, you are only intruding on my concentration.
序章: 「在你完成之前，你只是在打擾我的冥想。」

Introduction: That acorn tasted awfully bitter... just like an ordinary acorn.
序章: 那顆橡實嚐起來苦得要命…就像一顆普通的橡實。

Introduction: You find an ordinary acorn on the ground.
序章: 你在地上找到一顆普通的橡實。

Introduction: You dare to harm the Dryad of the forest!  You must be made less dangerous.
序章: 「你竟敢傷害森林的樹精！你必須變得不那麼危險。」

Introduction: Heed now my words, friend of the forest, and heed them well.
序章: 「現在就要聽我的話，森林的朋友，要聽清楚。」

Introduction: Friend, you must know that there is an evil in this valley which perverts the ways of nature.  The prophecy says that a hero will bring a young human from out of this darkness.
序章: 「朋友，你必須知道這個山谷裡有一股邪惡之力，它扭曲了自然的秩序。預言說會有一位英雄將一個年輕的人類從黑暗中拯救出來。」

Introduction: If you are the one to accomplish this task, there is a potion to break enchantments which the Healer can make for you.
序章: 「如果你能完成這項任務，治療師可以為你製作一個解除魔法的藥水。」

Introduction: You must gather these ingredients:
序章: 「你必須收集這些材料：」

Introduction: Flowers from Erana's Peace,
序章: 「伊瑞娜的安寧之地的花朵，」

Introduction: Green Fur,
序章: 「綠色的毛皮，」

Introduction: Fairy Dust,
序章: 「妖精之塵，」

Introduction: a Magic Acorn,
序章: 「一個魔法橡實，」

Introduction: and Flying Water.
序章: 「和飛行之水。」

Introduction: Farewell, friend.  I must return to my concentration.  May the forest forever surround you.
序章: 「再見了，朋友。我必須回去冥想。願森林永遠環繞著你。」

Introduction: You follow the stag into this forest corner.  You feel as though the eyes of the forest are watching you.
序章: 你跟著白鹿進入森林的角落。你感覺彷彿整個森林的眼睛都在注視著你。

Introduction: What a beautiful animal!
序章: 多麼美麗的動物！

Introduction: You need to become a friend of the forest.  Harm not the gentle creatures or plants.  See with all your senses the magic of the trees and wild places.
序章: 「你需要成為森林的朋友。不要傷害溫和的生物或植物。用你所有的感官去感受樹木和野生地方的魔法。」

Introduction: Wander and learn.  Then you too will be in harmony with the forest.
序章: 「漫步其中吧，並學習。然後你也會與森林和諧相處。」

Introduction: Give the seed to me.
序章: 「把種子給我。」

Introduction: Now this will boldly grow where none has grown before!
序章: 「現在它將大膽地在無人種植過的地方生長！」

Introduction: You have destroyed the plant that was to be preserved!
序章: 「你摧毀了本應被保護的植物！」

Introduction: As you have destroyed, so shall you now become!
序章: 「正如你所摧毀的，你現在也將變成這樣！」

Introduction: You have attempted to harm a free creature of the forest!
序章: 「你試圖傷害森林的自由生物！」

Introduction: You shall now become a part of the forest!
序章: 「你現在將成為森林的一部分！」

Introduction: You have misused your skills, oh Magician!  You have used the Flame Dart on my forest.
序章: 「你濫用了你的技能，哦，法師！你在我的森林上使用了火焰鏢。」

Introduction: You must be made less dangerous.
序章: 「你必須變得不那麼危險。」

Introduction: You are forced to confess that you lied to the Dryad.  You don't have the magic seed.
序章: 你被迫承認你對樹精撒謊了。你沒有魔法種子。

Introduction: It's not nice to fool Mother Nature.
序章: 「欺騙大自然是不禮貌的。」

Introduction: You tell the Dryad: "No amount of persuasion could convince the flowers to relinquish their seed easily, so I was forced to hack my way to the seed with my weapon."
序章: 你告訴樹精：「無論多少勸說都無法輕易說服花朵放棄它們的種子，所以我被迫用武器砍出一條路到達種子。」

Introduction: You pick it up and eat it.
序章: 你拿起它並吃了它。

Introduction: The acorn appears too ordinary.  You drop it back on the ground.
序章: 那顆橡實看起來太普通了。你把它掉回地上。

Introduction: I detect that you have in your possession such a seed.  Are you willing to give me the seed?
序章: 「我發現你擁有這樣一顆種子。你願意把種子給我嗎？」

Introduction: Have you brought the seed I requested?
序章: 「你帶來我要求的種子了嗎？」

Introduction: Well, Hero-to-be!  Are you yet a friend of the woods?
序章: 「好吧，未來的英雄！你是森林的朋友嗎？」

Introduction: The tree-woman speaks:  "I am the Dryad, keeper of the woods.  Are you one with the woods?"
序章: 樹精女人說：「我是樹精，森林的守護者。你與森林為一體嗎？」

Introduction: Again, you feel a sense of closeness with nature.
序章: 你再次感到與自然相近的感覺。

Introduction: You know you will sleep comfortably and well in this quiet and protected corner of the woods.
序章: 你知道在森林的這個安靜而受保護的角落，你會舒服地安睡。

Introduction: The stag is startled by your action.
序章: 白鹿被你的行動嚇到了。

Introduction: You watch the stag, fascinated with his grace and beauty.  There is something special about this place.
序章: 你看著白鹿，被它的優雅和美麗迷住了。這個地方有什麼特別的。

Introduction: The oak tree radiates magic.
序章: 橡樹散發出魔法。

Introduction: The Dryad radiates magic.
序章: 樹精散發出魔法。

Introduction: You can feel a gentle breeze wafting its way between the woods.  The scent of fresh flowers and green growing things fills you with a sense of peace.
序章: 你可以感覺到微風輕輕吹過森林。新鮮花朵和綠色植物的香氣充滿了你，讓你感到平靜。

Introduction: You are in a strange and beautiful part of the forest.  There is something special about this place.
序章: 你在森林的一個奇異而美麗的地方。這個地方有什麼特別的。

Introduction: The forest becomes so lush and thick to the west that it is difficult to see far.
序章: 森林向西變得非常茂密，很難看得遠。

Introduction: The brush and undergrowth has formed an impassable thicket to the south.
序章: 灌木叢和下層植被在南邊形成了無法通過的灌木林。

Introduction: You see thick brush, rocks, and heavy woods to the north.
序章: 你看到北邊有厚重的灌木叢、岩石和茂密的森林。

Introduction: You sense a certain magic about the huge oak tree that dominates the clearing.
序章: 你感受到主宰空地的巨大橡樹散發的某種魔法。

Introduction: That was interesting, but there's nothing here to open.
序章: 那是有趣的，但這裡沒有什麼可以打開的。

Introduction: The rocks are much to heavy to move.
序章: 岩石太重了，無法移動。

Introduction: The rocks to the north look steep.  There is a rock wall northeast of the large oak tree.
序章: 北邊的岩石看起來很陡峭。大橡樹的東北邊有一面岩壁。

Introduction: Don't be ridiculous; you can't talk to a rock.  Try the trees instead.
序章: 別傻了；你不能跟一塊岩石說話。試著跟樹說話吧。

Introduction: The stag looks more surprised than injured.
序章: 白鹿看起來更驚訝而不是受傷。

Introduction: He's beyond your reach.
序章: 他超出你的範圍。

Introduction: The majestic white stag is an outstanding example of the beauty of the animal kingdom.
序章: 莊嚴的白鹿是動物王國美麗的傑出典範。

Introduction: You detect a strange, magical aura in this place.
序章: 你在這個地方察覺到奇異的魔法光環。

Introduction: Your spell has no effect.
序章: 你的魔法沒有效果。

Introduction: Bushes solidly block your way south -- no shortcuts here.
序章: 灌木叢完全阻擋了你向南的路——這裡沒有捷徑。

Introduction: You seem to have startled the white stag.
序章: 你似乎嚇到了白鹿。

Introduction: The beautiful white stag is foraging for food.
序章: 美麗的白鹿正在覓食。

Introduction: The white stag takes a mighty leap.
序章: 白鹿縱身一躍。

Introduction: The stag looks more surprised than injured.
序章: 白鹿看起來更驚訝而不是受傷。

Introduction: You seem to have caused an Antwerp POPulation EXPLOSION!
序章: 你似乎導致了一場安特衛普人口爆炸！

Introduction: The bouncing baby Antwerps are all so cute, you can't bring yourself to interfere with their playing.
序章: 跳躍著的小安特衛普都很可愛，你捨不得打擾它們玩耍。

Introduction: Looking up, you can see a small, blue dot in the sky, getting bigger... and bigger... and BIGGER!
序章: 向上看，你可以看到天空中一個小的藍點，越來越大…還有更大…還有更大！

Introduction: Too late!
序章: 太遲了！

Introduction: There's no time for that!
序章: 沒有時間做那個！

Introduction: Your spell has no effect.
序章: 你的魔法沒有效果。

Introduction: The beautiful white stag is foraging for food.
序章: 美麗的白鹿正在覓食。

Introduction: The white stag takes a mighty leap.
序章: 白鹿縱身一躍。

Introduction: The stag looks more surprised than injured.
序章: 白鹿看起來更驚訝而不是受傷。

Introduction: Thick thorn bushes seal off the way south.
序章: 厚重的荊棘灌木叢封住了南邊的路。

Introduction: This man looks very tough.  From his clothing, you guess him to be a member of the Thieves' Guild.
序章: 這個人看起來非常硬漢。從他的衣著，你猜測他是盜賊公會的一員。

Introduction: Like a floral alliance of the Mason-Dixon and Maginot lines, a row of bushes prevent you from moving south.
序章: 就像梅森-迪克森線和馬其諾防線的花卉聯盟，一排灌木叢阻止了你向南移動。

Introduction: The hollow log looks somberly back with it's single giant eye, and you know at once that nothing is to be gained by investigating this gaunt relic of a more vertical past.
序章: 空心木頭用它唯一的巨大眼睛陰沉地看著，你立刻知道調查這個更垂直過去的瘦弱遺跡沒有任何收穫。

Introduction: Besides that huge hollow log, there's nothing particularly special about this part of the forest (unless you count the sound of rushing water to the east).
序章: 除了那個巨大的空心木頭，這部分森林沒有什麼特別的地方(除非你算上東邊湍急的水聲)。

Introduction: The water seems to be trying to fly as it leaps from the mountain above.
序章: 水似乎在從上面的山上躍下時試圖飛起。

Introduction: A river plunges for more than a hundred feet down the face of a cliff.  You see slick, vertical cliff walls.  A doorway has been built into the side of the cliff.
序章: 一條河流從懸崖面上垂直落下超過百英尺。你看到光滑的、垂直的懸崖牆。一扇門已經被建造在懸崖的一側。

Introduction: The grass is green with the freshness of spring.
序章: 草地是春天的新鮮的綠色。

Introduction: The door is built into the face of the mountainside.  You suspect that the person who lives behind it treasures privacy.
序章: 門被建造在山邊的表面。你懷疑住在它後面的人珍視隱私。

Introduction: It's a large door.  It must open pretty wide.
序章: 這是一扇大門。它一定開得相當寬。

Introduction: It's a little hard to knock on the door from where you're standing.
序章: 從你站的地方敲門有點困難。

Introduction: The door is so securely locked that it defies your abilities to unlock it. (You might have had an easier time if it had a keyhole.)
序章: 門鎖得非常牢固，超出了你的解鎖能力。(如果它有鑰匙孔，你可能會容易得多。)

Introduction: You can't do that from here.
序章: 你不能從這裡做那個。

Introduction: You're too close to throw a rock.
序章: 你太近了，無法投擲岩石。

Introduction: It's an iridescently shimmering magical ladder!
序章: 這是一個虹彩閃爍的魔法梯子！

Introduction: You don't see a ladder here.
序章: 你在這裡看不到梯子。

Introduction: There is a narrow ledge in front of the wide door.
序章: 寬門前有一個狹窄的岩架。

Introduction: You don't have an empty flask.
序章: 你沒有空的瓶子。

Introduction: The water gracefully flows towards Mirror Lake.
序章: 水優雅地流向鏡面湖。

Introduction: You already have some.  Don't be greedy.
序章: 你已經有一些了。不要貪心。

Introduction: Your flask is already full of water.
序章: 你的瓶子已經充滿了水。

Introduction: The rocks are too slick from the spray of the waterfall for your limited climbing skill.
序章: 由於瀑布的噴霧，岩石太光滑了，超出了你有限的攀爬技能。

Introduction: Flat... you feel very, very flat.
序章: 扁…你感覺非常、非常扁。

Introduction: 'ello?
Wow!
序章: 哇！

Introduction: Wheeee!
序章: 呼！

Introduction: You again?  Either come up, or go away.  I really doesn't cares which.
序章: 「又是你？要麼上來，要麼滾開。我真的不在乎哪一個。」

Introduction: You'll have to climb down if you want to get water.
序章: 如果你想要水，你得爬下去。

Introduction: The water comes from melting snow.  This early in the season, it's much too cold to swim.  Besides, you don't have a suit.
序章: 水來自融化的雪。在這季節這麼早，游泳太冷了。而且，你沒有衣服。

Introduction: Oh.  'ello!    Go on in.
序章: 「哦。嘿！進去吧。」

Introduction: BOY!  You must have really been thirsty!
序章: 天哪！你一定真的很渴！

Introduction: The roar of the waterfall fills your ears, and the cold spray dampens your face as you approach.
序章: 瀑布的轟鳴聲填滿了你的耳朵，當你靠近時，冷的噴霧潮濕了你的臉。

Introduction: You fill an empty flask with crystal-clear water from the waterfall.
序章: 你用瀑布的清澈的水填滿了一個空的瓶子。

Introduction: You look for a good place to climb.
序章: 你尋找一個好的攀爬地點。

Introduction: Mpfph..  grumble..  nobody 'ere, I guess.
序章: 「嗯…抱怨…我想這裡沒人。」

Introduction: All right, already!
序章: 「好吧，已經！」

Introduction: The rock makes a sharp sound as it hits the door.
序章: 岩石撞到門時發出尖銳的聲音。

Introduction: RAP!
序章: 敲擊！

Introduction: Man, that's a narrow ledge up there!
序章: 天啊，那是一個狹窄的岩架！

Introduction: You knock three times.
序章: 你敲了三下。

Introduction: A voice from inside says, "Just a minute!  Just a minute!"
序章: 裡面傳來一個聲音：「稍等！稍等！」

Introduction: Please move away from the door.
序章: 「請遠離門。」

Introduction: That was as easy... as climbing up a ladder.
序章: 那像…爬梯子一樣容易。

Introduction: That spell doesn't seem to work at this time.
序章: 那個魔法在這個時候似乎不起作用。

Introduction: Is someone there?
序章: 「有人嗎？」

Introduction: Oh, 'ello.  Come right up.
序章: 「哦，嘿。直接上來。」

Introduction: Just climb the ladder.
序章: 「就爬梯子吧。」

Introduction: You take another drink of water.  You were thirsty!
序章: 你又喝了一口水。你很渴！

Introduction: That was almost too easy... just like climbing a ladder.
序章: 那幾乎太容易了…就像爬梯子一樣。

Introduction: Missed.
序章: 射偏了。

Introduction: You take a drink of water from the icy mountain river.  It refreshes you.
序章: 你從冰冷的山河喝了一口水。它使你恢復活力。

Introduction: To the west, you see the forest.
序章: 向西，你看到森林。

Introduction: The hermit doesn't seem to hear you.
序章: 隱士似乎沒有聽到你。

Introduction: The only thing you can open here is the door, and you're not skilled enough to do that.
序章: 你唯一能在這裡打開的東西是門，而你沒有足夠的技能來做那個。

Introduction: As you prepare your spell...
序章: 當你準備你的魔法…

Introduction: The water is white as it splashes against the rocks with great force and purpose, and disappears into a narrow canyon to the south.
序章: 水與岩石猛烈撞擊時變成白色，帶著巨大的力量和目的，然後消失在南邊的狹窄峽谷中。

Introduction: It's the hermit's staff.  (He needs a large staff to help him record his memoirs for posterity.)
序章: 這是隱士的杖。(他需要一個大杖來幫助他記錄他的回憶錄給後代。)

Introduction: It's not polite to steal from your 'airy little 'ost.  You seem to have made the 'ermit un'appy.
序章: 從你那髮毛叢生的小房東那兒偷東西實在不禮貌。看來你惹得這隱士很不開心。

Introduction: A single candle sits upon the table.
序章: 一根蠟燭孤獨地擺在桌上。

Introduction: You'll have to get up first.
序章: 你得先起身才行。

Introduction: That's the swinging, rocking cave entrance.
序章: 那是搖晃擺動的洞穴入口。

Introduction: It's a chair carved out of rough stone.
序章: 這是用粗糙石頭雕刻出來的椅子。

Introduction: The fire is down to a set of dimly glowing coals.
序章: 火焰只剩下微微發光的炭火。

Introduction: You see a cot.
序章: 你看到一張簡易床。

Introduction: The fire has mostly burned down.  It doesn't seem to be accomplishing much in the way of heat or light for the cave.
序章: 火焰已燒得差不多了。對洞穴的溫度和照明幫助似乎不大。

Introduction: No thanks.  I'm not much o' one for those tree snacks.
序章: 「不了。我不太喜歡那些樹上的零食。」

Introduction: Nasty sorts, they is.  I sees them sneaking arund the woods all the times. Uther then the Warlock, I 'as nothing to do with them.
序章: 「那些傢伙很討厭。我常常看到它們在樹林裡偷偷摸摸。除了巫術師，我和它們沒什麼關係。」

Introduction: Well, it's dark and damp and the waters roars sos there's not a moment's quiet.  The ceiling drips and the walls are slimy and it's always cold.
序章: 「嗯，這地方又黑又潮濕，水聲咆哮得沒一刻安寧。天花板滴水，牆壁黏糊糊的，永遠冷得要命。」

Introduction: But, oh well, sez I, it's 'ome.
序章: 「但，嗯，我說啊，這是家。」

Introduction: I likes to play the game.  I can show you how to play, if you likes.
序章: 「我喜歡玩遊戲。要是你有興趣，我可以教你怎麼玩。」

Introduction: E's me friend, 'e and Fenrus.  Powerful wizard 'e is.
序章: 「他是我的朋友，他和芬魯斯。他是位強大的巫師。」

Introduction: I 'ere 'is 'ouse is sumthin' to see up north.
序章: 「我聽說他的房子在北邊，值得一看。」

Introduction: I don't make it out much, and 'e's pretty busy, sos I don't see 'em too often.  But when we do all get together, you can be sure we'll 'ave fun.
序章: 「我出門不多，他也很忙，所以我不常見到他們。但當我們聚在一起時，保證會很開心。」

Introduction: 'E loves to play 'is games, 'e do.
序章: 「他喜歡玩他的遊戲，真的。」

Introduction: This 'ere's wot's called the Flying Falls.
序章: 「這個地方叫做飛瀑。」

Introduction: I've doon sum flying falls arund 'ere meself.  That porch gets slippery sumtimes.  He, heh, he.
序章: 「我自己也在這附近飛過瀑布。那個陽台有時會很滑。哈，呵呵，呵。」

Introduction: Me family lives pretty far apart from each other.  We're all 'ermits, you know.
序章: 「我們一家住得很散。我們都是隱士。」

Introduction: Fenrus is Erasmus' familiar or is that the other way around?
序章: 「芬魯斯是伊拉斯謨的助手，還是說反過來啊？」

Introduction: Erasmus' favorite game is the Mage's Maze.  'Ave to be a magic user to play it, though.  Erasmus is awful good at it.
序章: 「伊拉斯謨最喜歡的遊戲是法師迷宮。雖然只有法師才能玩。伊拉斯謨特別擅長。」

Introduction: An 'ermit is one wot lives alone, far away from them wot talks too much or asks too many questions.  'Ermits are shy, quiet types wot don't say much.
序章: 「隱士就是獨自生活，遠離那些話太多或問題太多的傢伙。隱士是害羞、安靜、話不多的人。」

Introduction: Me brother 'Arry 'ardly says a word in a year.  Me sister 'Ortense 'asn't spoken since she were six.  I never talks at all.  Yes, we 'ermits nose how to keep a mouth shut.
序章: 「我哥哥哈瑞一年幾乎說不了一句話。我妹妹霍頓絲從六歲起就沒說過話。我根本不開口。是啊，我們隱士知道怎麼守口如瓶。」

Introduction: I nose sum magic.  Erasmus the wizard tot me sum spells.
序章: 「我懂一些魔法。法師伊拉斯謨教了我一些法術。」

Introduction: He's wot put the spell on me ladder sos the brigands don't get up.  Them wot climbs th' rocks gets a door opening in their nose.
序章: 「他就是在我的梯子上施法，讓山賊爬不上去。那些爬岩石的傢伙會被門打在鼻子上。」

Introduction: A magic mirror of reflection, it was.  If you use it, when a nasty spell was cast at you, it was wot sent it back at the one wot cast it.  Do un to others, I sez.  Heh, he, he.
序章: 「那是一面魔法反射鏡。要是有人對你施壞法術，它會把法術反射回施術者。就是以其人之身還治其人之身。哈，呵呵，呵。」

Introduction: I'm 'Enry.  'Enry the eighth I is.  Me farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry...
序章: 「我是亨利。我是第八個亨利。我爸爸是亨利，他爸爸是亨利，他爸爸是亨利，他爸爸是亨利…」

Introduction: ... and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry and 'is farther was an 'Enry.  And every one was an 'ermit.
序章: 「…他爸爸是亨利，他爸爸是亨利，他爸爸是亨利。每一個都是隱士。」

Introduction: Yes, I still have me magic scroll, but you didn't seem to want it. 'Ave you changed your mind?
序章: 「是啊，我還有我的魔法捲軸，但你似乎不想要。你改主意了嗎？」

Introduction: You already 'ave me only scroll.
序章: 「你已經有我唯一的捲軸了。」

Introduction: If you be wanting to learn the spell, I'll be pleased to share it to you. It's just getting molding in me 'iding place.  Do you want it?
序章: 「你要是想學法術，我樂意和你分享。它在我的躲藏地方發霉呢。」

Introduction: Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there.  You're welcome to stay if you share some rations and play some cribbage.
序章: 「我的客用床是那邊角落的稻草。要是你願意分享口糧和我玩幾盤牌戲，歡迎你留下。」

Introduction: The spells wot I nose is pretty simple maybe, but I like 'em.
序章: 「我懂的法術或許很簡單，但我喜歡。」

Introduction: I can make me ladder show up and me door close and even get rid o' pests using the trigger spell, you see.
序章: 「我可以用觸發術讓梯子出現，讓門關上，甚至除掉害蟲，你看。」

Introduction: It's the spell wot sets off uther spells.
序章: 「那是用來啟動其他法術的法術。」

Introduction: Like when you wants to see the ladder, you casts a trigger and there you 'ave it, large as life.
序章: 「比如說，你想看梯子時，你就施一個觸發術，嘿，梯子就在眼前。」

Introduction: Course, you gots to 'ave a spell on the invisible ladder to make it visible first.  But it 'as its uses.
序章: 「當然，你得先對隱形的梯子施法讓它現身。但它確實有用。」

Introduction: I thinks I 'ave a scroll with the spell, if you be knowing sum magic, that is.
序章: 「我想我有個捲軸裡有法術，假如你懂點魔法的話。」

Introduction: E's not so bad.  Got a good sense o' 'umor, 'e 'as.  I get the giggles just to think of 'im.  He heh he heh he.
序章: 「他還不錯。很有幽默感。我一想到他就咯咯笑。哈哈哈哈哈。」

Introduction: 'E's come by at times to chat.  Borrowed the mirror wot I borrowed from Erasmus 'e did.
序章: 「他有時會來聊天。他借走了我從伊拉斯謨那兒借來的鏡子。」

Introduction: You seem to have made the 'ermit un'appy.
序章: 看來你惹得這隱士很不開心。

Introduction: You see the shaggiest little man (you think it's a man, anyway) you've ever seen.
序章: 你看到一個毛髮蓬亂的小個子（你想應該是個男人啦）。

Introduction: 'Enry is wide awake, and that makes it impossible for you to sleep.
序章: 亨利毫無睡意，這讓你根本睡不著。
