Introduction: Me guest bed is the straw in the corner over there.  You're welcome to stay the night since you've shared your rations.  When you want to sleep, just say so.
序章: 「我的客用床是那邊角落的稻草。既然你分享了口糧，歡迎你留下睡一晚。想睡的時候就說一聲。」

Introduction: You can't share what you don't have.
序章: 你沒有的東西無法分享。

Introduction: You can't sleep in this old rickety chair.  Get up and sleep on the bed of straw.
序章: 你沒法在這把老舊搖搖晃晃的椅子上睡覺。起來睡稻草床吧。

Introduction: Cave
序章: 洞穴

Introduction: Cribbage
序章: 牌戲

Introduction: Falls
序章: 瀑布

Introduction: Family
序章: 家族

Introduction: Games
序章: 遊戲

Introduction: Ladder
序章: 梯子

Introduction: Mirror
序章: 鏡子

Introduction: Scroll
序章: 捲軸

Introduction: Sleep
序章: 睡眠

Introduction: It's a round chair.
序章: 這是張圓椅子。

Introduction: It's a relic from India, a snake charmer's basket.
序章: 這是來自印度的古物，印度吹蛇者的籃子。

Introduction: Every 'ermit ought to 'ave a few urns around, sez I.
序章: 「每個隱士都應該放些罈子在周圍，我是這麼說的。」

Introduction: It's an ancient Egyptian pot from the tomb of Anon-Ha.
序章: 這是來自古埃及安農—哈墓穴的陶罐。

Introduction: A place to keep grain for 'ermit's ale.
序章: 用來存放隱士啤酒穀物的地方。

Introduction: 'Enry always keeps a good supply of coal on hand.
序章: 亨利總是備著充足的煤炭。

Introduction: What's left of 'Enry's dinner.
序章: 亨利晚餐的剩菜。

Introduction: The remains of a Navaho ceremonial bridal pot.
序章: 納瓦霍婚禮儀式陶罐的殘骸。

Introduction: Do you want it?
序章: 「你要嗎？」

Introduction: Ello.  'Ow are you?  'Ave we met before?
序章: 「嗨。你好嗎？我們見過面嗎？」

Introduction: Come in, come in.  Good to see you again.  Get's kind o' tiresome sitten here an' thinken by meself all the time.
序章: 「進來進來。很高興又見到你。一個人坐在這裡想東想西有點煩人。」

Introduction: Let's see.  'Ow does Erasmus do it?  Oh, yes.
序章: 「讓我想想。伊拉斯謨怎麼做的？喔，對了。」

Introduction: Unfortunately, since the fungus on the walls shines at night so the room doesn't get dark, and the fact the hermit talks most of the night, you only get five hours rest.
序章: 很遺憾，由於牆上的真菌在夜間發光讓房間保持明亮，加上隱士幾乎整晚都在講話，你只睡了5小時。

Introduction: After having traded a ration, which the hermit promptly swallowed, and spending a rather boring hour of cribbage and listening to 'Enry chatter, you hit the hay.
序章: 交換了一份口糧（隱士立刻吞下肚），花了無聊的一小時玩牌戲和聽亨利嘮叨後，你就上床睡覺了。

Introduction: You already 'ave me only scroll.
序章: 「你已經有我唯一的捲軸了。」

Introduction: If you be ever needen a place to stay the night and you're in the area, I could be willen to put you up for the night for sum rations and a couple o' games o' cribbage.
序章: 「要是你以後需要地方睡覺又在這一帶，我樂意為了幾份口糧和幾盤牌戲讓你睡一晚。」

Introduction: Oh well, maybe sumone else can use it sumtime.
序章: 「嗯，或許別人以後用得上。」

Introduction: You can't sleep here, you got's no rations to give me.
序章: 「你沒法睡這裡，你沒有口糧給我。」

Introduction: I think you be 'avin too little magic for it to be any use to you.
序章: 「我想你的魔法太弱，對你沒用。」

Introduction: I'm 'Enry the 'ermit, that's me.  Me farther was an 'ermit and me murther was an 'ermit so's I come by me job rightly.
序章: 「我就是亨利隱士，沒錯。我爸爸是隱士，我媽媽也是隱士，所以我是家傳。」

Introduction: Don't 'ave too many visitors.  'Ermits don't, you know.  (Part o' the job description.)  I likes to see a new face, though.
序章: 「沒太多訪客。隱士就是不會有很多訪客，你知道。（那是職業的一部分。）但我還是喜歡看到新面孔。」

Introduction: Good to 'ear anurther's speaking besides meself.  Sos wot can I do for you?
序章: 「很高興除了自己還能聽到別人說話。那麼我能為你做什麼？」

Introduction: 'E summons it with a Trigger.  'Ere goes!
序章: 「他用觸發術召喚它。來吧！」

Introduction: There you 'as it.
序章: 「看吧，就這樣。」

Introduction: You can detect a faint aura of magic here.
序章: 你能感受到這裡有微弱的魔法光暈。

Introduction: You should wait until the hermit is asleep to check his place out.
序章: 你應該等隱士睡著才去查看他的地方。

Introduction: The cave is cold and clammy and it smells like mildew and wet dog hair in here!  The walls are covered with phosphorescent fungus.  The fungus glows eerily in the cave's dim light.
序章: 洞穴又冷又潮濕，聞起來像黴味和濕狗毛！牆壁上長滿了發光的真菌。真菌在洞穴的微弱光線中詭異地發光。

Introduction: There is no need to use magic to open anything here.
序章: 這裡沒有什麼需要魔法才能打開的。

Introduction: Try sleeping in the bed or on the floor -- this chair just doesn't make it.
序章: 試試睡在床上或地板上—這把椅子實在不行。

Introduction: The scroll vanishes even as you read the magical runes upon it.  You now have the knowledge to cast a "Trigger" spell.
序章: 當你讀著上面的魔法符文時，捲軸消失了。你現在掌握了施展「觸發術」的知識。

Introduction: It's on the table.
序章: 它在桌上。

Introduction: I don't 'ave much, but wot I 'ave, I 'ave, an' t'isn't for sale.
序章: 「我沒什麼，但我有的，我就有，不賣。」

Introduction: It's the headpiece of 'Enry's staff.
序章: 這是亨利魔杖的頂端。

Introduction: The straw is relatively fresh and looks more comfortable to lie upon than the rocks.
序章: 稻草相對新鮮，看起來比石頭舒服多了。

Introduction: Yes, what you see is a table.
序章: 是的，你看到的就是桌子。

Introduction: The hermit has all the amenities, including running water!
序章: 隱士擁有所有設施，包括流動的水！

Introduction: That's funny.  It bounced right off.
序章: 真有趣。它直接彈開了。

Introduction: You bravely attack the bouncing beast with your bare hands.
序章: 你勇敢地用赤手空拳攻擊那隻彈跳的怪獸。

Introduction: The only Antwerp known in these parts has split... into parts.
序章: 這一帶唯一的安特衛普已經分裂…成碎片了。

Introduction: The Antwerp seems to have flown the coop.
序章: 安特衛普似乎逃脫了。

Introduction: By golly, it's an Antwerp!  Antwerps are on the endangered species list. They are rarely seen.
序章: 天哪，這是安特衛普！安特衛普是瀕危物種。很少見到。

Introduction: They look a lot like bushes.
序章: 它們看起來很像灌木。

Introduction: Some shrubbery, perhaps?
序章: 也許是什麼灌木叢？

Introduction: You're bushed.  No, wait, *they're* bushes.
序章: 你累了。不對，等等，*它們*才是灌木。

Introduction: The rocks were left here by a receding glacier.
序章: 這些岩石是退縮的冰河留下的。

Introduction: You find a keyhole concealed in a crack in the rock.
序章: 你在岩石裂縫中發現了隱藏的鑰孔。

Introduction: There is a narrow cave entrance among the rocks.  Inside, you see a dark passage through the hillside.
序章: 岩石之間有個狹窄的洞穴入口。裡面你看到一條穿過山坡的黑暗通道。

Introduction: The Antwerp seems to have flown the coop.
序章: 安特衛普似乎逃脫了。

Introduction: The grass is luscious, just the thing for hungry herbivores.
序章: 草很茂盛，正是饑餓的草食動物喜歡的。

Introduction: The way is impassable.  Sheer rock cliffs rise to serious heights.
序章: 無法通行。陡峭的岩石懸崖高聳到驚人的高度。

Introduction: This is an especially rocky area.
序章: 這是個特別多岩石的區域。

Introduction: Antwerps have too bouncy a nature to ever be truly calm.
序章: 安特衛普天性太愛彈跳，永遠無法真正平靜。

Introduction: Antwerps aren't dazzled easily.
序章: 安特衛普不容易被眩目。

Introduction: You seem to have scared the Antwerp with your behavior.
序章: 你的行為似乎嚇到了安特衛普。

Introduction: Cockily, you loosen up to fight.
序章: 你傲慢地放鬆身體準備打鬥。

Introduction: Confident and loose, you approach the Antwerp.
序章: 你自信而放鬆地走近安特衛普。

Introduction: Holy Mackerel!
序章: 「天哪！」

Introduction: Your spell has unlocked the lock on the rock, but it is not yet powerful enough to open the rock door.
序章: 你的法術打開了岩石上的鎖，但還不夠強大到打開石門。

Introduction: Now that the big Antwerp has split, this corner of the forest seems strangely quiet.
序章: 現在大安特衛普已經分裂，森林的這個角落變得奇異地安靜。

Introduction: You look around and see rocks and grass, but no Antwerp.
序章: 你環顧四周，看到岩石和草，但沒有安特衛普。

Introduction: You see rocks, grass and an Antwerp.
序章: 你看到岩石、草和一隻安特衛普。

Introduction: The forest extends to the east and north.
序章: 森林向東和向北延伸。

Introduction: It's a rock, not a charcoal briquette!
序章: 這是塊岩石，不是木炭！

Introduction: Despite your mightiest efforts, the rock does not move.
序章: 儘管你盡力了，岩石還是沒有動。

Introduction: The rock door has already been opened.
序章: 石門已經打開了。

Introduction: You are not strong enough yet to open the rock door.
序章: 你還不夠強大無法打開石門。

Introduction: The key glows for an instant as you insert it into the lock, but nothing else happens.
序章: 當你把鑰匙插入鎖孔時，鑰匙閃了一下，但沒有其他事發生。

Introduction: The lock in the rock clicks open.
序章: 岩石上的鎖發出喀噠一聲打開了。

Introduction: The lock is beyond your present skill.  It might help if you had the key.
序章: 這把鎖超出你現在的能力。有鑰匙的話可能會有幫助。

Introduction: You'd have a much easier time of this if you had the key.
序章: 有鑰匙的話會容易得多。

Introduction: Ah, got it!  The lock in the rock clicks open.
序章: 啊，成功了！岩石上的鎖發出喀噠一聲打開了。

Introduction: ... Or at least a better set of tools.
序章: …或至少一套更好的工具。

Introduction: You don't see any locks nearby.
序章: 你附近看不到任何鎖。

Introduction: The rocks were left here by some receding glacier.
序章: 這些岩石是被某座退縮的冰河遺留下來的。

Introduction: There is a narrow cave entrance among the rocks.
序章: 岩石中有一個狹窄的洞穴入口。

Introduction: Ok, you say "Hiden Goseke".  Or was that "Hide 'n' go seek"?
序章: 好吧，你說「海登·葛賽克」。或者那是「躲貓貓」？

Introduction: You hear the sound of someone... or something... moving deeper into the cave to let you pass.
序章: 你聽到某人……或某物……在洞穴深處移動的聲音，似乎是要讓開給你通過。

Introduction: These rocks seem rather stiff.  Maybe they're just not in a talkative mood.
序章: 這些岩石似乎相當固執。也許它們只是不想說話。

Introduction: It's a magnificent tree, isn't it?
序章: 這是一棵宏偉的樹，不是嗎？

Introduction: The bouncing baby Antwerps are all so cute, you can't bring yourself to interfere with their playing.
序章: 跳躍著的小安特衛普都很可愛，你捨不得打擾它們玩耍。

Introduction: Too late!
序章: 太遲了！

Introduction: You're not going north through these bushes either.
序章: 你也不是要往北穿過這些灌木。

Introduction: You're not going north through those bushes.
序章: 你不是要往北穿過那些灌木。

Introduction: Look at the bushes on the north.  Ditto south.
序章: 看看北邊的灌木。南邊亦然。

Introduction: There!  Surely you saw that bush move?  You didn't?  You say there wasn't a path north that bush just blocked?  Wait!  You must be in with the bushes! You'd better get out of here!  But not to the north; not through the bushes of terror!
序章: 在那裡！你肯定看到那灌木動了？沒有？你說往北沒有通路，那灌木只是擋住了？等等！你一定是和灌木混在一起了！你最好快點滾出去！但不是往北；不要穿過恐怖灌木！

Introduction: That bush!  Did it move?  Are the bushes hemming you in?
序章: 那灌木！它動了嗎？灌木是不是在困住你？

Introduction: A slumbering sawn log.  Or at least a dead tree.
序章: 一根沉睡的鋸過的原木。或至少是一棵死樹。

Introduction: You see a lovely waterfall in the distance, feeding the lake with ice cold mountain water.
序章: 你看到遠處有一座美麗的瀑布，用冰冷的山泉餵養著湖泊。

Introduction: Delphineus is looking for a hero to help him save Eluria.
序章: 德爾菲尼烏斯在尋找英雄幫他拯救伊琉瑞亞。

Introduction: Have you seen a little boy named Adam?  I can't seem to find him around here, asks the dolphin.  Then he realizes he's in the wrong game, and swims back to Ecoquest.
序章: 「你有沒有看到一個叫亞當的小男孩？我似乎在這附近找不到他，」海豚問道。然後他意識到自己進錯了遊戲，游回生態任務。

Introduction: To the North, on the cliff, you see a cave.
序章: 在北邊，懸崖上，你看到一個洞穴。

Introduction: It must be your imagination.  What would Nessie be doing in this fantasy adventure?  Shades of "Conquests of the Longbow"!
序章: 那一定是你的想像。尼西為什麼會在這個奇幻冒險中出現？真像「征服長弓」的影子！

Introduction: I'm lost!  Could you please direct me to Scotland?
序章: 「我迷路了！你能指路我去蘇格蘭嗎？」

Introduction: You see your own reflection in the crystal-clear waters.
序章: 你在清澈透明的水中看到自己的倒影。

Introduction: The lake water tastes pretty good, if a bit flat.
序章: 湖水嚐起來相當不錯，只是有點沒味道。

Introduction: ... but this is not the water you want.
序章: ……但這不是你要的水。

Introduction: You fill a flask with the clear lake water.
序章: 你用清澈的湖水裝滿一個瓶子。

Introduction: You don't want to do that.  A walking pace is more appropriate for enjoying the majestic beauty of this scene.
序章: 你不想那樣做。漫步的步調更適合欣賞這場景的雄偉之美。

Introduction: You see a lovely waterfall in the distance, feeding the lake with ice cold mountain water.  The runoff river from the waterfalls forms this scenic lake.
序章: 你看到遠處有一座美麗的瀑布，用冰冷的山泉餵養著湖泊。瀑布的徑流河形成了這個風景如畫的湖泊。

Introduction: The rocky shore is blissfully relaxing.
序章: 石質海岸令人幸福地放鬆。

Introduction: You've been walking through the forest this long and you can't even recognize a tree???
序章: 你都走了這麼久的森林了，連一棵樹都認不出來？

Introduction: That has no effect on the water, try something else.
序章: 那對水沒有效果，試試別的。

Introduction: It's the beautiful Spiegelsee, or "Mirror Lake".  You pause for some peaceful reflection.
序章: 這是美麗的史畢格爾湖，或稱「鏡子湖」。你停下來進行一些平靜的反思。

Introduction: Swimming in the Spiegelsee is a bad idea.  The lake is fed from mountain run-off, and the water is ice cold.
序章: 在史畢格爾湖中游泳是個壞主意。這個湖由山區徑流餵養，水冰冷刺骨。

Introduction: The fungus is slimy and stuck tight to the cave walls.
序章: 真菌又滑膩，緊緊粘著洞穴的牆壁。

Introduction: Water lies in stagnant pools.
序章: 水積聚在停滯的池子裡。

Introduction: You have lost your sense of direction, as the cave passage twists around.
序章: 當洞穴通道蜿蜒時，你已經失去了方向感。

Introduction: The only entrance to this cave is the one you came through.
序章: 這個洞穴的唯一入口就是你進來的那個。

Introduction: No one answers.
序章: 沒人回答。

Introduction: You can watch the water ooze down the sides of the stalactites and drip to the ground.
序章: 你可以看著水從鐘乳石的側面滲出，滴到地面。

Introduction: All you hear is a distant "Echo".
序章: 你聽到的只是遠方的「回音」。

Introduction: You can watch the water drop down from the stalactites and ooze down the sides of the stalagmites.
序章: 你可以看著水從鐘乳石滴下，從石筍的側面滲下。

Introduction: The rocks look slick and are slimy to the touch.
序章: 岩石看起來光滑且摸起來有粘液。

Introduction: The light from the fungus is eerie and vaguely unpleasant.
序章: 真菌發出的光線詭異且隱隱令人不快。

Introduction: As your eyes adjust to the darkness, you see by the light of the fungus that this passageway twists its way through the mountain.  It is cold in here, and you can smell stagnant water and faint hints of decay.
序章: 當你的眼睛適應黑暗時，你借著真菌的光線看到這個通道蜿蜒穿過山脈。這裡很冷，你可以聞到停滯的水和衰爛的淡淡氣味。

Introduction: You hear the constant "drip... drip..." of water.
序章: 你聽到水的不斷「滴……滴……」聲。

Introduction: That spell is useless here.
序章: 那個法術在這裡沒有用。

Introduction: There is nothing here to calm.
序章: 這裡沒有什麼要平靜的。

Introduction: The Troll looks as though he will now calmly tear you to shreds.
序章: 巨魔看起來彷彿現在要平靜地把你撕成碎片。

Introduction: There is nothing here to dazzle.
序章: 這裡沒有什麼要眩目的。

Introduction: You'd think a Cave Troll would make a perfect Dazzle victim, wouldn't you? Unfortunately, this one was blind to start with, and he can still smell you!
序章: 你會認為洞穴巨魔是眩目術的完美受害者，不是嗎？不幸的是，這隻一開始就是瞎子，它仍然能聞到你！

Introduction: There is no magic in this cave.
序章: 這個洞穴裡沒有魔法。

Introduction: There is nothing here to use it on.
序章: 這裡沒有什麼東西可以用它來對付。

Introduction: Oops, it's one of those flame-resistant Trolls you've been warned about.
序章: 哎呀，這是那些你被警告過的火焰抗性巨魔之一。

Introduction: Before you can react, the Troll is upon you, and the encounter begins.
序章: 還沒等你反應，巨魔就撲向你，戰鬥開始了。

Introduction: You shouldn't have done that!  Now he's mad!
序章: 你不應該那樣做！現在他發怒了！

Introduction: There's nothing else worth taking from the Troll.
序章: 巨魔身上沒有什麼值得拿的了。

Introduction: The dead Troll's huge club is much too heavy for you to lift.
序章: 死去的巨魔的巨大棍棒對你來說太重了，抬不起來。

Introduction: You find nothing on the Troll.
序章: 你在巨魔身上找不到任何東西。

Introduction: You find nothing on the Troll but a little hair.
序章: 你在巨魔身上只找到一點毛髮。

Introduction: You find no more beard hair on the dead Troll.
序章: 你在死巨魔身上找不到更多的鬍鬚。

Introduction: You cut off the Troll's beard and put it away.
序章: 你剪下巨魔的鬍鬚並收了起來。

Introduction: A dead Troll lies in a pool of blue blood.
序章: 一隻死巨魔躺在藍色血液的水池中。

Introduction: The Troll lies dying upon the slimy floor of the cave.
序章: 巨魔躺在洞穴粘滑的地板上奄奄一息。

Introduction: You see no such creature here.
序章: 你在這裡看不到這樣的生物。

Introduction: A mean-looking Troll looms before you, a snarl on his lips.
序章: 一隻看起來兇惡的巨魔聳立在你面前，嘴角帶著咆哮。

Introduction: The Troll would just as soon eat you as talk to you.
序章: 巨魔寧可吃掉你也不想和你說話。

Introduction: A brigand archer!  From what you can see, you realize that you are in a very dangerous situation.
序章: 一個山賊弓箭手！從你能看到的，你意識到自己處於非常危險的局面。

Introduction: Yet another brigand archer!  And this one is wearing his archery merit badge!
序章: 又是一個山賊弓箭手！這一個還戴著他的射擊功績勳章！

Introduction: One bad dude!
序章: 一個狠角色！

Introduction: Yet another brigand archer!  Don't these guys know overkill when they see it?
序章: 又是一個山賊弓箭手！這些傢伙不知道什麼叫過度殺傷嗎？

Introduction: Yet another brigand archer!  If these guys shoot at all well, you soon will be impeccably dressed in your arrow shirt!
序章: 又是一個山賊弓箭手！如果這些傢伙射得相當不錯，你很快就會被完美地穿上箭衣！

Introduction: It's a nasty, pointy, barbed arrow.
序章: 這是一根討厭的、尖刺的、倒鉤的箭。

Introduction: You'd better keep your head up.
序章: 你最好抬起頭來。

Introduction: You have a bad feeling about this place.
序章: 你對這個地方有不好的感覺。

Introduction: You have an even worse feeling about this place.
序章: 你對這個地方有更糟糕的感覺。

Introduction: You have a splintering headache that's doing you in.
序章: 你有一個碎裂的頭痛，正在要了你的命。

Introduction: That's one way of collecting arrows.  Shame you don't have a bow.
序章: 那是收集箭的一種方法。可惜你沒有弓。

Introduction: The cliffs are rocky and riddled with niches that could conceal things. Brigand archers for instance.  There is a pile of logs off to the northwest.
序章: 懸崖是岩石質的，佈滿了可能隱藏東西的壁龕。例如山賊弓箭手。西北方有一堆原木。

Introduction: A brigand ground fighter!
序章: 一個山賊地面戰士！

Introduction: A Zulu warrior?  (Perhaps a refugee from Quest for Glory 3:  The Wages of War?)
序章: 祖魯戰士？(也許是來自「英雄傳奇3：戰爭報酬」的難民？)

Introduction: You may have killed that brigand, but there are five others pelting you with arrows.
序章: 你可能殺死了那個山賊，但還有五個在用箭襲擊你。

Introduction: It looks like the brigands have prepared a welcoming committee for you behind the logs.
序章: 看起來山賊在木頭後面為你準備了一個歡迎委員會。

Introduction: You'll have to get closer to the logs, defying withering arrow fire and death, before you can climb over them.
序章: 在你能爬過木頭之前，你必須更靠近木頭，無視猛烈的箭火和死亡。

Introduction: The rock walls look steep, and archers are perched on their summits.
序章: 岩牆看起來很陡峭，弓箭手棲息在其頂部。

Introduction: They'll pick you off before you get half-way up there!
序章: 在你爬到一半之前，他們會把你挑下來！

Introduction: A warning bell to alert all the brigands in the fortress.
序章: 用來警報要塞中所有山賊的警鐘。

Introduction: The bush sits next to the cliff.  There are marks on the ground near the bush.
序章: 灌木坐在懸崖旁邊。灌木附近的地面上有痕跡。

Introduction: You see a bush that conceals the secret entrance.
序章: 你看到一叢灌木隱蔽著秘密入口。

Introduction: The sign reads "Ring bell".
序章: 標誌上寫著「搖鐘」。

Introduction: Step over and get a close look.
序章: 踏過去仔細看。

Introduction: Why knock?  Use the bell.
序章: 為什麼要敲門？用鐘。

Introduction: The gate appears to be fastened somehow on the inside.  You'll have to force the gate open.
序章: 大門似乎以某種方式從內側被固定。你必須強行打開大門。

Introduction: Your lock picking skills are no match for the gate lock.
序章: 你的開鎖技巧無法對抗大門的鎖。

Introduction: The fortress gate -- a very *strong* gate.  It appears to be fastened somehow on the other side.
序章: 要塞大門──一扇非常*強大的*大門。它似乎以某種方式從另一側被固定。

Introduction: Beyond the open gate, you can see a storage area and some buildings.
序章: 打開的大門外，你可以看到一個儲存區和一些建築物。

Introduction: Even using your thief toolkit, you have no hope of picking this lock.  (The bar on the other side may have something to do with that.)
序章: 即使使用你的盜賊工具包，你也無法撬開這個鎖。(另一側的門栓可能與此有關。)

Introduction: OK.  Here's your chance.
序章: 好的。這是你的機會。

Introduction: Why bother?  He's out for the count.
序章: 為什麼還要折騰？他已經昏迷了。

Introduction: That's not a good idea.
序章: 那不是個好主意。

Introduction: You've got all the loot you're going to find.
序章: 你已經得到了你會找到的所有戰利品。

Introduction: You quickly search the fallen Minotaur and find nothing.
序章: 你迅速搜索倒下的牛頭人怪，什麼都沒找到。

Introduction: Wait a minute.
序章: 等一下。

Introduction: The Minotaur's flail looks peculiar.  You pick it up and examine it closely.
序章: 牛頭人怪的連枷看起來很奇怪。你把它撿起來仔細檢查。

Introduction: Cleverly concealed in the flail are 50 silvers.
序章: 連枷中巧妙地隱藏著 50 枚銀幣。

Introduction: A just reward for defeating such a valiant fighter, you tell yourself as you pocket the silvers.
序章: 「打敗這麼一位勇敢的戰士，真是恰當的報酬，」當你把銀幣放進口袋時對自己說。

Introduction: As you attempt the search, the sleeping Minotaur awakes, and...
序章: 當你試圖搜索時，沉睡的牛頭人怪醒了，而且……

Introduction: You'll have to give yourself room.
序章: 你必須給自己讓出空間。

Introduction: It's a Minotaur, half man, half bull.
序章: 這是一隻牛頭人怪，半人半牛。

Introduction: If you've seen one, you've seen them all.
序章: 如果你見過一隻，就見過它們全部。

Introduction: Good ears, but very poor peripheral vision.
序章: 耳朵不錯，但周邊視力很差。

Introduction: Before you can complete your spell, the Minotaur's upon you!
序章: 在你完成咒語之前，牛頭人怪就撲向你了！

Introduction: O.K.  Here's your chance.
序章: 好的。這是你的機會。

Introduction: You don't have enough experience to climb the wall.
序章: 你的經驗不足以爬牆。

Introduction: It's a loose pile of rocks and debris.
序章: 這是一堆鬆散的岩石和碎片。

Introduction: There is no good climbing place on the rock cliff to the west.
序章: 西邊的岩石懸崖上沒有好的攀爬地點。

Introduction: The cliff seems very hard to climb.
序章: 懸崖似乎很難攀爬。

Introduction: You can see marks in the ground, leading towards the bush.
序章: 你可以看到地面上的痕跡，指向灌木。

Introduction: You're in far too dangerous a place to sit down.
序章: 你所在的地方太危險了，無法坐下。

Introduction: It's just an old-time rock (won't roll).
序章: 這只是一塊老式岩石(不會滾動)。

Introduction: You can't climb the rock.
序章: 你無法攀爬岩石。

Introduction: The rocks look to be good hiding places.
序章: 岩石看起來是很好的藏身之處。

Introduction: This seems like a good place to climb, but you aren't skillful enough to manage it.
序章: 這似乎是一個很好的攀爬地點，但你的技巧不夠好。

Introduction: You're going to have to get a lot better at climbing to get into the fortress.
序章: 你必須大幅提高攀爬技巧才能進入要塞。

Introduction: Brigands's fortress.
序章: 山賊的要塞。

Introduction: You find a good purchase on the rocks to climb.
序章: 你在岩石上找到了一個很好的立足點來攀爬。

Introduction: The dirt is too loose for climbing here.
序章: 這裡的泥土太鬆散了，無法攀爬。

Introduction: Grumble Grumble Grumble.  Stupid guard duty, third time this week.  Why always me?  Grumble Grumble Grumble.
序章: 「碎碎念碎碎念碎碎念。該死的衛兵職責，本週第三次。為什麼總是我？碎碎念碎碎念碎碎念。」

Introduction: Well, I'll huff, and I'll puff, and I'll smash the gate down, you decide.
序章: 「好吧，我要吸氣，我要呼氣，我要砸爛大門，」你決定。

Introduction: Boy, that felt good.
序章: 「天哪，那感覺真好。」

Introduction: I'M BAAD!
序章: 「我是個壞傢伙！」

Introduction: I've been out here too long.  My imagination is taking over.
序章: 「我在這裡待太久了。我的想像力開始掌控了。」

Introduction: What was that noise?
序章: 「那是什麼聲音？」

Introduction: Must be those Brigands playing games.
序章: 「一定是那些山賊在玩把戲。」

Introduction: I guess I'm just getting jumpy.
序章: 「我想我只是有點緊張罷了。」

Introduction: Is anyone there?
序章: 「有人在嗎？」

Introduction: This place is spooky.
序章: 「這地方陰森森的。」

Introduction: Before you can escape, the Minotaur is upon you!
序章: 還沒等你逃開，牛頭人已經撲向你！

Introduction: You hear a snick as the hasp on the gate is opened.
序章: 你聽到一聲輕響，大門的門栓被打開了。

Introduction: DING DING DING DING....  Well, maybe the brigands aren't such inhospitable creatures after all.
序章: 「叮叮叮叮……」好吧，也許山賊也不是那麼不好客嘛。

Introduction: Then again, maybe they are.
序章: 不過話說回來，說不定他們是。

Introduction: There is no magical aura present.
序章: 偵測不到任何魔法氣息。

Introduction: Sheer cliff walls form a narrow box canyon where the brigands have built a fortress.  There is a pile of debris where the steep cliff walls meet the fortress.
序章: 陡峭的懸崖圍成狹窄的盒狀峽谷，山賊在此建造了堡壘。陡峭的懸崖與堡壘交會處有堆積的瓦礫。

Introduction: You see a large Minotaur patrolling the canyon.
序章: 你看到一隻碩大的牛頭人在峽谷巡邏。

Introduction: To the Minotaur, you look like a nice meal.
序章: 對牛頭人來說，你就像一頓美餐。

Introduction: His aim in life is to make shish kebab.  I hope you're not skewered.
序章: 他的人生目標是烤肉串。希望你別被串成烤肉。

Introduction: Life as an archer is never dull nor pointless.
序章: 弓箭手的日子既不沉悶也不無聊。

Introduction: He has an arrow with your name on it.
序章: 他手上有支刻著你名字的箭。

Introduction: He has an IQ so low he can't spell IQ.
序章: 他的智商低到連「智商」的英文都拼不出來。

Introduction: You grin sheepishly; the door has no lock, and swings open at your touch.
序章: 你尷尬地笑了；門上沒有鎖，輕輕一推就開了。

Introduction: If that ISN'T the safe way out of here, you are probably going to become an ex-hero.
序章: 如果這不是逃出去的安全方式，你大概會成為「前任英雄」。

Introduction: It looks like a plain, simple, harmless box to you.
序章: 在你眼裡，這就是個普通、簡單、人畜無害的盒子。

Introduction: Your life is in grave danger and you want to play with toys?  What kind of hero are you?
序章: 你的性命正在危險中，還想玩玩具？你算什麼英雄？

Introduction: Your life is in grave danger and you want to play with toys? What kind of hero are you?
序章: 你的性命正在危險中，還想玩玩具？你算什麼英雄？

Introduction: The chasm is too wide to jump and too deep to climb.  There seem to be a number of porcupines at the bottom.
序章: 裂谷太寬無法跳過，太深無法爬下。底部似乎有不少豪豬。

Introduction: Empty wine barrels.  The winters here are very cold.
序章: 空酒桶。這裡的冬天非常寒冷。

Introduction: It's a fortification for archers to stand behind.
序章: 這是弓箭手用來躲藏的防禦工事。

Introduction: They're too heavy to carry.
序章: 它們太重，搬不動。

Introduction: Sacks of grain.  Stored for the winter.
序章: 一袋袋穀物。為冬天儲存的。

Introduction: You can't see through the windows from here.
序章: 你從這裡看不穿窗戶。

Introduction: You see a group of brigands playing cards.  One looks back at you in surprise and starts yelling.
序章: 你看到一群山賊在打牌。其中一個驚訝地轉身看著你，開始大喊。

Introduction: All that can be seen through the window are vague shapes and outlines.
序章: 從窗戶透過去只能看到模糊的身影和輪廓。

Introduction: Hey!  Even the most rudimentary hero knows that the way to deal with a tripwire is to step over it, not cut it.  Now that you now it's there, you'll avoid it.
序章: 嘿！連最初級的英雄都知道對付絆絲的辦法是跨過去，不是剪斷它。既然你現在知道它在那裡，你就可以閃避它。

Introduction: You feel a trip wire.  Why, those sneaky brigands!  Now that you know it's there, you can easily step over it.
序章: 你摸到了一根絆絲。那些狡猾的山賊！既然你知道它在那裡，就可以輕鬆跨過去。

Introduction: There is a dark rope stretched tautly across the path.  Now that you know it's there, you can easily avoid it by stepping over it.
序章: 一根深色繩子繃得緊緊地橫在路上。既然你現在知道它在那裡，就可以輕鬆地跨過去避開它。

Introduction: You hear a snick as the hasp on the Jack in the Box is released.
序章: 你聽到跳跳盒上的門栓被打開時發出的輕響。

Introduction: That was a useless gesture.
序章: 那是個無用的動作。

Introduction: None of the brigands notices your Calm spell.  There is too much negative thought energy here.
序章: 沒有山賊注意到你的鎮定術。這裡的負面思想能量太強了。

Introduction: No spells are active here.
序章: 這裡沒有任何法術生效。

Introduction: This is not what you expected the brigands' fortress to look like in your imagination.  The barracks with the open half-doors to the east and west are fairly normal looking.
序章: 這和你想像中山賊要塞的樣子完全不同。東西兩側有著半開門的兵營看起來相當正常。

Introduction: The carpet lying near the gate, however, is a bit odd. The small chasm in the middle of this yard with the bridge across it seems strange, too.  There are two blockades near the gate and some barriers near the north.
序章: 不過，靠近大門的地毯看起來有點古怪。院子中央那條有橋跨越的小裂谷也挺奇怪的。大門附近有兩道障礙，北邊也有些屏障。

Introduction: That was a useless spell.
序章: 那是個無用的法術。

Introduction: The rug is too big to put into your backpack and too heavy to carry around. You do notice a pit trap under the spread-out carpet.
序章: 地毯太大放不進背包，也太重搬不動。不過你注意到攤開的地毯下有個陷阱。

Introduction: The carpets look rather new, perhaps recently stolen from the merchant in town.  There is a sign on the red carpet which reads, "Step Here".  It's written in a shaky hand, as if the author was laughing at the time he wrote it.
序章: 地毯看起來相當新，也許是最近從城裡商人那兒偷來的。紅地毯上有個牌子寫著「踩這裡」。字跡歪斜，彷彿作者寫字時在暗笑。

Introduction: Sturdy looking bridge with two support poles across the chasm.  A message (written in a clear, firm hand) reading "Cross Here" is on this bridge.
序章: 堅固的橋，有兩根支柱橫跨裂谷。橋上有則訊息，用清晰有力的字跡寫著「走這邊」。

Introduction: There is a single pole supporting this sturdy looking bridge across the chasm.
序章: 一根支柱撐著這座堅固的橋，橫跨裂谷。

Introduction: The back door.
序章: 後門。

Introduction: They remind you of a bunch of Knochelkopfs.
序章: 它們讓你想起一群 Knochelkopfs(碰頭怪)。

Introduction: There is a chandelier with a rope attached.
序章: 有一盞吊燈上面綁著繩子。

Introduction: The door is already blocked.
序章: 門已經被堵住了。

Introduction: No time for that again (although it WAS fun).  Just get the heck outta here.
序章: 現在沒時間再玩了(雖然確實很好玩)。趕快逃出這鬼地方。

Introduction: The chandelier is out of your reach.
序章: 吊燈超出你夠得著的範圍。

Introduction: Chandelier, used to hold up the rope.
序章: 吊燈，用來吊著繩子。

Introduction: He's been drinking Troll's sweat all night.
序章: 他整晚都在喝巨魔汗水酒。

Introduction: The front door is already barred.
序章: 前門已經被封住了。

Introduction: The floor is strewn with flotsam and jetsam.
序章: 地板上散落著各種廢棄物。

Introduction: You close and bar the door.
序章: 你關上門並用木條封住了。

Introduction: Oh, a wise guy, eh?
序章: 「喔，你想耍聰明啊？」

Introduction: There's no need to knock this guy out; the Troll's Sweat he's been drinking has already done the job for you.
序章: 不必敲昏這傢伙；他喝的巨魔汗水酒已經替你做好這件事了。

Introduction: You move the chair in front of the door.
序章: 你移動椅子到門前。

Introduction: Wooden tables, benches, and chairs fill the space of this brigand meeting area.  A chandelier lights the room and  an unlit candelabra stands in the corner.  A rope hangs from the chandelier.
序章: 木桌、長凳和椅子填滿了這個山賊集會區。一盞吊燈照亮房間，角落裡站著盞未點燃的燭台。繩子從吊燈垂下。

Introduction: None of the doors are locked.
序章: 沒有任何門被鎖上。

Introduction: Leave the rope alone for now.  It's serving a very important function by holding up the chandelier.
序章: 先別動繩子。它現在的功用很重要——吊著吊燈。

Introduction: The rope holds up the chandelier.  It's used to lower the chandelier so that it can be lit.
序章: 繩子吊著吊燈。它被用來放低吊燈好點燃它。

Introduction: Most of the tables are grimy with dried beer and such.  The head table, though, is clean.
序章: 大部分的桌子都粘著乾燥的啤酒漬之類的東西。不過主桌看起來很乾淨。

Introduction: You begin toppling the candelabra.
序章: 你開始推倒燭台。

Introduction: The candelabra has already fallen.
序章: 燭台已經倒下了。

Introduction: Sturdy-looking device for holding candles.
序章: 看起來很堅固的燭台。

Introduction: You can't do that from where you're standing.
序章: 你從現在的位置做不了這件事。

Introduction: In the "upstairs" doorway, there is a chain with a handle attached.
序章: 在「樓上」的門口，有條綁著把手的鍊子。

Introduction: You're not close enough.
序章: 你離得不夠近。

Introduction: Get closer.
序章: 靠近點。

Introduction: Have a nice day!
序章: 「祝你今天愉快！」

Introduction: It's a box of some sort...or so the sign says.  There is a button on the box.
序章: 這是某種盒子……至少牌子是這麼說的。盒子上有個按鈕。

Introduction: You'll have to get closer.
序章: 你得靠近點。

Introduction: It looks a button, but you'll have to be closer to push it.
序章: 看起來像個按鈕，不過你得靠近點才能按它。

Introduction: Engraved on the button are the words: "PUSH ME".
序章: 按鈕上刻著「按我」。

Introduction: Oops!  You thought this door led SOMEWHERE!
序章: 「哎呀！你以為這扇門會通往某個地方！」

Introduction: What a miserable experience!
序章: 「多麼慘烈的經歷！」

Introduction: You dare not unbar the door.  You hear hordes of brigands massing beyond it.
序章: 你不敢打開門。你聽到成群山賊在門外集結。

Introduction: You can't do it.
序章: 你辦不到。

Introduction: Elsa is right next door.  I'll go set up the secret exit for your escape.
序章: 「艾爾莎就在隔壁。我去準備秘密出口好讓你逃脫。」

Introduction: You bar the door behind you.
序章: 你在身後用木條封住門。

Introduction: You behold a most fascinating place.
序章: 你看到了一個最迷人的地方。

Introduction: The person labeled "ME" speaks:
序章: 被標籤為「我」的人說話了：

Introduction: Abandon mope, all ye who enter here!
序章: 「所有進入此地者，都要拋棄悶悶不樂！」

Introduction: Job hunting, or just sight-seeing?
序章: 「在找工作，還是只是來觀光？」

Introduction: I foresee a brilliant future for you in the fall.
序章: 「我預見你的輝煌未來…會在秋天到來。」

Introduction: If you've got something to say, then speak fast or forever hold your pieces.
序章: 「如果你想說什麼，就說快點，要不就永遠閉嘴。」

Introduction: State your case before I case your estate!
序章: 「在我接收你的遺產之前，先陳述你的理由！」

Introduction: It would serve no purpose.
序章: 那沒有用。

Introduction: You feel disoriented.
序章: 你感到暈眩。

Introduction: How does he DO this?
序章: 他到底是怎麼做到的？

Introduction: There's an ECHO IN HERE... Echo In Here... echo in here...
序章: 這裡有回音……有回音……有回音……

Introduction: M. C. Escher would LOVE this place.
序章: M. C. 艾舍爾一定會愛上這個地方。

Introduction: OK, enough fooling around!
序章: 好吧，別亂來了！

Introduction: This is getting old!
序章: 這傢伙太老了！

Introduction: A person COULD get used to this.
序章: 人是有可能習慣這個的。

Introduction: Your magic has no effect.  It must be something about this room.
序章: 你的魔法沒有效果。一定是這房間的關係。

Introduction: ME sees you preparing your spell and...
序章: 「我」看到你在準備法術……

Introduction: Nothing is locked.
序章: 沒有任何東西被鎖上。

Introduction: There is a deep pit with a walkway winding above it.
序章: 有個深坑，上面有條蜿蜒的走道。

Introduction: There appear to be stairs leading up (?) to nowhere.  There is also what looks like a bowl full of fireflies.
序章: 似乎有些階梯通往(?)無處可去。還有個看起來裝滿螢火蟲的碗。

Introduction: With the Brigands pounding at the door, you decide not to rest until you're safe.
序章: 山賊在門上狂敲，你決定在安全前不休息。

Introduction: Stairs lead up to the platform on which "ME" is located.
序章: 階梯通往「我」所在的平台。

Introduction: Why would you want to throw that?
序章: 你為什麼想丟那個？

Introduction: No more talking, my friend.  Let's have fun!
序章: 「別再囉唆了，我的朋友。我們來玩樂！」

Introduction: You're too busy.
序章: 你太忙了。

Introduction: It looks like the talking is over.
序章: 「看來談話結束了。」

Introduction: Would he be surprised to see his little girl now!
序章: 「要是他現在看到他的小女兒，一定會大吃一驚！」

Introduction: Everybody needs to know a few magic tricks to get by these days.  You'd be surprised how effective Sleeping Powder is for taking out the guards of a caravan.
序章: 「現在誰都得知道幾招魔法才能活下去。你會驚訝於催眠粉對付商隊守衛有多有效。」

Introduction: You know about the enchantment?  Then perhaps you have come to help. Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band.
序章: 「你知道詛咒的事？那你可能是來幫忙的。也許你有辦法把孩子從山賊手中救出。」

Introduction: Elsa doesn't know who she really is.  That's part of the enchantment.
序章: 「艾爾莎不知道她真正是誰。那是詛咒的一部分。」

Introduction: When Elsa was left with some brigands ten years ago, all her memories were taken away from her.
序章: 「十年前艾爾莎被留給山賊時，她的所有記憶都被奪走了。」

Introduction: I borrowed a Magic Mirror from a friend to try to break the spell, but it only works when spells are cast directly at you.
序章: 「我從朋友那兒借了面魔鏡想破解詛咒，不過它只有在法術直接施加在你身上時才有效。」

Introduction: If you're going to be the big Hero, I hope you brought a Dispel Potion or something.
序章: 「如果你要做大英雄，希望你帶了解除詛咒藥劑之類的。」

Introduction: The true Dispel Potion will break an enchantment.  You must splash the potion on Elsa as soon as you can, or she'll skewer you for sure.  She's more than a match for old 'Swordy Lordy' now!
序章: 「真正的解除詛咒藥劑能破解任何詛咒。你得盡快把藥劑潑在艾爾莎身上，否則她一定會把你串成烤肉。她現在比「劍客勳爵」還要強得多！」

Introduction: You know Elsa?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band.
序章: 「你認識艾爾莎？那你可能是來幫忙的。也許你有辦法把孩子從山賊手中救出。」

Introduction: After you have explained what you know about Elsa, Yorick speaks:
序章: 在你解釋了你對艾爾莎的了解後，尤瑞克說話了：

Introduction: It's about time that someone else helped Elsa before it's too late! Toro and I are the only friends she's got here.
序章: 「是時候該有人在太晚前幫助艾爾莎了！我和托羅是她在這兒唯一的朋友。」

Introduction: She's a lot like her dad, though.  She organized these brigands and changed them from mere thugs to major raiders!
序章: 「不過她很像她爸爸。她組織了這些山賊，把他們從小流氓變成大盜匪！」

Introduction: She plans to move the entire band away from the valley as soon as the snow clears.  If that happens, I know she will never be disenchanted.
序章: 「她計畫一旦積雪融化就帶著整個幫派離開山谷。要是那樣，我就知道她永遠不會被解除詛咒。」

Introduction: There are plenty of doors.  You wouldn't want me to spoil your fun, now!
序章: 「門多著呢。你應該不想我毀了你的樂趣吧！」

Introduction: Either you'll figure it out in the end, or you'll end it before you're out.
序章: 「你要不最後想通，要不就在逃出去前把自己終結。」

Introduction: This fort is the brigand's palace.  It has been well-constructed.
序章: 「這座要塞是山賊的宮殿。蓋得相當不錯。」

Introduction: For thirty years I was the Court Jester.  Now I jest enjoy watching other people take the pratfalls.
序章: 「三十年來我都是宮廷弄臣。現在我只是喜歡看別人出洋相。」

Introduction: I'll protect the Brigand Leader with everything I've got.  That's no laughing matter as I get madder.
序章: 「我會用盡全力保護山賊首領。這不是開玩笑，當我生氣時尤其如此。」

Introduction: This room is my way of keeping the brigands from bothering the Leader about raises, since it tends to raze the brigands.
序章: 「這個房間是我用來阻止山賊向首領要求加薪的辦法，因為它會把山賊消滅掉。」

Introduction: I designed it myself!  This place has people rolling in the aisles.
序章: 「我自己設計的！這地方會讓人笑得滾來滾去。」

Introduction: Can't you see that I am he?  I try to inspire (before they expire) a sense of awe and majesty in those that enter here.
序章: 「你沒看出來我就是他嗎？我試圖在進入這裡的人心中喚起(在他們完蛋前)敬畏和威嚴的感受。」

Introduction: After all, I am the Brigand Warlock.  One has to keep up one's appearances, or appear to keep up with one's peers.
序章: 「畢竟，我是山賊巫師。一個人必須維持自己的門面，或看起來要跟上自己的同僚。」

Introduction: Toro is the minotaur that guards this fortress.  Elsa found him when he was just a calf, and he's kowtowed to her ever since.
序章: 「托羅是守衛這座要塞的牛頭人。艾爾莎在他還是牛犢時找到他，他從那時起就對她言聽計從。」

Introduction: He guarantees that the brigands won't give Elsa any bull.
序章: 「他保證這些山賊不會跟艾爾莎胡鬧。」

Introduction: Ah yes, the 'Mirror with the Leer', as Erasmus calls it (or was it Fenrus?). It reflects a spell back on the caster of the spell.
序章: 「啊，沒錯，伊拉斯謨叫它『邪笑之鏡』（還是芬魯斯叫的？）。它會把法術反射回施法者身上。」

Introduction: Now where did I put that mirror... Was it on the desk in Elsa's office or else in the office on Elsa's desk?
序章: 「現在我把那面鏡子放在哪了……是放在艾爾莎辦公室的桌子上，還是放在艾爾莎桌子上的辦公室？」

Introduction: You snooze, you lose.  And my banana trick has appeal of its own.
序章: 「你打瞌睡就會輸。而且我的香蕉戲法有其獨特的魅力。」

Introduction: You must know the 'Blade Braggart'.  He used to show off in the castle courtyard all the time.
序章: 「你一定知道『刀光自大狂』。他老是在城堡中庭炫耀。」

Introduction: Elsa once asked if 'Rapier Ruler' would teach her to use the sword, and the 'Sword Bore' told her he would not waste his talent on females.
序章: 「艾爾莎曾經問過『長劍統治者』願不願意教她用劍，而『劍術悶蛋』卻說他不會把才華浪費在女性身上。」

Introduction: She came to me in tears.  After all, she was only nine at the time.
序章: 「她眼淚汪汪地來找我。畢竟她那時才九歲。」

Introduction: She can teach old 'Weapon Windbag' a thing or two now!  Her arm is true, and she's truly disarming.
序章: 「現在她可以教那老傢伙『武器老噠吧』一兩招了！她劍術高超，真是令人印象深刻。」

Introduction: Talking is a speciality of those who know not what they talk about.
序章: 「談話是那些不知道自己在說什麼的人的專長。」

Introduction: You know me?  Then perhaps you have come to help.  Perhaps you are able to take the child from the band.
序章: 「你認識我？那也許你是來幫忙的。也許你能把小孩從這幫人手中救出來。」

Introduction: Alas, poor Yorick!  I know me well.
序章: 「唉呀，可憐的尤瑞克！我太瞭解我自己了。」

Introduction: Ten years ago, I swore I would find Elsa no matter what.  It took me two years to find her, here with the brigands, and I've posed as her warlock ever since.
序章: 「十年前，我發誓一定要找到艾爾莎，無論如何。我花了兩年時間才在這裡找到她跟這些山賊在一起，從那時起我就一直偽裝成她的巫師。」

Introduction: I couldn't break the spell on her, nor could I leave her to grow up into an attractive woman surrounded by rogues and scoundrels.
序章: 「我無法破除她身上的詛咒，也無法任由她長大成為一個被無賴和騙子包圍的美女。」

Introduction: Someone had to defend her honor!
序章: 「得要有人為她的名譽辯護！」

Introduction: Take yourself there.
序章: 「你自己去那裡。」

Introduction: ME sees you preparing your spell and...
序章: 「我」看到你在準備法術……

Introduction: Now??
序章: 現在？？

Introduction: It's 'ME'.
Caravan
序章: 商隊

Introduction: Disenchantment
序章: 解除詛咒

Introduction: Dispel
序章: 驅散術

Introduction: Be more specific, my friend.
序章: 「請說得更具體一點，朋友。」

Introduction: Exit
序章: 離開

Introduction: Fortress
序章: 要塞

Introduction: Jester
序章: 弄臣

Introduction: If you have business to do, then get busy doing your business.
序章: 「如果你有事要做，那就開始做吧。」

Introduction: Maze
序章: 迷宮

Introduction: Me
序章: 我

Introduction: Minotaur
序章: 牛頭人

Introduction: Can't you see that's nothing to me?  You'd better flee.
序章: 「你看不出對我來說那算什麼嗎？你最好逃命。」

Introduction: The brigands will come to spoil your fun.  You'll be undone.
序章: 「山賊會來破壞你的樂趣。你會完蛋。」

Introduction: The brigands tend to hold a grudge, and they won't begrudge to tend to you.
序章: 「這些山賊往往懷恨在心，他們也不會介意對付你。」

Introduction: If you have any famous last statements, you'd better say the secret word, or you'll lament your last state.
序章: 「如果你有什麼著名的遺言，你最好說出那個秘密詞語，否則你會為你的最後處境哀嘆。」

Introduction: Potion
序章: 藥水

Introduction: Of which potion do you speak?
序章: 「你說的是哪種藥水？」

Introduction: Powder
序章: 粉末

Introduction: Swordy Lordy
序章: 劍術老兄

Introduction: Yorick
序章: 尤瑞克

Introduction: A quick but thorough search of the desk discloses two Healing Potions and a mirror.
序章: 一次迅速但徹底的搜索發現了桌上放著兩瓶治療藥水和一面鏡子。

Introduction: You find nothing more.
序章: 你沒有發現更多東西。

Introduction: You see a mirror.
序章: 你看到一面鏡子。

Introduction: You find two Healing Potions.
序章: 你找到兩瓶治療藥水。

Introduction: You see a plain wooden desk.  On the desk are two potion bottles and a hand mirror.
序章: 你看到一張樸素的木製桌子。桌上放著兩瓶藥水和一面手鏡。

Introduction: That would be foolhardy.  You would let the brigands in.
序章: 那太愚蠢了。你會讓山賊進來。

Introduction: The chest full of gems is monogrammed with the name "Abdulla Doo".  He'll sure be happy to get it back.
序章: 那個裝滿寶石的箱子上刻著「阿布杜拉·嘟」的名字。他一定會很高興能拿回它。

Introduction: There are more diamonds there than in Tiffany's!
序章: 那裡的鑽石比蒂芬妮還多！

Introduction: A lot of townsfolk will be happy to get their stolen goods back.
序章: 很多鎮民會很高興能拿回他們被偷的東西。

Introduction: You throw the Dispel Potion on the brigand leader.
序章: 你把驅散術藥水扔在山賊首領身上。

Introduction: Good idea, but you don't have that potion.
序章: 好主意，但你沒有那個藥水。

Introduction: The brigand leader wears a sword, and it looks well used.
序章: 山賊首領配著一把劍，看起來用過很多次。

Introduction: Elsa looks much better in a dress.
序章: 艾爾莎穿著衣服看起來好多了。

Introduction: It depends on your taste, though, I suppose.
序章: 不過那取決於你的品味，我想。

Introduction: Everybody else is gone.
序章: 其他人都走了。

Introduction: The Brigand Leader stares at you coldly.  It seems he's not interested in chatter.
序章: 山賊首領冷冷地盯著你。看來他對閒聊不感興趣。

Introduction: We've no time to talk.  I'll meet you at the castle.
序章: 「我們沒時間說話。我會在城堡那邊見你。」

Introduction: That black bird looks strangely familiar.  You sense that its destiny is irrevocably entwined with yours, in a strange foreshadowing of the rest of the "Quest for Glory" series.
序章: 那隻黑鳥看起來奇怪地熟悉。你感覺到它的命運與你的命運不可逆轉地糾纏在一起，預示著《英雄傳奇》系列其餘故事的奇異徵兆。

Introduction: Oodles and boodles of ill-booten gotty.
序章: 一大堆一大堆的壞掉的怪東西。

Introduction: Let's see, was it the "dragon with the flagon", or the "flagon with the dragon"?
序章: 讓我想想，是「帶著長頸瓶的龍」還是「帶著龍的長頸瓶」？

Introduction: That gold ornament probably weighs so much it would crumple the hood of your car.
序章: 那個金色裝飾品可能重到足以壓坍你的車頂。

Introduction: It looks like King Midas's shield -- worth exactly its weight in gold.
序章: 它看起來像邁達斯國王的盾牌——價值正好等於它的重量。

Introduction: You're not near the mirror.
序章: 你離那面鏡子太遠了。

Introduction: You already have the mirror.
序章: 你已經有那面鏡子了。

Introduction: You pick up the mirror and store it away.
序章: 你拿起鏡子並把它收起來。

Introduction: A strange face leers at you when you look into the mirror.  At least, you *hope* you don't look like that!
序章: 當你看進鏡子裡時，一張奇怪的臉對著你齜牙咧嘴。至少，你*希望*你看起來不是那樣！

Introduction: You place the two Healing Potions into your pack.
序章: 你把那兩瓶治療藥水放進你的背包。

Introduction: You are not near the healing potions.
序章: 你離那些治療藥水太遠了。

Introduction: It's a healing potion.
序章: 那是一瓶治療藥水。

Introduction: You can't imagine how good it feels to know who I am again.  After all these years, to remember my name is Elsa von Spielburg!
序章: 「你無法想像再次知道我是誰的感覺有多好。經歷了這麼多年，重新記起我的名字是艾爾莎·馮·史畢柏格！」

Introduction: I used to wish I was not a brigand's child but actually an enchanted princess.  Now I know that I really was enchanted, and I don't have to be a brigand any more!
序章: 「我過去希望我不是山賊的小孩，而是一個被施魔法的公主。現在我知道我真的被施了魔法，我不必再做山賊了！」

Introduction: Thank you so much for freeing me.
序章: 「非常感謝你救了我。」

Introduction: I've got to hurry home to father and get his guards to capture the rest of the brigands before they manage to escape with the treasure. It must be returned to the rightful owners.
序章: 「我得趕緊回家去找父親，讓他的衛兵去抓捕其他山賊，免得他們趁機逃走帶走寶藏。寶藏必須歸還給合法的所有人。」

Introduction: Yorick!  Yorick!  I'm me again!
序章: 「尤瑞克！尤瑞克！我又是我自己了！」

Introduction: Sorry I took so long.  I got lost.
序章: 「抱歉我花了這麼久。我迷路了。」

Introduction: Will your Dad be glad that your spell's been repelled!  We need to go before the brigands know or they'll spoil our show.
序章: 「你爸爸會很高興你的詛咒被解除了！我們得在山賊知道之前離開，否則他們會破壞我們的計畫。」

Introduction: C'mon!  We'd better make our getaway before they get in our way.
序章: 「來吧！我們最好在他們擋住我們之前逃脫。」

Introduction: Yorick and I can return to the castle with the amulet I wear, but I'm afraid you'll have to get there on your own.
序章: 「尤瑞克和我可以用我戴著的護身符回到城堡，但我恐怕你得自己到那裡去。」

Introduction: There are two Healing Potions on my desk that you may take with you.
序章: 「我桌子上有兩瓶治療藥水你可以帶走。」

Introduction: You should use this secret passage and escape while they are arguing over the treasure.  If they find you in here, you'll be overwhelmed and killed.
序章: 「你應該用這條密道逃脫，趁他們還在為寶藏爭執。如果他們在這裡發現你，你會被壓倒並殺死。」

Introduction: Thank you again, and good luck!  I'll make sure you are richly rewarded for your bravery.
序章: 「再次感謝你，祝你好運！我會確保你因你的勇敢而得到豐厚的獎勵。」

Introduction: If you decide to counter the curser, then mind the mirror over mere minds. So tip the canoe and toodleloo!  Elsa, if you do the honors, I'll honor your due.
序章: 「如果你決定對抗施詛咒者，那就注意鏡子勝於平常心。所以傾斜獨木舟，再見！艾爾莎，如果你做好了，我會尊重你的正當待遇。」

Introduction: The brigands are trying to break into the room.
序章: 山賊正在試圖破門而入。

Introduction: Thinking about all that treasure cost you time and more.  Some folks just don't know when to leave.
序章: 淨想著寶藏讓你浪費時間，甚至更多。有些人就是不懂什麼時候該閃人。

Introduction: Elsa, my dear, there's a problem I fear.  To our dismay, that spell's here to stay, for alas you did slay your hero today.
序章: 「艾爾莎，我的親愛的，我擔心有個問題。令我們沮喪的是，那個詛咒會一直留下來，因為唉呀，你今天殺死了你的英雄。」

Introduction: You finally made it through Yorick's room.
序章: 你終於通過了尤瑞克的房間。

Introduction: The brigand leader taunts you with his stillness.  You're sure that at the first wrong move, you'll become an ex-hero.
序章: 山賊首領用他的靜止來嘲笑你。你確信只要稍微動錯了，你就會成為過氣英雄。

Introduction: Too late!
序章: 太遲了！

Introduction: There's so much magic in here that it's hard for you tell which is which.
序章: 這裡的魔法太多了，你很難分辨哪個是哪個。

Introduction: After hours of hard and dangerous labor collecting the components to make the dispel potion, in a moment of sudden genius, you throw the potion away.  It's a brilliant and unexpected strategem.
序章: 經過數小時艱苦危險的工作收集製造驅散術藥水的成分，在突然的靈光一閃之下，你把藥水扔掉了。那是個光彩奪目又出人意料的策略。

Introduction: Unfortunately, it is also a very bad idea.  You no longer have the Dispel Potion.
序章: 不幸的是，那也是個非常糟糕的主意。你再也沒有驅散術藥水了。

Introduction: The Leader's room is filled with treasures.
序章: 首領的房間裡堆滿了寶藏。

Introduction: That looks like a Persian carpet.  It has a license plate that says "Abdulla Doo 222."
序章: 那看起來像波斯地毯。它有個牌子寫著「阿布杜拉·嘟222號」。

Introduction: Sheesh!  Even the lamps have enough gold to be worth more than Jose Canseco's annual salary.  Well, maybe not quite *that* much.
序章: 哇！連燈都有足夠的黃金，價值超過何塞·坎塞科的年薪。好吧，也許沒那麼*多*啦。

Introduction: What a funny guy!
序章: 多滑稽的傢伙！

Introduction: He's not here.
序章: 他不在這裡。

Introduction: The arrows point to various orifices.
序章: 箭頭指向各種孔口。

Introduction: It's another door!
序章: 又是一扇門！

Introduction: It's an entrance... or is it an exit?
序章: 這是入口……還是出口？

Introduction: The attic exit?
序章: 閣樓出口？

Introduction: It looks like a picture of an door painted on the wall.
序章: 它看起來像牆上畫的門的圖片。

Introduction: A pet door?  It's shorter than you are.
序章: 寵物門？它比你還短。

Introduction: It's a chain of events.
序章: 這是一連串的事件。

Introduction: It reminds you of a tie you once received as a present on All-Fools Day.
序章: 它讓你想起你曾經在愚人節收到的一條領帶。

Introduction: It IS!  It's a bowl of fireflies, used as a light source.  What won't they think of next?
序章: 它正是！那是一碗螢火蟲，用作光源。他們下一步還會想到什麼？

Introduction: What an interestingly hinged trap door.  What do you suppose would happen if you stepped on it?
序章: 多有趣的鉸鏈陷阱門。你覺得如果你踩上去會發生什麼？

Introduction: You eat the Magic Acorn.  It tastes terrible.  Better stick to real food next time.
序章: 你吃了魔法橡實。味道很糟。下次還是堅持吃真正的食物。

Introduction: That would make a pretty silly-looking hat.
序章: 那會做成相當愚蠢的帽子。

Introduction: Inscribed on the bottom you read, "Property of John B. Nimble."
序章: 在底部刻著的是「約翰·B·尼姆布爾的財產」。

Introduction: You absent-mindedly clean your fingernails with one of the Cheetaur's claws.
序章: 你心不在焉地用其中一隻獵豹人的爪子清潔指甲。

Introduction: You don't feel anything.  Perhaps this was not the correct way to use this potion.
序章: 你沒有感覺到任何東西。也許這不是使用這種藥水的正確方式。

Introduction: You do a quick equipment check.  Everything seems to be in place.
序章: 你做了一次快速的裝備檢查。一切看起來都沒問題。

Introduction: You sprinkle just a smidge of the magical dust on and about your person...
序章: 你在你身上灑了一點點魔法粉塵……

Introduction: ... and try to fly.  But it appears that none of those Fairies was named Tinker Bell.
序章: ……並試著飛。但似乎那些妖精裡沒有一個叫小仙女的。

Introduction: It's empty.  Maybe you should recycle it.
序章: 它是空的。也許你應該回收它。

Introduction: Your mother always told you to stop and smell the flowers.
序章: 你媽媽總是告訴你停下來聞花香。

Introduction: You drink the clear water from the flask, draining it completely.
序章: 你喝了水瓶裡的清水，完全喝乾。

Introduction: You eat some of the apples.  They actually taste quite good.
序章: 你吃了一些蘋果。它們實際上味道相當不錯。

Introduction: It would make a great coat for a Leprechaun.
序章: 那會是妖精的好外套。

Introduction: The drink soothes as it goes down.
序章: 飲料在下去時撫慰了你。

Introduction: You fought so hard for this key, you just can't bear to part with it.
序章: 你為這把鑰匙奮力戰鬥，捨不得放手。

Introduction: The leather makes you think kinky thoughts.  (And you wondered why adventurers dress up this way.)
序章: 皮革讓你想到古怪的念頭。（而且你想知道為什麼冒險者要這樣打扮。）

Introduction: You delicately insert the lockpick into your left nostril.
序章: 你輕輕地把開鎖工具插進你的左鼻孔。

Introduction: Success!  Your nose is now open.
序章: 成功！你的鼻子現在開放了。

Introduction: Unfortunately, you push it in too far, causing yourself a cerebral hemorrhage.  Guess you should have been practicing on less difficult locks.
序章: 不幸的是，你插得太深，導致你自己腦溢血。看來你應該練習更容易的鎖。

Introduction: The surgeon general warns . . .
序章: 外科醫生長官警告……

Introduction: Whatta guy!!
序章: 多棒的傢伙！！

Introduction: You pause to admire the way the gem glows with its own inner light.
序章: 你停下來欣賞那顆寶石用自己內在的光芒閃耀的方式。

Introduction: What's this?  A chink in your armor?  No, it's just a rust spot.
序章: 這是什麼？你盔甲上的縫隙？不，只是個生鏽的地方。

Introduction: Baba Yaga is welcome to it!  If it tastes half as bad as it smells...
序章: 歡迎芭芭雅嘎拿它！如果它的味道有聞起來的一半那麼糟……

Introduction: You foxy devil, you!
序章: 你這狐狸魔鬼！

Introduction: That's funny.  I could have sworn I warned you about eating too many Magic Mushrooms.  Your mind permanently warped, you die a garishly polka-dotted death.
序章: 真搞笑。我發誓我警告過你別吃太多魔法蘑菇。你的心智永久變形，以花俏的圓點花紋死亡。

Introduction: Where did all the pretty horses go?
序章: 那些漂亮的馬都去哪裡了？

Introduction: Hmm, that was interesting.  But it would probably not be a good idea to repeat the experience.
序章: 嗯，那很有趣。但重複那種經驗可能不是個好主意。

Introduction: You don't need to do that right now.
序章: 你現在不需要這樣做。

Introduction: Your senses gradually return to normal.
序章: 你的感官逐漸恢復正常。

Introduction: You ingest a few of the mushrooms from the Fairy Ring.  They taste delicious.
序章: 你吃下妖精環的幾顆蘑菇。它們嚐起來很美味。

Introduction: Wow!  Look at all the nice paisley horses!
序章: 哇！看看所有漂亮的西洋花紋馬！

Introduction: Not to mention the beautiful neon sky.
序章: 更別說那美麗的霓虹天空。

Introduction: The mushrooms from the Fairy Ring are pretty good, so you hear.  However, when they're mixed with Kobold toadstools... that's a horse of a different color.
序章: 據你所聽，妖精環的蘑菇相當不錯。不過，當它們與地精菌類混在一起時。..那就另當別論了。

Introduction: You eat some of the Kobold's fungus.  Within seconds your insides feel like they're on fire.  Guess you're not a Kobold, huh?
序章: 你吃了一些地精的菌類。幾秒內你的內臟就像著火一樣。看來你不是地精，是吧？

Introduction: Didn't anyone warn you about eating other people's food?  Death by Toadstools doesn't leave mushroom for improvement.
序章: 沒人警告你不要吃別人的食物嗎？被菌類毒死不會讓人進步。

Introduction: You think that you'd better take it easy for a while until you recover.
序章: 你認為你最好休息一陣子，直到康復為止。

Introduction: Play it again, Ludwig.
序章: 再彈一次，路德維希。

Introduction: You read it again and puzzle over its meaning.
序章: 你再讀一次，思考它的意義。

Introduction: They're nice, but they don't go with your outfit.
序章: 它們很漂亮，但與你的裝扮不搭。

Introduction: The rations are tasteless but filling.
序章: 口糧沒味道但很飽。

Introduction: It's not your size.
序章: 這不是你的尺寸。

Introduction: After wiping the plant saliva from it, you examine the large seed.
序章: 擦掉上面的植物分泌物後，你檢查這顆大種子。

Introduction: That wasn't quite what the Surgeon General meant when he suggested you use a shield.
序章: 這不太是醫療總署長建議你拿盾牌時的意思。

Introduction: You check to see if your money's still there. Yep, it is.
序章: 你查看你的錢是否還在。是的，在。

Introduction: The drink is invigorating.
序章: 這飲料令人精神振奮。

Introduction: You prepare to fall on your sword, but you finally decide that it would be uncomfortable.
序章: 你準備自刎，但最後決定那會不舒服。

Introduction: You remain amazed at the number and versatility of the little gimcracks and thingamabobs inside your thief's tool kit.
序章: 你對盜賊工具包內小工具和各種小玩意兒的數量和多功能性依然驚嘆。

Introduction: The picture on your thief's license doesn't look a bit like you.
序章: 你的盜賊執照上的照片完全不像你。

Introduction: You try on the troll beard, but the goat-like smell convinces you to remain clean-shaven for a while.
序章: 你試戴巨魔的鬍子，但那羊一樣的味道使你決定暫時保持光潔。

Introduction: The vase makes an even worse hat than the candelabra.
序章: 花瓶作為帽子比燭台更糟。

Introduction: You eat some vegetables.  You think they would have been better saved for cows or horses.  Real Heroes eat preserved dry rations.
序章: 你吃了一些蔬菜。你認為它們本應留給牛或馬。真正的英雄吃乾糧。

Introduction: Mumble, mumble... overworked... mumble... underpaid... mumble, mumble... no control over my life...
序章: 碎碎念。..累死了。..碎碎念。..薪水太少。..碎碎念。..對人生無法掌控。..

Introduction: You pour the contents of the flask all over.  Maybe you're not doing this right.
序章: 你把烈酒瓶的內容物倒得滿地。也許你做得不對。

Introduction: There's no point in trying to use one of those there.
序章: 沒有必要試著在那邊用那個。

Introduction: It's not much to look at.
序章: 它看起來沒什麼。

Introduction: To whom were you trying to speak?
序章: 你想跟誰說話？

Introduction: There's nothing to do there.
序章: 那邊沒什麼可做的。

Introduction: Your money's no good here.
序章: 你的錢在這邊沒用。

Introduction: Aggressive, aren't you?
序章: 你很好戰嘛？

Introduction: Picky, picky, picky.
序章: 挑剔，挑剔，挑剔。

Introduction: You fought so hard for this key, you just can't bear to part with it.
序章: 你為這把鑰匙奮力戰鬥，捨不得放手。

Introduction: You might be able to get some money for the candelabra, but, other than that, it's useless.
序章: 你也許能靠燭台換一些錢，不過除此之外，它沒用。

Introduction: Behind the velvet curtains is an alcove leading to the offices of the Baron.
序章: 絲絨窗簾後有一個通往男爵辦公室的小拐角。

Introduction: I sincerely hope you will continue your valiant adventures in our land.  If anyone can rid the land of brigands, it will be you.
序章: 「我衷心希望你能在我們的土地上繼續英勇的冒險。如果有人能清除山賊，那就是你。」

Introduction: Someday in the near future, I can envision holding a ceremony in your honor and bestowing upon you the title, `Hero of Spielburg'.
序章: 「不久的將來某一天，我能想像為你舉辦典禮，並賜予你『史畢柏格英雄』的頭銜。」

Introduction: As you leave the castle tomorrow, you will receive the reward money I long ago posted for the safe return of my son, as a token of my thanks.
序章: 「當你明天離開城堡時，你將收到我早前為兒子安全歸來而懸賞的獎金，作為我感謝的表證。」

Introduction: You will, of course, be expected to dine with us and be our guest in the castle tonight.
序章: 「當然，你將被期待與我們共進晚宴，並在城堡內作我們的客人過夜。」

Introduction: Words cannot express the gratitude I have towards you.
序章: 「言語無法表達我對你的感謝。」

Introduction: Not only have you freed the Baronet from enchantment and allowed my son to return to me, but you have renewed my hope that the curse may be lifted.
序章: 「你不僅解除了男爵之子的魔咒，讓我的兒子回到我身邊，還使我重新燃起詛咒被解除的希望。」

Introduction: My son, Baronet Barnard von Spielburg, also desires to acknowledge his indebtedness.
序章: 「我的兒子，史畢柏格男爵之子巴納德，也想表達他的感謝。」

Introduction: Barnard.
序章: 「巴納德。」

Introduction: Do you have any questions?
序章: 「你有任何問題嗎？」

Introduction: A servant is awaiting you outside to show you to your quarters.  We will see you at supper later.
序章: 「有個僕人在外面等著帶你到你的房間。我們晚餐時見。」

Introduction: Furthermore, I understand you have rid our valley of a dangerous Kobold Magic User in order to free the Baronet von Spielburg.
序章: 「此外，我瞭解你消滅了一個危險的地精法師來解救史畢柏格男爵之子。」

Introduction: You risked your life to singlehandedly defeat the foul Spellcaster.  You are a true hero, and I thank you.
序章: 「你冒著生命危險獨自擊敗了邪惡的咒語使者。你是真正的英雄，我感謝你。」

Introduction: Curse
序章: 詛咒

Introduction: Daughter
序章: 女兒

Introduction: Brigand Leader
序章: 山賊首領

Introduction: I have attempted everything in my power to get her to go away.  I fear it will take some kind of magic to be able to counteract her magic.
序章: 「我已竭盡所能要她離開。我擔心需要某種魔法才能對抗她的魔法。」

Introduction: I am Stefan von Spielburg, 12th Baron of Spielburg castle.
序章: 「我是史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格，史畢柏格城堡的第 12 任男爵。」

Introduction: If I had more guards, I would wipe the brigands out myself now that I have my son at my side.
序章: 「如果我有更多守衛，既然有我的兒子在身邊，我會親自消滅山賊。」

Introduction: It will take some time before this castle and land have recovered from my sorrow, so I still think it will take a Hero to destroy the brigands.
序章: 「城堡和領地需要一些時間才能從我的悲傷中恢復，所以我仍然認為需要一個英雄來摧毀山賊。」

Introduction: I know they have some kind of fortress at the far south of the valley.  I have heard rumors they have set up an ambush for all that approach.
序章: 「我知道他們在山谷的最南端有某種要塞。我聽說他們已為所有接近的人設置了埋伏。」

Introduction: It is also rumored that there is another way into their fortress, but I have no further knowledge about it.
序章: 「還有傳言說有另一條進入他們要塞的路，但我沒有更多的資訊。」

Introduction: This castle has been in the family for two centuries now as a gift from King Siegfried the Third.
序章: 「這座城堡現在已經是我們家族 200 年的古蹟，是齊格弗里德三世國王的禮物。」

Introduction: Some years back, I tried to force the Ogress Baba Yaga to leave our valley and she put a curse upon me that I should lose everything I held dear.
序章: 「多年前，我試圖強行讓食人妖芭芭雅嘎離開我們的山谷，她對我施加了詛咒，使我失去一切所愛的事物。」

Introduction: When I lost my daughter and my son, I lost all interest in living.  My lands suffered because I suffered.

序章: 「你有幸遇見巴納德·馮·史畢柏格貴族少爺。我們很高興你打破了我們的魔法。也許我們的父親男爵會在你訪問我們的城堡時獎勵你。」
