Introduction: As the sun begins to rise, the Troll runs off to some dark hole.
序章: 當太陽開始升起時，巨魔跑進某個黑暗的洞。

Introduction: You could not escape.  The battle is on.
序章: 你無法逃脫。戰鬥開始了。

Introduction: A monster just wandered by.  You hid in the bushes until it passed.
序章: 一隻怪物剛好走過。你躲在灌木叢裡直到它經過。

Introduction: Although that spell is not useful here, you at least had a chance to practice it.
序章: 雖然那個咒語在這裡沒有用，但你至少有機會練習它。

Introduction: You don't see anything closed around here.
序章: 你沒看到周圍有任何關閉的東西。

Introduction: You sense no magic here.
序章: 你感覺不到這裡有魔法。

Introduction: Certainly you don't want to Fetch THAT!
序章: 你當然不想用取物術取那個！

Introduction: Good practice, but there's nothing worth Fetching here.
序章: 很好的練習，但這裡沒有值得取的東西。

Introduction: You search your opponent.
序章: 你搜查你的對手。

Introduction: The monster doesn't seem very calm.  Maybe it didn't like you hurting it.
序章: 那隻怪物似乎不太冷靜。也許它不喜歡你傷害它。

Introduction: You remove as much of the Troll's beard as you can and put it away in your pack.
序章: 你盡可能地去除巨魔的鬍子並放入你的背包。

Introduction: Some of the Cheetaur's claws have been broken off or damaged, apparently in battle.  You remove the remaining claws and put them away in your pack.
序章: 獵豹人的一些爪子已被打斷或損壞，顯然是在戰鬥中。  你移除了剩下的爪子並放進背包。

Introduction: Unfortunately, the Mantray appears not to have been affected by your spell.
序章: 不幸的是，魔鬼魟似乎沒有受到你的咒語影響。

Introduction: You find %d silver coins, and carefully place them in your pouch.
序章: 你找到了 %d 枚銀幣，小心翼翼地放進荷包裡。

Introduction: You find a single silver coin, carefully polish it, and place it in your pouch.  What a way to make a living!
序章: 你找到了一枚銀幣，小心翼翼地擦拭它，然後放進荷包裡。  真是謀生的好辦法！

Introduction: What a waste!  No treasure!
序章: 真是浪費！  沒有寶藏！

Introduction: Some of the Cheetaur's claws have been broken off or damaged, apparently in battle.  You remove the remaining claws and put them away in your pack.
序章: 獵豹人的一些爪子已被打斷或損壞，顯然是在戰鬥中。  你移除了剩下的爪子並放進背包。

Introduction: You thought that the Troll might have concealed some of its treasure in that thick beard, but you didn't find any there.
序章: 你原以為巨魔可能在那厚厚的鬍子裡藏了些寶藏，但你什麼都沒找到。

Introduction: Uh oh, this looks like one of the brigands that have been terrorizing the Valley.  Better hope his friends aren't around.
序章: 哎呀，這看起來像是一直在騷擾山谷的山賊中的一個。  最好希望他的朋友不在附近。

Introduction: It's a Cheetaur, a Cheetah Centaur, one of the deadliest creatures in the land.  Hope you've saved your game recently.
序章: 這是獵豹人，一種獵豹半人馬，陸地上最致命的生物之一。  希望你最近存檔了。

Introduction: Oh, no!  It's a fierce (and hungry) looking Saurus Rex (smarter than your average Saurus).
序章: 哦，天哪！  這是一隻兇猛（且飢餓）的索魯斯雷克斯（比普通索魯斯更聰明）。

Introduction: It's an ugly little Goblin.
序章: 這是一隻醜陋的小哥布林。

Introduction: It's dead, of course.
序章: 當然，它已經死了。

Introduction: It doesn't smell very good, either.
序章: 它的味道也不太好。

Introduction: It's a Mantray, a former sea creature that took flight many generations ago and moved to the mountains.  Don't get stung!
序章: 這是魔鬼魟，一種多代前飛上天空並遷移到山脈的海生生物。  小心別被刺中！

Introduction: It's an Ogre, big, mean, and ugly. Ogres are said to eat human flesh.
序章: 這是食人妖，又大又兇又醜。  據說食人妖會吃人肉。

Introduction: It's a Program Bug!
序章: 這是程式漏洞！

Introduction: Don't worry, it's just a little purple Saurus.  Watch out for those teeth though.
序章: 別擔心，只是一隻小紫色索魯斯。  不過要小心那些牙齒。

Introduction: It's a Troll, hard as stone.
序章: 這是巨魔，堅硬如石。

Introduction: You can't help but smell it.
序章: 你不禁聞到了它。

Introduction: You don't know that spell.
序章: 你不知道那個咒語。

Introduction: You cannot escape this encounter.
序章: 你無法逃脫這次遭遇。

Introduction: You cannot make the arcane gestures to cast spells while carrying your shield.
序章: 你無法在持盾牌時做出施法的奧秘手勢。

Introduction: Take care!
序章: 「保重！」

Introduction: Say 'Hi' to the Dryad for us!
序章: 「替我們向樹精問好！」

Introduction: Bye bye!  See ya!
序章: 「拜拜！  回見！」

Introduction: Kiss a tree for me!
序章: 「替我親吻一棵樹！」

Introduction: The gate is securely barred.  It's apparently an Otto-matic gate, since it would take a Goon to open it.
序章: 這扇門被牢牢地鎖住了。  顯然是一個奧圖式自動門，因為需要一個食人妖才能打開它。

Introduction: You'd better not try to climb the wall any more right now.  You've been at this long enough that someone is likely to notice.
序章: 你最好現在別再試著爬牆了。  你已經花了很久時間，可能有人會注意到。

Introduction: The wall is too smooth to climb with your level of skill, but keep practicing.
序章: 以你目前的技能等級，牆面太光滑了，無法攀爬。  但繼續練習吧。

Introduction: The gate is securely barred.  There's no lock for you to open.
序章: 這扇門被牢牢地鎖住了。  沒有讓你打開的鎖。

Introduction: The shades are drawn, and it appears that the Inn is closed.
序章: 窗簾被拉上了，客棧似乎已經關閉了。

Introduction: The windows glow from the light inside. The sign reads "Hero's Tale Inn".
序章: 窗戶從裡面的燈光發出光芒。  招牌上寫著「英雄傳奇客棧」。

Introduction: The door is bolted.
序章: 門被上了栓。

Introduction: You wooden stab that poor defenseless door, wood you?
序章: 你敢用木頭戳那可憐的無防禦之門嗎？

Introduction: Good try, but that isn't an inn key.  (It must be off-key.)
序章: 不錯的嘗試，但那不是客棧鑰匙。  （一定是跑調了。）

Introduction: You see the cheery, inviting glow of a hearth fire within the inn.
序章: 你看到了客棧內壁爐火的溫暖邀請光芒。

Introduction: It's an empty jail cell.  Fortunately, you're on the outside (at the moment).
序章: 這是一間空牢房。  幸運的是，你在外面（目前）。

Introduction: That's the jail cell door.  If you really want to go in, try stealing from someone and getting caught.  (This may not be the best way to become a Hero, though.)
序章: 那是牢房門。  如果你真的想進去，試著從某人那裡偷東西並被抓住。  （但這可能不是成為英雄的最好辦法。）

Introduction: This must be some tough town when they put bars on the jailhouse window.
序章: 如果他們在監獄窗戶上裝欄杆，這肯定是個很強悍的城鎮。

Introduction: The only way you can break this jailhouse window is with a jailhouse rock.
序章: 你打破這個監獄窗戶的唯一方式是用監獄石頭。

Introduction: The sign says "Out to lunch."  The barber must keep odd lunch  hours.
序章: 招牌上說「外出午餐」。  理髮師一定保持著奇怪的午餐時間。

Introduction: There's a sign on the door of the barber shop.  It says "Out to lunch."
序章: 理髮店門上有一個招牌。  上面寫著「外出午餐」。

Introduction: The door is locked.
序章: 門是鎖著的。

Introduction: The sign reads "Hero's Tale Inn"
序章: 招牌上寫著「英雄傳奇客棧」

Introduction: The Sheriff's office doubles as a jail for the town and a hangout for the Sheriff and the Goon.
序章: 治安官辦公室兼作城鎮監獄和治安官與食人妖的聚集地。

Introduction: The Sign of the Scissors above the door indicates that the building contains a Barber Shop.
序章: 門上方的剪刀標誌表示該建築包含理髮店。

Introduction: The town gate is standing wide open, as it does every day.  It is made of sturdy logs.
序章: 城鎮大門像往常一樣敞開著。  它由堅固的木頭製成。

Introduction: That's the Sheriff's job.
序章: 那是治安官的工作。

Introduction: There's more than a hint of Ogre about this strange and bulky character. He seems cheerful, though.
序章: 這個奇怪且笨重的角色身上有超過一絲食人妖的氣息。  不過他似乎很高興。

Introduction: Otto only expresses himself physically.
序章: 「奧圖只通過身體來表達自己。」

Introduction: Spielburg remains colorful, even at night.
序章: 即使在晚上，史畢柏格依然色彩繽紛。

Introduction: This seems like a quiet little town.  On the porch ahead of you are two people.  The standing one is large, rather ugly, and playing with a yo-yo. The seated person smoking a pipe looks like he might be the Sheriff.
序章: 這看起來像是一個安靜的小城鎮。  你前面的門廊上有兩個人。  站著的那個又大又醜，在玩溜溜球。  坐著抽煙鬥的那個人看起來可能是治安官。

Introduction: Just a friendly suggestion, but whatever you do, don't drink the Dragon's Breath.  Not even Otto can drink Dragon's Breath!
序章: 「只是友善的建議，但無論你做什麼，都不要喝龍息。  連奧圖都喝不了龍息！」

Introduction: The man with the pipe greets you.
序章: 那個抽煙鬥的男人向你問好。

Introduction: Welcome to our town.  You are lucky to have made it down from the mountains before the snow blocked the pass again.  It's gotten pretty dangerous outside of town, I understand.
序章: 「歡迎來到我們的城鎮。  你很幸運在雪再次堵住山口之前從山上下來。  據我了解，城鎮外已經變得相當危險。」

Introduction: Many monsters have been trapped around here with the late snow.  Between them and the brigands, we certainly could use a Hero around here.
序章: 「許多怪物隨著遲到的雪被困在這裡。  在他們和山賊之間，我們肯定需要一位英雄。」

Introduction: I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson.  This is Otto Von Goon, my assistant.
序章: 「我是治安官舒爾茨·邁斯特松。  這是我的助手奧圖·馮·食人妖。」

Introduction: Good luck in your quest.
序章: 「祝你冒險順利。」

Introduction: However, this was not a good place to sleep.  You're stiff and sore all over, and your wallet feels very light.
序章: 然而，這不是睡覺的好地方。  你全身僵硬酸痛，錢包感覺輕飄飄的。

Introduction: As safe as our streets are, you still should not sleep on them.  It is a good way to get robbed.
序章: 儘管我們的街道很安全，你還是不應該在上面睡覺。  這是被搶劫的好辦法。

Introduction: This is the door to the Sheriff's office.
序章: 這是治安官辦公室的門。

Introduction: You'd better not. The Goon would probably clobber you with his yo-yo if he thought you were going into the Sheriff's office without permission.
序章: 你最好別這樣做。  如果食人妖認為你在未經許可的情況下進入治安官辦公室，他可能會用他的溜溜球敲打你。

Introduction: The door appears to be locked, and breaking into the Sheriff's office might evoke the wrath of Otto von Goon.
序章: 門似乎被鎖上了，闖入治安官辦公室可能會激怒奧圖·馮·食人妖。

Introduction: Schultz appears to be an affable old coot, content to smoke his pipe. There's a hardness behind his eyes, however, that gives you reason to believe that he's no pushover.
序章: 舒爾茨看起來是個親切的老傢伙，滿足於抽煙鬥。  然而，他的眼睛後面有著堅毅，讓你有理由相信他不是好惹的。

Introduction: Adventure
序章: 冒險

Introduction: The Baron
序章: 男爵

Introduction: The Brigands
序章: 山賊

Introduction: The Castle
序章: 城堡

Introduction: Danger
序章: 危險

Introduction: The Guild Hall
序章: 公會大廳

Introduction: The Inn
序章: 客棧

Introduction: The Merchant
序章: 商人

Introduction: The Mountain
序章: 山脈

Introduction: Prisoners
序章: 囚犯

Introduction: Treasure
序章: 寶藏

Introduction: Wolfgang
序章: 沃夫岡

Introduction: Unhand that sheriff!
序章: 放開那個治安官！

Introduction: You'll need to talk to the Baron about that.  Or go ask Wolfgang down at the Guild Hall, which is just down the  street.
序章: 「你需要和男爵談論這個。  或者去公會大廳找沃夫岡，那就在街道下方。」

Introduction: So maybe you're going to be a hero?  Well, we could surely use one around here, what with all the brigands.
序章: 「所以也許你要成為英雄？  好吧，憑藉這些山賊，我們肯定能用上一個。」

Introduction: Baron Stefan keeps mostly to himself at his castle north of here.  We haven't seen him here in town for years.
序章: 「史蒂芬男爵大部分時間待在他在這裡北邊的城堡裡。  我們多年沒有在城鎮裡看到他了。」

Introduction: There's a whole band of brigands hiding out somewhere in the mountains around us.  They robbed a merchant just last week.  Got a fair amount of treasure, I hear.
序章: 「有一整支山賊隊伍躲在我們周圍的某個山區。  他們上週剛搶劫了一個商人。  據我聽說，他們得到了不少寶藏。」

Introduction: My friend, this world's filled with dangers.  Sometimes I think it might be better to choose your danger, rather than sitting around, waiting for it, as I seem to do.
序章: 「我的朋友，這個世界充滿了危險。  有時我認為選擇你的危險可能更好，而不是像我似乎那樣坐著等待。」

Introduction: But enough of this philosophical rambling.  I am content enough to be Sheriff of Spielburg.
序章: 「但這種哲學性的漫談已經夠了。  我很滿足於成為史畢柏格的治安官。」

Introduction: The Guild Hall is at the southwest corner of town.  If you're looking for work, that's a good place to go.
序章: 「公會大廳位於城鎮的西南角。  如果你在找工作，那是個不錯的去處。」

Introduction: Go talk to the Innkeeper.  He will help you there.
序章: 「去和旅館老闆談談。  他會在那裡幫助你。」

Introduction: Just next to the Inn is a small Magic Shop.  The  owner, Zara, will let you in if you have some abilities in that area.  She's a strange one, all right!
序章: 「就在客棧旁邊是一個小魔法店。  老闆莎拉會在你在那個領域有一些能力的情況下讓你進去。  她確實是個奇怪的傢伙！」

Introduction: The one who was robbed?  He's staying at the Inn, I believe.
序章: 「被搶劫的那個？  我相信他待在客棧。」

Introduction: The monsters come down from the mountains every winter, but they're usually gone by now.  The Baron just doesn't have enough men to handle them.
序章: 「怪物每年冬天都從山上下來，但通常到現在已經消失了。  男爵就是沒有足夠的人手來對付它們。」

Introduction: Wolfgang is the one to talk to about monsters.  He's had a lot of experience with them.
序章: 「沃夫岡是談論怪物的人選。  他與他們打過許多交道。」

Introduction: You should know. You came down from there.
序章: 「你應該知道。  你是從那裡下來的。」

Introduction: I am Sheriff Schultz Meistersson, and this is Otto von Goon, my  assistant.
序章: 「我是治安官舒爾茨·邁斯特松，這是我的助手奧圖·馮·食人妖。」

Introduction: Otto is a big help to me.  He's very smart for a Goon.  His prisoners only suffer a few broken bones when he grabs them.
序章: 「奧圖對我幫助很大。  他對一個食人妖來說非常聰慧。  他抓住囚犯時，他們只會遭受幾處骨折。」

Introduction: It took a bit of doing to teach him to bring prisoners back alive, but he hardly ever forgets, now.
序章: 「花了一些功夫教他活著帶回囚犯，但他現在幾乎不會忘記。」

Introduction: It's simple.  When somone breaks the law, and we catch them, we hold them prisoner.
序章: 「很簡單。  當有人違反法律，我們抓住他們時，我們就把他們關起來。」

Introduction: A policeman's lot is not a happy one.
序章: 「警察的命運並不快樂。」

Introduction: This town was named after the Barons of Spielburg.  It used to be a lively place to be, before the brigands came and drove all the business away.
序章: 「這個城鎮是以史畢柏格的男爵命名的。  在山賊來臨並趕走所有商業活動之前，這曾經是一個充滿活力的地方。」

Introduction: You can find a place to stay at the Inn next door.  You can learn about jobs available at the GuildHall down the street.  If you are interested in magic, you should visit the Magic Shop.  There is still plenty to do and see here in Spielburg.
序章: 「你可以在隔壁的客棧找到住處。  你可以在街道下方的公會大廳了解可用的工作。  如果你對魔法感興趣，你應該參觀魔法店。  在史畢柏格這裡還有很多要做和要看的。」

Introduction: Obtaining treasure involves putting yourself in danger.  If you're brave, foolish, or lucky, you might get some treasure.
序章: 「獲得寶藏涉及冒著生命危險。  如果你勇敢、愚蠢或幸運，你可能會得到一些寶藏。」

Introduction: Wolfgang, down at the Guild hall, is the one to talk to about monsters. He's had a lot of experience with them.
序章: 「位於公會大廳的沃夫岡是談論怪物的人選。  他與他們打過許多交道。」

Introduction: The ugly statue seems to be watching you.
序章: 那尊醜陋的雕像似乎在看著你。

Introduction: As you lay your hands on the ugly statue, a swirling miasma of emotions overcomes you.  You feel the sculptor's pain and suffering, his disgust with his condition, and the conflicting urges to enjoy and flee from his talent.
序章: 當你把手放在那尊醜陋的雕像上時，一股漩渦般的情緒籠罩著你。  你感到雕刻家的痛苦和苦難、他對自己狀況的厭惡，以及享受和逃離他天賦的相互衝突的衝動。

Introduction: No, that's not it.  The real reason for the disgust you feel has more to do with the pigeon droppings on your hands.  You surreptitiously wipe them clean.
序章: 不，那不是。  你感到厭惡的真正原因與你手上的鴿子糞便有關。  你偷偷地擦拭乾淨。

Introduction: The handsome statue seems to be watching you.
序章: 那尊英俊的雕像似乎在看著你。

Introduction: The statue feels cold and clean, and smells faintly of soap.
序章: 雕像摸起來冷而乾淨，隱隱有肥皂的味道。

Introduction: If this is the town water supply, no wonder nobody ever comes here.
序章: 如果這是城鎮的水供應，難怪沒人來這裡。

Introduction: Gosh!  That's an authentic "whamma blamma three scoops of mana hilarious gregarious floats in the airious" yoyo!
序章: 天哪！  那是一個正宗的「呃呃呃三勺法力滑稽合群浮在天空」溜溜球！

Introduction: Are you crazy?  That goon would tear you limb from limb if you tried to steal his yoyo!
序章: 你瘋了嗎？  如果你試圖偷他的溜溜球，那個食人妖會把你撕成碎片！

Introduction: Are you crazy?  That goon would rend your spleen asunder if you did anything to his yoyo!
序章: 你瘋了嗎？  如果你對他的溜溜球做任何事，那個食人妖會把你的脾臟撕裂！

Introduction: Your kindness overwhelms me.  I can tell you will someday be a great hero.
序章: 「你的善良讓我不知所措。  我能看出你將來會成為一位偉大的英雄。」

Introduction: Now you will have a secret.  Among the items they stole from me was a magic rug.  I alone know the words to command it.
序章: 「現在你就擁有了一個秘密。  他們從我這裡偷走的物品中，有一條魔法地毯。  只有我知道驅動它的言語。」

Introduction: If you help recover my treasure, I will take you, Shameen, and Shema back to our land by way of the flying carpet.  Shapeir needs powerful heroes, too!
序章: 「如果你幫我恢復我的寶藏，我會通過飛毯帶著你、沙敏和席瑪回到我們的土地。  夏皮爾也需要強大的英雄！」

Introduction: Oh, it is indeed sad and dangerous times we live in when a man who struggles daily to keep from starving should be robbed of all his earthly possessions!
序章: 「哦，確實這些是悲傷和危險的時代，一個每天都在與挨餓作鬥爭的人被搶劫了他所有的塵世財產！」

Introduction: Hello again, my friend.
序章: 「又見面了，我的朋友。」

Introduction: The band of brigands ambushed us just as we crossed the pass into the valley. There were about twelve brigands, including a huge minotaur.
序章: 「當我們剛進入山谷時，山賊隊伍伏擊了我們。  大約有十二個山賊，包括一隻巨大的牛頭人。」

Introduction: There was also a leader, and some sort of wizard.  They first used some sort of magic which blinded us. Then, they overwhelmed my six guards and my assistants.
序章: 「還有一個首領和某種巫師。  他們首先使用了某種魔法使我們失明。  然後，他們壓倒了我的六名衛兵和助手。」

Introduction: All my trade goods taken from before me!  I am now but a beggar, living off the generosity of my friends.
序章: 「我所有的交易商品都被奪走了！  我現在只是一個乞丐，靠朋友的慷慨生活。」

Introduction: A treasure fit for the Sultan that I found upon my travels.  I alone know the commands which make it fly!
序章: 「我在旅行中發現的適合蘇丹的寶藏。  只有我知道使它飛行的命令！」

Introduction: I will show you how I fly the carpet when I get my treasure back.  Alas, that may never be.  I have lost everything!
序章: 「當我取回我的寶藏時，我會向你展示我如何駕駛地毯。  唉，那可能永遠不會發生。  我失去了一切！」

Introduction: Even though they, too, lost a fortune when the brigands stole from me, Shameen and Shema are caring for this frail shadow of a great man.
序章: 「儘管沙敏和席瑪在山賊從我這裡偷走東西時也失去了一筆財富，他們仍在照顧這位偉大男人的脆弱影子。」

Introduction: Here I pay good money for men to protect me, and what do they do when I need protection?  They run away.
序章: 「我為男人付了很多錢來保護我，當我需要保護時他們做了什麼？  他們逃跑了。」

Introduction: Not one man died to defend my treasure.   You just can't hire good guards any more.
序章: 「沒有一個人為了保護我的寶藏而死。  你已經雇不到好衛兵了。」

Introduction: The leader was wearing a cloak, so I couldn't see his face.  His voice was rather high pitched, though.
序章: 「首領穿著斗篷，所以我看不到他的臉。  但他的聲音相當尖銳。」

Introduction: The Wizard threw something at us that caused my eyes to be blinded with tears and my nose to be forced to sneeze.  I was helpless.
序章: 「巫師向我們投擲了什麼東西，導致我的眼睛被淚水致盲，我的鼻子被迫打噴嚏。  我無能為力。」

Introduction: I trade the treasures of one land for that of another.  But the brigands have stolen every treasure I had and I am a merchant no more.
序章: 「我用一個地方的寶藏換取另一個地方的寶藏。  但山賊偷走了我所有的寶藏，我不再是商人了。」

Introduction: The Minotaur is a creature with the head of a bull and the body of a man. They are said to be very strong.
序章: 「牛頭人是一種有著公牛頭和人類身體的生物。  據說它們非常強大。」

Introduction: I am Abdulla Doo, son of Ali, grandson of Hasan, and former master merchant of Shapeir.  Now I am but a penniless burden upon my friends.
序章: 「我是阿布杜拉·嘟，阿里之子，哈桑之孫，夏皮爾的前首席商人。  現在我只是朋友們的無錢負擔。」

Introduction: Two weeks ago I was going to be wealthy beyond what you could dream of!  I, Abdulla Doo, would be the first merchant into this valley this year.  But my life was shattered by brigands.
序章: 「兩週前我本應變得富有得超乎你的想象！  我阿布杜拉·嘟將是今年第一個進入這個山谷的商人。  但我的生活被山賊摧毀了。」

Introduction: He is the very prince of Kattas.
序章: 「他是卡塔族的真正王子。」

Introduction: Ah, Shapeir, beautiful land of golden sands and shining sun.  The heart of civilization!
序章: 「啊，夏皮爾，美麗的黃金沙子和閃耀陽光的土地。  文明的心臟！」

Introduction: Alas, she is plagued with fierce Djinn and Efreets who seek to drive all men and Kattas from the land.
序章: 「唉，它被兇猛的靈魔和火精所困擾，他們試圖把所有人類和卡塔族趕出這片土地。」

Introduction: But I can speak no more of the homeland I shall never see again.  Instead, I will die in this cold, forsaken land, bereft of all I love.
序章: 「但我再也無法談論我將永遠無法再見到的家鄉。  相反，我將死在這個寒冷、被遺棄的土地上，失去了我所愛的一切。」

Introduction: She is the finest of cooks (outside of myself, that is).  But her fine food is as dust in my mouth, for I am but a beggar.
序章: 「她是最好的廚師(當然除了我自己啦)。不過她做的美食對我來說就像塵埃一樣，因為我已經淪落到乞丐的地步。」

Introduction: I had treasures carefully brought from Shapeir with me.  I even had a Magic Carpet with me!
序章: 「我帶來了從沙佩爾小心翼翼運來的珍寶。我甚至還帶來了一條魔法毯！」

Introduction: The Wizard was very short, and he giggled most of the time.  I couldn't understand what he was saying.
序章: 「那位巫師個子很矮，大部分時間都在咯咯笑。我根本聽不懂他在說什麼。」

Introduction: What did Abdulla do to you that you should do him in?
序章: 你對阿布杜拉做了什麼，以至於他會想害你？

Introduction: The merchant is a rather fat man with a small moustache and beard.  He wears a turban.
序章: 商人是個相當肥胖的傢伙，長著小鬍子和鬍鬚。他戴著頭巾。

Introduction: The merchant gratefully accepts your food ration, and consumes it in record time.
序章: 商人感激地接受你的食物配給，並以創紀錄的速度吃完了它。

Introduction: The merchant gratefully accepts your offer.
序章: 商人感激地接受了你的提議。

Introduction: Abdulla Doo consumes his meal in record time.
序章: 阿布杜拉·嘟以創紀錄的速度吃完了他的飯菜。

Introduction: Gladly will I allow you to buy me a meal, O Prince among Heroes.
序章: 「我很樂意讓你請我吃飯，英雄中的王子啊。」

Introduction: Your offer is gracious, but keep your coins.  Abdulla Doo is no beggar.
序章: 「你的提議雖然慷慨，但把硬幣留著吧。阿布杜拉·嘟不是乞丐。」

Introduction: Your lips say yes, but your purse says no.
序章: 你嘴上說好，但你的錢包卻說不行。

Introduction: Shema, prepare for me the feast of six houris.  I am willing to wait.
序章: 「席瑪啊，給我準備六仙女的盛宴。我願意等。」

Introduction: Shema, prepare for me the thrice brewed tea of the Southern Seas.  I am willing to wait.
序章: 「席瑪啊，給我泡南海的三次釀造茶。我願意等。」

Introduction: Abdulla already has one expensive-sounding order pending.
序章: 阿布杜拉已經有一個聽起來很貴的訂單待處理。

Introduction: Grateful is my parched throat, noble Hero.  Your generosity is unequalled in seven kingdoms.
序章: 「我乾渴的喉嚨感到感激，高尚的英雄。你的慷慨在七個王國裡無人能及。」

Introduction: Carpet
序章: 地毯

Introduction: Commands
序章: 命令

Introduction: Friends
序章: 朋友

Introduction: Merchant
序章: 商人

Introduction: Robbery
序章: 搶劫

Introduction: Shameen
序章: 沙敏

Introduction: Shapeir
序章: 沙佩爾

Introduction: Shema
序章: 席瑪

Introduction: Wealth
序章: 財富

Introduction: Wizard
序章: 巫師

Introduction: The bench is finely made, in contrast to the rough-hewn tables.
序章: 長椅做得很精緻，與粗糙的桌子形成了對比。

Introduction: The odor of cooking food wafts through the curtains.
序章: 烹飪食物的香氣飄過窗簾。

Introduction: The warmth of the fire feels good on such a chilly day.  The proprietor of the inn apparently thinks so, too.  Cats always seem to gravitate to a warm hearth.
序章: 在這樣寒冷的日子裡，火的溫暖令人感到舒服。客棧老闆顯然也這麼認為。貓咪似乎總是喜歡靠近溫暖的爐火。

Introduction: A well-fed fire purrs happily in the fireplace.
序章: 一團燒得很旺的火在壁爐裡快樂地「呼呼」作聲。

Introduction: The cheery firelight reflects off the freshly waxed floor.
序章: 歡樂的火光映照在剛剛打蠟的地板上。

Introduction: You practically licked the plate clean.  That was delicious.
序章: 你幾乎把盤子舔得乾乾淨淨。那真是美味！

Introduction: The food looks unusual, but smells surprisingly good.
序章: 食物看起來很奇怪，但聞起來出奇地好。

Introduction: You look closely and read:  "Genie's lamp for rent.  Inquire in 'Quest for Glory 2:  Trial By Fire' for details."  Oh, it's just another cheap plug.
序章: 你仔細看，讀到：「精靈神燈出租。詳情請參閱《英雄傳奇2：烈火之試》。」唔，又是另一個廉價的廣告。

Introduction: Ouch.
序章: 哎喲！

Introduction: This is the idiot half-brother candle.  (It's only half-bright.)
序章: 這是那個笨蛋半兄弟的蠟燭。(它只有一半亮。)

Introduction: This authentic Polynesian mask is far, far from home.
序章: 這個道地的波利尼西亞面具離家太遠太遠了。

Introduction: This well-used ale tankard has seen fuller days.
序章: 這個用舊了的啤酒杯曾經盛滿過。

Introduction: That well-stuffed pillow looks more comfortable than these hard wooden benches.
序章: 那個鼓鼓囊囊的枕頭看起來比這些硬木板凳舒服多了。

Introduction: Collection of designer plates loaned to the Hero's Inn by Mary Meister.
序章: 由瑪麗·麥斯特先生借給英雄客棧的設計師盤子合集。

Introduction: One of Hiram Plugmeister's double-barreled spitoons.
序章: 海勒姆·普勒格邁斯特先生的雙管痰盂之一。

Introduction: The food tastes surprisingly good, and the drink is delicious.
序章: 食物的味道出奇地好，飲料也很美味。

Introduction: The beverage goes down smoothly and well.
序章: 飲料順利爽快地下了肚。

Introduction: Hey!  Stop that!  What do you think this is: some kind of biker bar?
序章: 嘿！別鬧了！你以為這是什麼？機車酒吧嗎？

Introduction: You take a seat at the table nearest the fire.
序章: 你在最靠近火爐的桌子前坐了下來。

Introduction: You'd better check your skill list.  Ornamental carving isn't on there.
序章: 你最好檢查一下你的技能列表。裡面沒有裝飾雕刻。

Introduction: You see a sign that says, "Please don't touch."  Besides, Shameen is watching you.
序章: 你看到一個牌子上寫著「請勿觸碰」。另外，沙敏正在看著你。

Introduction: That wood never work; the table is too board and logy to respond.  "Planks anyway," you tell it.
序章: 那木頭不會有反應；這桌子太硬、太遲鈍了。「算了，就是塊木板。」你這樣告訴它。

Introduction: This looks like an alpine inn, complete with dishes on the wall and heavy beams on the ceiling.  It is pleasantly warm, and the aroma of strange spices fills the air.
序章: 這看起來像是一家山區客棧，牆上掛著盤子，天花板上有粗重的樑柱。裡面溫暖宜人，奇異香料的香氣充滿了空間。

Introduction: You must pay the innkeeper for a room.
序章: 你必須為房間付錢給客棧老闆。

Introduction: Finish your drink.
序章: 喝完你的飲料。

Introduction: Eat your meal first.
序章: 先吃你的飯菜。

Introduction: You should wait for Shema to bring you what you ordered.
序章: 你應該等席瑪把你點的東西帶過來。

Introduction: This is the brighter candle.
序章: 這是那個比較亮的蠟燭。

Introduction: Welcome!  Welcome, traveler, to the Hero's Tale Inn!  I am Shameen, at your service.  May you find what you seek here.
序章: 「歡迎！歡迎，旅人，來到英雄客棧！我叫沙敏，為你效勞。希望你能在這裡找到你要找的東西。」

Introduction: May you dream of all the rewards you deserve.
序章: 「願你夢到所有你應得的獎勵。」

Introduction: You thank Shameen and pay him 5 silvers for the room.
序章: 你感謝沙敏，為房間付了他 5 枚銀幣。

Introduction: He is a merchant from my homeland of Shapeir, a dear friend and guest here.
序章: 「他是來自我的故鄉沙佩爾的商人，我的好友和客人。」

Introduction: I know little about them except they must be wealthy now.
序章: 「我對他們知之甚少，只知道他們現在一定很有錢。」

Introduction: In my homeland, a caravan is the only way to travel from city to city.
序章: 「在我的故鄉，駝隊是城市間唯一的交通方式。」

Introduction: Very good food, very good drink, finest in town.  Sit, rest, you will be served by my Shema.
序章: 「食物很好，飲料也很好，鎮上最好的。坐著，休息一下，我的席瑪會招待你。」

Introduction: We live far to the south in the desert lands of Shapeir. Someday we may yet return there.
序章: 「我們住在遙遠的南方，沙佩爾的沙漠地帶。也許有一天我們能回到那裡。」

Introduction: This is the Hero's Tale Inn, where Shema and I will be delighted to serve you.
序章: 「這是英雄客棧，席瑪和我都很樂意為你效勞。」

Introduction: The softest and safest of beds, the richest and finest of foods awaits you. You have but to command.
序章: 「最柔軟、最安全的床，最豐富、最精緻的食物等著你。你只需下令。」

Introduction: We are a race of desert dwellers and wanderers of the endless sands.
序章: 「我們是沙漠居民的種族，是無盡沙漠的漂泊者。」

Introduction: Poor Abdulla sits and tries to forget his loss in food and drink.  I think he will not be sorry if you join him at his table.
序章: 「可憐的阿布杜拉坐著，試著用食物和飲料來忘卻他的損失。我想如果你與他一起坐，他不會反對。」

Introduction: My friend, Abdulla, is recovering from his robbery last week. He should be here around suppertime.
序章: 「我的朋友阿布杜拉正在從上週的搶劫中恢復。他應該會在晚餐時間前後到達。」

Introduction: I am Shameen, a poor unfortunate, forced to seek a fortune in this cold land far away from my homeland and my Katta people.
序章: 「我叫沙敏，一個可憐的不幸者，被迫在這個寒冷的地方遠離我的故鄉和卡塔族人去尋求財富。」

Introduction: There is no end to the food that that one can eat!
序章: 「那傢伙能吃的食物根本沒有盡頭！」

Introduction: Alas, the brigands stole all of Abdulla's wealth and mine own when they robbed his caravan.  Now I will be forced to stay in this wet, cold land.
序章: 「唉呀，那些山賊搶劫了他的駝隊時，連阿布杜拉和我的所有財富都偷走了。現在我被迫留在這個濕冷的地方。」

Introduction: Very nice room, very soft beds -- finest in town.  Only five silvers a night.
序章: 「房間很不錯，床也很軟——鎮上最好的。一晚只要 5 枚銀幣。」

Introduction: A land of golden sand and tall mountains, a city of tall buildings and winding streets!
序章: 「一片黃金沙漠和高大的山脈，一座有著高聳建築和蜿蜒街道的城市！」

Introduction: It is our homeland, and a place we long to be.
序章: 「這是我們的故鄉，也是我們渴望回到的地方。」

Introduction: Shema is my beloved, and she can cook and keep an Inn as few others.
序章: 「席瑪是我的摯愛，她的烹飪和經營客棧的本領無人能及。」

Introduction: He comes in with his wife sometimes for food.  He is very wise and generous.
序章: 「他有時會和他的妻子一起來吃飯。他非常聰慧且慷慨。」

Introduction: This is a cold land for a Katta.
序章: 「對卡塔族人來說，這是個寒冷的地方。」

Introduction: Spielburg is a sad place for an Innkeeper, for few travelers visit because of the brigands.
序章: 「史畢柏格對於客棧老闆來說是個悲哀的地方，因為很少有旅人來訪，都是因為那些山賊。」

Introduction: I hoped to return to my homeland with the profits Abdulla, Shema, and I would make from the caravan.  Now, I fear, we will remain here unless someone deals with the brigands and returns our stolen trade items.
序章: 「我原本希望能用駝隊生意的利潤和阿布杜拉、席瑪一起回到故鄉。現在，我擔心，除非有人解決掉那些山賊並歸還我們被盜的商品，否則我們會留在這裡。」

Introduction: You think better of it; those claws look sharp.
序章: 你還是打消了這個念頭；那些爪子看起來很尖銳。

Introduction: You see a small, cat-like creature known as a Katta.  You've heard that Kattas are common in the southern deserts.
序章: 你看到一隻小貓咪似的生物，叫做卡塔族。你聽說過卡塔族人在南方沙漠中很常見。

Introduction: Because your silver is too low, you must find other accomodations.  Perhaps you can find work somewhere.
序章: 因為你的銀幣不夠，你必須尋找其他住處。也許你能在某個地方找到工作。

Introduction: It's too early to go to bed; you don't feel tired enough to sleep.
序章: 現在去睡覺太早了；你還不夠累，睡不著。

Introduction: I dare not offend my wife by letting you rent a room before she has finished serving you.
序章: 「我不敢在我老婆還沒有招待你的時候就讓你租房間而惹惱她。」

Introduction: You are sure that you would not be able to sleep, because you are so hungry.
序章: 你確信你無法入睡，因為你實在太餓了。

Introduction: Very good food, very good drink, finest in town.  Sit, rest, you will be served by my Shema.
序章: 「食物很好，飲料也很好，鎮上最好的。坐著，休息一下，我的席瑪會招待你。」

Introduction: Abdulla
序章: 阿布杜拉

Introduction: Food
序章: 食物

Introduction: Homeland
序章: 故鄉

Introduction: Inn
序章: 客棧

Introduction: Katta
序章: 卡塔族

Introduction: Room
序章: 房間

Introduction: Spielburg
序章: 史畢柏格

Introduction: I bring you that which you ordered.  May it please and satisfy you.
序章: 「我為你帶來了你所點的東西。希望它能取悅並滿足你。」

Introduction: I am Shema.  Allow me to serve you, Wanderer from Afar.  Do you wish food or drink?
序章: 「我叫席瑪。讓我為你效勞，遠方的流浪者。你要食物還是飲料？」

Introduction: Permit me to clear the table for you.
序章: 「請讓我為你收拾桌子。」

Introduction: Abdulla, too, is from the land of Shapeir.
序章: 「阿布杜拉也是來自沙佩爾。」

Introduction: It is true I love to dance, but my soul can not fly so far from home.
序章: 「我確實喜歡跳舞，但我的靈魂無法飛得離家這麼遠。」

Introduction: We have rich black coffee, freshly ground and brewed as only my people do. Such coffee will bring you to your full alertness.  It is but a silver.
序章: 「我們有濃郁的黑咖啡，剛現磨現泡的，只有我們卡塔族人才這樣做。這樣的咖啡能讓你保持完全清醒。只要一枚銀幣。」

Introduction: We have very good stew of newborn lamb along with honey chicken for a mere three silvers.  Coffee is included in the price of the meal.
序章: 「我們有很好的羊羔燉菜和蜂蜜雞，只要 3 枚銀幣。咖啡已經包括在飯菜的價格裡了。」

Introduction: Our homeland is far to the south.  It is called Shapeir.
序章: 「我們的故鄉遠在南方。叫沙佩爾。」

Introduction: Katta are a quiet, gentle people.
序章: 「卡塔族人是個安靜、溫柔的民族。」

Introduction: Abdulla will always be our guest and friend.
序章: 「阿布杜拉永遠是我們的客人和朋友。」

Introduction: I am Shema, which means in the words of the Katta, 'She of the Dance.'
序章: 「我叫席瑪，用卡塔族的語言，意思是『舞者』。」

Introduction: It is Shameen who is the speaker for the house, for I have much work to do to serve you.
序章: 「家裡的發言人是沙敏，因為我還有很多工作要做來招待你。」

Introduction: If you wish to know more about such things, then you should speak to him.
序章: 「如果你想知道更多這方面的事，你應該和他說。」

Introduction: Abdulla will be honored to tell you of that.
序章: 「阿布杜拉會很樂意為你講述那些。」

Introduction: You have but to give Shameen five silvers, and you may have a bed to dream upon.
序章: 「你只需給沙敏 5 枚銀幣，就能有張床來躺著做夢。」

Introduction: He is my soulmate.
序章: 「他是我的靈魂伴侶。」

Introduction: Shapeir is where we Katta come from.  It is warm and glowing and the sun is always bright.
序章: 「沙佩爾是我們卡塔族人的故鄉。那裡溫暖閃亮，陽光永遠燦爛。」

Introduction: Her claws look too sharp for you to risk her (or her husband's) wrath.
序章: 她的爪子看起來太尖了，你不敢冒她(或她丈夫)的怒火。

Introduction: You see a rather attractive female Katta wearing her native costume.
序章: 你看到一隻長得相當漂亮的母卡塔族人，穿著她的民族服裝。

Introduction: One more cup of coffee and you'll be up all night.  Better quit now.
序章: 再來一杯咖啡你就整晚睡不著了。還是現在戒了吧。

Introduction: You caffeine addicts are all alike.
序章: 你們這些咖啡因上癮的傢伙都一樣。

Introduction: There's no need.  You're not hungry.
序章: 沒必要。你不餓。

Introduction: You pay Shema for a meal.
序章: 你付錢給席瑪買飯菜。

Introduction: You decide not to buy that.
序章: 你決定不買那個了。

Introduction: You are ravenous and immediately pay for a meal.
序章: 你非常飢餓，立刻付錢買飯菜。

Introduction: Dance
序章: 舞蹈

Introduction: Drink
序章: 飲料

Introduction: Questions
序章: 問題

Introduction: That door leads to the sleeping rooms.  If you desire a room, it will cost a mere 5 silvers.
序章: 「那扇門通向睡眠室。如果你想要一間房間，只要 5 枚銀幣。」

Introduction: A door to some other room.  Perhaps the proprietor can tell you what's in there.
序章: 通往其他房間的門。也許客棧老闆能告訴你裡面有什麼。

Introduction: You are too polite to enter the kitchen uninvited.
序章: 你太禮貌了，不敢未經邀請進入廚房。

Introduction: The odor of cooking wafts through the curtains.
序章: 烹飪食物的香氣飄過窗簾。

Introduction: The table feels rough to the touch, but very solid.
序章: 桌子摸起來很粗糙，但非常牢固。

Introduction: The tables look like they have been rough-cut from some of the local pines.
序章: 這些桌子看起來像是用當地松木粗糙地切割而成的。

Introduction: The coffee is black as night, hot as hell, and sweet as a lover's kiss.
序章: 咖啡黑如夜晚，熱如地獄，甜如戀人之吻。

Introduction: You see an assortment of tools used to maintain the fire.
序章: 你看到各種用來維護火爐的工具。

Introduction: The sleep heals and refreshes you.
序章: 睡眠治癒並恢復了你的精力。

Introduction: You can just see a thin thread of light under the door, so there is probably someone inside.
序章: 你隱約看到門下傳來一絲光線，所以可能裡面有人。

Introduction: The Guild Hall building appears to be dark and closed up.
序章: 冒險者公會的建築看起來很暗，而且關門了。

Introduction: You enter the Adventurers' Guild Hall.
序章: 你進入了冒險者公會大廳。

Introduction: The door appears to be locked.
序章: 門似乎是鎖著的。

Introduction: Your dirk doesn't work on the door.
序章: 你的短匕首在這扇門上沒有用。

Introduction: That key doesn't fit this lock.
序章: 那把鑰匙不適合這個鎖。

Introduction: Judging from what the sign says, the building at the end of the street is the Guild Hall.
序章: 根據標誌上的內容判斷，街道盡頭的建築就是冒險者公會。

Introduction: This looks like a nice, neat little house.  There is a grandmotherly Little Old Lady rocking on the front porch.
序章: 這看起來是一棟漂亮、整潔的小房子。有一位溫暖如祖母般的老奶奶在前廊搖椅上搖晃。

Introduction: The house looks closed up.  There is no sign of the Little Old Lady.
序章: 房子看起來關門了。看不到那位老奶奶。

Introduction: The door appears to be locked, and there is no answer to your knock.  The occupant is out (right in front of you, in fact).
序章: 門看起來被鎖上了，敲門也沒人應。住戶不在（其實就在你正前方）。

Introduction: No one seems to be home, and the door is securely locked.
序章: 似乎沒人在家，門也鎖得很牢。

Introduction: This isn't the best place to practice for your woodcarving merit badge.
序章: 這裡不是練習木雕獎章的最好地方。

Introduction: The key doesn't fit.
序章: 鑰匙不適合。

Introduction: She's the only sane person here.  At least she's not off her rocker.
序章: 她是這裡唯一清醒的人。至少她還沒失心瘋。

Introduction: The little old lady is sleeping so soundly that no amount of shaking will awaken her.
序章: 這位老奶奶睡得很沉，再怎麼搖也叫不醒她。

Introduction: She's really sleeping soundly and doesn't notice your presence.
序章: 她睡得真沉，沒注意到你的存在。

Introduction: ZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ!
序章: 「呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼！」

Introduction: Grmpf..snrt..hmphspft!
序章: 「唔哦…唔…唔普！」

Introduction: The eye over the Magic Shop door seems to be watching you.
序章: 魔法店門上的眼睛似乎在盯著你看。

Introduction: However, this was not a good place to sleep.  You're stiff and sore all over and your wallet feels very light.
序章: 不過，這裡不是睡覺的好地方。你全身酸痛，錢包也變輕了不少。

Introduction: Picking locks looked a lot easier in the instruction book.
序章: 開鎖在教科書裡看起來容易多了。

Introduction: The door isn't locked.  Just turn around.
序章: 門沒鎖。轉身就行了。

Introduction: The owners seem to have been made nervous by the recent rash of house burglaries.  The door is barred from the inside.  But you do get some valuable lock-picking practice.
序章: 業主似乎被最近頻繁的入室盜竊嚇得神經兮兮。門從裡面被擋住了。但你確實獲得了寶貴的開鎖練習。

Introduction: You hear a "Snick!".  The lock is open!
序章: 你聽到了「卡！」一聲。鎖打開了！

Introduction: You have come to the end of the main street.  The town wall is to the south. You have an eerie feeling that someone is watching you.
序章: 你來到了主街的盡頭。城牆在南邊。你有種被誰盯著的怪異感覺。

Introduction: There's no getting around it... a shop with its eye on you makes you nervous.
序章: 無可奈何…一家眼睛盯著你的店鋪讓你神經緊張。

Introduction: Not the sturdiest lock you've ever seen.
序章: 不是你見過最堅固的鎖。

Introduction: This is certainly a weird one!  You've never seen anything quite like it.
序章: 這肯定很詭異！你從未見過這樣的東西。

Introduction: The plaque reads: "Antwerp -- slain by Two Guys From Andromeda".
序章: 銘牌上寫著：「安特衛普——被仙女座來的兩個傢伙殺死」。

Introduction: A past adventurer's armor suit.  You wonder why he doesn't need it any more. (Then you see the Cheetaur claw marks.)
序章: 一套過去冒險者的盔甲。你想知道他為什麼不再需要它。（然後你看到了獵豹人的爪痕。）

Introduction: The armor won't fit you -- they made them smaller in those days.
序章: 盔甲不適合你——他們那時做得比較小。

Introduction: On the table is an old but ornate leather-bound book.  There is also a quill pen in the ink bottle.
序章: 桌上有一本舊但華麗的皮革精裝書。墨瓶裡還有一支鵝毛筆。

Introduction: The writing on this page is very recent.  It says "I, %s, have come to Spielburg to become a hero."
序章: 這頁上的字跡很新。它寫著「我，%s，來到史畢柏格成為英雄。」

Introduction: This entry was made several years ago. It says: 'Baronet Barnard von Spielburg killed a Troll near the Flying Falls on this 23rd day of Octember'.
序章: 這項記錄是幾年前寫的。它寫著：「子爵巴納德·馮·史畢柏格在十月二十三日在飛瀑附近殺死了一隻巨魔」。

Introduction: The other pages record the exploits of the great adventurers of Spielburg's past.  The names Wolfgang Abenteuer and Baron Stefan von Spielburg appear most often.
序章: 其他頁面記錄了史畢柏格過去偉大冒險者的事蹟。沃夫岡·阿本特爾和史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵的名字最常出現。

Introduction: The remaining pages are blank, awaiting further tales of adventure.
序章: 剩下的頁面是空白的，等待更多冒險故事。

Introduction: You sign your name into the Adventurer's Log Book with a flourish.
序章: 你在冒險者日誌中簽下你的名字，做得很瀟灑。

Introduction: But you've already done that!
序章: 但你已經做過了！

Introduction: The fine crystal chandelier lights the room.
序章: 精緻的水晶吊燈照亮了房間。

Introduction: You can't reach it.
序章: 你摸不著。

Introduction: The head is like a panther's, but with a strong human-like quality.  It is still rather frightening.
序章: 這頭像豹子的頭，但帶著強烈的人類特質。看起來還是很嚇人。

Introduction: The plaque reads: "Cheetaur -- slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
序章: 銘牌上寫著：「獵豹人——被沃夫岡·阿本特爾殺死」。

Introduction: The table holds a pen, an inkwell, and the Adventurer's Guild Registration and Log Book.
序章: 桌上放著一支筆、墨瓶和冒險者公會登記冊及日誌。

Introduction: The table is formed from fine fir wood.  There's nothing you need here.
序章: 桌子是用優質冷杉木製成的。這裡沒有你需要的東西。

Introduction: Even in death, this monster remains awesome.
序章: 即使死了，這隻怪物仍然令人敬畏。

Introduction: The plaque reads: "Dragon -- slain by Baron Stefan von Spielburg".
序章: 銘牌上寫著：「龍——被史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵殺死」。

Introduction: The fire blazing cheerily in the fireplace helps warm the Guild Master's old bones.
序章: 壁爐裡歡快燃燒的火焰幫助溫暖了公會主人的老骨頭。

Introduction: You feel like you're frying your fingers.  Forget it.
序章: 你覺得你的手指要烤焦了。算了。

Introduction: This crossbreed of eagle and lion could have torn a man apart when it was alive.
序章: 這隻鷲獅雜交獸活著的時候可能能撕裂一個人。

Introduction: The plaque reads: "Gryphon -- slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
序章: 銘牌上寫著：「獅鷲——被沃夫岡·阿本特爾殺死」。

Introduction: The plaque under the Moose says: "Courtesy of the Sierra Online Prop Dept."
序章: 駝鹿下的銘牌寫著：「蒙《Sierra Online》道具部提供」。

Introduction: The tapestry is so rotten, it would probably fall apart at the slightest touch.
序章: 掛毯爛得很厲害，稍微一碰可能就會散開。

Introduction: The tapestry feels very old and very fragile.
序章: 掛毯摸起來非常古老，也非常脆弱。

Introduction: This Adventurer's Guild Hall reminds you of the one in your home town.  The traditional Moose head and other stuffed monsters (Saurus, Troll, Gryphon, Dragon, Cheetaur, and the terrible Antwerp) adorn the walls.
序章: 這個冒險者公會大廳讓你想起你家鄉的那個。傳統的駝鹿頭和其他標本怪物（索魯斯、巨魔、獅鷲、龍、獵豹人和可怕的安特衛普）裝飾著牆壁。

Introduction: You see a registration book on the table, and the bulletin board full of job listings.  The man seated near the window must be the Guild Master.  He is snoring.
序章: 你看到桌上有本登記冊，公告板上滿是工作清單。坐在窗邊的男人一定是公會主人。他在打呼。

Introduction: There is nothing here that you need, and the Guild Master may be more aware than he seems.
序章: 這裡沒有你需要的東西，公會主人可能比看起來更清醒。

Introduction: The Guild Hall would be barren if you took the trophies.  Besides, they're out of reach.
序章: 如果你拿走獎杯，公會大廳就會空蕩蕩的。而且，它們也夠不著。

Introduction: You never saw a purple Saurus before you came to Spielburg, but it looks like a really stupid monster.
序章: 來史畢柏格之前，你從未見過紫色的索魯斯，但它看起來像一隻非常愚蠢的怪物。

Introduction: The plaque reads: "Saurus slain by Hans Halfwitten".
序章: 銘牌上寫著：「索魯斯被漢斯·哈夫威騰殺死」。

Introduction: The view from here isn't exactly clear.
序章: 從這裡看的景色不太清楚。

Introduction: It looks like it must have been a particularly nasty Troll.  You wouldn't want to meet him in a dark forest.
序章: 看起來這一定是隻特別凶惡的巨魔。你不會想在暗森林裡遇見他。

Introduction: The plaque reads: "Troll slain by Wolfgang Abenteuer".
序章: 銘牌上寫著：「巨魔被沃夫岡·阿本特爾殺死」。

Introduction: On the walls hang dusty, moth-eaten monster heads.  You recognize the purple Saurus, the Troll, the Gryphon, and the Dragon.
序章: 牆上掛著佈滿灰塵、被蟲蛀的怪物頭顱。你認出了紫色的索魯斯、巨魔、獅鷲和龍。

Introduction: The window looks out over the countryside.
序章: 窗戶俯瞰鄉村。

Introduction: The window doesn't open.
序章: 窗戶打不開。

Introduction: Welcome back!  It is good to see you again.  So many adventurers never return.  Someday, you and I will go on an adventure together.
序章: 「歡迎回來！很高興再看到你。那麼多冒險者都再也回不來了。有一天，你和我會一起去冒險。」

Introduction: Hrrmph!  Ahem!  Yes, as I was saying...  Where were we?
序章: 「呃唔！咳！是的，我剛才說到…我們說到哪裡了？」

Introduction: Oh, what a glorious day it was!  I...  Oh, hello there!
序章: 「噢，那是多麼光榮的一天啊！我…噢，你好！」

Introduction: You fiendish witch!  I will have you...  Oh, it's you, my friend!
序章: 「你這邪惡的女巫！我會…噢，是你啊，我的朋友！」

Introduction: Oh, Barnard, bravest of the brave, you cannot be gone from us so soon... What?  What?  You're still here, my fellow adventurer?
序章: 「噢，巴納德，最勇敢的勇者，你不能這麼快就離開我們…什麼？什麼？你還在這裡，我的冒險家朋友？」

Introduction: ZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZZ.
序章: 「呼呼呼呼呼呼呼呼。」

Introduction: Ach!  I was so busy I didn't notice you come in.  Welcome, welcome! It is so seldom that we have new adventurers here.  Most people think this valley is cursed!
序章: 「哎呀！我太忙了，沒注意到你進來。歡迎，歡迎！我們這裡很少有新冒險者。大多數人都認為這個山谷被詛咒了！」

Introduction: It looks like this old Guild Master doesn't do too much adventuring any more. Still, he looks like he was plenty tough in his day, and he probably has many a tale to tell.
序章: 看起來這位年邁的公會主人不再進行太多冒險了。不過，他看起來在年輕時很強硬，他可能有很多故事要講。

Introduction: Adventurers
序章: 冒險者

Introduction: Antwerp
序章: 安特衛普

Introduction: Bakery
序章: 麵包店

序章: 公告板

序章: 屠夫

序章: 半人馬

序章: 獵豹人

序章: 男爵之女
