序章: 龍

Introduction: Forests
序章: 森林

Introduction: Dry Goods
序章: 雜貨

Introduction: Goon
序章: 打手

Introduction: Guild Hall
序章: 公會大廳

Introduction: Hans Halfwitten
序章: 漢斯·哈夫威騰

Introduction: Hero
序章: 英雄

Introduction: Magic Shop
序章: 魔法店

Introduction: Mantrays
序章: 曼崔斯

Introduction: Guild Master
序章: 公會主人

Introduction: Moose
序章: 駝鹿

Introduction: Mountains
序章: 山脈

Introduction: Ogres
序章: 食人妖

Introduction: Saurus Rex
序章: 索魯斯王

Introduction: Saurus
序章: 索魯斯

Introduction: Schultz Meistersson
序章: 舒爾茨·麥斯特松

Introduction: Baron's Son
序章: 男爵之子

Introduction: Tavern
序章: 酒館

Introduction: Tourists
序章: 觀光客

Introduction: Troll
序章: 巨魔

Introduction: Trophies
序章: 獎杯

Introduction: Waterfalls
序章: 瀑布

Introduction: The guildmaster appears to be hard of hearing.  Perhaps you should get closer.
序章: 公會主人似乎聽力不太好。也許你應該靠近一些。

Introduction: Ach, I was so busy I did not notice you come into the Adventurer's Guild Hall.  Welcome, welcome, it is so seldom we have new adventurers here.  Most people think this valley is cursed.
序章: 「哎呀，我太忙了，沒注意到你進入冒險者公會大廳。歡迎，歡迎，我們這裡很少有新冒險者。大多數人都認為這個山谷被詛咒了。」

Introduction: Hrrmph!  Ahem!  Yes, as I was saying...  Where were we?
序章: 「呃唔！咳！是的，我剛才說到…我們說到哪裡了？」

Introduction: Oh, what a glorious day it was!  I...  Oh, hello there.
序章: 「噢，那是多麼光榮的一天啊！我…噢，你好。」

Introduction: Then she turned around and I was staring into the biggest...  Oh, it's you, my friend.
序章: 「然後她轉過身來，我盯著最大的…噢，是你啊，我的朋友。」

Introduction: It was just yesterday that I drew my sword and started out...  Or was that the day before?  No, then last week...  Why, What was I talking about?
序章: 「那是昨天啊，我拔劍出發…或是前天？不，上週的事…為什麼，我在說什麼呢？」

Introduction: Try getting a little closer.
序章: 試著靠近一些。

Introduction: Did I ever tell you about the time when Schultz and I rid this valley of Antwerps?  Ja, we were real adventurers then, and this was a real Guild Hall. Now we are just old men and this is just a place to tell old stories.
序章: 「我有沒有告訴過你，舒爾茨和我清除這個山谷裡安特衛普的那段日子？是的，我們那時是真正的冒險者，這也是真正的公會大廳。現在我們只是老人，這只是個講老故事的地方。」

Introduction: This valley was overrun by those odd monsters one year.  Schultz and I fought long and hard to rid the valley of the things.  We might have failed even so, had it not been for those two peculiar tourists who came to our aid.
序章: 「有一年，這個山谷被那些古怪的怪物佔領了。舒爾茨和我努力戰鬥來清除山谷中的那些東西。即使如此，我們可能已經失敗了，但多虧了那兩位奇特的觀光客來幫助我們。」

Introduction: She is the center of our problems, I think.  Baba Yaga is a powerful Ogre. Baron von Spielburg tried to force her to leave this valley, but she cursed him.
序章: 「我認為她是我們問題的中心。芭芭雅嘎是一個強大的食人妖。史畢柏格男爵試圖強迫她離開這個山谷，但她詛咒了他。」

Introduction: Now the Baron has lost everything but his land, and I don't know how long he will keep that.  What our valley needs is a Hero.
序章: 「現在男爵失去了一切，只剩下他的土地，我不知道他能保留多久。我們的山谷需要的是一位英雄。」

Introduction: I think the Bakery is almost always closed now.  The Baker tries to stay away from his wife.
序章: 「我認為麵包店現在幾乎總是關門的。麵包師傅試圖遠離他的妻子。」

Introduction: Baron von Spielburg was once a brave protector of our valley.  We used to adventure together when I was younger.
序章: 「史畢柏格男爵曾經是我們山谷的勇敢保護者。我年輕時，我們常常一起冒險。」

Introduction: No brigand or monster would dare show his face here if the Baron had not angered Baba Yaga.  Now it is said he goes nowhere and sees no one.
序章: 「如果男爵沒有惹怒芭芭雅嘎，沒有山賊或怪物敢在這裡露面。現在據說他哪裡也不去，誰都不見。」

Introduction: Over on the wall over there is where the jobs are posted for adventurers. Some have been there a long time now.  There just aren't too many good adventurers around here, lately.
序章: 「那邊牆上就是發佈冒險家工作的地方。有些工作已經貼在那裡很久了。最近這裡就是沒有太多優秀的冒險者。」

Introduction: There is a reward for anyone who can stop the brigands by capturing or killing the leaders of the brigands.  You should read the bulletin board for more information.
序章: 「任何能夠通過抓捕或殺死山賊頭目來制止山賊的人都會獲得獎勵。你應該看看公告板以獲得更多信息。」

Introduction: I don't think the Butcher Shop is open much any more, either.
序章: 「我認為屠夫店現在也很少開門了。」

Introduction: The castle is just north of the Healer's house.
序章: 「城堡就在治療師房子的北邊。」

Introduction: There are not too many Centaurs in this land.   Heinrich and Hilde are good people.
序章: 「這片土地上的半人馬不太多。海因里希和希爾德是好人。」

Introduction: Watch out for Cheetaurs!  I bare the scars of my fight with the one on the wall to this day.  Still, it is stuffed, and I am still alive.
序章: 「小心獵豹人！我至今還留著和牆上那一隻戰鬥的疤痕。不過，它是標本，我還活著。」

Introduction: Ja, with the Baron losing his son and daughter, Baba Yaga, all the monsters, and now the brigands, it has just been one thing after another.
序章: 「是啊，男爵失去了他的兒子和女兒、還有芭芭雅嘎、所有怪物，現在又有山賊，真是接二連三。」

Introduction: Elsa  was a beautiful eight year old child with blond hair and sky  blue eyes.  She was her father the Baron's joy.  Ten years ago, she was carried off by something which flew over the wall and away with her.
序章: 「艾爾莎是個美麗的八歲小孩，有著金色的頭髮和湛藍的眼睛。她是她父親男爵的喜悅。十年前，她被某種飛越城牆的東西帶走了。」

Introduction: The search for her lasted many years, but at last everyone gave up except the Baron's Jester, Yorick.
序章: 「尋找她持續了許多年，但最後除了男爵的弄臣尤瑞克外，所有人都放棄了。」

Introduction: One day years ago, a pair of dragons tried to take over our valley.  We adventurers rode out to meet them.
序章: 「多年前的某一天，一對龍試圖接管我們的山谷。我們冒險者騎馬出去迎戰他們。」

Introduction: I can still see Stefan von Spielburg charging forward on his black horse. He slew that dragon whose head you see on the wall, and the other one flew off.
序章: 「我仍然可以看到史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格騎著黑馬衝向前去。他殺死了你在牆上看到的那條龍，另一條飛走了。」

Introduction: I hear people see that other dragon flying high overhead sometimes, but it has never dared to attack us again.
序章: 「我聽說人們有時會看到那另一條龍在頭頂高飛，但它從未敢再攻擊我們。」

Introduction: The forests are full of monsters and strange creatures.
序章: 「森林裡滿是怪物和奇異的生物。」

Introduction: There are some nice waterfalls to the  southeast of town.  They fill Mirror Lake, which is lovely, but icy cold all year round.
序章: 「鎮南方有幾道漂亮的瀑布。它們注入鏡湖，景色宜人，不過一年四季都冷得要命。」

Introduction: Caspar at the store would like to be an adventurer, or so he says. He spends too much time dreaming and not enough doing, I think.
序章: 「店裡的卡斯帕說他想成為冒險者。我看他光做夢的時間太多，真正付諸行動的太少了。」

Introduction: Goblins are not tough, but when they gang up, they can take out an unwary adventurer.
序章: 「哥布林單隻不難對付，但當它們聚在一起時，就能幹掉不小心的冒險者。」

Introduction: A Goon is a lot like an Ogre, not as tough, but a little smarter.  The Sheriff's Otto and Crusher at the tavern are both Goons.
序章: 「古恩有點像食人妖，不那麼強悍，但聰明一點。治安官的奧圖和客棧裡的克拉什都是古恩。」

Introduction: This is where an adventurer can find out who needs someone brave and courageous.  There are many jobs on the bulletin board over there.  It is also a good place to talk about adventures on a cold afternoon.
序章: 「這是冒險者能找到誰需要勇敢者的地方。那邊的布告欄上有許多工作。冷天下午也是談論冒險故事的好地方。」

Introduction: We used to play cards here once a week as well, but there are too few adventurers in Spielburg anymore.  They all died from monsters or brigands, or they just got too old.
序章: 「我們也曾每週在這裡打一次牌，但史畢柏格裡冒險者太少了。他們都死在怪物或山賊手下，要不就老到不行了。」

Introduction: He wasn't much of an adventurer, but he did manage to kill a Saurus before he made the mistake of tangling with a Troll.
序章: 「他不是什麼偉大的冒險者，但他確實殺過一隻索魯斯，可惜他後來犯了個錯誤，惹上了巨魔。」

Introduction: Her house is just north of the town gate.  She's a nice woman.
序章: 「她的房子在城鎮大門北邊。她是個好女人。」

Introduction: A real Hero is someone who didn't start out strong or powerful, but uses his courage and brains and skills to become the best he can be.
序章: 「真正的英雄不是天生就強大，而是用勇氣、頭腦和技能來成為最好的自己。」

Introduction: I don't know much about magic, but the woman who runs that place scares me.
序章: 「我對魔法不太了解，但經營那地方的女人把我嚇到了。」

Introduction: Those weird things are magical.  They are best avoided.
序章: 「那些怪東西是有魔法的。最好別碰它們。」

Introduction: The Farmer's Mart is run by the Centaurs, Heinrich Pferdefedern and his daughter, Hilde.
序章: 「農夫市集由半人馬海因里希·佩德費德恩和他的女兒希爾德管理。」

Introduction: I am supposed to keep track of all the adventurers and make sure they sign their names in the log.  There was a time when I would go out and rescue foolhardy adventurers who got into too much trouble, but I don't get out much now.
序章: 「我得追蹤所有冒險者，確保他們在日誌上簽名。曾經有段時間我會出去救被困的冒險者，但現在我很少出門了。」

Introduction: Cheetaurs are ferocious creatures that charge out of nowhere and try to tear you to shreds.  Sometimes they succeed.
序章: 「獵豹人是兇悍的傢伙，會從天而降，想把你撕成碎片。有時候它們真的會成功。」

Introduction: You can see some of the types of monsters that live around here if you look at our walls.  There are Trolls, Griffins, Cheetaurs, Mantrays, and Goblins wandering in the woods.
序章: 「你在牆上可以看到這一帶棲息的怪物。巨魔、獅鷲、獵豹人、曼特雷和哥布林在森林裡遊蕩。」

Introduction: I hear there are even some Ogres and Saurus Rexes back in this valley. There was a time when Schultz and I had gotten rid of most of the monsters around here, but we are just too old for that now.
序章: 「我聽說山谷裡還有食人妖和巨型索魯斯。從前舒爾茨和我清除了這一帶大部分的怪物，但現在我們年紀太大了。」

Introduction: That was the most vicious moose I ever saw.  Nearly bit my nose off.
序章: 「那是我見過最兇的麋鹿。差點咬掉我的鼻子。」

Introduction: The Mountains surrounding this valley are very high.  Snow covers the tops all year long.  The only way out of Spielburg Valley is through the pass, though I hear that it is blocked by snow again.
序章: 「環繞山谷的群山非常高聳。山頂一年到頭都被積雪覆蓋。離開史畢柏格山谷的唯一途徑是經過山口，不過我聽說又被雪封住了。」

Introduction: I am Wolfgang Abenteuer, the Guild Master of this hall.
序章: 「我是沃爾夫剛·阿班圖爾，這個大廳的公會總長。」

Introduction: Ogres are a lot like Goons, but even meaner.  Not as bright, though.
序章: 「食人妖類似古恩，但更兇殘。不過沒那麼聰明。」

Introduction: I like Otto, even if he is a Goon.  Schultz has him almost trained.
序章: 「我喜歡奧圖，雖然他是個古恩。舒爾茨差不多把他馴服了。」

Introduction: You need to talk to either the Baron or the Healer about that.
序章: 「關於這事，你得和男爵或治療師談談。」

Introduction: Don't mistake those things for Sauruses.   When they come at you, it is sometimes best just to run away.
序章: 「別把那些東西誤認為索魯斯。它們來襲時，有時候跑才是上策。」

Introduction: I remember when Hans killed the one there.  It was a tough fight, for him.
序章: 「我記得漢斯在那裡殺過一隻。對他來說那是場硬仗。」

Introduction: Schultz Meistersson and I have been friends for a long time.  He, too, was once a real adventurer.  Now he is just the Sheriff of this town.
序章: 「舒爾茨·邁斯特松和我是老友。他也曾是真正的冒險者。現在他只是這個鎮的治安官。」

Introduction: Now, there was a hero, and worthy of the name Barnard von Spielburg. He rode off to hunt one morning five years ago and his horse returned with large claw marks on it.  No sign Barnard's body was ever found.
序章: 「說到英雄，巴納德·馮·史畢柏格就算得上名副其實。五年前某個早上他騎馬去打獵，回來的只有馬，馬身上還有大爪痕。從未找到巴納德的屍體。」

Introduction: Spielburg Town is small now that most people have been driven off by the Brigands.  This is such a pretty place, too, with the mountains, forests, and waterfalls.
序章: 「史畢柏格鎮現在很小，大多數人都被山賊趕跑了。這本來是個漂亮的地方，有山、森林和瀑布。」

Introduction: I have never liked that place.  The bartender smiles all the time, but he is not very friendly.  Crusher lives up to his name.  He is nasty even for a Goon.
序章: 「我從沒喜歡過那地方。酒保整天都在笑，但不太友善。克拉什活得起他的名字。即使是古恩，他也特別凶惡。」

Introduction: They said they were from some place called Andromeda.  Must be someplace south of here, I guess.  They certainly were odd.
序章: 「他們說來自叫安卓美達的地方。大概在這兒南邊吧。他們確實很古怪。」

Introduction: This town used to be a thriving city before the brigands drove most of the people off.
序章: 「這座鎮在山賊趕走大多數人之前，曾是個繁榮的城市。」

Introduction: Trolls are very tough.  Fortunately, they only come out at night.  The woods are very dangerous at night.
序章: 「巨魔非常強悍。幸好它們只在夜間出沒。森林在夜間非常危險。」

Introduction: Schultz and I killed most of the monsters on those walls.
序章: 「舒爾茨和我殺了牆上大部分怪物。」

Introduction: The Saurus is a monster easy to avoid and easy to kill for a skilled adventurer.
序章: 「索魯斯是種容易躲避、技巧高超的冒險者容易對付的怪物。」

Introduction: I remember the day I killed one.  It was the biggest I had ever seen.  It put up a tough fight, but I was tougher.  You can see the results on the wall.
序章: 「我記得我殺死一隻的那天。那是我見過最大的。它打得很兇，但我更兇。你可以在牆上看到戰績。」

Introduction: There are some nice waterfalls to the southeast of town.  They fill Mirror Lake, which is lovely, but icy cold all year round.
序章: 「鎮南方有幾道漂亮的瀑布。它們注入鏡湖，景色宜人，不過一年四季都冷得要命。」

Introduction: Yorick was a funny little man, but brave.  He swore he would spend his life searching for the Baron's Elsa.
序章: 「尤瑞克是個有趣的小個子，但很勇敢。他發誓要用一生去尋找男爵的艾爾莎。」

Introduction: A pile of wood is stacked against the wall.
序章: 一堆木頭靠著牆堆放。

Introduction: The firewood will serve more purpose here than in your pack.
序章: 柴火在這兒比在你背包裡用處更大。

Introduction: The knitting bag is decorated on the outside with knitted figures.  The Little Old Lady must do knitting when she's not asleep.
序章: 編織袋外面裝飾著編織人物。小老太太沒睡覺時定在織東西。

Introduction: This is not the best place to practice dagger throwing.
序章: 這不是練習匕首投擲的好地方。

Introduction: The birdcage is on a tall brass stand.  There is a cover draped over it to keep the bird warm and quiet.
序章: 鳥籠放在高的黃銅架上。上面蓋著布以保持鳥溫暖和安靜。

Introduction: Who would think a little birdie could be so noisy!
序章: 誰能想到一隻小鳥能這麼吵！

Introduction: CHIRP! CHIRP!
序章: 唧唧！唧唧！

Introduction: You quickly cover the cage and hope the bird shuts up.
序章: 你迅速蓋住籠子，希望鳥能閉嘴。

Introduction: You hear a voice from upstairs saying, "Kitty, you'd better not be bothering the little birdie again!"
序章: 你聽到樓上有聲音說，「小貓，你最好別再去惹小鳥了！」

Introduction: Little Birdie
序章: 小鳥

Introduction: Little Old Lady
序章: 小老太太

Introduction: You take the silver candlesticks and stow them in your pack.
序章: 你拿起銀燭臺並把它們放進背包。

Introduction: The candlesticks are heavy, ornate and made of sterling silver.
序章: 燭臺很沉，裝飾華麗，由純銀製成。

Introduction: You have a bad feeling about the very deep, low growl emanating from the cat.
序章: 你對貓發出的低沉、深重咆哮有種不祥的感覺。

Introduction: This is a very insistent cat. You give it one of your rations.
序章: 這是隻很堅持的貓。你給它一份你的口糧。

Introduction: You feed the nice kitty a crumb.
序章: 你給可愛的小貓一點麵包屑。

Introduction: The cat really likes being fed.  You give it a leftover morsel.
序章: 貓真的喜歡被餵食。你給它一點剩菜。

Introduction: You have nothing substantial to feed the cat.
序章: 你沒有什麼實質的東西可以餵貓。

Introduction: You pet the nice kitty.
序章: 你撫摸可愛的小貓。

Introduction: The cat really likes being petted.
序章: 貓真的喜歡被撫摸。

Introduction: This is a very insistent cat.
序章: 這是隻很堅持的貓。

Introduction: The little house cat seems harmless, but strangely restless.
序章: 小家貓似乎無害，但詭異地不安。

Introduction: The chair looks singularly uncomfortable.
序章: 椅子看起來特別不舒服。

Introduction: How does she keep those candles lit all night long?
序章: 她怎麼把那些蠟燭點著整晚？

Introduction: The chest is filled with old quilts, doilies, and sweaters five sizes too large for anyone to wear.  You really don't want any of them, so you close the chest again.
序章: 箱子裡裝滿了舊被子、蕾絲墊圈和大五號的毛衣，沒人穿得了。你真的不想要任何東西，所以你又關上箱子。

Introduction: A large chest sits beside the bureau.
序章: 一個大箱子放在櫥櫃旁邊。

Introduction: KITTY!!!
SQUEEEEAK!
序章: 吱呀呀呀呀！

Introduction: CREEEEAAK!
序章: 吱嘎呀呀呀！

Introduction: The owner of the house awakens...
序章: 房子的主人醒了……

Introduction: Help!  Burglars!  Sheriff!  Help!  Kitty!  Kitty!
序章: 「救命！小偷！治安官！救命！小貓！小貓！」

Introduction: SCRAAAAWK!
序章: 「呱呀呀呀呀！」

Introduction: You find 3 silvers that have gotten caught in the cracks.
序章: 你找到了 3 個銀幣卡在裂縫裡。

Introduction: You find nothing else by searching the couch.
序章: 你在沙發上搜索沒找到其他東西。

Introduction: The couch looks every bit as uncomfortable as your maiden aunt's.
序章: 沙發看起來和你未婚姑母的一樣不舒服。

Introduction: You find a single silver in one of the desk drawers.  You find nothing else of any value to you.
序章: 你在辦公桌的某個抽屜裡找到一個銀幣。你沒找到其他對你有價值的東西。

Introduction: The stairs lead to the door to an upstairs room.
序章: 樓梯通向樓上房間的門。

Introduction: Against the wall, there is a large wooden desk with many drawers.
序章: 靠著牆有一個大木桌，有很多抽屜。

Introduction: The fire is out, but you can still feel the heat from the hearth.
序章: 火熄了，但你仍能感到壁爐的熱。

Introduction: It's a hurricane lamp.  Funny, you've never heard of hurricanes in Spielburg.
序章: 這是颶風燈。有趣的是，你從沒在史畢柏格聽說過颶風。

Introduction: The light of the hurricane lamp.
序章: 颶風燈的光。

Introduction: Nothing but dirt and leaves there.
序章: 那兒只有灰塵和樹葉。

Introduction: It's a Shrubbery!
序章: 那是灌木叢！

Introduction: The Little Old Lady left her purse on the couch.  Mighty careless of her.
序章: 小老太太把錢包留在沙發上。她真粗心。

Introduction: That'll teach you to play daggers with a cat!
序章: 那就是你和貓玩匕首的下場！

Introduction: Oh, Oh!  Looks like you've blundered into the cat's territory.
序章: 哎呀！看起來你誤入了貓的領地。

Introduction: The smell of lavender and dust fills your nose as you walk in.  This reminds you of a great aunt's house you once visited.
序章: 當你走進去時，薰衣草和灰塵的味道充滿了你的鼻子。這讓你想起你曾經去過的姑母的房子。

Introduction: There is a covered birdcage near the stairs, and a knitting basket beside the couch.
序章: 樓梯附近有一個蓋著的鳥籠，沙發旁邊有一個編織籃。

Introduction: A string of pearls seems to have fallen into the bag among the knitting.  You take the pearls, of course.  (The knitting you can do without.)
序章: 一串珍珠似乎掉進了編織物中的袋子裡。你當然拿走了珍珠。（編織物你可以不要。）

Introduction: You find nothing else of value in the desk.
序章: 你在桌子裡沒找到其他有價值的東西。

Introduction: In the purse, you find 20 silvers and some soiled hankies.  You take the silver.
序章: 在錢包裡，你找到 20 個銀幣和一些髒手帕。你拿走銀幣。

Introduction: The smell of lavender and dust fills your nose as you walk in.  This reminds you of a great aunt's house you once visited.
序章: 當你走進去時，薰衣草和灰塵的味道充滿了你的鼻子。這讓你想起你曾經去過的姑母的房子。

Introduction: The couch looks every bit as uncomfortable as your aunt's.  There is a covered birdcage near the stairs, and a knitting basket beside the couch.
序章: 沙發看起來和你姑母的一樣不舒服。樓梯附近有一個蓋著的鳥籠，沙發旁邊有一個編織籃。

Introduction: The squared rug matches the walls.  It appears the floor has recently been painted.
序章: 方形地毯和牆相配。地板似乎剛被粉刷過。

Introduction: It's not locked.  In fact, the lock is broken.
序章: 沒有鎖。實際上，鎖壞了。

Introduction: It's a large, wooden chest, undoubtedly filled with priceless treasures.
序章: 這是一個大的木製箱子，無疑裝滿了無價之寶。

Introduction: It's not locked.  In fact, the lock is broken.
序章: 沒有鎖。實際上，鎖壞了。

Introduction: There is an assortment of plastic fruit on the old wooden table.
序章: 舊木桌上有各種塑膠水果。

Introduction: The wood on this table has been polished into total submission.  A lace doily guards it from dust and water rings.
序章: 這張桌子的木頭被拋光得發亮。蕾絲墊圈保護它不被灰塵和水漬傷害。

Introduction: You have a weird feeling that someone is watching you, and you decide you'd better not touch anything.
序章: 你有種奇怪的感覺，有人在盯著你，所以你決定最好別碰任何東西。

Introduction: Accoutrements and equipment.
序章: 裝飾品和器材。

Introduction: This mysterious orb feels as smooth as glass, and icy cold to the touch.
序章: 這個神祕的球體摸起來光滑如玻璃，冷得像冰。

Introduction: This strange globe radiates with an eerie light.
序章: 這個奇異的球體散發著詭異的光。

Introduction: That's way out of reach.
序章: 那遠遠超出範圍。

Introduction: It's not a pretty sight.
序章: 這不是好看的景象。

Introduction: You can only guess at the original contents of this bottle since it is now empty.
序章: 既然瓶子現在是空的，你只能猜測它原本的內容物。

Introduction: It's a blue bottle of blue bottle flies.
序章: 這是裝著藍瓶蒼蠅的藍色瓶子。

Introduction: Since you don't have time to read, you leave them.
序章: 因為你沒時間讀，你把它們留下了。

Introduction: The titles are totally unfamiliar to you.  The books dealing with sorcery, necromancy and other occult subjects.
序章: 標題對你完全陌生。這些書涉及巫術、死靈法術和其他神祕主題。

Introduction: Not knowing what the potions are, they will do you no good.
序章: 不知道藥水是什麼，它們對你沒有用處。

Introduction: Bottles of mephitic potions.
序章: 充滿惡臭的藥水瓶。

Introduction: Afraid that the contents might jump out at you, you leave the jar alone.
序章: 害怕內容物會跳出來，你不敢動這個罐子。

Introduction: A brown jar of crushed cockroach essence.
序章: 一個裝著碾碎蟑螂精華的棕色罐子。

Introduction: The ceiling is shrouded with clouds of smoke.
序章: 天花板被烈烈升起的煙霧籠罩。

Introduction: You feel like you're floating on air.
序章: 你感覺自己在飄浮。

Introduction: As your hand nears the coals, you discover that they are burning.
序章: 當你的手靠近煤炭時，你發現它們在燃燒。

Introduction: The coals are glowing.
序章: 煤炭在發光。

Introduction: You don't know how to use a crystal ball.
序章: 你不知道怎麼用水晶球。

Introduction: Crystal balls, useful only to those initiated into the ways of wizardry.
序章: 水晶球，只對懂得巫術之道的人有用。

Introduction: Whether it's because that thing is too far to reach, has sharp, nasty claws, and wicked teeth, or because it glares at you, you decide you'd better not touch it.
序章: 不管是因為那東西太遠夠不著、有尖銳可怕的爪子和兇惡的牙齒，還是因為它瞪著你，你決定最好別碰它。

Introduction: It looks like a sculpture of some weird, bat-winged creature.
序章: 它看起來像某種怪異的蝙蝠翅膀生物的雕像。

Introduction: The bat-winged creature must be some sort of familiar.  There is an uncanny look in its eyes, and you don't want to look at it too closely.  It is clearly magical.
序章: 蝙蝠翅膀生物應該是某種魔寵。它的眼睛有種詭異的光，你不想太仔細看它。它顯然是有魔法的。

Introduction: It's not familiar enough with you to want to chat.
序章: 它和你還不夠熟，不想聊天。

Introduction: On closer observation, you discover that they cute little fish are piranhas.  You wisely keep your fingers out.
序章: 仔細看，你發現那些可愛的小魚是食人魚。你明智地把手指遠遠放著。

Introduction: It's a bowl of pretty little fish.
序章: 這是一碗漂亮的小魚。

Introduction: The floor has an oily, glassy feel to it.
序章: 地板摸起來油膩、有玻璃感。

Introduction: The floor is almost completely covered by a strange symbol.
序章: 地板幾乎完全被一個奇異的符號覆蓋。

Introduction: Don't touch -- that stuff looks corrosive.
序章: 別碰——那東西看起來是腐蝕性的。

Introduction: The jar is filled with a corrosive-looking green substance.
序章: 罐子裡裝滿了看起來腐蝕性的綠色物質。

Introduction: You'll never get a head that way!  Besides, you don't really need a face-lift yet.
序章: 那樣你永遠成不了大人物！而且，你還真的不需要拉皮手術。

Introduction: A replacement part for a magical face-lift.
序章: 魔法拉皮手術的替換零件。

Introduction: It's a jar of dried Mantray jerky.  Yum!  (Not.)
序章: 這是一罐乾曼特雷肉乾。好吃！（其實不然。）

Introduction: Don't touch the mortar -- you'd be terribly embarrassed if you made it accidentally fire.
序章: 別碰杵臼——如果你不小心讓它發射，會超級尷尬。

Introduction: A mortar, complete with the traditional pestle.
序章: 完整的杵臼。

Introduction: Your garage is already full of junk; you don't need to add to the clutter.
序章: 你的車庫已經堆滿雜物；用不著再增加亂七八糟的東西。

Introduction: Odds and ends.
序章: 零零碎碎的東西。

Introduction: Ends and odds.
序章: 五花八門的玩意。

Introduction: Things and stuff.
序章: 各種各樣的東西。

Introduction: Jeff's jimcracks.
序章: 傑夫的小玩意。

Introduction: Magical junk and mystical garbage.
序章: 魔法垃圾和神祕廢物。

Introduction: You may not be a Wizard, but you have learned that touching a pentagram can be bad for one's health.
序章: 你不是法師，但你已經知道碰五芒星對身體不好。

Introduction: A pentagram, used in incantations to conjure spirits.
序章: 五芒星，用來在咒語中召喚靈魂。

Introduction: Glimpsing the skull and crossbones on this jar, you wisely leave it alone.
序章: 看到這罐子上的骷髏骨頭標誌，你明智地不去碰它。

Introduction: The label says "Instant Fire -- Just Add Water."
序章: 標籤上寫著「即時之火——只需加水」。

Introduction: Someone is watching you.
序章: 有人在看著你。

Introduction: Tall shelves with books and odds and ends suited to magic line the walls. The ceiling and the far wall are mysterious indeed.  There is a strange symbol on the floor.  You also see a wide variety of arcane objects.
序章: 高聳的書架和各式魔法用品堆滿了牆壁。天花板和遠牆神祕莫測。地上有奇異的符號。你也看到形形色色的秘術物品。

Introduction: You see a wide variety of arcane objects.  For example:
序章: 你看到形形色色的秘術物品。舉例來說：

Introduction: Scrolls covered with mysterious symbols and arcane incantations.
序章: 布滿神祕符號和秘術咒語的卷軸。

Introduction: A toaster oven, very rarely used -- electricity hasn't been invented yet.
序章: 烤箱，很少用到——電力還沒被發明出來。

Introduction: Crystal balls, useful only to those initiated into the ways of witches.
序章: 水晶球，只有通曉巫術之道的人才用得上。

Introduction: Books dealing with sorcery, necromancy, and other occult subjects.
序章: 討論魔法、死靈術和其他神祕學的書籍。

Introduction: Dried Mantray jerky.
序章: 乾製曼特雷肉乾。

Introduction: A balance scale.  Zara uses it to measure powders and to maintain the magical balance.
序章: 天秤。札拉用它來量取粉末，維持魔法平衡。

Introduction: A hooded robe for when it rains in here.
序章: 下雨時穿的帽篷斗篷。

Introduction: Tinsel and chaff, very good for avoiding radar.
序章: 金屬絲和糠秕，非常擅長躲避雷達。

Introduction: It wouldn't be a good idea to throw things in here.  You might break something and anger Zara.
序章: 在這裡亂扔東西不是好主意。你可能會打破什麼東西，惹怒札拉。

Introduction: You've already done that.
序章: 你已經做過了。

Introduction: As you read the spell scroll, the spell is ingrained in your mind.
序章: 當你讀著法術卷軸時，法術刻入了你的心靈。

Introduction: You weigh the consequences of taking the balance scale, and decide that, on balance, it's not such a good idea.
序章: 你權衡拿走天秤的後果，最後決定這不是好主意。

Introduction: An ancient balance scale.  Zara thinks it will help her maintain a sense of balance.
序章: 一個古老的天秤。札拉認為它能幫助她維持平衡感。

Introduction: The burning incense fills the room with a strange, fiery scent.
序章: 燃燒的香料充滿房間，散發出奇異的火熱香氣。

Introduction: None of the scrolls is in a language you have ever seen.
序章: 所有卷軸上的語言都是你沒見過的。

Introduction: There are a few Dead Sea scrolls, a couple of Red Sea scrolls, a C++ scroll (written in assembly language), and others even more esoteric.
序章: 有一些死海卷軸、幾份紅海卷軸、一個 C++ 卷軸（用組合語言寫的），還有其他更深奧的。

Introduction: The tinsel reminds you that there are only 250 shopping days left until Christmas.
序章: 金屬絲讓你想起距離聖誕節只剩 250 個購物日。

Introduction: Tinsel left over from last Christmas.
序章: 去年聖誕節留下的金屬絲。

Introduction: You wish you had your great-great-great-great-grandson here so he could tell you how to work this.  But since he isn't even born yet, you decide you have no use for the toaster oven.
序章: 你希望你的曾曾曾曾孫在這裡，這樣他可以告訴你怎麼用。但既然他還沒出生，你決定烤箱對你沒用。

Introduction: I am Zara, and my companion is Damiano.  The items in this shop are designed for those skilled in the use of magic.
序章: 「我是札拉，我的夥伴是達米亞諾。本店的物品是為精於施魔法的人設計的。」

Introduction: We have very little for ones such as you who have not been initiated, but what we have could prove useful.
序章: 「對於像你這樣未入門的人，我們幾乎沒什麼，但我們有的東西可能會派上用場。」

Introduction: So, you return to my shop.  Ask of me what you will, but keep it to the point.
序章: 「所以，你又回來了。儘管問我吧，但別囉嗦。」

Introduction: An Aura is a Spell of protection surrounding something.  The town is still protected by the Aura cast by the great spellcaster Erana.
序章: 「光環是一種保護咒語，圍繞著什麼東西。這座鎮仍然受到偉大法師伊瑞娜施的光環保護。」

Introduction: She is a powerful and wicked hag.  You would be wise to avoid her.  She cursed the Baron von Spielburg when he tried to drive her away.
序章: 「她是一位強大且邪惡的女巫。你應該避開她。當史蒂芬·馮·史畢柏格男爵試圖驅趕她時，她詛咒了他。」

Introduction: He was once a great leader of this land until he was foolish enough to anger Baba Yaga.
序章: 「他曾是這片土地的偉大領導者，直到他愚蠢地惹怒了芭芭雅嘎。」

Introduction: This is Baba Yaga's curse, cast upon the Baron years ago:
序章: 「這是芭芭雅嘎在多年前對男爵施下的詛咒：」

Introduction: 'Upon von Spielburg and all his clan, this the curse I now demand:  What I Will shall come full measure, so shall ye lose all that ye treasure.'
序章: 「『在馮·史畢柏格及其全族之上，我現在宣判此詛咒：我之意志將如願實現，你們將失去一切珍寶。』」

Introduction: There is always a way to break a curse; possibly Erasmus knows more about this.
序章: 「打破詛咒總有辦法；伊拉斯謨可能知道更多。」

Introduction: Damiano is both my familiar and my friend.  We share our lives and our magic.
序章: 「達米亞諾既是我的精靈伙伴，也是我的朋友。我們分享彼此的生命和魔法。」

Introduction: That is the spell that lets you cast a magical flame upon your enemy.  The spell scroll will cost you 60 silver.
序章: 「那是讓你對敵人施魔火的咒語。法術卷軸要花你 60 銀幣。」

Introduction: Erasmus makes a point of studying the effects of Magic upon mortals.  There is little about Magic in this area that Erasmus does not know.
序章: 「伊拉斯謨特別研究魔法對凡人的影響。這片區域的魔法事項，伊拉斯謨幾乎無所不知。」

Introduction: Erana was a powerful Spellcaster who lived long ago.  She brought peace to this valley.  Even now her spell protects this town from violence or foul magic.
序章: 「伊瑞娜是一位很久以前的強大法師。她為這個山谷帶來了平和。至今她的咒語仍保護著這座鎮不受暴力或黑暗魔法傷害。」

Introduction: Her final resting place is due north of town, and it is a place of both safety and healing.  It is known as Erana's Peace.
序章: 「她最後的安息之所在鎮正北方，那是一個安全又治癒的地方。它被稱為伊瑞娜的安寧之地。」

Introduction: Erasmus is a Wizard and a Spellcaster who knows much about this area.  He lives in a tower northeast of town at the top of a steep mountain called Zauberberg.
序章: 「伊拉斯謨是一位法師和法術施放者，對這片區域知之甚詳。他住在鎮東北方一座陡峭山頂上的塔裡，那座山叫做魔法山。」

Introduction: He can be very helpful if approached properly, but he has a strange sense of humor.
序章: 「如果方式得當，他會很樂意幫助你，但他的幽默感很奇特。」

Introduction: We are a people of Power and Magic, and live in the forests far beyond the mountains to the west.
序章: 「我們是力量與魔法之民，住在西邊山脈遠方的森林裡。」

Introduction: A Familiar is a creature of magical abilities which has been summoned to serve the Wizard.
序章: 「精靈伙伴是一種具有魔法能力的生物，被召喚來侍奉法師。」

Introduction: The Summoning  of a Familiar is a most powerful spell, and can be cast but once.  It binds the Wizard and the Familiar to the ends of their lives.
序章: 「召喚精靈伙伴是最強大的咒語，只能施放一次。它將法師和精靈伙伴綁定到生命的終點。」

Introduction: The more powerful the Wizard, the more powerful the Familiar, although you cannot always judge power by a Familiar's shape.
序章: 「法師越強大，精靈伙伴就越強大，雖然你不能根據精靈伙伴的外形判斷力量。」

Introduction: The Fetch Spell allows you to lift or lower a lightweight object from a distance.  The spell scroll will cost you 40 silvers.
序章: 「取物術讓你能從遠處提起或放下輕物體。法術卷軸要花你 40 銀幣。」

Introduction: The Healing Potion will cost you 50 silver.  It is made by the healer outside of town.
序章: 「治癒藥劑要花你 50 銀幣。它是由鎮外的治療師製作的。」

Introduction: Master the Arts Arcane, use those skills to vanquish the evil curse, and you will become a true Hero!
序章: 「精通秘術藝術，用那些技能消滅邪惡的詛咒，你就會成為真正的英雄！」

Introduction: Baba Yaga's hut is magical.  It stands on chicken legs, and you must know the rhyme to enter.
序章: 「芭芭雅嘎的小屋是魔法的。它站在雞腿上，你必須知道咒語才能進入。」

Introduction: When you have mastered nine spells, have the Power to cast all, and proven yourself worthy by accomplishing a great deed, then you must undergo initiation to become full Wizard.
序章: 「當你精通九道法術、具有施放所有法術的力量，並通過成就偉大事跡證明自己的價值時，你就必須接受入會儀式成為正式的法師。」

Introduction: There is a place in the distant south where you must journey, but first you must become a hero here.
序章: 「在遠方的南邊有一個你必須前往的地方，但首先你必須在這裡成為英雄。」

Introduction: There are many entrances to the Halls of Magical Mastery in the south.
序章: 「在南邊的魔法精通殿堂有很多入口。」

Introduction: After you have gained skill and a good reputation in Magic, you should journey south.
序章: 「在你獲得魔法的技能和良好聲譽後，你應該向南前往。」

Introduction: If you are indeed worthy of being a Wizard, you will find a way to those Halls.
序章: 「如果你確實值得成為法師，你會找到通往那些殿堂的路。」

Introduction: If you have the skill of Magic, you can learn the way to cast spells by reading magical scrolls.
序章: 「如果你有魔法的技能，你可以通過閱讀魔法卷軸來學習施放咒語的方法。」

Introduction: Without the skill, you have no Power.  The more you practice spell casting, the greater you shall become.
序章: 「沒有技能，你就沒有力量。你練習施法越多，你就會變得越強大。」

Introduction: It is a spell that allows you at first to unfasten simple locks and knots.  As you gain in skill, you can even open doors with it.  The spell scroll will cost you 30 silver.
序章: 「這是一個咒語，最初讓你打開簡單的鎖和繩結。隨著技能提升，你甚至能用它打開門。法術卷軸要花你 30 銀幣。」

Introduction: To the north of the town, surrounded by the mountains, is an Alpine valley that is rumored to be the final resting place for Erana, although no one is certain what happened to her.
序章: 「在鎮北邊，被群山環繞的地方，有一個高山谷地，據傳是伊瑞娜最後的安息之所，但沒人確定她發生了什麼。」

Introduction: It is certainly a magical place of safety. There is also supposed to be a gift there for Magic Users who are clever enough to find it.
序章: 「那肯定是個魔法的安全之地。據說那裡還有一份禮物等著夠聰明的魔法使用者去尋找。」

Introduction: A true Magic User has the innate affinity for Magic.  There is also the ability to store Power, which ordinary mortals do not have.
序章: 「真正的魔法使用者具有與魔法的內在親和力。還有儲存力量的能力，這是普通凡人沒有的。」

Introduction: We have potions of Healing, Vigor, and Power for purchase from our shop.
序章: 「我們的店裡有治癒、活力和力量藥劑供購買。」

Introduction: Power is the essence of Magic, that which the Wizard shapes and is shaped by.
序章: 「力量是魔法的本質，法師既形成它，也被它形成。」

Introduction: The Power Potion restores your magical energy, and costs 75 silver.
序章: 「力量藥劑恢復你的魔法能量，價格是 75 銀幣。」

Introduction: I have no interest in entering Baba Yaga's hut and therefore do not know the rhyme.
序章: 「我對進入芭芭雅嘎的小屋沒有興趣，因此不知道咒語。」

Introduction: The scrolls we sell are magical.  You have but to read the spell, and you will learn it.
序章: 「我們賣的卷軸是魔法的。你只要讀了咒語，就會學到它。」

Introduction: What I sell here are merely tools to help those who have power.
序章: 「我在這裡賣的只是幫助那些有力量的人的工具。」

Introduction: You may purchase restorative potions and scrolls from which you may learn spells, if you have the potential.
序章: 「如果你有潛力，你可以購買補藥和卷軸來學習咒語。」

Introduction: We sell several Magical Spells on study scrolls.  You may purchase Fire Dart for 60 silver, Fetch for 40 silver, or the Open Spell for 30 silver.
序章: 「我們在學習卷軸上出售多種魔法咒語。你可以花 60 銀幣購買火焰鏢、花 40 銀幣購買取物術，或花 30 銀幣購買開啟術。」

Introduction: I also know you can find a spell if you learn the secret of Erana's Peace.
序章: 「我也知道如果你學會伊瑞娜的安寧之地的祕密，你可以找到一個咒語。」

Introduction: There is much magic in this world for those who know how to use it.  There is magic in this little town, and a good deal of magic in this valley.
序章: 「對那些知道如何使用的人來說，這個世界裡有很多魔法。這個小鎮裡有魔法，山谷裡有大量魔法。」

Introduction: There is an Aura protecting this town from danger.  Within most of its walls, there can be no act of violence or cruel magic.  Even so, it is prudent to avoid dark places.
序章: 「有一個光環保護這座鎮免受危害。在它的大多數牆壁內，不可能有暴力或殘忍魔法的行為。即便如此，避開暗處也是明智的。」

Introduction: There is much power in this valley, and it attracts those who use magic.  I am here, Erasmus has his house on Zauberberg, and even Baba Yaga has her hut cooped up somewhere around here.
序章: 「這個山谷裡有很多力量，吸引那些使用魔法的人。我在這裡，伊拉斯謨在魔法山有他的房子，甚至芭芭雅嘎的小屋也在這一帶某處。」

Introduction: Vigor potions restore your Stamina after you have exerted yourself.  They cost 25 silver each.
序章: 「活力藥劑在你消耗體力後恢復你的耐力。每瓶 25 銀幣。」

Introduction: A Wizard is one who both shapes Magic and is shaped by it.  Erasmus and I are the only Wizards in this valley.
序章: 「法師是既形成魔法，也被魔法形成的人。伊拉斯謨和我是這個山谷裡唯一的法師。」

Introduction: That is the Spell that lets you place magical energy upon a weapon.  The energy will be released as magical damage when you hit your enemy.
序章: 「那是讓你把魔法能量附加在武器上的咒語。當你擊中敵人時，能量會以魔法傷害的形式釋放出去。」

Introduction: I am both human and Faery Folk.  I draw my power from both.  My name is Zara.  This is my shop.
序章: 「我既是人類，也是妖精。我從兩者身上汲取力量。我叫札拉。這是我的店。」

Introduction: It's a good thing you can't get close to Zara.  With her electrifying personality, the results could be shocking.
序章: 好在你靠不近札拉。以她電擊般的性格，後果可能會很令人震驚。

Introduction: Zara has a mysterious, unearthly look to her.  You have the feeling that she is not a person to trifle with.
序章: 札拉看起來神祕而超凡。你有種感覺，她不是可以輕易對付的人。

Introduction: The powers of magic are reserved for those with the potential to use them.  I sense no such potential in you.
序章: 「魔法的力量是為那些有潛力使用它們的人保留的。我在你身上感不到這樣的潛力。」

Introduction: I detect that you have not the money to purchase that item.  Do not toy with me!
序章: 「我看出你沒有錢買那個東西。別跟我玩花樣！」

Introduction: May this aid you on your Quest!
序章: 「願這能幫助你完成你的任務！」

Introduction: Aura
序章: 光環

Introduction: Damiano
序章: 達米亞諾

Introduction: Fire Dart
序章: 火焰鏢

Introduction: Faery Folk
序章: 妖精

Introduction: Familiar
序章: 精靈伙伴

Introduction: Healing
序章: 治癒

Introduction: Journey
序章: 旅程

Introduction: Magic
序章: 魔法

Introduction: Potential
序章: 潛力

Introduction: Power
序章: 力量

Introduction: Power Potion
序章: 力量藥劑

Introduction: Scrolls
序章: 卷軸

Introduction: Vigor
序章: 活力

Introduction: Zap
序章: 閃電

Introduction: Zara
序章: 札拉

Introduction: Reward for return of lost ring. Inquire at the Healer's.
序章: 「失物招領：遺失的戒指。詢問治療師。」

Introduction: This poster is rather dusty and faded.  The picture is of a small child with braids.  "Reward of 50 gold coins for the safe return of Elsa von Spielburg. Inquire at Spielburg Castle gates."
序章: 這張海報相當塵埃落定且褪色。圖片是一個梳著辮子的小女孩。「艾爾莎·馮·史畢柏格安全歸來獎勵 50 金幣。詢問史畢柏格城堡門衛。」

Introduction: Reward of 30 gold coins for the Capture or Death of the Brigand Warlock. Description:  short, ugly, and wears brightly colored robes.  Has habit of laughing continually.  Inquire at Spielburg Castle gates.
序章: 「山賊術士懸賞。獎賞：30 金幣。敘述：身材矮小、相貌醜陋、穿著色彩鮮豔的長袍。有持續大笑的習慣。詢問史畢柏格城堡門衛。」

Introduction: Wanted:  Brigand Leader.  Description:  Unknown appearance, wears a cloak. Must provide proof of leader's identity.  Reward of 60 gold coins and title of Hero of the Realm.  Inquire at Spielburg Castle gates.
序章: 「通緝：山賊首領。敘述：外貌不詳，穿著斗篷。必須提供首領身份的證明。獎賞：60 金幣及領地英雄稱號。詢問史畢柏格城堡門衛。」

Introduction: Notice:  Spell components needed.  Cash or trade for potions.  Inquire at the Healer's.
序章: 「告示：需要法術成分。以現金或藥劑交易。詢問治療師。」

Introduction: This poster seems to have been here a while.  It has a picture of a handsome, but arrogant young man.  "Reward of 50 gold coins for information leading to the return of Baronet Barnard von Spielburg.  Inquire at Spielburg Castle gates."
序章: 這張海報似乎貼了一段時間。上面有一個英俊但傲慢的年輕男性的圖片。「巴納德·馮·史畢柏格小爵爺失蹤。獎賞：50 金幣。詢問史畢柏格城堡門衛。」

Introduction: If you take the poster, no other adventurer will be able to read it.
序章: 如果你拿走這張海報，其他冒險者將無法讀到它。

Introduction: There are a variety of vegetables for sale, and the shop keeper is a Centaur.
序章: 有各種各樣的蔬菜出售，店主是一位半人馬。

Introduction: The barrels look too heavy to move.
序章: 這些桶子看起來太重了，移不動。

Introduction: The door is locked.
序章: 門是鎖著的。

Introduction: You see a dry goods store through the window.
序章: 你通過窗戶看到一家乾貨店。

Introduction: The store is locked.
序章: 店是鎖著的。

Introduction: There is no response.  The store must be closed for the evening.  The lock appears to be an extra heavy-duty model.
序章: 沒有回應。商店想必今晚打烊了。鎖看起來是特別堅固的款式。

Introduction: There is no response.  The lock is of a simple type.
序章: 沒有回應。鎖是普通款式。

Introduction: You see a nicely furnished living room through the window.
序章: 你通過窗戶看到一間裝飾典雅的客廳。

Introduction: You'd probably get arrested if you carved your initials in the door.
序章: 如果你在門上刻你的名字首字母，你可能會被逮捕。

Introduction: The key doesn't seem to work.
序章: 鑰匙似乎不起作用。

Introduction: The white picket fence surrounds a small yard.
序章: 白色柵欄圍繞著一個小院子。

Introduction: The creature in charge of the Farmer's Mart is a rather lovely and shy-looking young centaur.
序章: 農業市集的負責人是一位相當可愛且害羞的年輕半人馬。

Introduction: You see a charming young centaur with the upper torso of a teenager and the body of a filly.
序章: 你看到一位迷人的年輕半人馬，上半身是少年身材，下半身是小馬體型。

Introduction: Good day, and welcome to Spielburg.  Would you like to buy some of my nice fresh fruits or vegetables?
序章: 「你好，歡迎來到史畢柏格。你想買我的新鮮水果或蔬菜嗎？」

Introduction: I'm sorry, but I had to move you out of my stall!  It is very dangerous to sleep in the street at night.  I fear you have been robbed.
序章: 「抱歉，但我必須把你從我的攤位裡搬出來！在街上睡覺很危險。恐怕你被搶了。」

Introduction: My father and I are the only centaurs in this valley.
序章: 「我父親和我是這個山谷裡唯一的半人馬。」

Introduction: You can smell apples as you approach this corner.
序章: 當你接近這個角落時，你可以聞到蘋果的香氣。

Introduction: Perhaps another time, then.
序章: 「那也許下次再說吧。」

Introduction: Apples are ten for a silver.  Do you want to buy my apples?
序章: 「蘋果是十個銀幣一份。你想買我的蘋果嗎？」

Introduction: Good day and welcome to Alpinehurst.  Do you wish to buy some nice, fresh vegetables?
序章: 「你好，歡迎來到阿爾皮尼赫斯特。你想買我的新鮮蔬菜嗎？」

Introduction: How much do you wish to spend?
序章: 「你想花多少錢？」

Introduction: Apples
序章: 蘋果

Introduction: Carrots
序章: 紅蘿蔔

Introduction: Date
序章: 約會

Introduction: Market
序章: 市場

Introduction: Farmer
序章: 農夫

Introduction: Farm
序章: 農場

Introduction: Father
序章: 父親

Introduction: Mother
序章: 母親

Introduction: Parsnips
序章: 歐洲防風草

Introduction: Robbery
序章: 搶劫

Introduction: Rutabagas
序章: 蕪菁

Introduction: Sheriff
序章: 治安官

Introduction: Turnips
序章: 蔓菁

Introduction: Vegetables
序章: 蔬菜

Introduction: My father won't let me date strangers.
序章: 「我父親不允許我和陌生人約會。」

Introduction: There are still some apples left from last season in the barrel.  You may buy 10 for a silver, since they are so small.
序章: 「桶裡還有上季留下的蘋果。因為很小，你可以用一枚銀幣買 10 個。」

Introduction: They have twice robbed my father of his money, and they have tried to steal our food during this winter.  That is because we farm outside of town.
序章: 「他們搶過我父親兩次，今年冬天還想偷我們的食物。那是因為我們在城外務農。」

Introduction: The brigands would not dare try that in town.  The Sheriff would stop them fast.
序章: 「山賊不敢在城裡那樣做。治安官會立刻阻止他們。」

Introduction: Carrots are my favorite vegetable.  They are so crisp and sweet.
序章: 「紅蘿蔔是我最喜歡的蔬菜。脆又甜。」

Introduction: Thank you, but my Father thinks I am too young.  He would not permit it. But perhaps next year...
序章: 「謝謝你，但我父親認為我太年輕了。他不會同意。也許明年……」

Introduction: This is where I sell things from my father's farm.
序章: 「我在這裡賣我父親農場的東西。」

Introduction: My father is a fine farmer.
序章: 「我父親是個優秀的農夫。」

Introduction: We have some land to the north of town.  It isn't very big, but we grow many things.
序章: 「我們在城北有些土地。不是很大，但我們種了許多東西。」

Introduction: You should be here during harvest time.  Then you would see many fruits and vegetables.
序章: 「你應該在收割季來。那時你會看到許多水果和蔬菜。」

Introduction: My father is Heinrich Pferdefedern.
序章: 「我父親是海因里希·費德費德恩。」

Introduction: My mother has been dead for three years now.  I still miss her.
序章: 「我母親已經去世三年了。我還是想念她。」

Introduction: I am Hilde, daughter of Heinrich Pferdefedern, the farmer.
序章: 「我是希爾德，海因里希·費德費德恩的女兒，他是農夫。」

Introduction: Parsnips can be made into a tasty pie.
序章: 「歐洲防風草可以做成美味的派。」

Introduction: Many robbers ran up to father as he trotted back to town.  My father tried to fight, but they hurt him badly.  They then ran away to the southwest.
序章: 「許多強盜在我父親小跑回城時撲向他。我父親試圖反擊，但他們把他打得很慘。之後他們向西南逃走。」

Introduction: Rutabagas keep well over the winter months.
序章: 「蕪菁在冬季保存得很好。」

Introduction: Our Sheriff is very brave.  He told me so himself.
序章: 「我們的治安官非常勇敢。他自己跟我說的。」

Introduction: Turnips are good if you cook them in stews.
序章: 「蔓菁煮成燉菜很好吃。」

Introduction: We have many kinds of fresh vegetables for sale today.  There are carrots, rutabagas, parsnips, and potatoes right from the ground at five for a silver. They are very good for you.  We also have some apples.
序章: 「我們今天有許多新鮮蔬菜要賣。有紅蘿蔔、蕪菁、歐洲防風草和剛挖出來的馬鈴薯，5 個一枚銀幣。對你非常有益。我們還有一些蘋果。」

Introduction: I'm sorry, but my Father won't allow me to accept flowers from strangers.
序章: 「抱歉，但我父親不允許我接受陌生人的花。」

Introduction: This house is the same color as the Sheriff's office around the corner.
序章: 這棟房子的顏色和附近的治安官辦公室一樣。

Introduction: You are in the northeast section of town.   The buildings are all brightly painted and the air is filled with the fragrance of apples.  This appears to be a market street.
序章: 你在城鎮的東北部。建築物都漆成鮮艷的顏色，空氣中飄著蘋果的香氣。這看起來像是市場街。

Introduction: Unlike an Ogre, YOU can read!
序章: 不像食人妖，你是看得懂字的！

Introduction: The lock appears to be an extra heavy-duty model.
序章: 這個鎖看起來是特別堅固的型號。

Introduction: Turn around.
序章: 轉身。

Introduction: The door appears to be locked, but the lock is of a simple type.
序章: 門看起來是鎖著的，但鎖是簡單的類型。

Introduction: You reach for your money but come up empty-handed.  Unable to pay for your goods, you put the merchandise back.
序章: 你摸向荷包卻一無所獲。因為付不起錢，你把商品放回去。

Introduction: You're a little short on cash.  Perhaps you can find work somewhere.
序章: 你的現金有點不足。也許你可以找到工作。

Introduction: You can smell apples as you approach this corner.
序章: 當你接近這個角落時，你可以聞到蘋果的香氣。

Introduction: You pick out the freshest-looking vegetables you can find, and pay the lady a silver piece.
序章: 你挑出看起來最新鮮的蔬菜，付給那位女士一枚銀幣。

Introduction: You carefully select ten of the best apples from the barrel, and pay Hilde a silver piece.
序章: 你小心地從桶裡選出 10 個最好的蘋果，付給希爾德一枚銀幣。

Introduction: Vegetables are five for a silver.  Would you like to buy some?
序章: 「蔬菜 5 個一枚銀幣。你要買一些嗎？」

Introduction: The scales are used to weigh the vegetables.
序章: 這個天平是用來稱量蔬菜的。

Introduction: Ah, fresh air!
序章: 啊，新鮮空氣！

Introduction: It is but a small stall, used for the daily sale of whatever local produce has not been confiscated by the brigands.
序章: 這只是個小攤位，用來販售每天沒被山賊沒收的當地農產品。

Introduction: The selection is meagre and the appearance is not of the best, but the aromas are strangely enticing.  A barrel contains some small apples, and there are several types of vegetables on the counter.
序章: 選擇不多，外觀也不怎樣，但香氣卻奇異地誘人。一個桶裡裝著一些小蘋果，櫃檯上還有幾種蔬菜。

Introduction: There is no one around.  The markets are closed for the night.
序章: 附近沒人。市場已經打烊。

Introduction: All of the produce has been taken in for the night.
序章: 所有農產品都已經收進來過夜。

Introduction: Round stones have been set in the ground to make a walkway.
序章: 圓石被鑲入地面做成走道。

Introduction: Square stones make up the walkway.
序章: 方形石頭組成走道。

Introduction: It is a simple but prosperous-looking general store.  It looks like it might provide some staples that would prove useful to a traveler (such as if an adventuring party asks you to join them).
序章: 這是一家簡樸但看起來很興隆的雜貨店。看起來可能有一些對旅行者有用的日用品（比如冒險隊要你加入時）。

Introduction: You can see very little through the window.
序章: 你從窗戶看不到多少。

Introduction: The candelabra looks as though it is made of solid gold.
序章: 這個燭台看起來像是純金做的。

Introduction: Only the Shadow knows for sure.
序章: 只有暗影知道。

Introduction: Looks like solid oak.
序章: 看起來像是實心橡木。

Introduction: You've looked in there already.
序章: 你已經看過裡面了。

Introduction: The glowing embers from the fire cast a dim light in the room.
序章: 火焰中發光的餘燼在房間裡投下昏暗的光線。

Introduction: You don't know much about art, but you know what you like... and that's not it.
序章: 你對藝術不太了解，但你知道自己喜歡什麼……而這不是。

Introduction: It's much too high to reach.
序章: 太高了，夠不著。

Introduction: The cold, dark eyes of the Gryphon make you feel a bit uncomfortable, especially since it's about ten times larger than any Gryphon you've ever heard of.
序章: 獅鷲冷漠的黑眼睛讓你感到有點不舒服，尤其是因為它比你聽過的任何獅鷲都大約十倍。

Introduction: You're not tall enough to reach it.
序章: 你不夠高，夠不著。

Introduction: The Giant Gryphon has long since departed the world of the living.  It doesn't answer you.
序章: 巨大的獅鷲早已離開人世。它不回應你。

Introduction: It is a small, beautifully decorated metal box with a hinged lid.
序章: 這是一個小的、精美裝飾的金屬盒子，有活動蓋。

Introduction: An uncomfortable looking chair.
序章: 看起來很不舒服的椅子。

Introduction: The chair seems to be bolted to the floor.  You can't move it.
序章: 椅子似乎被螺栓固定在地板上。你無法移動它。

Introduction: It's an overgrown bonsai.
序章: 這是一個過度長大的盆景。

Introduction: Even though it's a valuable 50-year-old Sego palm, it's much too big to fit in your pack.
序章: 雖然這是一棵珍貴的 50 年老塞戈棕櫚，但太大了放不進你的背包。

Introduction: Inside the safe is a bag of coins containing 50 silvers.
序章: 保險箱裡面有一個裝著 50 枚銀幣的錢袋。

Introduction: Oh, my mistake.  There *used* to be 50 silvers in the bag.  It's empty all of a sudden.
序章: 哦，我搞錯了。袋子裡原本有 50 枚銀幣。突然間就空了。

Introduction: It looks very sturdy, indeed.
序章: 看起來確實很牢固。

Introduction: There is an empty coin bag in the safe.
序章: 保險箱裡有一個空錢袋。

Introduction: A solid looking table.
序章: 看起來很實心的桌子。

Introduction: There is an ornately carved box sitting atop the oak table.
序章: 在橡木桌上放著一個雕花的盒子。

Introduction: You forgot to bring the moving van.
序章: 你忘了帶搬家車。

Introduction: Over the safe is a charming portrait of the Sheriff and his wife.
序章: 保險箱上方是一幅迷人的治安官和他妻子的肖像。

Introduction: The people who own this house must have some money.  Everything looks new and there is not a speck of dust visible.
序章: 擁有這棟房子的人一定有些錢。所有東西看起來都很新，看不到一絲塵埃。

Introduction: The room smells vaguely of sauerkraut and bratwurst, with just a faint odor of smoke from pinewood.  From somewhere in the house you can hear someone snoring.
序章: 房間裡隱隱散發著酸菜和德式香腸的味道，還有一絲松木煙火的淡淡氣味。你可以聽到房子某處傳來打鼾聲。

Introduction: Gently and stealthily, you lift the lid on the little box.
序章: 你輕輕地、悄悄地打開小盒子的蓋子。

Introduction: As the little music box begins to play, you hear the Sheriff yell out: "Otto, stop playing with that music box and GO TO BED!"
序章: 當小音樂盒開始演奏時，你聽到治安官大聲叫道：「奧圖，停止玩那個音樂盒，去睡覺！」

Introduction: Boy, did YOU make a mistake!
序章: 哎呀，你可真犯了個錯誤！

Introduction: That was close!  The goon must've been so dumb or sleepy or both that he didn't even see you standing there.
序章: 差點就暴露了！那傢伙一定是太笨、太瞌睡，或者兩者都有，才沒看到你站在那裡。

Introduction: You take the vase carefully from the mantle and place it gently on the floor.
序章: 你小心地從壁爐架上拿起花瓶，輕輕地放在地板上。

Introduction: As you reach toward the painting...
序章: 當你伸向畫……

Introduction: From somewhere upstairs, you hear the Sheriff shout:  "Otto!"
序章: 你聽到樓上某處傳來治安官的喊聲：「奧圖！」

Introduction: Obviously, you shouldn't have done that!
序章: 顯然，你不應該這樣做！

Introduction: By lifting the painting, you can see what certainly must be a safe, hidden in the wall.
序章: 藉由抬起畫，你可以看到一定是隱藏在牆裡的保險箱。

Introduction: In the desk drawers is an assortment of mostly worthless objects, but you also find three silvers, which you take.
序章: 抽屜裡有各式各樣的大部分沒什麼價值的東西，但你也找到了三枚銀幣，你拿了。

Introduction: You carefully lower the painting into its original position.
序章: 你小心地把畫放回原來的位置。

Introduction: Uh Oh!  As you see a pillow flying toward you, you hear the Sheriff's wife say...
序章: 哎呀！當你看到一個枕頭飛向你時，你聽到治安官妻子說……

Introduction: SCREEEEEEEEEEEECH!
序章: 「啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊啊！」

Introduction: You've already robbed the desk.
序章: 你已經搶過桌子了。

Introduction: You'd better shut the safe, first.
序章: 你最好先關好保險箱。

Introduction: You place the vase carefully in your pack beneath your cape.
序章: 你小心地把花瓶放進背包裡，藏在你的披風下面。

Introduction: You place the candelabra carefully in your pack beneath your cape.
序章: 你小心地把燭台放進背包裡，藏在你的披風下面。

Introduction: You quickly toss the box into your pack.
序章: 你迅速地把盒子扔進背包。

Introduction: There is some fairly loud snoring going on in this room. You hear: "Brckawwwww... zup-zup-zup-zup-zzzzz."
序章: 房間裡傳來相當響亮的打鼾聲。你聽到：「Brckawwwww……zup-zup-zup-zup-zzzzz。」

Introduction: Very faintly, you hear snoring:  "Prrrrrt... prrrrrt... prrrrrt."
序章: 你聽到非常微弱的打鼾聲：「Prrrrrt……prrrrrt……prrrrrt。」

Introduction: Someone (or someTHING) is snoring up a storm. You hear:  "Nkaawww... rspft... honk... buh-buh-buh-buh-buh-buh-phweep."
序章: 某人（或某個東西）打著可怕的鼾聲。你聽到：「Nkaawww……rspft……honk……buh-buh-buh-buh-buh-buh-phweep。」

Introduction: Ah, got it!
序章: 啊，搞定了！

Introduction: You find fifty silvers and add them to your collection.  You then put the empty bag back in the safe.
序章: 你找到了 50 枚銀幣，加入你的收藏。然後你把空錢袋放回保險箱裡。

Introduction: Since you have already robbed the safe, you close the door, narrowly missing your fingers in the process.
序章: 既然你已經搶過保險箱，你關上了門，期間險些夾到你的手指。

Introduction: Otto can't find the music box, but he's too dim and sleepy to figure it out, so he heads on back to bed
序章: 奧圖找不到音樂盒，但他太遲鈍又太瞌睡，想不出來，所以他就回床上去。

Introduction: Otto, even in his sleepy state, winds the music box and closes the lid before he heads back to bed.
序章: 奧圖即使在瞌睡狀態下，也在回床之前把音樂盒上了發條並關上蓋子。

Introduction: Otto stares sleepily at the closed music box for a moment and heads back to bed.
序章: 奧圖睡眼惺忪地看著關上的音樂盒片刻，然後回床上去。

Introduction: Cracking safes looked a lot easier in the instruction book.
序章: 撬保險箱在說明書裡看起來簡單多了。

Introduction: You see a bag of coins.
序章: 你看到一個錢袋。

Introduction: The room is dark and still.  The candelabra looks like it is made of gold. The vase on the mantle might be worth taking.  There is also an odd little box on the table.
序章: 房間黑暗寂靜。燭台看起來像是金製的。壁爐架上的花瓶可能值得拿。桌上還有一個古怪的小盒子。

Introduction: Naw, don't do that.
序章: 不，別那樣做。

Introduction: You see a bag full of silver coins.
序章: 你看到一個裝滿銀幣的錢袋。

Introduction: The delicate porcelain vase looks like it could be worth some money, although it would take up a lot of space in your pack.
序章: 精緻的瓷質花瓶看起來可能值些錢，雖然它會佔用你背包裡很多空間。

Introduction: None of the clothes look like they fit.
序章: 沒有一件衣服看起來合身。

Introduction: The clothes come in two sizes -- Dwarf and Goon.
序章: 衣服只有兩種尺寸——矮人和傢伙。

Introduction: The jars of beets and sauerkraut look like they were canned locally.
序章: 這些甜菜和酸菜罐子看起來像是在本地罐裝的。

Introduction: There are barrels of flour and salt next to the counter.  There also seem to be jars of pickled pigs' feet, linen cloth, and lots of other things in which you have very little interest.
序章: 櫃檯旁邊有桶桶的麵粉和鹽。似乎還有醃豬腳罐子、亞麻布，和很多你不感興趣的其他東西。

Introduction: It's the matador's red cape, recently repaired after he waved it in front of a minotaur.
序章: 這是鬥牛士的紅披風，最近才修過因為他在牛頭人面前揮舞過它。

Introduction: You see bottles, jugs, and canned goods more useful to a housewife than a Hero.
序章: 你看到的瓶子、罐子和罐裝食品對家庭主婦比對英雄更有用。

Introduction: There are some wooden poles leaning against the wall.
序章: 有些木竿靠在牆上。

Introduction: Wow, the latest technology in toothpicks by the bulk!  What will they think of next?
序章: 哇，批量生產的牙籤最新科技！他們接下來會想到什麼？

Introduction: This looks like a dry goods store, but it smells like a musty library.  The stove feels nice on such a crisp day.  Behind the counter and on the shelves, there are a variety of items for sale.  The shopkeeper appears to ignore you while he reads a book.
序章: 這看起來像雜貨店，但聞起來像霉濕的圖書館。在這個清爽的日子裡，爐火感覺很舒適。櫃檯後面和架子上有各種物品要賣。店主看起來在讀書時忽視你。

Introduction: Try paying the clerk for what you want to buy.
序章: 試著為你要買的東西付錢給店員。

Introduction: Behind the counter are weapons, armor, and such equipment as you would more often find in an "Adventurer's Shop".
序章: 櫃檯後面有武器、盔甲和你更常在「冒險者商店」找到的裝備。

Introduction: On the counter and on the shelves are assortments of canned goods, honey jars, sewing items, and odds and ends that people in a small town would need.
序章: 櫃檯上和架子上有罐裝食品、蜂蜜罐、縫製物品，和小鎮居民需要的各式雜物。

Introduction: Please don't throw things in here.  You might break a flask.
序章: 「請不要把東西扔進這裡。你可能會打破瓶子。」

Introduction: You should give your money to the shop owner.
序章: 你應該把錢給店主。

Introduction: This is the door to the back storage room.
序章: 這是通往後面儲藏室的門。

Introduction: A large, lumpy sack sits by the wall.
序章: 一個又大又不規則的布袋坐在牆邊。

Introduction: Those look like oversized shields suitable for Ogres, Goons, and Frost Giants.
序章: 那些看起來像是為食人妖、爪牙和霜凍巨人設計的超大盾牌。

Introduction: The barrels appear to be filled with flour and salt and look rather heavy.
序章: 那些桶子看起來裝滿了麵粉和鹽，看起來相當沉重。

Introduction: Oh, I'm sorry!  I didn't notice you coming in.
序章: 「哦，我很抱歉！我沒注意到你進來。」

Introduction: My, you look like an adventurer!  We don't see too many around here.  The brigands tend to get rid of most adventurers before they make it to town.
序章: 「天哪，你看起來像個冒險家！我們這裡不常見到。山賊通常會在冒險家到達鎮上之前就解決掉他們。」

Introduction: I do try to stock some things adventurers can use.  I actually would rather be an adventurer than a shopkeeper, you see.
序章: 「我確實會進一些冒險家能用的東西。老實說，我其實寧願做冒險家而不是店主，你懂吧。」

Introduction: My items are pretty ordinary, though.  You probably already have most of them if you've done any adventuring at all.
序章: 「不過我的商品相當普通。如果你做過任何冒險活動，你可能已經有大部分了。」

Introduction: You can get really good protection from my chain mail armor.  It's very heavy though, and I would have to charge you 500 silvers for it.
序章: 「我的鎖甲能為你提供非常好的保護。不過它很重，我得向你收 500 銀幣。」

Introduction: I always wanted to be an adventurer.  I read about them all all the time. The book I was just reading is about adventuring.
序章: 「我一直想成為冒險家。我經常閱讀關於他們的故事。我剛才正在讀的書就是關於冒險的。」

Introduction: Oh, this book?  It's about an adventurer who is trying to become a Hero. The title is 'Quest for Glory:  A Hero's Death'.
序章: 「哦，這本書？是關於一個冒險家想成為英雄的故事。書名是「英雄傳奇：英雄之死」。」

Introduction: I don't like to talk about other people behind their backs.
序章: 「我不喜歡在背後談論別人。」

Introduction: Ah!  The use of the dagger is a most skillful art.  Actually, this particular weapon is longer than most, but still easily concealable.  A bargain at 20 silvers.
序章: 「啊！匕首的使用是一門高超的藝術。實際上，這把武器比大多數都長，但仍然容易隱藏。只要 20 銀幣，划算得很。」

Introduction: Unfortunately, I have only standard weapons and equipment.  I carry daggers and chain armor.  Maybe someday I'll be able to carry magic ones, though.
序章: 「很遺憾，我只有標準武器和裝備。我只賣匕首和鎖甲。也許有一天我能賣魔法武器，不過。」

Introduction: It's a very good idea to carry an empty flask or two, in case you want to pick up a liquid or something else that needs a container.  Our flasks are a great bargain at 2 silvers each.
序章: 「隨身帶一兩個空瓶子是個不錯的主意，以防你想裝液體或其他需要容器的東西。我們的瓶子便宜得很，一個只要 2 銀幣。」

Introduction: Adventuring rations aren't the tastiest food in the world, but they will keep you healthy and alert as you go along.  A pack of 10 rations will cost you just 5 silvers.
序章: 「冒險用的口糧不是世上最好吃的食物，但能讓你在旅途中保持健康和警覺。一包 10 份口糧只要 5 銀幣。」

Introduction: Hilde sells fine produce for a reasonable price.
序章: 「希爾德以合理的價格出售優質農產品。」

Introduction: Why, that's me!  Kaspar!
序章: 「怎麼，那就是我！卡斯帕！」

Introduction: The Sheriff and Otto protect this town.  The Sheriff used to be a real adventurer, you know!
序章: 「治安官和奧圖保護這個鎮。治安官以前是真正的冒險家呢！」

Introduction: The shopkeeper is a small, balding man who wears glasses.  He looks as if he can barely lift the book he is reading.  Judging from his clothes, he is fairly well-to-do.
序章: 店主是個矮小禿頭、戴著眼鏡的男人。他看起來好像連他正在讀的書都勉強拿得起。從他的衣著看，他過得相當不錯。

Introduction: Here you are; thank you for your patronage.
序章: 「給你；感謝你的光顧。」

Introduction: Thank you, my adventurer friend!  I hope this serves you well.
序章: 「謝謝你，我的冒險家朋友！希望這對你有幫助。」

Introduction: You won't be needing your old leather armor anymore.  I shall give you a 50 silver trade-in discount.
序章: 「你不再需要你的舊皮甲了。我給你 50 銀幣的折舊折扣。」

Introduction: As you count your money, you realize that you don't have enough to purchase this particular item.
序章: 你數著你的錢時，才發現你沒有足夠的金錢來買這件東西。

Introduction: Chain Armor
序章: 鎖甲

Introduction: Adventurer
序章: 冒險家

Introduction: Book
序章: 書

Introduction: Brigand
序章: 山賊

Introduction: Dagger
序章: 匕首

Introduction: Equipment
序章: 裝備

Introduction: Flask
序章: 瓶子

Introduction: Either the shopkeeper can't hear you or he's ignoring you.  Try striding right up to the counter.
序章: 要麼店主聽不到你，要麼他在忽視你。試著直接走到櫃檯。

Introduction: The stove is burning merrily away, making the shop seem almost too warm.
序章: 爐子熱烈地燒著，讓店裡幾乎熱得不得了。

Introduction: It looks like a standard broadsword behind that counter.
序章: 那櫃檯後面看起來像是一把標準的闊劍。

Introduction: The proprietor's teapot.
序章: 老闆的茶壺。

Introduction: Bright sunlight streams in through the open window.
序章: 明亮的陽光灑進敞開的窗戶。

Introduction: Through the open window you can see that night has fallen.
序章: 透過敞開的窗戶你可以看到夜幕已降。

Introduction: It's dark, but there seems to be someone in there.
序章: 很黑，但裡面似乎有人。

Introduction: It's very dark, but you can see something shining on the ground at the far end of the alley.
序章: 很黑，但你可以看到小巷遠處地上有東西在閃閃發光。

Introduction: The shop seems to be closed although there are some dried-up cupcakes in the window.  There is a sign on the door which says, 'Gone Fishing'.
序章: 店似乎關閉了，雖然窗戶裡還有一些乾掉的杯子蛋糕。門上有個牌子寫著「去釣魚了」。

Introduction: You can't seem to open the door.  It might be locked.
序章: 你似乎打不開門。它可能是鎖著的。

Introduction: Sorry, that's a dagger, not a fire axe.
序章: 抱歉，那是匕首，不是消防斧。

Introduction: The door seems to be barred on the inside.
序章: 門似乎從裡面被堵住了。

Introduction: The key doesn't work.
序章: 鑰匙不起作用。

Introduction: The bakery shop.
序章: 麵包店。

Introduction: The bakery seems to be closed, although there are some dried-up cupcakes in the window.
序章: 麵包店似乎關閉了，雖然窗戶裡還有一些乾掉的杯子蛋糕。

Introduction: Other than the sorry-looking goods in the window, it is too dark to make out much of anything.
序章: 除了窗戶裡那些看起來可憐的商品，太黑了什麼也看不清。

Introduction: You can't seem to open the window.
序章: 你似乎打不開窗戶。

Introduction: The shop seems to be closed.  There is a sign on the door which says, 'Gone Fishing'.
序章: 店似乎關閉了。門上有個牌子寫著「去釣魚了」。

Introduction: Don't -- you'll dull the dagger.
序章: 別——你會把匕首磨鈍。

Introduction: A butcher shop.
序章: 肉舖。

Introduction: The Butcher's shop seems to be closed.  Through the window, you see what appears a layer of dust over most of the interior.
序章: 肉舖似乎關閉了。透過窗戶，你可以看到裡面大部分都蓋了一層灰塵。

Introduction: It is too dark to make out anything.
序章: 太黑了什麼也看不清。

Introduction: You see light glinting from something shiny in the alley, but you can't make out what it is.
序章: 你看到小巷裡有東西閃閃發光，但看不清是什麼。

Introduction: The pots appear to be just junk.
序章: 那些鍋看起來只是垃圾。

Introduction: After dusting yourself off, you check to make sure you're still in one piece.
序章: 拍掉塵埃後，你檢查自己是否還完好無損。

Introduction: Unfortunately, you discover that while you were "out", someone managed to relieve you of all of your money.
序章: 不幸的是，你發現在你「昏迷」期間，有人把你的所有錢都偷走了。

Introduction: Now you'll have to find a way to get some money. The street is not the most comfortable place to sleep, and you have to eat, sooner or later.
序章: 現在你得想辦法弄些錢。街道不是最舒服的睡覺地方，而且遲早你得吃飯。
