序章: 「B - 他開始起疑了。暫時別碰面，我會靠便條通知你。- B」

Introduction: Hey, he's not one of us!  Take care of him!
序章: 「欸，他不是我們的人！解決掉他！」

Introduction: You put back the note.
序章: 你把便條放回去。

Introduction: Oh yeah?  What's the password?
序章: 「是嗎？密碼是什麼？」

Introduction: Oh yeah?  So what is it?
序章: 「是嗎？那是什麼？」

Introduction: Smells like Troll's Sweat.  Tastes like Troll's Sweat.  By golly, it IS Troll's Sweat...
序章: 聞起來像巨魔汗水。嚐起來像巨魔汗水。天哪，它真的就是巨魔汗水…

Introduction: You seem to have gone a little overboard on the ale (or at least falling off the seat).
序章: 你好像在麥酒上有點過頭了(至少從座位上摔下來了)。

Introduction: ... and one Troll's Sweat is too many.
序章: …而一杯巨魔汗水就太多了。

Introduction: You inform the bartender that you seem to be temporarily strapped for funds.
序章: 你告訴酒保你現在好像臨時沒錢。

Introduction: Hey Crusher!  Get this deadbeat bum outta here!
序章: 「嘿粉碎者！把這個賴皮鬼趕出去！」

Introduction: The bartender glares at you as you enter, and so does the ugly goon on the left.  You get the impression that you are not welcome.
序章: 你進去時酒保瞪了你，左邊那個醜陋的打手也是。你有種不受歡迎的感覺。

Introduction: The grimy window lets little light into this tavern.  It smells like stale ale and other more unpleasant things.  The floor is covered with dirt, and the bar with sticky beer.
序章: 髒污的窗戶讓很少的光進入這間酒館。聞起來像陳舊的麥酒和其他更難聞的東西。地板覆著污垢，吧台黏著啤酒。

Introduction: Smoke appears to be rising from the center cask behind the bar.  To your right, two gamblers are playing cards.
序章: 煙霧似乎從吧台後面的中央酒桶升起。你右邊，兩個賭徒在玩牌。

Introduction: Not a pleasant sight!  There seems to be something slimy spilled the stool, and it is eating into the wood.  You think perhaps you should sit on the next stool over.
序章: 不是個愉快的景象！凳子上好像灑了什麼黏稠的東西，正在侵蝕木頭。你想也許應該坐下一個凳子。

Introduction: Looks like some kind of card game.
序章: 看起來像某種牌戲。

Introduction: That's a mighty unusual keg, there.
序章: 那是個非常不尋常的酒桶呢。

Introduction: There appears to be a trapdoor beneath the goon, but he's blocking it very effectively.
序章: 打手下面好像有個活板門，但他擋得很有效。

Introduction: The window is open, but not much light seems to make it in here.
序章: 窗戶是開的，但似乎進不來多少光。

Introduction: Affects where knife goes.
序章: 影響刀子的著彈位置。

Introduction: Place hand on red dot.
序章: 把手放在紅點上。

Introduction: The barrels are large enough to hide several unlucky adventurers.  Let's hope you're not that unlucky.
序章: 這些桶子大到足以藏下好幾個倒楣的冒險者。希望你沒那麼倒楣。

Introduction: It looks a bit like a dart board, only bigger.
序章: 看起來有點像飛鏢靶，不過大一點。

Introduction: There sure are a lot of daggers in the board.  You wonder where the Chief Thief keeps them all.
序章: 板上真的有很多匕首。你納悶盜賊首領把它們全放在哪兒。

Introduction: The game is called Dag-Nab-It.  You take turns with the Chief Thief throwing daggers at the board.  Each player gets three turns, and throws three daggers in each turn.
序章: 遊戲叫「Dag-Nab-It」。你和盜賊首領輪流向棋盤投擲匕首。每個玩家有三輪，每輪投擲三把匕首。

Introduction: More points are scored the closer each dagger hits to the center of the board.  Highest score wins the bet.
序章: 匕首越接近棋盤中心，得分就越高。最高分贏得賭注。

Introduction: Adjust force and angle of throw with the arrow keys or mouse, according to your hand position.  Press Enter or click on the small dag-nab-it board to throw.
序章: 根據你的手部位置，用方向鍵或滑鼠調整投擲的力度和角度。按 Enter 或點擊小的 dag-nab-it 棋盤來投擲。

Introduction: You can call me 'Boris'.  I'm the Chief Financial Officer for the Guild.
序章: 「你可以叫我『鮑里斯』。我是公會的財務主管。」

Introduction: We sell lockpicks and thief toolkits here.
序章: 「我們這裡賣開鎖工具和盜賊工具組。」

Introduction: Our very special items are reserved for our guild members.  No scabs allowed.
序章: 「我們最特殊的物品只保留給公會成員。不收臨時工。」

Introduction: Fencing
序章: 銷贓

Introduction: License
序章: 執照

Introduction: Lock Pick
序章: 開鎖工具

Introduction: Services
序章: 服務

Introduction: Tool Kit
序章: 工具組

Introduction: Tools
序章: 工具

Introduction: A Thief's license will cost you 25 silvers.  You will need a license to use any of our services.
序章: 「盜賊執照要 25 銀幣。使用我們的任何服務都需要執照。」

Introduction: You can't fence something you don't have.
序章: 「沒有的東西沒法銷贓。」

Introduction: We buy only from our members.
序章: 「我們只跟成員收購。」

Introduction: That doesn't look like something we can resell.
序章: 「那看起來不像我們能轉賣的東西。」

Introduction: We are happy to give recompense for any items you may have borrowed from others.
序章: 「我們很樂意為你向別人『借來』的任何物品給予賠償。」

Introduction: A Thief's License will cost you 25 silvers.  You will need a license to use any of our services.
序章: 「盜賊執照要 25 銀幣。使用我們的任何服務都需要執照。」

Introduction: How else can you borrow things effectively?  The investment of your lifetime for fifteen silvers.
序章: 「不然你要怎麼有效地『借』東西？終身投資只要 15 銀幣。」

Introduction: The license allows you to steal in town without us tipping the Sheriff, allows you to purchase professional quality tools, provides fencing services at no extra charge, and pays for the flowers on your grave in the event of an untimely accident.
序章: 「執照讓你在城裡偷東西而我們不會通風報信治安官，讓你購買專業級工具，提供免費銷贓服務，還有如果你不幸身亡會幫你墓上買花。」

Introduction: It's a steal for only one hundred silvers.  (heh, heh)
序章: 「只要 100 銀幣真是划算。(嘿嘿)」

Introduction: Perhaps I could sell you a good lockpick for fifteen silvers, or a wonderful Acme Thief Tool Kit for one hundred silvers.
序章: 「也許我可以賣你一把好開鎖工具 15 銀幣，或一套棒的 Acme 盜賊工具組 100 銀幣。」

Introduction: Welcome to the Thieves' Guild Union, Local 1313.
序章: 「歡迎來到盜賊公會工會，第 1313 分會。」

Introduction: Don't bother.  You've already paid, stupid.
序章: 「別費事了。你早就付過了，傻瓜。」

Introduction: I'm sorry, but you don't seem to have enough silver.  Come back when you qualify.
序章: 「抱歉，你似乎銀幣不夠。等你有錢再來吧。」

Introduction: There you are.
序章: 「你來了。」

Introduction: Let's see now... deducting the Guild cut, and allowing for resale markup, that comes to... 40 silvers.  Here you are.
序章: 「讓我算算……扣掉公會的佣金，加上轉售加成，總共是……40 銀幣。給你。」

Introduction: Let's see now... deducting the Guild cut, and allowing for resale markup, that comes to... 150 silvers.  Here you are.
序章: 「讓我算算……扣掉公會的佣金，加上轉售加成，總共是……150 銀幣。給你。」

Introduction: Let's see now... deducting the Guild cut, and allowing for resale markup, that comes to... 90 silvers.  Here you are.
序章: 「讓我算算……扣掉公會的佣金，加上轉售加成，總共是……90 銀幣。給你。」

Introduction: Let's see now... deducting the Guild cut, and allowing for resale markup, that comes to... 100 silvers.  Here you are.
序章: 「讓我算算……扣掉公會的佣金，加上轉售加成，總共是……100 銀幣。給你。」

Introduction: Let's see now... deducting the Guild cut, and allowing for resale markup, that comes to... 500 silvers.  Here you are.
序章: 「讓我算算……扣掉公會的佣金，加上轉售加成，總共是……500 銀幣。給你。」

Introduction: The boxes must be used to store what the thieves are lucky enough to steal in this town.
序章: 這些箱子是用來放盜賊們在城裡撈到的戰利品。

Introduction: You can tell where the bar is by the seep marks from spilled beer.
序章: 你可以從灑出來的啤酒漬跡找到酒吧。

Introduction: I don't have time to talk to unlicensed scum.  Pay your guild license fee, and then we can talk.  Why don't you do something constructive like stealing Otto's yo-yo?
序章: 「我沒時間和沒執照的傢伙囉嗦。先付了公會執照費，我們再談。你為什麼不做點有建設性的事，比如偷奧圖的溜溜球？」

Introduction: Hey, nice game.  It's a tie.
序章: 「嘿，好遊戲。平手。」

Introduction: You got me that time.  Say, you're pretty tough!
序章: 「你這次贏了我。說，你很強啊！」

Introduction: So, you think you're pretty hot stuff, eh?  Well, we'll see about that.
序章: 「所以，你認為你很了不起，嗯？好吧，我們就看看吧。」

Introduction: If you don't bet, you can't play.
序章: 「如果你不下注，就不能玩。」

Introduction: It's a tie.  Let's double the wager.
序章: 「平手。我們加倍賭注吧。」

Introduction: Looks like I won that one.  Must have gotten lucky.
序章: 「看起來我贏了那一局。肯定是運氣好。」

Introduction: Hey!  This is cash on the table.  You can't bet what you don't have.
序章: 「嘿！這是現金。你不能賭你沒有的東西。」

Introduction: Now, let's not get carried away.
序章: 「現在，別太過分了。」

Introduction: Beginners!  Why do they keep giving me beginners?
序章: 「新手！為什麼老是給我配上新手？」

Introduction: It will cost you 25 silvers to work in town.  Just pay Boris at the door. If you don't have the cash, go steal some.
序章: 「在城裡工作要付 25 銀幣。只要付錢給門口的柏瑞斯就行。沒錢的話就去偷啊。」

Introduction: I tell you, Crusher, that a thief of my stature and abilities should be stuck in a Po-Dunk town besieged by brigands who aren't even Union Members is the height of injustice!
序章: 「我跟你說，克拉什，一個我這樣身份和能力的盜賊被困在這個被非工會山賊包圍的鳥不生蛋之地，根本是天大的不公道！」

Introduction: From his expensive clothes to his carefully trimmed mustache, there is an air of sophistication about this villian.
序章: 從他華麗的衣著到精心修剪的鬍子，這個惡棍身上散發著老練的氣質。

Introduction: Accountant
序章: 會計

Introduction: Bet
序章: 賭注

Introduction: Board
序章: 木板

Introduction: Boris
序章: 柏瑞斯

Introduction: Daggers
序章: 匕首

Introduction: Fence
序章: 贓物商

Introduction: Game
序章: 遊戲

Introduction: Thieves' Guild
序章: 盜賊公會

Introduction: Otto
序章: 奧圖

Introduction: Scabs
序章: 工賊

Introduction: Secrets
序章: 秘密

Introduction: Spy
序章: 間諜

Introduction: Stealing
序章: 偷竊

Introduction: Thieves
序章: 盜賊

Introduction: Tips
序章: 竅門

Introduction: Town
序章: 城鎮

Introduction: If you can't pay, don't play.
序章: 「沒錢就別玩。」

Introduction: I have no time for idle talk.  Why don't you do something constructive like stealing Otto's yo-yo?
序章: 「我沒時間閒聊。你為什麼不做點有建設性的事，比如偷奧圖的溜溜球？」

Introduction: We could start small, perhaps a silver a throw.  We each throw a dagger at the board and the dagger closest to center wins.
序章: 「我們可以小額開始，每次一銀幣吧。我們各拋一把匕首在木板上，最接近中心的匕首贏。」

Introduction: That is a specially designed Dagger Board.
序章: 「那是特別設計的匕首木板。」

Introduction: Boris is our accountant.
序章: 「柏瑞斯是我們的會計。」

Introduction: Those non-union cretins are giving thieves a bad name.  I'm sure they have a spy planted in our organization.
序章: 「那些非工會的白癡在給盜賊丟臉。我敢說他們在我們組織裡插了間諜。」

Introduction: Crusher is our personnel manager.
序章: 「克拉什是我們的人事經理。」

Introduction: A dagger is a thief's best friend.  If you ever get good with one, then perhaps we could play a little game.
序章: 「匕首是盜賊最好的朋友。如果你哪天練得不錯，也許我們可以玩個小遊戲。」

Introduction: Just give your stolen items to Boris and you will be duly recompensed.  Or in terms more suited to your apparent intellect, You get cash for your loot.
序章: 「只要把偷來的東西交給柏瑞斯，你就會得到應得的報酬。或者用更適合你腦子的說法，你拿贓物換現金。」

Introduction: You and I could throw some daggers at the board over there and bet upon which of us will throw better.
序章: 「你和我可以朝那邊的木板拋匕首，打賭誰拋得更準。」

Introduction: Where else but here can one mingle freely with others of our chosen profession, sharing tips and secrets?
序章: 「還有哪裡能像這兒一樣，讓我們能和同行自在地交流、分享竅門和秘密？」

Introduction: Where else can you safely fence stolen items?  Where else can you ask questions like these and not be thrown into jail?
序章: 「還有哪裡能安全地銷售贓物？還有哪裡敢像這樣提問而不被丟進監獄？」

Introduction: The Guild is truly your home away from home.
序章: 「公會真的是你的第二個家。」

Introduction: I am known as 'The Chief'.
序章: 「我叫『首領』。」

Introduction: He is the Sheriff's goon.
序章: 「他是治安官的打手。」

Introduction: Those who do not pay their union dues are the lowest form of life.
序章: 「不交工會費的傢伙是最低等的生物。」

Introduction: That would be telling.
序章: 「那可不能說。」

Introduction: The Sheriff provides a useful service in this town.  He arrests anyone too stupid to be a good thief.
序章: 「治安官為這城鎮提供了有用的服務。他抓走所有太蠢而當不了好盜賊的傢伙。」

Introduction: Someone is leaking our insider information to the Brigands.  You just can't trust anyone these days.
序章: 「有人把我們的內部情報洩露給山賊。現在真是沒人可信。」

Introduction: I have had beginners before, but this guild reached a new low when you joined.  Child, you are a thief.  Stealing is how you make a living.
序章: 「我見過新手，但自從你加入，這個公會跌到了新低點。孩子，你是盜賊。偷竊就是你的謀生方式。」

Introduction: So go out there and steal something and hopefully you'll get, that is, you won't get caught.
序章: 「所以出去偷點東西，希望你能……也就是說，你別被抓。」

Introduction: There are only a few members of our guild still around here.  With the snow and the brigands keeping away the tourists, it's hard to earn a decent living.
序章: 「我們公會在這兒的成員沒幾個了。雪和山賊把遊客都嚇跑了，很難有好收入。」

Introduction: Don't get caught.
序章: 「別被抓。」

Introduction: We will gladly sell you for a modest fee a lock pick.  However, for the true professional, we have a Thief Tool Kit which will allow you to pick anything with a little practice.  Just talk to Boris.
序章: 「我們很樂意以合理的價格賣給你開鎖工具。不過，真正的專業人士會用我們的盜賊工具組，只要稍加練習就能打開任何鎖。找柏瑞斯聊聊。」

Introduction: There are a few places around town where a clever and skilled thief can make some silver.  Perhaps even you could steal something.
序章: 「城鎮裡有幾個地方，聰明又靈巧的盜賊可以撈到銀幣。也許連你都能偷到東西。」

Introduction: Try it, and I'll slit you from guggle to zatch!
序章: 「你敢試試，我就把你從喉嚨切到下巴！」

Introduction: What do you think this is, your home?  Crusher, deal with this deadbeat.
序章: 「你把這兒當你家啊？克拉什，去對付這個賴皮的。」

Introduction: It looks like yesterday's unfinished dinner.
序章: 看起來像是昨天沒吃完的晚餐。

Introduction: Crusher ignores you.
序章: 克拉什不理你。

Introduction: Crusher is his usual, amiable self, "every bit the Goon".
序章: 克拉什還是老樣子，親切得令人生厭，「十足的打手」。

Introduction: While Crusher's leathery skin might make a good strop for your daggers, you don't think you'd enjoy being compressed to something the size of a softball by an angry Goon.
序章: 雖然克拉什的皮膚看起來很適合磨刀，但被一個生氣的打手壓成壘球大小可不是什麼好滋味。

Introduction: The daggers are nicely made, and apparently designed for throwing.  Besides the Chief Thief's seemingly endless collection, there are two matched sets with differently-colored hilts for playing Dag-Nab-It.
序章: 這些匕首製作精良，顯然是為了投擲而設計。除了首領盜賊似乎無窮無盡的收藏外，還有兩套配對的匕首，柄部顏色不同，用來玩「匕首擲準遊戲」。

Introduction: Stealing from a dagger-throwing thief?   That's a dangerous -- and stupid -- idea!
序章: 想從會拋匕首的盜賊手裡偷東西？那是個危險──而且愚蠢──的主意！

Introduction: The opening in the door has a wire grate on it, but there is space below it where objects can be passed through.  The mysterious-looking man on the other side is Boris, the cashier and fence for the Guild.
序章: 門上的開口裝著鐵柵欄，但下面有空隙可以傳遞東西。另一邊是神祕兮兮的傢伙柏瑞斯，是公會的出納和贓物商。

Introduction: Picking locks in the Thieves' Guild is a singularly stupid idea.
序章: 在盜賊公會裡開鎖根本是個蠢到不行的主意。

Introduction: Affects how far knife flies.
序章: 影響刀能飛多遠。

Introduction: Place hand on slider.
序章: 把手放在滑塊上。

Introduction: The knife the Chief Thief threw.
序章: 首領盜賊拋出來的刀。

Introduction: Stealing from a dagger-throwing thief?  That's a dangerous -- and stupid -- idea!
序章: 想從會拋匕首的盜賊手裡偷東西？那是個危險──而且愚蠢──的主意！

Introduction: The ladder to the tavern is very sturdy.  It has to be to support Crusher's weight.
序章: 通往酒館的梯子非常牢固。得這樣才能承受克拉什的體重。

Introduction: It's a prelude to the modern electric bulb, a marked improvement over darkness.
序章: 這是現代電燈泡的前身，比起黑暗可強多了。

Introduction: A dagger is strategically located on the table.
序章: 一把匕首戰略性地放在桌上。

Introduction: Stealing from a dagger-throwing thief?   That's a dangerous -- and stupid -- idea!
序章: 想從會拋匕首的盜賊手裡偷東西？那是個危險──而且愚蠢──的主意！

Introduction: Even thieves get thirsty.
序章: 連盜賊都會口渴。

Introduction: This dark, seedy place is less than you expected of a Thieves' Guild.  You feel uneasy about the sinister mood here.
序章: 這個陰暗、邋遢的地方比你想像中的盜賊公會還糟。你感到這兒的詭異氣氛讓人不安。

Introduction: The man seated by the table greets you with a scowl, Crusher is his usual, amiable self, and you can't quite make out what lurks behind that massive door.
序章: 坐在桌邊的男人用眉頭歡迎你，克拉什還是老樣子親切得令人生厭，你看不太清楚那扇巨大的門後面藏著什麼。

Introduction: The door is locked, and Boris won't let you in.
序章: 門上了鎖，柏瑞斯不讓你進去。

Introduction: The floor is surprisingly clean compared to the bar above.  The plaster is chipping in several places.  This place gives you the creeps.
序章: 地板比樓上的酒吧出奇地乾淨。但灰泥在幾個地方剝落了。這地方讓人毛骨悚然。

Introduction: You're probably wondering if there is any honor among thieves.
序章: 你可能在想盜賊之間是否有榮譽。

Introduction: That ring belongs to the Healer.  You better give it to her before she gets mad.
序章: 「那枚戒指是治療師的。你最好在她生氣前交給她。」

Introduction: The table is as rough as the man sitting next to it.
序章: 桌子和坐在旁邊的傢伙一樣粗糙。

Introduction: Dag-Nab-It board.
序章: 匕首擲準遊戲木板。

Introduction: The trapdoor allows access to the bar above.
序章: 活板門連通樓上的酒吧。

Introduction: Don't open the lid!  Who knows what will jump out?!
序章: 別打開蓋子！誰知道會跳出什麼東西？！

Introduction: The barred window lets in the foul stench from the alley.
序章: 有鐵條的窗戶讓巷子裡的惡臭飄進來。

Introduction: The barrel is too heavy to pick up.
序章: 酒桶太重了，拿不起來。

Introduction: The barrels are empty and smell of sour beer.
序章: 酒桶是空的，聞起來像酸啤酒。

Introduction: Alms?  Alms for the poor?
序章: 「施捨？誰能施捨一點給窮人？」

Introduction: A word of warning to you -- don't drink the Dragon's Breath!
序章: 「給你個忠告──別喝龍息酒！」

Introduction: A Beggar gets cash only when others get cash, and nobody's got much money here right now, 'cept maybe the Brigands.
序章: 「乞丐只有在別人有錢的時候才有錢，現在這裡沒人有多少錢，除了可能的山賊們。」

Introduction: Rumor has it someone smart took over.  Organized them, trained them -- that sort of thing, maybe.  Nowadays, no one's safe outside of town.
序章: 「傳言說有個聰明的傢伙接管了他們。組織他們、訓練他們──這類事情，也許吧。現在，城外沒人是安全的。」

Introduction: Up to that time, the Brigands were just pains -- dumb guys who sometimes tried to beat up traders outside of town.
序章: 「在那之前，山賊只是麻煩──一些蠢傢伙偶爾會在城外毆打商人。」

Introduction: They sure hurt business.  No one's getting rich but them.
序章: 「他們確實破壞生意。除了他們以外誰都賺不到錢。」

Introduction: There's honest work up at the Baron's castle, I hear.  But begging's my business.
序章: 「聽說男爵城堡裡有誠實的工作。但要飯是我的行當。」

Introduction: I heard Amblin got scared of the Brigands and decided to move away. I still remember the taste of his cider, it was so good.  It was called, 'Amblin Entertainment' from Spielburg.
序章: 「聽說安布林被山賊嚇跑了。我還記得他的蘋果酒味道好極了。那叫『安布林娛樂』，來自史畢柏格。」

Introduction: He works at the tavern.  He lives up to his name.
序章: 「他在酒館工作。他真是名副其實。」

Introduction: Nobody's ever lived to talk what the Night Gaunts are.  I sure don't want to find out.
序章: 「沒人活著說過夜影怪是什麼。我可不想去發現。」

Introduction: I have lots of luck, it's just that it's bad.
序章: 「我運氣多著呢，只是都是壞運氣。」

Introduction: My name is Sam.  I've lived here for five years now, and I think I'll be heading on once the pass clears of snow.  There's supposed to be some good begging towns to the south.  Nice talking to you.  Alms for the poor, Alms!
序章: 「我叫山姆。我在這兒住了五年，等山路積雪融化我就要離開了。聽說南邊有些不錯的要飯城鎮。很高興跟你聊天。施捨給窮人，施捨啊！」

Introduction: I remember when people used to stroll around town after dark.  There's a spell on Spielburg so that it glows at night.  Now, the only people out late are the thieves.
序章: 「我記得以前天黑後人們還會在城裡漫步。史畢柏格有法術讓它在夜裡發光。現在，只有盜賊敢出來活動。」

Introduction: And out of town, man, you don't dare be out there without a light when the sun goes down.  The Night Gaunts'll get you.
序章: 「城外的傢伙，太陽一下山你都不敢沒著火光出去。夜影怪會抓住你。」

Introduction: Heard Silmaria is a good place to be.  It's by the sea.  Think I'll head there after this.
序章: 「聽說西爾馬里亞是個好地方。在海邊。我想等這陣子過了就去那兒。」

Introduction: I've heard talk that some kind of magic keeps things peaceful around here. Folks just don't feel like fighting in town, 'cept maybe Crusher.
序章: 「我聽說某種魔法讓這兒保持平靜。人們就是不想在城裡打架，除了可能克拉什。」

Introduction: Kind of wonder about this alley at times, though.  Feels funny -- sort of like the spell missed it.  It's dark at night, too.
序章: 「但我有時候在想這條巷子。感覺怪怪的──好像法術漏掉了它。夜裡也很黑。」

Introduction: I'm not usually bothered by them, since I don't have much to steal.  They are around, though.  It's not safe to sleep in the streets at night.  Outside you get eaten, inside you get robbed.
序章: 「他們通常不理我，因為我沒什麼可偷的。但他們確實在。晚上在街上睡覺不安全。在外面會被吃掉，在裡面會被搶。」

Introduction: Tourists used to come here regularly for the Spielburg Cider Festival. After the cider industry closed, not much point for the festival without the cider part.
序章: 「遊客以前常來參加史畢柏格蘋果酒節。蘋果酒產業關閉後，沒有蘋果酒的節慶就沒意義了。」

Introduction: Understand this used to be a great place to be.   Traders going in and out; tourists coming and going... perfect begging conditions.
序章: 「聽說這兒曾經是個不錯的地方。商人進出、遊客來去……要飯的天堂。」

Introduction: Things were starting to dry up about the time I got here, just my luck.  The Brigands started getting tough.
序章: 「我到這兒的時候事情就開始蕭條了，真是我的運氣。山賊變得兇狠起來。」

Introduction: Traders used to stop here on the way south to pick up barrels of some special cider made here by a guy named Amblin.
序章: 「商人以前南下時會在這兒停下來，買一些安布林做的特製蘋果酒。」

Introduction: You can get a job cleaning the stable, I understand.  It's great if you want to build up muscles or have a place to spend the night, but you won't get rich. I'd rather beg, myself.
序章: 「據我所知，你可以找個打掃馬廄的工作。如果想練肌肉或找個地方睡覺，那很不錯，但不會發財。我還是比較喜歡要飯。」

Introduction: Stealing from a beggar is not a heroic action.
序章: 從乞丐手裡偷東西不是英雄的舉動。

Introduction: Thank-you.  I really like apples.
序章: 「謝謝你。我真的很喜歡蘋果。」

Introduction: A thin man with very patched clothing holds out an empty bowl.  It's the standard Beggar's bowl every beggar is issued when he graduates from an acredited Beggar's School.
序章: 一個衣衫襤褸的瘦男人伸出空碗。這是每個從認可的乞丐學校畢業的乞丐都會拿到的標準要飯碗。

Introduction: A thin man with very patched clothing holds out a bowl containing only your kind contribution.
序章: 一個衣衫襤褸的瘦男人伸出碗，裡面只有你慈悲的施捨。

Introduction: Thanks.  You know, it's really tough trying to make a living begging in this town since the brigands started scaring all the trade away.
序章: 「謝謝。你知道，自從山賊把生意都嚇跑了，在這城鎮靠要飯維生就變得非常困難。」

Introduction: This used to be such a nice place to live.  My advice to you is not to take up begging.  It just doesn't pay.  And don't go out at night.
序章: 「這地方以前很不錯。我給你的建議是別從事要飯行業。根本賺不到錢。而且別在晚上出門。」

Introduction: Looks like you're about as broke as I am!
序章: 「看起來你窮得跟我一樣！」

Introduction: Thank-you.  Hilde's vegetables are always good.
序章: 「謝謝你。希爾德的蔬菜總是很好吃。」

Introduction: Begging
序章: 要飯

Introduction: Cash
序章: 現金

Introduction: Cider
序章: 蘋果酒

Introduction: Gaunts
序章: 瘦骨怪

Introduction: Luck
序章: 幸運

Introduction: Silver for the poor, Gold for the poor.
序章: 「銀幣給窮人，金幣給窮人。」

Introduction: Night
序章: 夜晚

Introduction: About time I got back to work.  Alms for the poor.  Alms!
序章: 「該回去工作了。施捨給窮人。施捨！」

Introduction: South
序章: 南方

Introduction: Spell
序章: 法術

Introduction: Thieves
序章: 盜賊

Introduction: Traders
序章: 商人

Introduction: The back wall is one of the best examples of a perpendicular wall that you have ever seen.  It reminds you of the time when you thought about climbing Mount Everest.
序章: 這面後牆是你見過垂直牆壁的最佳典範。它讓你想起了曾考慮攀登聖母峰的時光。

Introduction: An early model of a hot dog stand -- or could it be a hamburger stand?
序章: 一個早期型號的熱狗攤——或者它是漢堡攤？

Introduction: You can't make them out from here.
序章: 你從這裡看不清楚他們。

Introduction: Some sharp, pointy object appears to have been thrown at these walls several times.
序章: 看起來有些尖銳的物體被多次投擲到這些牆上。

Introduction: You'll need more practice before you'll be able to climb this wall.
序章: 你需要更多練習才能攀爬這面牆。

Introduction: This grubby place must be an alley.  It feels strange here, colder than the rest of town.  There are some barrels discarded here.  A Beggar holds out a bowl towards you.
序章: 這個骯髒的地方一定是條小巷。這裡感覺很奇怪，比城鎮其他地方更冷。這裡有些丟棄的桶子。一個乞丐朝你伸出一個碗。

Introduction: Nice place.  How does this guy expect to make a living begging in this alley?
序章: 好地方。這傢伙怎麼指望在這條小巷裡靠乞討維生呢？

Introduction: The walls seem pretty normal, but there are some curious marks on the north ends of the buildings.
序章: 牆壁看起來很正常，但建築物的北端有些奇怪的痕跡。

Introduction: The barrels are empty.  One smells of sour beer.  Another barrel reeks of Troll's Sweat.
序章: 桶子是空的。一個聞起來像酸啤酒。另一個桶子散發著巨魔之汗的臭味。

Introduction: Looks like this barrel once contained Dragon's Breath.  The bottom's eaten out.
序章: 看起來這個桶子曾經盛過龍息。底部已經被腐蝕穿了。

Introduction: That's a pretty tall wall.
序章: 這是一堵相當高的牆。

Introduction: That wall sure is tall.  You'll need to practice some more before you'll be ready to climb it.
序章: 那堵牆確實很高。你需要再練習一下才能準備好攀爬它。

Introduction: You just see a glint from here.
序章: 你從這裡只能看到一道閃光。

Introduction: It's a glowing golden coin.
序章: 這是一枚發光的金幣。

Introduction: Some sharp object appears to have been thrown at these walls several times.
序章: 看起來有些尖銳的物體被多次投擲到這些牆上。

Introduction: This place seems sinister at night.  Some strange object is glowing at the other end of the alley.
序章: 這個地方在夜間顯得陰森。在小巷的另一端有些奇怪的物體在發光。

Introduction: You mean that the first victim we've had in months is a thief?  Here I thought we were finally going to make some money.
序章: 「你是說我們好幾個月來遇到的第一個受害者是小偷？我以為我們終於要賺錢了。」

Introduction: Tell Crusher that the password is 'schwertfisch'.  He's in the tavern.
序章: 「告訴克拉舍密碼是『schwertfisch』。他在酒館裡。」

Introduction: Now go before I forget you made the sign and I make you pay anyway.
序章: 「現在滾蛋，趁我還沒忘記你做出那個手勢，不然我讓你付出代價。」

Introduction: You had better check in at the Thieves' Guild before you get in trouble for practicing without a license.  Find Crusher in the tavern and give him the password.
序章: 「你最好先到盜賊公會報到，免得因為沒執照就練習而惹上麻煩。在酒館裡找克拉舍，給他密碼。」

Introduction: What's that supposed to be?  You trying to pretend you're a thief or something?  Har, har.
序章: 「那是什麼東西？你在假裝你是小偷什麼的？哈，哈。」

Introduction: The thieves seem to be ignoring you.
序章: 盜賊們似乎在忽視你。

Introduction: The whole place is in need of repair.
序章: 整個地方都需要修繕。

Introduction: Yeah, yeah, we know, you're in the Guild too.  How's a poor independent businessman supposed to make a living around here with all this competition?
序章: 「是是是，我們知道你也在公會裡。一個可憐的獨立商人怎麼樣才能在這裡的這麼多競爭中謀生呢？」

Introduction: The dagger looks sharp and very deadly.
序章: 匕首看起來很鋒利，非常致命。

Introduction: This man may be smiling at you, but he doesn't seem very friendly.  His florid face tells you that this thief is a regular at the "Aces and Eights" tavern.
序章: 這個男人可能在向你微笑，但他看起來不太友好。他紅潤的臉龐告訴你這個盜賊是「Aces and Eights」酒館的常客。

Introduction: This man may be smiling at you, but he doesn't seem very friendly.  He may be short, but that dagger makes him look 7 feet tall.
序章: 這個男人可能在向你微笑，但他看起來不太友好。他可能很矮，但那把匕首讓他看起來有7英尺高。

Introduction: See that dagger?  The next one is centered on your back.
序章: 「看到那把匕首了嗎？下一把就對準你的背了。」

Introduction: Give Sneak there your cash and you walk out of here.  It's a simple trade, your life for your money.
序章: 「把你的錢給斯尼克，然後你可以走出這裡。這很簡單，用你的生命換你的錢。」

Introduction: That 'Coin Light' spell gets them every time.  See that dagger?  The next one is centered on your back.
序章: 「那個『Coin Light』法術每次都能騙到他們。看到那把匕首了嗎？下一把就對準你的背了。」

Introduction: Give Sneak there your cash and you walk out of here. It's a simple trade, your life for your money.
序章: 「把你的錢給斯尼克，然後你可以走出這裡。這很簡單，用你的生命換你的錢。」

Introduction: Aw, ya forgot to pay.  What a shame.
序章: 「啊，你忘記付錢了。太可惜了。」

Introduction: Hey, Sneak, this guy's hard of hearing or something.  I told him to stay out of our alley, and he didn't listen.  Let's teach him a little lesson about listening to his betters.
序章: 「嘿，斯尼克，這傢伙好像是個聾子還是什麼。我告訴他別進我們的小巷，他沒聽。讓我們教他一堂關於聽從比他高明的人的課。」

Introduction: As Slink reaches for his dagger, you draw your Thieves' Guild license...  You win by a close margin.
序章: 當斯林克伸手去拔匕首時，你拿出你的盜賊公會執照。..你差點就贏了。

Introduction: All right, so you got your license.  How nice.  Guess we don't get to kill you right now.  But stay out of our alley, 'cause we got real short memories, like.
序章: 「好吧，所以你有執照。很好。看來我們現在沒法殺你。但別進我們的小巷，因為我們的記性特別差，懂吧。」

Introduction: As Slink reaches for his dagger, you draw your Thieves' Guild license...  You *almost* make it.
序章: 當斯林克伸手去拔匕首時，你拿出你的盜賊公會執照。..你*幾乎*成功了。

Introduction: Hey, Sneak, this guy's hard of hearing or something.  I told him to get a license last time he was here, and he didn't listen.
序章: 「嘿，斯尼克，這傢伙好像是個聾子還是什麼。我上次告訴他要去辦執照，他沒聽。」

Introduction: Let's teach him a little lesson about listening to his betters.
序章: 「讓我們教他一堂關於聽從比他高明的人的課。」

Introduction: Why, how nice to see you again.  I do hope (for your sake) you brought us some nice shiny new silver.
序章: 「哦，很高興再次見到你。我希望(為了你好)你帶來了一些漂亮的新銀幣。」

Introduction: As you start to draw your weapon, the thieves jump you.
序章: 當你開始拔武器時，盜賊們撲向你。

Introduction: That's all right.  We already have all your money, but it was certainly nice of you to offer.
序章: 「沒關係。我們已經拿了你全部的錢，但你的好意還是很好。」

Introduction: Thanks, and have a nice evening.  It's been a pleasure doing business with you.  You can find your way out, I'm sure.  But make it fast, before my dagger slips.
序章: 「感謝，祝你晚上愉快。與你做生意很愉快。我相信你能找到出口。但快點，免得我的匕首滑出去。」

Introduction: Hey!  You don't have enough money to be worth our while.  How do you expect us poor independent businessmen to make a living?
序章: 「嘿！你的錢不夠我們費心。你怎麼指望我們這些可憐的獨立商人謀生呢？」

Introduction: My finger is getting itchy, so just pay up and get out alive.
序章: 「我的手指在發癢，所以乖乖付錢，活著滾出去。」

Introduction: My finger is starting to get a bit twitchy, so forget about questions, just pay the man and get out alive.
序章: 「我的手指開始有點抽搐，所以別問問題，乖乖付錢給這傢伙，活著滾出去。」

Introduction: Affects where knife goes.
序章: 「影響刀的方向。」

Introduction: Place hand on red dot.
序章: 「把手放在紅點上。」

Introduction: Hey, nice game.  It's a tie.
序章: 「嘿，好遊戲。平手。」

Introduction: You got me that time.  Say, you're pretty tough!
序章: 「你這次贏了我。說，你很強啊！」

Introduction: So, you think you're pretty hot stuff, eh?  Well, we'll see about that.
序章: 「所以，你認為你很了不起，嗯？好吧，我們就看看吧。」

Introduction: If you don't bet, you can't play.
序章: 「如果你不下注，就不能玩。」

Introduction: It's a tie.  Let's double the wager.
序章: 「平手。我們加倍賭注吧。」

Introduction: Looks like I won that one.  Must have gotten lucky.
序章: 「看起來我贏了那一局。肯定是運氣好。」

Introduction: Hey!  This is cash on the table.  You can't bet what you don't have.
序章: 「嘿！這是現金。你不能賭你沒有的東西。」

Introduction: Now, let's not get carried away.
序章: 「現在，別太過分了。」

Introduction: Affects how far knife flies.
序章: 「影響刀飛多遠。」

Introduction: Place hand on slider.
序章: 「把手放在滑塊上。」

Introduction: It's your hand.
序章: 「這是你的手。」

Introduction: Shall we play again?
序章: 「我們再玩一次嗎？」

Introduction: That's the "Dag-Nab-It" board.
序章: 那是「Dag-Nab-It」棋盤。

Introduction: The game is called Dag-Nab-It.  You take turns with the Chief Thief throwing daggers at the board.  Each player gets three turns, and throws three daggers in each turn.
序章: 遊戲叫「Dag-Nab-It」。你和盜賊首領輪流向棋盤投擲匕首。每個玩家有三輪，每輪投擲三把匕首。

Introduction: More points are scored the closer each dagger hits to the center of the board.  Highest score wins the bet.
序章: 匕首越接近棋盤中心，得分就越高。最高分贏得賭注。

Introduction: Adjust force and angle of throw with the arrow keys or mouse, according to your hand position.  Press Enter or click on the small dag-nab-it board to throw.
序章: 根據你的手部位置，用方向鍵或滑鼠調整投擲的力度和角度。按 Enter 或點擊小的 dag-nab-it 棋盤來投擲。

Introduction: Quest for Glory I was designed and directed by Lori Ann Cole and Corey Cole. If you liked this game, then you'll love the other fine Sierra games they have designed: "Quest for Glory 2:  Trial By Fire", "The Castle of Dr. Brain", and, for the younger crowd, "Mixed Up Fairy Tales".
序章: 《英雄傳奇 I》由 Lori Ann Cole 和 Corey Cole 設計和導演。如果你喜歡這個遊戲，你也會喜歡他們設計的其他優秀 Sierra 遊戲：《英雄傳奇 2：火焰審判》、《顱骨博士的城堡》和針對年輕人的《混亂的童話故事》。

Introduction: And look for "Quest for Glory 3:  The Wages of War" in your local stores starting in Autumn, 1992.
序章: 並在 1992 年秋季開始在你當地商店尋找《英雄傳奇 3：戰爭的代價》。

Introduction: The incredible artwork was tastefully rendered by:  Jon Bock, Gerald Moore, Arturo Sinclair, Jeff Crowe, Gloria Garland, Willis Wong, Diana Wilson, Eric Kasner, Richard Powell, and Roger Hardy.
序章: 令人難以置信的藝術作品由以下人員精心繪製：Jon Bock、Gerald Moore、Arturo Sinclair、Jeff Crowe、Gloria Garland、Willis Wong、Diana Wilson、Eric Kasner、Richard Powell 和 Roger Hardy。

Introduction: Original Music by:  Mark Seibert, Aubrey Hodges, and Spike Jones.
序章: 原始音樂由 Mark Seibert、Aubrey Hodges 和 Spike Jones 創作。

Introduction: The nastiest monsters in the game were, as always, the bugs.  Our heroic gang of bugbusters deserve thanks:  Joe Carper, Dave Clingman, Mike Pickhinke, and a cast of dozens.
序章: 遊戲中最討厭的怪物，一如既往，是程式錯誤。我們英勇的除蟲隊值得感謝：Joe Carper、Dave Clingman、Mike Pickhinke 和許多其他人。

Introduction: The Peaks of Programming Perfection were scaled by:  Tom DeSalvo, Vana Baker, Richard Aronson, Carlos Escobar, Robert Mallory, Oliver Brelsford, Brett Miller, Gary Kamigawachi, Sean Mooney, and Bob Fischbach.

序章: 你沒有武器可以發動衝鋒。

Introduction: Cast what???
序章: 施什麼魔法？??

Introduction: Perhaps you shouldn't explore dark alleys at night unless you are looking for trouble.  Or you should look in your manual to learn how to deal with thieves.
序章: 也許你不應該在夜間探索黑暗巷弄，除非你在找麻煩。或者你應該看看手冊上如何對付盜賊。

Introduction: Erana missed this spot.
序章: 伊瑞娜漏掉了這個地方。

Introduction: Talk about an unpleasant trip to Belgium.  Next time avoid antwerp (and the trip rope) altogether.
序章: 比利時之旅真不愉快。下次乾脆別去安特衛普(還有那條絆馬繩)。

Introduction: You hit some lousy fall weather....
序章: 你碰上了糟糕的秋天天氣……

Introduction: You're obviously in no shape to continue the game.
序章: 你顯然沒有體力繼續遊戲了。

Introduction: Trounced by a bounce!
序章: 被彈跳怪狠狠揍了一頓！

Introduction: Congratulations!  Thanks to your efforts as a moving target, the Spielburg Bandits are a cinch to win the archery contest in next year's Brigandiad.
序章: 恭喜！感謝你當靶子的努力，史畢柏格山賊輕輕鬆鬆就能贏得明年「山賊運動會」的射箭比賽。

Introduction: Next Stop:  Pincushion City.
序章: 下一站：針墊城。

Introduction: That's funny.  I could have sworn I warned you about eating too many Magic Mushrooms.  Your mind permanently warped, you die a garishly polka-dotted death.
序章: 真搞笑。我發誓我警告過你別吃太多魔法蘑菇。你的心智永久變形，以花俏的圓點花紋死亡。

Introduction: Where did all the pretty horses go?
序章: 那些漂亮的馬都去哪裡了？

Introduction: It was a tough battle, and you lost.  Never fear!  All you have to do is restore your game, and...  What do you mean, "Restore WHAT game?"
序章: 這場戰鬥很激烈，你輸了。別害怕！你只需要讀檔，然後……等等，你說什麼「讀什麼檔？」

Introduction: What a monster!
序章: 真是個怪物！

Introduction: Too late, you remember... bear claws weren't on your diet.
序章: 太遲了，你才想起……熊的爪子不在你的食譜上。

Introduction: Realty Whirled
序章: 現實旋風

Introduction: The brigands have an even temperament... all bad!  You seem to have a knack for doing the wrong thing.
序章: 山賊個性很平穩……全都很壞！你好像特別擅長做錯事。

Introduction: You ring the bell and your bell gets rung.
序章: 你敲了鐘，結果被敲了一頓。

Introduction: At least you could have said "Yes" before you croaked!
序章: 你至少可以在掛掉之前說聲「好」!

Introduction: Breakfast of Champions?
序章: 冠軍的早餐？

Introduction: Now you've done it!  It's hard to be a hero when you're locked up for breaking and entering (or entering and breaking, as the case may be).  Be a little more intelligent (and a lot quieter) if you ever try something like this again.
序章: 現在你完蛋了！當你因為非法侵入(或侵入後破壞，不管哪種)被鎖起來時，很難成為英雄。下次要是再試什麼花樣，聰明點(也安靜點)。

Introduction: Otto and the Sheriff rise with the dawn, and find you in their house.  Be a little more intelligent (and a lot faster) if you ever try something like this again.
序章: 奧圖和治安官在黎明時起床，發現你在他們家。下次要是再試什麼花樣，聰明點(也快點)。

Introduction: Up Past Your Bedtime
序章: 超過就寢時間

Introduction: Criminal carelessness.
序章: 犯罪性的疏忽。

Introduction: Die drei Knochelkopfen grab you off the table.  You should have waited until they were out of reach.
序章: 三個骷髏頭怪搶過去把你從桌上抓走。你應該等到他們夠不著你再說。

Introduction: Nyuk Nyuk Nyuk -- Mee Mee Mee Mee Mee...
序章: 紐啊紐啊紐啊──咪咪咪咪咪……

Introduction: There are still too many brigands for you to fight in here.  You need to find some way to block one of the doors.
序章: 這裡的山賊還是太多，你打不過。你得想辦法封住其中一扇門。

Introduction: You're half right but completely dead.
序章: 你一半對，但已經死透了。

Introduction: You'd better find a way to prevent the brigands from getting at you from all sides if you want to survive this.
序章: 你想活下來就得想辦法防止山賊四面夾擊。

Introduction: Don't you feel a draft?
序章: 你沒感覺到吹風嗎？

Introduction: You're hopelessly surrounded.  You should have stopped those brigands somehow.  Maybe you'll see the light.
序章: 你無可救藥地被包圍了。你應該想辦法阻止那些山賊。也許你會看到光明。

Introduction: Those guys look familiar.
序章: 那些傢伙看起來很眼熟。

Introduction: Done in by poisoned daggers and desperate desperadoes!  Try to avoid annoying that deadly duo next time.
序章: 被毒匕首和孤注一擲的亡命漢幹掉！下次別惹那對致命組合。

Introduction: Timing is everything.
序章: 時機就是一切。

Introduction: Whoops!  Who'd have thought that guys that look that stupid could figure out to go around the table beFORE they ran all the way up to the fallen candelabra.
序章: 哎呀！誰想得到看起來這麼呆的傢伙居然能想到在撞上掉下來的燭台之前，先從桌子四周繞過去。

Introduction: Timing is everything.
序章: 時機就是一切。

Introduction: Hmm...  Maybe letting the brigands see you block the door with the chair wasn't a good idea.
序章: 嗯……也許讓山賊看到你用椅子堵門不是個好主意。

Introduction: One peek is worth two finesses.
序章: 一眼勝過千般心計。

Introduction: You could not withstand the force of the blast from the Kobold's magically protected chest.
序章: 你無法承受地精的魔法保護寶箱的爆破力。

Introduction: Blast!
序章: 爆炸！

Introduction: Didn't anyone ever tell you that a hero is supposed to be brave even when he doesn't feel that way?  Looks like you found the coward's way out... sauteed in wine sauce.
序章: 沒人告訴過你英雄即使不覺得勇敢也得表現出勇敢嗎？看起來你找到了逃兵的出路……用葡萄酒醬燒烤。

Introduction: Shame on you!
序章: 你真丟臉！

Introduction: This was not the ending you had in mind when you read the manual on "How to be a Hero."  Either you just aren't ready to take on this situation, or there is a better way to accomplish your mission.
序章: 這不是你讀「如何成為英雄」手冊時想到的結局。要嘛你還沒準備好面對這種情況，要嘛有更好的方法完成任務。

Introduction: You knew this job was dangerous...
序章: 你知道這工作很危險……

Introduction: You're not a quick learner.  Now you know why the grass grows so well around the porticullis.
序章: 你學得不快。現在你懂為什麼草在吊閘周圍長得那麼好了。

Introduction: OH NOOOOOOO!
Done in by daggers and a desperate desperado!  Try to avoid annoying that deadly duo next time.
序章: 被匕首和孤注一擲的亡命漢幹掉！下次別惹那對致命組合。

Introduction: Daggered to death.
序章: 被匕首戳死。

Introduction: You can't believe the Chief Thief's reflexes as he instantly reacts to your intention to pull a fast one.  A dagger flies like lightning and...
序章: 你不敢相信盜賊頭子的反應速度，他一下子就察覺到你想耍花招。一把匕首快如閃電般飛來……

Introduction: These guys are MEAN!
序章: 這些傢伙凶狠！

Introduction: For such wimpy-looking creatures, those fairies sure can play rough!  You never danced so hard in your entire life (which is now over).
序章: 外表看起來這麼柔弱的生物，妖精們可真夠兇狠！你這輩子沒被打得這麼慘過(現在你的人生結束了)。

Introduction: Land of 1000 Dances
序章: 千舞之地

Introduction: I don't mean to throw this in your face, but why did you throw that in your face?
序章: 我不是想揭你的傷疤，但你為什麼要往自己臉上砸？

Introduction: The Little Old Lady's screams finally brought the sheriff and Otto.  Next time you'd better finish your "work" before dawn!
序章: 小老太太的尖叫聲終於引來治安官和奧圖。下次你最好在天亮前完成你的「工作」!

Introduction: You Hate Morning People
序章: 你討厭起床的人

Introduction: Four brigand archers, shooting at close range from cover, was too much for even a correspondence-school Hero like you to survive.  There are times to fight, and there are times to run.  This was one of the latter.
序章: 四個山賊弓箭手在掩護下近距離射擊，就連你這種函授學校英雄也活不下去。有時候要戰鬥，有時候要逃跑。這次是後者。

Introduction: You're All a-Quiver
序章: 你渾身發抖

Introduction: The brigands were waiting for you to come back.  They expected you to make another try for some of their treasure.
序章: 山賊在等你回來。他們以為你會再試一次搶他們的寶藏。

Introduction: Greedy Greedy Greedy
序章: 貪心貪心貪心

Introduction: Yes, it was possible that this drunkard would somehow regain his senses, leap to his feet, realize that you didn't belong there, and do you in.  On the other hand, you might have done a little better worrying about the wide-awake brigands closing in on you with their spears.
序章: 沒錯，這個酒鬼有可能恢復神智、跳起來、發現你不該來這裡，然後把你宰了。另一方面，你應該更擔心那些清醒的山賊用長槍包圍你。

Introduction: The lesser of two evils.
序章: 兩害相權取其輕。

Introduction: Thinking about all that treasure cost you time and more.  Some folks just don't know when to leave.
序章: 淨想著寶藏讓你浪費時間，甚至更多。有些人就是不懂什麼時候該閃人。

Introduction: Wearing out your welcome
序章: 欠缺應對時機

Introduction: The brigand leader is deadly with a sword.  The magic spell that possesses her makes her an invincible warrior.
序章: 山賊頭子劍術致命。附身她的魔法咒語讓她成為不敗戰士。

Introduction: One thrust... and that's it???
序章: 刺一下……就這樣？??

Introduction: That was a little too much for you.  You collapse from exhaustion and die.
序章: 你有點承受不住。你累到倒下並死亡。

Introduction: Death from Overwork
序章: 過勞死

Introduction: You need to guard your flanks before a frontal assault is possible.
序章: 在正面攻擊之前，你得保護你的側翼。

Introduction: A side of Hero
序章: 英雄側寫

Introduction: Had you been healthier, you probably could have survived that fall. In your weakened condition, however, you succumbed to your injuries.
序章: 要是你身體更健康，也許能活下來。但在你體弱的狀況下，你死於傷口。

Introduction: Your figure remains still and silent.
序章: 你的身體靜止無聲。

Introduction: I score that about a 3.  Those diving lessons were a complete waste.  Maybe you should have taken up bowling.
序章: 我打分 3 分。那些跳水課真是浪費。也許你該去學保齡球。

Introduction: An abridged ending....
序章: 簡短的結局……

Introduction: ... as you realize those large porcupines down here are really other heroes who collected arrows the hard way.
序章: ……當你意識到下面那些大豪豬其實是用硬方式收集箭矢的其他英雄。

Introduction: Quills run up your spine....
序章: 刺毛沿著你的脊椎豎起……

Introduction: A small sign reads "Booby Trap."  The brigands approach to see the booby they've caught.  You think to yourself, "If it looks like a trap, and it feels like a trap, maybe it IS a trap."
序章: 一塊小牌子寫著「陷阱」。山賊走近去看他們抓到的「獵物」。你心想「看起來像陷阱、感覺像陷阱，那它就是陷阱啦」。

Introduction: The old "rug over the pit" trick.
序章: 老套的「地毯蓋坑洞」把戲。

Introduction: You're hopelessly outnumbered.  You should have left this room when you had a chance.
序章: 你被絕望地包圍。你應該在還有機會時離開這個房間。

Introduction: Molasses in January.
序章: 一月的糖蜜

Introduction: It seems a shame to have such a promising career go up in flames (assuming she decides to flambe you).  Wouldn't it have been better just to do a small favor for such a sweet little old Ogress?
序章: 看起來很可惜，前途似錦的事業就這樣付之一炬(假設她決定把你烤了)。就不能為了這個甜蜜的小食人妖老奶奶幫點小忙嗎？

Introduction: Of all the lazy...!
序章: 好懶的……!

Introduction: Next time you are asked a question by someone who has just turned you into a frog, perhaps you should be more polite.  It's better than being fricasseed!
序章: 下次有人把你變成青蛙然後問你問題時，也許你該更禮貌。比被燉了好多了！

Introduction: Now you're really in the soup!
序章: 現在你真的麻煩大了！

Introduction: Was it something I said?
序章: 是我說的什麼話嗎？

Introduction: The End of the Trail...
序章: 小路的盡頭……

Introduction: As the ghosts suck the life out of your body, and you crumple into the dust of the old graveyard, you think unspeakable thoughts of torture and torment directed at vicious programmers and insidious game designers.  (But please buy our next game anyway!)
序章: 當鬼魂吸乾你的生命，你在古老墓園的塵埃中崩潰時，你心想對狠毒程式員和陰險遊戲設計師進行難以名狀的刑罰和折磨。(但拜託還是買我們下一款遊戲啦！)

Introduction: Oh-h-h-h-h-h-h!
Next time, pick on someone your own size.
序章: 下次找個你體格相當的對手。

Introduction: The bigger they come, the harder they hit
序章: 個子越大，打得越狠

Introduction: Obviously, getting in the goon's way was not one of your brighter ideas. You've had it now!
序章: 很顯然，擋在手下的路上不是你的聰慧之舉。現在你完蛋了！

Introduction: You hear a guard yell for help.  Four guards inside the barracks grab their weapons and overwhelm you.  As they drag you into the castle and down into the dungeon, you realize that was not quite the right thing to do.
序章: 你聽到衛兵喊救命。兵營裡的四個衛兵拿起武器壓倒你。當他們把你拖進城堡、拖進地牢時，你意識到這確實不是正確的做法。

Introduction: Good Guard!
序章: 好衛兵！

Introduction: No sooner do you draw your weapon than the guards have their weapons out and quickly disarm you.  They promptly drag you into the castle and down into the dungeon, which is not a very likely spot for the attainment of hero status.
序章: 你剛拔出武器，守衛就也拔出武器並快速繳了你的械。他們馬上把你拖進城堡、拖進地牢，那不是獲得英雄地位的好地方。

Introduction: En Garde!
序章: 劍外！

Introduction: And dead...  You feel dead, too.  In your weakened condition, you succumbed to a mild-mannered hermit's propensity for Tarzan imitations.  Back up and play it again, Sam.
序章: 然後死了……你也感覺自己死了。在你體弱的狀況下，你死於一個溫和隱士學泰山叫聲的傾向。退出重新玩吧，山姆。

Introduction: ... and thin...
Next time you swing at innocent inanimate objects, make sure you are strong enough to survive when they swing back at you!
序章: 下次你砍無辜的無生命物體時，確認你夠強健能活下來它們反擊的時候！

Introduction: Your plan seems to have backfired
序章: 你的計畫似乎適得其反

Introduction: It's hard to be a hero when you're sitting in a jail cell.  The Sheriff apologized for your broken arm, but he did warn you (didn't he?) that Otto was only partly trained.  Now, you'll probably be more careful when you're robbing someone.
序章: 當你坐在牢房裡時很難成為英雄。治安官為你的斷臂道歉，但他確實警告過你(不是嗎？)奧圖只是半訓練狀態。現在你下次搶人時應該會更小心。

Introduction: Well, it looks like the Dryad really made you eat dirt.  You're the spitting image of a Spirea plant, and you'll be hanging around for a long, long time.
序章: 嗯，看起來樹精真的讓你吃土了。你簡直就是繡球花的翻版，你會在那邊掛很久很久。

Introduction: Plant it, fella!
序章: 種它吧，老兄！

Introduction: You are staggered by the Dryad's spell.  She appears to enjoy having lots of bucks.
序章: 你被樹精的咒語震撼。她好像喜歡有一堆錢。

Introduction: Oh, deer.
序章: 喔，親愛的。

Introduction: It takes too long to draw your weapon against someone with a dagger in his hand.  It doesn't pay to try to fight someone who uses poisoned daggers.
序章: 對著拿著匕首的人拔武器太慢了。想和用毒匕首的人打鬥根本不划算。

Introduction: Congratulations!  You've just won a gold medal in the log jump. Posthumously.
序章: 恭喜！你剛贏得了原木跳躍的金牌。死後。

Introduction: So much for your endorsements....
序章: 就這樣丟了你的代言機會……

Introduction: When the Little Old Lady awakens to see what's going on, you have to concede to her (through lips that are as raw as hamburger) that you've been licked! She summons the Sheriff and his goon, Otto.
序章: 當小老太太醒來看看發生什麼事時，你得承認(用嘴唇腫成漢堡的樣子)你被舔敗了！她召喚治安官和他的手下奧圖。

Introduction: What a cute little kitty!
序章: 多麼可愛的小貓咪！

Introduction: The Little Old Lady's cries finally bring the Sheriff and Otto to her rescue. They find you pinned to the floor, unconscious, dreaming deliriously of death by sandpaper.
序章: 小老太太的尖叫聲終於把治安官和奧圖吸引來救她。他們發現你被釘在地板上、昏迷、做著被砂紙磨死的譫妄夢。

Introduction: Better stay downstairs next time.
序章: 下次最好待在樓下。

Introduction: You're mad as heck, and you're not going to take it anymore.  As a matter of fact, you CAN'T take it anymore.  Restore your strength and health and try again.
序章: 你氣炸了，再也受不了。事實上，你真的受不了了。恢復你的力量和生命力再試一次。

Introduction: You're the Fall Guy again.
序章: 你又成替罪羊了。

Introduction: In matters of magic, I am your master Seek not to slay me with Sendings so small.
序章: 「在魔法之事，我是你的主人莫用如此渺小的魔法來殺我。」

Introduction: Just because he's big doesn't mean he's stupid!
序章: 就因為他個子大不代表他很蠢！

Introduction: Because you failed to meet Baba Yaga's DEADline, her curse turns you into a frog on the spot, and you are forced to live out your years dodging Sauruses (Saurii?) with large feet.
序章: 因為你沒有趕上芭芭雅嘎的死亡期限，她的詛咒當場把你變成青蛙，你被迫用剩下的日子躲避有大腳掌的索魯斯(索魯西？)。

Introduction: Curses!
序章: 詛咒！

Introduction: While you were asleep, something decided to make a meal of you.  You're not sure what it was, but you don't really care at this point.  You shouldn't go to sleep where the creatures of the night can get you.
序章: 你睡著時，什麼東西決定拿你當大餐。你不確定是什麼，但現在你真的不在乎。你不應該在夜間生物能抓到你的地方睡覺。

Introduction: Night Gaunt Got Ya.
序章: 夜間幽靈逮到你了。

Introduction: You're hopelessly surrounded.  You should have taken quicker action against these Knochelkopfs.  Elevate your thoughts to a higher plane.
序章: 你無可救藥地被包圍了。你應該更快對付這些骷髏頭怪。把你的思想提升到更高的境界。

Introduction: Woo Woo Woo -- Ruff Ruff Ruff.
序章: 烏烏烏──汪汪汪。

Introduction: Caught in combat with neither sword nor dagger, you are unable to resist the monster's fierce onslaught.  Try to be better equipped in your next life.
序章: 在沒有劍或匕首的戰鬥中，你無法抵抗怪物的猛烈攻擊。下輩子記得帶齊全裝備。

Introduction: Caught in combat with neither sword nor dagger, you are unable to resist the monster's fierce onslaught. Try to be better equipped in your next life.
序章: 在沒有劍或匕首的戰鬥中，你無法抵抗怪物的猛烈攻擊。下輩子記得帶齊全裝備。

Introduction: Bare Hands vs. Teeth and Claws, etc.
序章: 赤手空拳對牙齒和爪子等

Introduction: When it comes to breaking in, it looks like the only thing broken is your head!  When at last you come to your senses, you find yourself in a jail cell. By the time you get out of here, you'll be too old to be a hero.
序章: 說到撬鎖，看起來只有你的腦袋被撬破了！當你終於清醒時，你發現自己在牢房裡。等你出獄，你已經老到不能當英雄了。

Introduction: Criminal carelessness
序章: 犯罪性的疏忽

Introduction: You are overcome by the power of the Kobold Wizard's magic.  You need either to practice your skills or to plan a better attack strategy.
序章: 你被地精巫師的魔法力量擊敗。你得要嘛練習技能，要嘛想出更好的攻擊策略。

Introduction: Struck By Lightning
序章: 被閃電擊中

Introduction: You never dreamed a feather pillow could be so HARD!  Or was it the floor? You also didn't realize how hard it is for a thief to be a Hero.  The Thief of Baghdad never had this kind of luck!
序章: 你從沒夢想過羽毛枕頭能這麼硬！或者是地板？你也沒意識到盜賊想當英雄有多難。巴格達盜賊從沒遭過這種運氣！

Introduction: The old ticker just couldn't keep going.  Maybe you shouldn't have missed the annual visit to your local Healer.
序章: 老心臟再也跳不動了。也許你不該錯過去當地治療師的年度檢查。

Introduction: Before you confront someone who is obviously more powerful than you are, give yourself a chance to reflect.
序章: 在你對抗明顯比你強大的人之前，先給自己反省的機會。

Introduction: Baba Yaga says you have good taste.
序章: 芭芭雅嘎說你的品味不錯。

Introduction: Maybe you really shouldn't have tried the Dragon's Breath!  Better luck next time, and we hope you saved your game.
序章: 也許你本來不應該嘗試龍息！下次好運，我們希望你存檔了。

Introduction: Talk about a "fiery brew"
序章: 說什麼「烈火熬湯」

Introduction: That's it!!!  You've hurt you for the last time!
序章: 夠了！！！你最後一次傷害自己了！

Introduction: You searched for silver, but all you found was iron.  Iron arrowheads, that is.
序章: 你找銀幣，但你找到的只是鐵。也就是鐵箭頭啦。

Introduction: Greedy, greedy...
序章: 貪心，貪心……

Introduction: Naughty, naughty.  The Sheriff and Otto arrive on the scene and arrest you for "blatant power-gaming".  You have to *work* at it to become a *real* hero!
序章: 壞壞。治安官和奧圖現身逮捕你「明目張膽亂遊戲」。你得真的去*努力*才能成為*真正*的英雄！

Introduction: Try to stay "in character" next time
序章: 下次試試保持「角色設定」

Introduction: The brigands oblige you by making your pleasant rest permanent.
序章: 山賊樂意地讓你永遠休息。

Introduction: You curl up for a nice little nap.  The brigands break down the door.  You wake up dead.
序章: 你蜷縮著打了個小盹。山賊破門而入。你醒了，已經死了。

Introduction: He who hesitates...
序章: 猶豫不決的人……

Introduction: Uh oh.  You made too much noise.  The Sheriff and Otto arrive on the scene and arrest you for "criminal carelessness".  Work a little faster next time!
序章: 天哪。你太吵了。治安官和奧圖現身逮捕你「魯莽犯罪」。下次快一點！

Introduction: Nobody said this job was easy
序章: 沒人說這工作很簡單。

Introduction: He saw you casting a spell, and he already had a dagger in his hand.  It doesn't pay to try to fight someone who uses poisoned daggers.
序章: 他看到你施法，已經拔出匕首。試著和用毒匕首的人對戰不值得。

Introduction: Hold the arsenic, please
序章: 請別用砒霜

Introduction: There must be reasons to stop and let five archers draw beads on you while you attempt to cast a spell, but none come to mind.
序章: 肯定有理由讓你停下來被五個弓箭手瞄準，同時你試著施法，但我想不出原因。

Introduction: Wear any targets lately?
序章: 最近有當過靶子嗎？

Introduction: You really brought down the house!  Too bad you were under it at the time.
序章: 你真的把房子給拆了！可惜你當時在底下。

Introduction: Bad strategy!
序章: 策略差勁！

Introduction: That's it!  You've hurt you for the last time!
序章: 夠了！你最後一次傷害自己了！

Introduction: They say that the cops don't catch any rocket scientists.  Well, they sure didn't this time!
序章: 他們說警察不會抓到火箭科學家。好吧，這次肯定沒抓到！

Introduction: Criminal stupidity
序章: 犯罪級的蠢

Introduction: Hey, man, like, when it's time to swing, it's time to SWING!
序章: 嘿老兄，你知道嗎，當該擺動時就是該擺動！

Introduction: No sense of rhythm
序章: 沒有節奏感

Introduction: That hermit seems to know his "Trigger" spells pretty well.  He sure pulled the trigger on you (not to mention the plug) by teleporting you to the top of the falls without a barrel.
序章: 那隱士看起來很懂「觸發術」。他肯定對你發動了觸發（更別提直接結束了），把你傳送到瀑布頂端卻沒給桶子。

Introduction: You're all wet
序章: 你全身濕透了

Introduction: It was a tough battle, and you lost.  But never fear!  All you have to do is restore your game, and...  What do you mean, "Restore WHAT game?"
序章: 那是場硬戰，你輸了。但別害怕！你只要還原遊戲，然後……你說什麼呢，「還原什麼遊戲？」

Introduction: What a monster!
序章: 真是個怪物！

Introduction: This time the joke fell flat.  That was a truly dirty trick.  Too bad you won't have a chance to get even... or will you?
序章: 這次玩笑沒成功。那真是個卑鄙的把戲。太遺憾你沒機會報仇……或者說，有嗎？

Introduction: This way to the Egress
序章: 出口在這邊

Introduction: Cancel
序章: 取消

Introduction: New save-game directory:
序章: 新的存檔目錄：

Introduction: %s is not a valid directory.
序章: %s 不是有效的目錄。

Introduction: You must type a description for the game.
序章: 你必須為遊戲輸入描述。

Introduction: OK
序章: 確認

Introduction: Change Directory
序章: 變更目錄

Introduction: Your save game disk is full.  You must either use another disk or save over an existing saved game.
序章: 你的存檔磁片已滿。你必須使用另一張磁片或覆蓋現有的存檔。

Introduction: GAME
序章: 遊戲

Introduction: Insert the %s disk in drive %s.
序章: 將 %s 磁片插入 %s 磁碟機。

Introduction: OK
序章: 確認

Introduction: SAVE GAME
序章: 存檔

Introduction: That game was saved under a different interpreter.  It cannot be restored.
序章: 該遊戲是用不同的直譯器存檔的。無法還原。

Introduction: This icon is for actions and special items.
序章: 這個圖示是用來進行動作和使用特殊物品的。

Introduction: This icon lets you cast one of your amazing spells.
序章: 這個圖示讓你施放你的神奇法術。

Introduction: This icon brings up the control panel.
序章: 這個圖示開啟控制面板。

Introduction: Use this icon to do things.
序章: 使用此圖示來做事。

Introduction: Here's where you learn more than you care to know about the creators of this game.
序章: 這裡讓你了解你想知道或不想知道的遊戲創作者資訊。

Introduction: Raise this level to increase the amount of background animation.  Lower it if game play seems sluggish.
序章: 提高此等級以增加背景動畫量。若遊戲速度緩慢則降低它。

Introduction: To learn about the other items in this window, first click here, then pass the question mark over the other items.
序章: 要瞭解此視窗的其他項目，先按一下這裡，然後把問號移到其他項目上。

Introduction: Select this icon to resume game play.
序章: 選擇此圖示以繼續遊戲。

Introduction: Select this icon to leave the game.
序章: 選擇此圖示以離開遊戲。

Introduction: Select this icon if you wish to restart the game from the very beginning.
序章: 如果你想從頭開始遊戲，請選擇此圖示。

Introduction: Select this icon to restore a game you saved earlier.
序章: 選擇此圖示以還原之前存檔的遊戲。

Introduction: Select this icon to save the current state of your game.  When you later select Restore, everything will be exactly as it is now.
序章: 選擇此圖示以存檔當前遊戲狀態。當你稍後選擇「還原」時，一切都將與現在完全相同。

Introduction: This adjusts your hero's speed, within the limits of your computer's capabilities.
序章: 此項調整你英雄的速度，受電腦能力限制。

Introduction: This control will adjust the volume on some sound boards and synthesizers.
序章: 此控制可調整某些音效卡和音樂合成器的音量。

Introduction: You are carrying so much that you can hardly move.  You'd better drop something soon.
序章: 你帶著太多東西，幾乎無法移動。你最好趕快丟掉一些東西。

Introduction: The tingling sensation is wearing off.
序章: 刺痛感正在消退。

Introduction: The tingling sensation is gone.
序章: 刺痛感消失了。

Introduction: You've seen it before.
序章: 你之前見過它。

Introduction: You get no response.
序章: 沒有回應。

Introduction: You can't do much with that.
序章: 你用那個做不了什麼。

Introduction: It doesn't work.
序章: 它不起作用。

Introduction: Memory fragmented.
序章: 記憶體零碎化。

Introduction: Who cares
序章: 誰在乎

Introduction: Debug
序章: 除錯

Introduction: You are no longer overloaded.  That's a load off your mind!
序章: 你不再超載了。那真是輕鬆啊！

Introduction: Hope we'll see you again soon!
序章: 希望很快再看到你！

Introduction: Getting out while you still can?
序章: 趁著還能逃現在就要跑嗎？

Introduction: Quit
序章: 離開

Introduction: Don't Quit
序章: 不離開

Introduction: To learn about the other icons, first click here, then pass the question mark over the other icons.
序章: 要瞭解其他圖示，先按一下這裡，然後把問號移到其他圖示上。

Introduction: Use this icon to bring up your inventory window.
序章: 使用此圖示開啟你的物品欄視窗。

Introduction: Use this icon to look at things.
序章: 使用此圖示查看物品。

Introduction: %d of %d
序章: %d / %d

Introduction: Your spells don't seem to work here.
序章: 你的法術在這裡似乎不起作用。

Introduction: So You've Become A Hero!               [ Score: %d of 500 ]
序章: 你成為英雄了！               [ 分數：%d / 500 ]

Introduction: Quest for Glory I                       [ Score: %d of 500 ]
序章: 英雄傳奇 I                        [ 分數：%d / 500 ]

Introduction: Use this icon to talk to other characters.
序章: 使用此圖示與其他角色交談。

Introduction: Select this icon to use your current inventory object.
序章: 選擇此圖示以使用目前的物品欄物品。

Introduction: Use this icon to move your character.
序章: 使用此圖示移動你的角色。

Introduction: Got any %d's?
序章: 「有 %d 個嗎？」

Introduction: Wow!  You're Really A Hero!  [score %d of 500]
序章: 哇！你真的成為英雄了！[分數 %d / 500]

Introduction: Error in (%s check:) totAngle [%d] != +-360
序章: (%s check:) 中的錯誤 totAngle [%d] != +-360

Introduction: DRAGGING: %d, %d
序章: 拖動：%d, %d

Introduction: The file '%s' already exists
序章: 檔案「%s」已存在

Introduction: Error opening '%s'
序章: 開啟「%s」時出錯

Introduction: Log file "%s" exists
序章: 日誌檔「%s」已存在

Introduction: Comment line %d of %d:
序章: 註解行 %d / %d：

Introduction: File: %s
序章: 檔案：%s

Introduction: %s [Collection of size %d]
序章: %s [集合大小 %d]

Introduction: %s [List of size %d]
序章: %s [列表大小 %d]

Introduction: %s [Set of size %d]
序章: %s [集合大小 %d]
